Contents of https://github.com/gastownhall/beads:
## bd - Beads

[](#bd---beads)

**Distributed graph issue tracker for AI agents, powered by [Dolt](https://github.com/dolthub/dolt).**

**Platforms:** macOS, Linux, Windows, FreeBSD

[![License](https://camo.githubusercontent.com/2638502991fd9e3be00ccd91c3e6588494d24d4271b8e1fe406c62156826c533/68747470733a2f2f696d672e736869656c64732e696f2f6769746875622f6c6963656e73652f676173746f776e68616c6c2f6265616473)](/gastownhall/beads/blob/main/LICENSE) [![Go Report Card](https://camo.githubusercontent.com/a78caf72b1dd4537c4855fe85e04893753e306c943e9d211783407f646c9f4fc/68747470733a2f2f676f7265706f7274636172642e636f6d2f62616467652f6769746875622e636f6d2f737465766579656767652f6265616473)](https://goreportcard.com/report/github.com/steveyegge/beads) [![Release](https://camo.githubusercontent.com/584a70295ee589a2e7cae5524f9a790557a7f7cf3128e155c8c12a029644cc90/68747470733a2f2f696d672e736869656c64732e696f2f6769746875622f762f72656c656173652f676173746f776e68616c6c2f6265616473)](https://github.com/gastownhall/beads/releases) [![npm version](https://camo.githubusercontent.com/23904a9aebe5248dc61c200c0b61e3f40539fd92e7f8d91500d12c188980cce5/68747470733a2f2f696d672e736869656c64732e696f2f6e706d2f762f4062656164732f6264)](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@beads/bd) [![PyPI](https://camo.githubusercontent.com/5b012486136be90fdf526de0b121faa1fa5521048e075e5b343550dd0618a625/68747470733a2f2f696d672e736869656c64732e696f2f707970692f762f62656164732d6d6370)](https://pypi.org/project/beads-mcp/)

**Docs:** [https://gastownhall.github.io/beads/](https://gastownhall.github.io/beads/)

Beads provides a persistent, structured memory for coding agents. It replaces messy markdown plans with a dependency-aware graph, allowing agents to handle long-horizon tasks without losing context.

## ⚡ Quick Start

[](#-quick-start)

# Install beads CLI (system-wide - don't clone this repo into your project)
curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/gastownhall/beads/main/scripts/install.sh | bash

# Initialize in YOUR project
cd your-project
bd init

# Tell your agent
echo "Use 'bd' for task tracking" \>> AGENTS.md

**Note:** Beads is a CLI tool you install once and use everywhere. You don't need to clone this repository into your project.

## 🛠 Features

[](#-features)

*   **[Dolt](https://github.com/dolthub/dolt)\-Powered:** Version-controlled SQL database with cell-level merge, native branching, and built-in sync via Dolt remotes.
*   **Agent-Optimized:** JSON output, dependency tracking, and auto-ready task detection.
*   **Zero Conflict:** Hash-based IDs (`bd-a1b2`) prevent merge collisions in multi-agent/multi-branch workflows.
*   **Compaction:** Semantic "memory decay" summarizes old closed tasks to save context window.
*   **Messaging:** Message issue type with threading (`--thread`), ephemeral lifecycle, and mail delegation.
*   **Graph Links:** `relates_to`, `duplicates`, `supersedes`, and `replies_to` for knowledge graphs.

## 📖 Essential Commands

[](#-essential-commands)

Command

Action

`bd ready`

List tasks with no open blockers.

`bd create "Title" -p 0`

Create a P0 task.

`bd update <id> --claim`

Atomically claim a task (sets assignee + in\_progress).

`bd dep add <child> <parent>`

Link tasks (blocks, related, parent-child).

`bd show <id>`

View task details and audit trail.

## 🔗 Hierarchy & Workflow

[](#-hierarchy--workflow)

Beads supports hierarchical IDs for epics:

*   `bd-a3f8` (Epic)
*   `bd-a3f8.1` (Task)
*   `bd-a3f8.1.1` (Sub-task)

**Stealth Mode:** Run `bd init --stealth` to use Beads locally without committing files to the main repo. Perfect for personal use on shared projects. See [Git-Free Usage](#-git-free-usage) below.

**Contributor vs Maintainer:** When working on open-source projects:

*   **Contributors** (forked repos): Run `bd init --contributor` to route planning issues to a separate repo (e.g., `~/.beads-planning`). Keeps experimental work out of PRs.
*   **Maintainers** (write access): Beads auto-detects maintainer role via SSH URLs or HTTPS with credentials. Only need `git config beads.role maintainer` if using GitHub HTTPS without credentials but you have write access.

## 📦 Installation

[](#-installation)

brew install beads           # macOS / Linux (recommended)
npm install -g @beads/bd     # Node.js users

**Other methods:** [install script](/gastownhall/beads/blob/main/docs/INSTALLING.md#quick-install-script-all-platforms) | [go install](/gastownhall/beads/blob/main/docs/INSTALLING.md#a-note-on-go-install-capability) | [from source](/gastownhall/beads/blob/main/docs/INSTALLING.md#build-dependencies-contributors-only) | [Windows](/gastownhall/beads/blob/main/docs/INSTALLING.md#windows-11) | [Arch AUR](/gastownhall/beads/blob/main/docs/INSTALLING.md#linux)

**Requirements:** macOS, Linux, Windows, or FreeBSD. See [docs/INSTALLING.md](/gastownhall/beads/blob/main/docs/INSTALLING.md) for complete installation guide.

### Security And Verification

[](#security-and-verification)

Before trusting any downloaded binary, verify its checksum against the release `checksums.txt`.

The install scripts verify release checksums before install. For manual installs, do this verification yourself before first run.

On macOS, `scripts/install.sh` preserves the downloaded signature by default. Local ad-hoc re-signing is explicit opt-in via `BEADS_INSTALL_RESIGN_MACOS=1`.

See [docs/ANTIVIRUS.md](/gastownhall/beads/blob/main/docs/ANTIVIRUS.md) for Windows AV false-positive guidance and verification workflow.

## 💾 Storage Modes

[](#-storage-modes)

Beads uses [Dolt](https://github.com/dolthub/dolt) as its database. Two modes are available:

### Embedded Mode (default)

[](#embedded-mode-default)

bd init

Dolt runs in-process — no external server needed. Data lives in `.beads/embeddeddolt/`. Single-writer only (file locking enforced). This is the recommended mode for most users.

### Server Mode

[](#server-mode)

bd init --server

Connects to an external `dolt sql-server`. Data lives in `.beads/dolt/`. Supports multiple concurrent writers. Configure the connection with flags or environment variables:

Flag

Env Var

Default

`--server-host`

`BEADS_DOLT_SERVER_HOST`

`127.0.0.1`

`--server-port`

`BEADS_DOLT_SERVER_PORT`

`3307`

`--server-socket`

`BEADS_DOLT_SERVER_SOCKET`

(none; uses TCP)

`--server-user`

`BEADS_DOLT_SERVER_USER`

`root`

`BEADS_DOLT_PASSWORD`

(none)

`BEADS_DOLT_CLI_DIR`

local Dolt database path for CLI push/pull

**Unix domain sockets:** Use `--server-socket` to connect via a Unix socket instead of TCP. This avoids port conflicts between concurrent projects and is useful in sandboxed environments (e.g., Claude Code) where file-level access control is simpler than network allowlists. The Dolt server must be started with `dolt sql-server --socket <path>`. Auto-start is not supported in socket mode.

When `BEADS_DOLT_SERVER_MODE=1` points at a Dolt server managed outside Beads, set `BEADS_DOLT_CLI_DIR` if `bd dolt push` / `bd dolt pull` must use the local `dolt` CLI (for example git-protocol remotes or credentials that only exist in the current shell). Use the actual Dolt database directory, not the server root.

### Backup & Migration

[](#backup--migration)

Back up your database and migrate between modes using `bd backup`:

# Set up a backup destination and push
bd backup init /path/to/backup
bd backup sync

# Restore into a new project (any mode)
bd init           # or bd init --server
bd backup restore --force /path/to/backup

See [docs/DOLT.md](/gastownhall/beads/blob/main/docs/DOLT.md#migrating-between-backends) for full migration instructions.

## 🌐 Community Tools

[](#-community-tools)

See [docs/COMMUNITY\_TOOLS.md](/gastownhall/beads/blob/main/docs/COMMUNITY_TOOLS.md) for a curated list of community-built UIs, extensions, and integrations—including terminal interfaces, web UIs, editor extensions, and native apps.

## 🚀 Git-Free Usage

[](#-git-free-usage)

Beads works without git. The Dolt database is the storage backend — git integration (hooks, repo discovery, identity) is optional.

# Initialize without git
export BEADS\_DIR=/path/to/your/project/.beads
bd init --quiet --stealth

# All core commands work with zero git calls
bd create "Fix auth bug" -p 1 -t bug
bd ready --json
bd update bd-a1b2 --claim
bd prime
bd close bd-a1b2 "Fixed"

`BEADS_DIR` tells bd where to put the `.beads/` database directory, bypassing git repo discovery. `--stealth` sets `no-git-ops: true` in config, disabling all git hook installation and git operations.

This is useful for:

*   **Non-git VCS** (Sapling, Jujutsu, Piper) — no `.git/` directory needed
*   **Monorepos** — point `BEADS_DIR` at a specific subdirectory
*   **CI/CD** — isolated task tracking without repo-level side effects
*   **Evaluation/testing** — ephemeral databases in `/tmp`

For daemon mode without git, use `bd daemon start --local` (see [PR #433](https://github.com/gastownhall/beads/pull/433)).

## 📝 Documentation

[](#-documentation)

*   [Documentation site](https://gastownhall.github.io/beads/) (versioned) | [Installing](/gastownhall/beads/blob/main/docs/INSTALLING.md) | [Agent Workflow](/gastownhall/beads/blob/main/AGENT_INSTRUCTIONS.md) | [Copilot Setup](/gastownhall/beads/blob/main/docs/COPILOT_INTEGRATION.md) | [Articles](/gastownhall/beads/blob/main/ARTICLES.md) | [Sync Branch Mode](/gastownhall/beads/blob/main/docs/PROTECTED_BRANCHES.md) | [Troubleshooting](/gastownhall/beads/blob/main/docs/TROUBLESHOOTING.md) | [FAQ](/gastownhall/beads/blob/main/docs/FAQ.md)
*   [![Ask DeepWiki](https://camo.githubusercontent.com/0f5ae213ac378635adeb5d7f13cef055ad2f7d9a47b36de7b1c67dbe09f609ca/68747470733a2f2f6465657077696b692e636f6d2f62616467652e737667)](https://deepwiki.com/gastownhall/beads)
```

</details>

<summary>218 lines</summary>

```
1. #!/bin/bash
2. # =============================================================================
3. # Cross-Harness Skill Learner & Sync
4. # =============================================================================
5. # 1. Runs the 4-stage extraction pipeline on all harness histories
6. # 2. Tracks feedback/decay for existing learned skills
7. # 3. Syncs learned skills to all harnesses (codex, pi, openclaw)
8. # 4. Optionally prunes decayed skills
9. #
10. # Usage:
11. #   ./sync-learned-skills.sh              # full extract + sync
12. #   ./sync-learned-skills.sh --dry-run    # preview only
13. #   ./sync-learned-skills.sh --verbose    # detailed output
14. #   ./sync-learned-skills.sh --stats      # show usage/decay stats
15. #   ./sync-learned-skills.sh --prune      # archive decayed skills
16. #   ./sync-learned-skills.sh --sync-only  # skip extraction, just sync
17. # =============================================================================
18. 
19. set -e
20. 
21. DRY_RUN=false
22. VERBOSE=false
23. STATS_ONLY=false
24. PRUNE=false
25. SYNC_ONLY=false
26. USE_LLM=true  # LLM distillation is now default
27. SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
28. EXTRACTOR="$SCRIPT_DIR/extract-conversation-skills.py"
29. LEARNED_DIR="$HOME/.claude/skills/learned"
30. LOG_FILE="$SCRIPT_DIR/shared-learnings/sync.log"
31. LEARNINGS_MD="$SCRIPT_DIR/shared-learnings/learnings.md"
32. 
33. BLUE='\033[0;34m'; GREEN='\033[0;32m'; YELLOW='\033[1;33m'; CYAN='\033[0;36m'; NC='\033[0m'
34. log()     { echo -e "${BLUE}[LEARN]${NC} $1"; }
35. success() { echo -e "${GREEN}[OK]${NC} $1"; }
36. warn()    { echo -e "${YELLOW}[WARN]${NC} $1"; }
37. info()    { echo -e "${CYAN}[INFO]${NC} $1"; }
38. 
39. for arg in "$@"; do
40.     case $arg in
41.         --dry-run)   DRY_RUN=true ;;
42.         --verbose)   VERBOSE=true ;;
43.         --stats)     STATS_ONLY=true ;;
44.         --prune)     PRUNE=true ;;
45.         --sync-only) SYNC_ONLY=true ;;
46.         --no-llm)    USE_LLM=false ;;
47.     esac
48. done
49. 
50. mkdir -p "$SCRIPT_DIR/shared-learnings"
51. mkdir -p "$LEARNED_DIR"
52. 
53. # Stats-only mode
54. if $STATS_ONLY; then
55.     python3 "$EXTRACTOR" --stats ${VERBOSE:+--verbose}
56.     exit 0
57. fi
58. 
59. # Prune mode
60. if $PRUNE; then
61.     log "Pruning decayed skills..."
62.     python3 "$EXTRACTOR" --prune ${DRY_RUN:+--dry-run} ${VERBOSE:+--verbose}
63.     exit 0
64. fi
65. 
66. echo ""
67. echo "╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗"
68. echo "║   Cross-Harness Skill Learner                            ║"
69. echo "╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝"
70. echo ""
71. log "Started: $(date)"
72. 
73. # =============================================================================
74. # Step 1: Extract learned skills from all harness histories
75. # =============================================================================
76. 
77. if ! $SYNC_ONLY; then
78.     log "Step 1: Extracting patterns from conversation histories (4-stage pipeline)..."
79. 
80.     if [ ! -f "$EXTRACTOR" ]; then
81.         warn "Extractor not found: $EXTRACTOR"
82.         warn "Skipping extraction — will still sync existing learned skills"
83.     else
84.         EXTRACT_ARGS="--output $LEARNED_DIR"
85.         $VERBOSE && EXTRACT_ARGS="$EXTRACT_ARGS --verbose"
86.         $DRY_RUN && EXTRACT_ARGS="$EXTRACT_ARGS --dry-run"
87.         # Incremental extraction: only process new conversation files
88.         EXTRACT_ARGS="$EXTRACT_ARGS --incremental"
89. 
90.         # LLM distillation is now default; use --no-llm to disable
91.         if $USE_LLM; then
92.             EXTRACT_ARGS="$EXTRACT_ARGS --llm"
93.             log "LLM distillation enabled (default; use --no-llm to disable)"
94.         else
95.             log "LLM distillation disabled (--no-llm)"
96.         fi
97. 
98.         if python3 "$EXTRACTOR" $EXTRACT_ARGS; then
99.             success "Extraction complete"
100.         else
101.             warn "Extraction failed — see error output above"
102.         fi
103.     fi
104. else
105.     log "Step 1: Skipping extraction (--sync-only)"
106. fi
107. 
108. LEARNED_COUNT=$(ls "$LEARNED_DIR"/*.md 2>/dev/null | grep -v "/.usage" | wc -l | tr -d ' ')
109. log "Current learned skills: $LEARNED_COUNT"
110. 
111. # =============================================================================
112. # Step 2: Sync learned skills to all harnesses
113. # =============================================================================
114. 
115. log "Step 2: Syncing learned skills to all harnesses..."
116. SYNCED=0
117. 
118. sync_skill_to_harness() {
119.     local src_file="$1"
120.     local dst_dir="$2"
121.     local harness="$3"
122.     local skill_name
123.     skill_name=$(basename "$src_file" .md)
124. 
125.     # Skip dotfiles and SKILL.md
126.     [[ "$skill_name" == .* ]] && return 0
127.     [[ "$skill_name" == "SKILL" ]] && return 0
128. 
129.     local dst_skill_dir="$dst_dir/$skill_name"
130. 
131.     if $DRY_RUN; then
132.         info "  [dry-run] Would sync: $skill_name → $harness"
133.         return 0
134.     fi
135. 
136.     if [ -f "$dst_skill_dir/SKILL.md" ] && \
137.        [ "$src_file" -ot "$dst_skill_dir/SKILL.md" ]; then
138.         $VERBOSE && info "  Up-to-date: $skill_name in $harness"
139.         return 0
140.     fi
141. 
142.     mkdir -p "$dst_skill_dir"
143.     cp "$src_file" "$dst_skill_dir/SKILL.md"
144.     info "  Synced: $skill_name → $harness"
145.     SYNCED=$((SYNCED + 1))
146. }
147. 
148. # Sync to codex
149. if [ -d "$HOME/.codex/skills" ]; then
150.     for skill_file in "$LEARNED_DIR"/*.md; do
151.         [ -f "$skill_file" ] || continue
152.         sync_skill_to_harness "$skill_file" "$HOME/.codex/skills" "codex"
153.     done
154.     success "Codex: synced"
155. fi
156. 
157. # Sync to pi
158. PI_SKILLS_DIR="$HOME/.pi/agent/skills"
159. if [ -d "$HOME/.pi/agent" ]; then
160.     mkdir -p "$PI_SKILLS_DIR"
161.     for skill_file in "$LEARNED_DIR"/*.md; do
162.         [ -f "$skill_file" ] || continue
163.         sync_skill_to_harness "$skill_file" "$PI_SKILLS_DIR" "pi"
164.     done
165.     success "Pi: synced"
166. fi
167. 
168. # Sync to openclaw workspace skills
169. OPENCLAW_WS_SKILLS="$HOME/.openclaw/workspace/skills"
170. if [ -d "$HOME/.openclaw/workspace" ]; then
171.     mkdir -p "$OPENCLAW_WS_SKILLS"
172.     for skill_file in "$LEARNED_DIR"/*.md; do
173.         [ -f "$skill_file" ] || continue
174.         skill_name=$(basename "$skill_file" .md)
175.         [[ "$skill_name" == .* ]] && continue
176.         [[ "$skill_name" == "SKILL" ]] && continue
177.         dst_dir="$OPENCLAW_WS_SKILLS/$skill_name"
178.         if $DRY_RUN; then
179.             info "  [dry-run] Would sync: $skill_name → openclaw"
180.         elif [ ! -f "$dst_dir/SKILL.md" ] || [ "$skill_file" -nt "$dst_dir/SKILL.md" ]; then
181.             mkdir -p "$dst_dir"
182.             cp "$skill_file" "$dst_dir/SKILL.md"
183.             info "  Synced: $skill_name → openclaw"
184.             SYNCED=$((SYNCED + 1))
185.         fi
186.     done
187.     success "OpenClaw: synced"
188. fi
189. 
190. # Claude skills directory symlink
191. CLAUDE_SKILLS_LEARNED="$HOME/.claude/skills/learned"
192. if [ -d "$HOME/.claude/skills" ] && [ ! -d "$CLAUDE_SKILLS_LEARNED" ]; then
193.     ln -sfn "$LEARNED_DIR" "$CLAUDE_SKILLS_LEARNED" 2>/dev/null || true
194. fi
195. 
196. # =============================================================================
197. # Step 3: Update learnings log
198. # =============================================================================
199. 
200. log "Step 3: Updating shared learnings log..."
201. {
202.     echo ""
203.     echo "## Sync: $(date)"
204.     echo "- Learned skills total: $LEARNED_COUNT"
205.     echo "- Newly synced: $SYNCED"
206.     echo "- Harnesses updated: codex, pi, openclaw"
207. } >> "$LEARNINGS_MD" 2>/dev/null || true
208. 
209. echo "$(date) | learned=$LEARNED_COUNT synced=$SYNCED dry_run=$DRY_RUN" >> "$LOG_FILE" 2>/dev/null || true
210. 
211. echo ""
212. if $DRY_RUN; then
213.     warn "DRY RUN — no changes written"
214. else
215.     success "Sync complete: $SYNCED skills propagated across harnesses"
216.     success "Learned skills total: $LEARNED_COUNT"
217. fi
218. echo ""
```

</details>

/Users/chazh/Documents/GitHub/ai-skillweave/install.sh (lines 1-100)

<details>
<summary>100 lines</summary>

```
1. #!/bin/bash
2. # =============================================================================
3. # install.sh — Master setup for Ollama Agent Harness
4. # =============================================================================
5. # One-command setup for all AI agent harnesses on a new machine.
6. # Configures: Claude Code (MCP), OpenClaw, Pi, Codex, Copilot CLI, shell skills layer.
7. #
8. # Requirements:
9. #   - Ollama installed and running (https://ollama.com)
10. #   - At least one model: ollama pull <model-name>
11. #   - Claude Code, OpenClaw, Pi, Codex, and/or Copilot installed as needed
12. #
13. # Usage:
14. #   ./install.sh                           # Full setup (all harnesses + all skills)
15. #   ./install.sh --without-science         # Skip K-Dense scientific skills
16. #   ./install.sh --model llama3.2:3b       # Use a specific Ollama model
17. #   ./install.sh --skip-skills             # Skip ECC skills installation
18. #   ./install.sh --only claude             # Only configure claude
19. #   ./install.sh --only openclaw           # Only configure openclaw
20. #   ./install.sh --uninstall               # Remove shell integrations
21. #   ./install.sh --verify                  # Run health check only
22. # =============================================================================
23. set -e
24. 
25. REPO_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
26. 
27. # =============================================================================
28. # Platform detection
29. # =============================================================================
30. case "$(uname -s)" in
31.     Darwin*)                 OS_TYPE="macOS" ;;
32.     Linux*)                  OS_TYPE="Linux" ;;
33.     MINGW*|MSYS*|CYGWIN*)   OS_TYPE="Windows" ;;  # untested; use WSL
34.     *)                       OS_TYPE="Unknown" ;;
35. esac
36. 
37. # Detect user's login shell for RC file references
38. case "${SHELL##*/}" in
39.     zsh)  USER_SHELL="zsh";  SHELL_RC="~/.zshrc" ;;
40.     bash) USER_SHELL="bash"; SHELL_RC="~/.bashrc" ;;
41.     *)    USER_SHELL="bash"; SHELL_RC="~/.bashrc" ;;
42. esac
43. 
44. # Platform-aware package install hint
45. case "$OS_TYPE" in
46.     macOS)   PKG_HINT="brew install" ;;
47.     Linux)
48.         if command -v apt-get >/dev/null 2>&1; then
49.             PKG_HINT="apt-get install"
50.         elif command -v dnf >/dev/null 2>&1; then
51.             PKG_HINT="dnf install"
52.         elif command -v pacman >/dev/null 2>&1; then
53.             PKG_HINT="pacman -S"
54.         else
55.             PKG_HINT="your package manager to install"
56.         fi
57.         ;;
58.     Windows) PKG_HINT="your package manager to install" ;;
59.     *)       PKG_HINT="your package manager to install" ;;
60. esac
61. 
62. BLUE='\033[0;34m'; GREEN='\033[0;32m'; YELLOW='\033[1;33m'; RED='\033[0;31m'; CYAN='\033[0;36m'; NC='\033[0m'
63. log()     { echo -e "${BLUE}[INSTALL]${NC} $1"; }
64. success() { echo -e "${GREEN}[OK]${NC} $1"; }
65. warn()    { echo -e "${YELLOW}[WARN]${NC} $1"; }
66. error()   { echo -e "${RED}[ERROR]${NC} $1"; exit 1; }
67. section() { echo ""; echo -e "${CYAN}═══════════════════════════════════════${NC}"; echo -e "${CYAN}  $1${NC}"; echo -e "${CYAN}═══════════════════════════════════════${NC}"; }
68. 
69. # =============================================================================
70. # Parse arguments
71. # =============================================================================
72. OLLAMA_MODEL="qwen3.6"
73. SKIP_SKILLS=false
74. WITH_SCIENCE=true
75. WITH_CURATED=true
76. WITH_BIO=true
77. WITH_LEARN=true
78. ONLY_TARGET=""
79. 
80. while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
81.     case "$1" in
82.         --model)            OLLAMA_MODEL="$2"; shift 2 ;;
83.         --skip-skills)      SKIP_SKILLS=true; shift ;;
84.         --with-science)     WITH_SCIENCE=true; shift ;;
85.         --without-science)  WITH_SCIENCE=false; shift ;;
86.         --with-curated)     WITH_CURATED=true; shift ;;
87.         --without-curated)  WITH_CURATED=false; shift ;;
88.         --with-bio)         WITH_BIO=true; shift ;;
89.         --without-bio)      WITH_BIO=false; shift ;;
90.         --learn)            WITH_LEARN=true; shift ;;
91.         --no-learn)         WITH_LEARN=false; shift ;;
92.         --only)             ONLY_TARGET="$2"; shift 2 ;;
93.         --uninstall)
94.             "$REPO_DIR/safe-install.sh" --uninstall
95.             exit 0
96.             ;;
97.         --verify)
98.             bash "$REPO_DIR/scripts/verify.sh"
99.             exit 0
100.             ;;
```

</details>

### ✅ `view`

/Users/chazh/Documents/GitHub/ai-skillweave/hooks/learning-capture.sh

<details>
<summary>100 lines</summary>

```
1. #!/bin/bash
2. # BMO-Style Learning Event Capture
3. # Runs on UserPromptSubmit to detect corrections as they happen
4. # Based on: https://github.com/joelhans/bmo-agent/blob/main/skills/learning-event-capture.md
5. 
6. set -e
7. 
8. LEARNINGS_DIR="$HOME/.claude/skills/learned/events"
9. mkdir -p "$LEARNINGS_DIR"
10. 
11. # Read stdin from Claude Code hook
12. INPUT_JSON=$(cat)
13. if [ -z "$INPUT_JSON" ]; then
14.   exit 0
15. fi
16. 
17. # Extract user message
18. USER_MSG=$(echo "$INPUT_JSON" | python3 << 'PYEOF'
19. import json, sys
20. try:
21.     data = json.load(sys.stdin)
22.     msg = data.get("message", "")
23.     session = data.get("session_id", "unknown")
24.     project = data.get("cwd", "unknown")
25.     print(f"{session}\t{project}\t{msg}")
26. except Exception as e:
27.     print("")
28. PYEOF
29. )
30. 
31. if [ -z "$USER_MSG" ]; then
32.   exit 0
33. fi
34. 
35. SESSION_ID=$(echo "$USER_MSG" | cut -f1)
36. PROJECT=$(echo "$USER_MSG" | cut -f2)
37. MESSAGE=$(echo "$USER_MSG" | cut -f3-)
38. 
39. # Detect learning events using BMO's recognition cues
40. LEARNING_TYPE=""
41. CONFIDENCE="low"
42. 
43. # === CORRECTIONS (type: "correction") ===
44. # User says "no", "not that", "wrong", "actually..."
45. # User repeats an instruction you missed
46. # User undoes something you did
47. if echo "$MESSAGE" | grep -qiE "^no[,\.!]|\b(not that|wrong|incorrect|that's not|that's not what|you should|you must)\b"; then
48.   LEARNING_TYPE="correction"
49.   CONFIDENCE="high"
50. fi
51. 
52. # User provides the correct answer after your attempt
53. if echo "$MESSAGE" | grep -qiE "\b(actually|i meant|what i meant|what i want|instead use|use .* instead)\b"; then
54.   LEARNING_TYPE="correction"
55.   CONFIDENCE="high"
56. fi
57. 
58. # === PREFERENCES (type: "preference") ===
59. # User specifies a style choice
60. # User chooses between options you offered
61. # User describes their workflow or habits
62. # User says "I always...", "I prefer...", "I like..."
63. if echo "$MESSAGE" | grep -qiE "\b(i prefer|we prefer|i always|we always|i usually|we usually|i like|we like)\b"; then
64.   LEARNING_TYPE="preference"
65.   CONFIDENCE="medium"
66. fi
67. 
68. # === PATTERNS (type: "pattern") ===
69. # Recurring task types or workflows
70. if echo "$MESSAGE" | grep -qiE "\b(best practice|convention|standard|idiomatic|typically|usually|normally)\b"; then
71.   LEARNING_TYPE="pattern"
72.   CONFIDENCE="medium"
73. fi
74. 
75. # If we detected a learning event, log it
76. if [ -n "$LEARNING_TYPE" ]; then
77.   TIMESTAMP=$(date +%Y%m%d_%H%M%S)
78.   EVENT_FILE="$LEARNINGS_DIR/${TIMESTAMP}_${SESSION_ID:0:8}.json"
79.   
80.   # Escape message for JSON
81.   ESCAPED_MSG=$(echo "$MESSAGE" | python3 -c 'import json,sys; print(json.dumps(sys.stdin.read().strip()))')
82.   
83.   cat > "$EVENT_FILE" << EVENTEOF
84. {
85.   "timestamp": "$(date -Iseconds)",
86.   "session": "$SESSION_ID",
87.   "project": "$PROJECT",
88.   "type": "$LEARNING_TYPE",
89.   "confidence": "$CONFIDENCE",
90.   "message": $ESCAPED_MSG,
91.   "status": "pending",
92.   "captured_by": "learning-capture.sh"
93. }
94. EVENTEOF
95.   
96.   echo "[LEARNING] Captured $LEARNING_TYPE ($CONFIDENCE): ${MESSAGE:0:80}..."
97. fi
98. 
99. exit 0
100.
```

</details>

1. #!/bin/bash
2. # BMO-Style Session Reflection Hook
3. # Runs at session end to consolidate learning events into skills
4. 
5. set -e
6. 
7. echo "[REFLECTION] Session ending, consolidating learnings..."
8. 
9. # Run consolidation script
10. SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")/.." && pwd)"
11. CONSOLIDATE_SCRIPT="$SCRIPT_DIR/scripts/consolidate-learnings.py"
12. 
13. if [ -f "$CONSOLIDATE_SCRIPT" ]; then
14.   python3 "$CONSOLIDATE_SCRIPT"
15. else
16.   echo "[WARN] Consolidation script not found: $CONSOLIDATE_SCRIPT"
17. fi
18. 
19. exit 0
20.

/Users/chazh/Documents/GitHub/ai-skillweave/hooks/codesight-redirect.sh

<details>
<summary>85 lines</summary>

```
1. #!/bin/bash
2. # =============================================================================
3. # codesight-redirect.sh — PreToolUse hook
4. # =============================================================================
5. # Intercepts broad Glob/Grep codebase searches when codesight is configured.
6. # Reminds Claude to call codesight_get_summary first (once per session/project).
7. #
8. # Exit 2 = block tool + send message to Claude
9. # Exit 0 = allow tool to proceed
10. # =============================================================================
11. 
12. input=$(cat)
13. 
14. # Extract tool name, pattern, and path from hook payload
15. tool=$(echo "$input" | python3 -c "
16. import json, sys
17. d = json.load(sys.stdin)
18. print(d.get('tool_name', ''))
19. " 2>/dev/null)
20. 
21. session_id=$(echo "$input" | python3 -c "
22. import json, sys
23. d = json.load(sys.stdin)
24. print(d.get('session_id', 'default')[:24])
25. " 2>/dev/null || echo "default")
26. 
27. pattern=$(echo "$input" | python3 -c "
28. import json, sys
29. inp = json.load(sys.stdin).get('tool_input', {})
30. print(inp.get('pattern', '') or inp.get('glob', ''))
31. " 2>/dev/null)
32. 
33. path=$(echo "$input" | python3 -c "
34. import json, sys
35. inp = json.load(sys.stdin).get('tool_input', {})
36. print(inp.get('path', '') or inp.get('file_path', ''))
37. " 2>/dev/null)
38. 
39. # Only intercept broad searches (** glob patterns indicating codebase exploration)
40. is_broad=false
41. [[ "$pattern" == *"**"* ]] && is_broad=true
42. [[ -z "$path" && "$tool" == "Glob" ]] && is_broad=true
43. $is_broad || exit 0
44. 
45. # Walk up from search path to find nearest .codesight/ directory
46. check_dir="${path:-$(pwd)}"
47. [[ -f "$check_dir" ]] && check_dir=$(dirname "$check_dir")
48. codesight_dir=""
49. depth=0
50. while [[ "$check_dir" != "/" && $depth -lt 10 ]]; do
51.     if [[ -d "$check_dir/.codesight" ]]; then
52.         codesight_dir="$check_dir"
53.         break
54.     fi
55.     check_dir=$(dirname "$check_dir")
56.     ((depth++))
57. done
58. 
59. # No codesight configured — allow through
60. [[ -n "$codesight_dir" ]] || exit 0
61. 
62. # Only remind once per session per project (state tracked in /tmp)
63. state_dir="/tmp/claude-hooks-${session_id}"
64. mkdir -p "$state_dir"
65. # Hash the project dir for a short key
66. state_key=$(echo "$codesight_dir" | md5 2>/dev/null || echo "$codesight_dir" | cksum | cut -d' ' -f1)
67. state_key="${state_key:0:8}"
68. reminded_file="$state_dir/codesight-${state_key}.done"
69. 
70. # Already reminded this session — allow through
71. [[ -f "$reminded_file" ]] && exit 0
72. 
73. touch "$reminded_file"
74. 
75. cat << EOF
76. CODESIGHT REDIRECT: $codesight_dir has codesight configured.
77. 
78. Before running broad $tool searches, call codesight_get_summary first:
79.   mcp__codesight__codesight_get_summary(directory="$codesight_dir")
80. 
81. This returns a compact project map (~500 tokens) — routes, schema, hot files, env vars.
82. After calling codesight_get_summary, run targeted searches as needed (this reminder won't repeat).
83. EOF
84. exit 2
85.
```

</details>

/Users/chazh/Documents/GitHub/ai-skillweave/install.sh (lines 100-250)

<details>

```
100.             ;;
101.         --help|-h)
102.             echo "Usage: ./install.sh [--model MODEL] [--skip-skills] [--with-science] [--without-science] [--with-bio] [--without-bio] [--only TARGET] [--uninstall] [--verify]"
103.             echo ""
104.             echo "  --with-science     Include K-Dense scientific skills (default)"
105.             echo "  --without-science  Skip K-Dense scientific skills"
106.             echo "  --with-bio         Include ClawBio bioinformatics skills (default)"
107.             echo "  --without-bio      Skip ClawBio bioinformatics skills"
108.             exit 0
109.             ;;
110.         *) shift ;;
111.     esac
112. done
113. 
114. echo ""
115. echo "╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗"
116. echo "║   ai-skillweave — Full Setup                           ║"
117. echo "╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝"
118. echo ""
119. log "Platform: $OS_TYPE ($USER_SHELL)"
120. log "Model:    $OLLAMA_MODEL"
121. log "Repo:     $REPO_DIR"
122. echo ""
123. 
124. if [ "$OS_TYPE" = "Windows" ]; then
125.     warn "Native Windows detected. This installer targets bash-based systems."
126.     warn "For best results, run inside WSL (Windows Subsystem for Linux)."
127.     warn "Continuing anyway — some steps may not apply."
128.     echo ""
129. fi
130. 
131. # =============================================================================
132. # Step 0a: Bootstrap prerequisites (git, node/npm, ollama, python3)
133. # =============================================================================
134. section "Bootstrap"
135. 
136. _install_if_missing() {
137.     local cmd="$1"
138.     local name="$2"
139.     local install_cmd="$3"
140.     if command -v "$cmd" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
141.         success "$name already installed: $(command -v "$cmd")"
142.         return 0
143.     fi
144.     log "Installing $name..."
145.     if eval "$install_cmd"; then
146.         success "$name installed"
147.         return 0
148.     else
149.         warn "$name installation failed — install manually, then re-run"
150.         return 1
151.     fi
152. }
153. 
154. # --- git ---
155. if [ "$OS_TYPE" = "macOS" ]; then
156.     _install_if_missing git "git" "brew install git"
157. else
158.     if command -v apt-get >/dev/null 2>&1; then
159.         _install_if_missing git "git" "sudo apt-get update -qq && sudo apt-get install -y -qq git"
160.     elif command -v dnf >/dev/null 2>&1; then
161.         _install_if_missing git "git" "sudo dnf install -y git"
162.     elif command -v pacman >/dev/null 2>&1; then
163.         _install_if_missing git "git" "sudo pacman -S --noconfirm git"
164.     fi
165. fi
166. 
167. # --- node + npm ---
168. if ! command -v node >/dev/null 2>&1 || ! command -v npm >/dev/null 2>&1; then
169.     log "Node.js/npm not found — installing..."
170.     if [ "$OS_TYPE" = "macOS" ]; then
171.         brew install node 2>/dev/null || warn "brew install node failed"
172.     else
173.         # Use NodeSource setup for LTS Node 22
174.         curl -fsSL https://deb.nodesource.com/setup_22.x | bash - 2>/dev/null && \
175.             apt-get install -y nodejs 2>/dev/null || \
176.             warn "NodeSource install failed — install node manually"
177.     fi
178.     # Verify
179.     if command -v node >/dev/null 2>&1 && command -v npm >/dev/null 2>&1; then
180.         success "Node.js $(node --version) + npm $(npm --version) installed"
181.     else
182.         warn "Node.js/npm still not available — npx-based MCP servers won't work"
183.     fi
184. else
185.     success "Node.js $(node --version) + npm $(npm --version) already installed"
186. fi
187. 
188. # --- ollama ---
189. if ! command -v ollama >/dev/null 2>&1; then
190.     log "Ollama not found — installing..."
191.     curl -fsSL https://ollama.com/install.sh | sh && \
192.         success "Ollama installed" || \
193.         warn "Ollama install failed — install manually from https://ollama.com"
194. else
195.     success "Ollama already installed: $(command -v ollama)"
196. fi
197. 
198. # --- python3 + pip ---
199. if ! command -v python3 >/dev/null 2>&1; then
200.     log "python3 not found — installing..."
201.     if [ "$OS_TYPE" = "macOS" ]; then
202.         brew install python3 2>/dev/null || warn "brew install python3 failed"
203.     else
204.         if command -v apt-get >/dev/null 2>&1; then
205.             sudo apt-get update -qq && sudo apt-get install -y -qq python3 python3-pip python3-venv
206.         elif command -v dnf >/dev/null 2>&1; then
207.             sudo dnf install -y python3 python3-pip
208.         elif command -v pacman >/dev/null 2>&1; then
209.             sudo pacman -S --noconfirm python python-pip
210.         fi
211.     fi
212. fi
213. if command -v python3 >/dev/null 2>&1; then
214.     success "python3: $(command -v python3)"
215. else
216.     error "python3 is required but could not be installed automatically"
217. fi
218. 
219. # =============================================================================
220. # Step 0: Preflight checks
221. # =============================================================================
222. section "Preflight"
223. 
224. command -v python3 >/dev/null 2>&1 || error "python3 required ($PKG_HINT python3)"
225. 
226. # Install Python dependencies for auto-learning pipeline
227. log "Installing Python dependencies for skill extraction (sentence-transformers, scikit-learn)..."
228. _install_python_deps() {
229.     local pkgs="sentence-transformers>=3.0.0 scikit-learn>=1.5.0"
230.     # Detect virtualenv: --user is forbidden inside venv/conda
231.     local in_venv=""
232.     if python3 -c "import sys; sys.exit(0 if (sys.prefix != sys.base_prefix) else 1)" 2>/dev/null; then
233.         in_venv=1
234.     fi
235.     if [ -n "$in_venv" ]; then
236.         log "Detected Python virtualenv — skipping --user flag"
237.         if python3 -m pip install --quiet $pkgs 2>&1; then
238.             success "Python deps installed (venv)"
239.             return 0
240.         fi
241.     else
242.         # Try multiple pip invocation strategies; show errors so user can diagnose
243.         if python3 -m pip install --user --quiet $pkgs 2>&1; then
244.             success "Python deps installed (user site)"
245.             return 0
246.         fi
247.         warn "pip --user failed — trying without --user..."
248.         if python3 -m pip install --quiet $pkgs 2>&1; then
249.             success "Python deps installed (system site)"
250.             return 0
```

</details>

<sub>⏱️ 1m 49s</sub>

### ✅ `view`

**view the file at /Users/chazh/Documents/GitHub/ai-skillweave/configs/global-claude-md.md.**

/Users/chazh/Documents/GitHub/ai-skillweave/configs/global-claude-md.md

<details>
<summary>45 lines</summary>

```
1. # Global Instructions
2. 
3. ## Output Style
4. - Be concise. No summaries of what you just did — the user can see the diff.
5. - No trailing recaps, status tables, or "here's what happened" blocks unless asked.
6. - When editing files, show the change, not a paragraph explaining the change.
7. - Prefer 1-2 sentences over bullet lists. Skip pleasantries.
8. 
9. ## MCP Tool Usage — USE THESE PROACTIVELY
10. 
11. You have MCP servers available. Use them INSTEAD of raw built-in tools when applicable:
12. 
13. ### codesight (USE FIRST when exploring any repo)
14. - BEFORE using Grep/Glob/Read to explore a codebase, call `codesight_get_summary` or `codesight_scan` to get a compact map of routes, schema, components, and dependencies.
15. - Use `codesight_get_routes` instead of grepping for API endpoints.
16. - Use `codesight_get_schema` instead of reading model files one by one.
17. - Use `codesight_get_hot_files` to find high-impact files before exploring.
18. - This saves tokens: one codesight call (~500 tokens) replaces 10+ file reads (~5000+ tokens).
19. 
20. ### token-optimizer (USE for large contexts)
21. - When context is growing large (long conversations, many file reads), use `optimize_session` or `compress_text` to reduce token usage.
22. - Use `smart_read` instead of raw Read for large files — it returns only the relevant parts.
23. - Use `smart_grep` instead of raw Grep — it deduplicates and compresses results.
24. - Use `smart_diff` instead of raw git diff — it summarizes changes compactly.
25. 
26. ### context7 (USE for library/framework questions)
27. - When the user asks about ANY library, framework, or API, call `resolve-library-id` then `query-docs` BEFORE answering from training data.
28. - Your training data may be outdated. Context7 has current docs.
29. 
30. ### exa-web-search (USE for current information)
31. - When the user asks about anything that may have changed since your training cutoff, search first.
32. 
33. ### memory (USE for cross-session continuity)
34. - Save important context that should persist across conversations.
35. 
36. ### sequential-thinking (USE for complex reasoning)
37. - For multi-step problems, architecture decisions, or debugging — use this to structure your thinking.
38. 
39. ## Token Discipline
40. - Do NOT read entire files when you only need a few lines. Use line ranges.
41. - Do NOT re-read files you just edited — the harness tracks file state.
42. - Do NOT run exploratory commands (find, ls -R, cat) when codesight or smart_read can answer faster.
43. - Prefer targeted Grep over broad file scanning.
44. - When multiple independent searches are needed, run them in parallel (single message, multiple tool calls).
45.
```

</details>

<sub>⏱️ 1m 49s</sub>

### ✅ `view`

**view the file at /Users/chazh/Documents/GitHub/ai-skillweave/scripts/setup-hooks.sh.**

/Users/chazh/Documents/GitHub/ai-skillweave/scripts/setup-hooks.sh

<details>
<summary>90 lines</summary>

```
1. #!/bin/bash
2. # =============================================================================
3. # setup-hooks.sh — Install Claude Code pre-tool-use hooks
4. # =============================================================================
5. # Installs ~/.claude/hooks/codesight-redirect.sh and registers it in
6. # ~/.claude/settings.json as a PreToolUse hook for Glob|Grep.
7. #
8. # The hook intercepts broad codebase searches and redirects Claude to call
9. # codesight_get_summary first when codesight is configured for the project.
10. # This enforces the "codesight before Grep/Glob" rule from CLAUDE.md via
11. # a hard block rather than just a soft instruction.
12. #
13. # Safe to re-run (idempotent).
14. # =============================================================================
15. set -e
16. 
17. REPO_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")/.." && pwd)"
18. HOOKS_DIR="$HOME/.claude/hooks"
19. SETTINGS_FILE="$HOME/.claude/settings.json"
20. HOOK_SRC="$REPO_DIR/hooks/codesight-redirect.sh"
21. HOOK_DEST="$HOOKS_DIR/codesight-redirect.sh"
22. 
23. BLUE='\033[0;34m'; GREEN='\033[0;32m'; YELLOW='\033[1;33m'; NC='\033[0m'
24. log()     { echo -e "${BLUE}[HOOKS]${NC} $1"; }
25. success() { echo -e "${GREEN}[OK]${NC} $1"; }
26. warn()    { echo -e "${YELLOW}[WARN]${NC} $1"; }
27. 
28. # Install hook script
29. mkdir -p "$HOOKS_DIR"
30. cp "$HOOK_SRC" "$HOOK_DEST"
31. chmod +x "$HOOK_DEST"
32. success "Hook installed: $HOOK_DEST"
33. 
34. # Register in settings.json
35. if [ ! -f "$SETTINGS_FILE" ]; then
36.     echo '{}' > "$SETTINGS_FILE"
37. fi
38. 
39. python3 << PYEOF
40. import json
41. 
42. settings_path = "$SETTINGS_FILE"
43. hook_path = "$HOOK_DEST"
44. 
45. with open(settings_path) as f:
46.     settings = json.load(f)
47. 
48. # Validate and fix malformed hooks structure
49. if "hooks" not in settings:
50.     settings["hooks"] = {}
51. 
52. # Fix any hook category that is not a list or contains entries without a "hooks" array
53. for hook_type in list(settings["hooks"].keys()):
54.     entries = settings["hooks"][hook_type]
55.     if not isinstance(entries, list):
56.         settings["hooks"][hook_type] = []
57.         continue
58.     cleaned = []
59.     for entry in entries:
60.         if not isinstance(entry, dict):
61.             continue
62.         if "hooks" not in entry or not isinstance(entry["hooks"], list):
63.             # Fix malformed entry by adding empty hooks array
64.             entry["hooks"] = []
65.         cleaned.append(entry)
66.     settings["hooks"][hook_type] = cleaned
67. 
68. hook_entry = {
69.     "matcher": "Glob|Grep",
70.     "hooks": [{"type": "command", "command": hook_path}]
71. }
72. 
73. pre = settings["hooks"].get("PreToolUse", [])
74. # Remove stale codesight-redirect entries (idempotent)
75. pre = [h for h in pre if not any(
76.     hook.get("command", "").endswith("codesight-redirect.sh")
77.     for hook in h.get("hooks", [])
78. )]
79. pre.append(hook_entry)
80. settings["hooks"]["PreToolUse"] = pre
81. 
82. with open(settings_path, "w") as f:
83.     json.dump(settings, f, indent=2)
84. 
85. print(f"PreToolUse hook registered in {settings_path}")
86. PYEOF
87. 
88. success "Hooks configured in $SETTINGS_FILE"
89. log "Effect: Claude will be redirected to call codesight_get_summary before broad Glob/Grep searches in codesight-enabled projects"
90.
```

</details>

```
250.             return 0
251.         fi
252.     fi
253.     if command -v pip3 >/dev/null 2>&1 && pip3 install --quiet $pkgs 2>&1; then
254.         success "Python deps installed via pip3"
255.         return 0
256.     fi
257.     warn "All pip install attempts failed (see errors above)"
258.     warn "Try manually: python3 -m pip install sentence-transformers scikit-learn"
259.     return 1
260. }
261. _install_python_deps || warn "Python deps missing — the learning pipeline will fail until fixed"
262. 
263. command -v node >/dev/null 2>&1 || warn "node not found — npx-based MCP servers won't work ($PKG_HINT node)"
264. command -v git >/dev/null 2>&1 || warn "git not found — ECC skills install will be skipped"
265. 
266. if command -v ollama >/dev/null 2>&1; then
267.     success "ollama: $(command -v ollama)"
268.     if curl -s --max-time 3 http://localhost:11434/api/status >/dev/null 2>&1; then
269.         success "Ollama server: running"
270. 
271.         # Check if requested model is available
272.         if ollama list 2>/dev/null | grep -q "^${OLLAMA_MODEL}"; then
273.             success "Model available: $OLLAMA_MODEL"
274.         else
275.             warn "Model '$OLLAMA_MODEL' not in local list (may be cloud model — OK)"
276.             warn "If local model needed: ollama pull $OLLAMA_MODEL"
277.         fi
278.     else
279.         if [ "$OS_TYPE" = "macOS" ]; then
280.             warn "Ollama server not running — start it: open /Applications/Ollama.app"
281.         else
282.             warn "Ollama server not running — start it: ollama serve"
283.         fi
284.         warn "Continuing setup; start Ollama before using harnesses"
285.     fi
286. else
287.     warn "ollama not installed — install from https://ollama.com"
288.     warn "Continuing setup without Ollama; harnesses that need it will fail at runtime"
289. fi
290. 
291. # Disable Zscaler tray if running (macOS only, no sudo needed)
292. if [ "$OS_TYPE" = "macOS" ] && launchctl list 2>/dev/null | grep -q "zscaler"; then
293.     log "Detected Zscaler — disabling tray (no sudo needed)..."
294.     bash "$REPO_DIR/scripts/disable-zscaler.sh" --tray 2>/dev/null || true
295. fi
296. 
297. # =============================================================================
298. # Step 0b: Pre-install npx-based MCP servers globally (prevents timeout on first launch)
299. # =============================================================================
300. if command -v npm >/dev/null 2>&1; then
301.     log "Pre-installing MCP server packages globally (prevents first-launch timeouts)..."
302.     # Install silently in background; failures are non-fatal (npx will fallback)
303.     npm install -g @modelcontextprotocol/server-memory \
304.         @modelcontextprotocol/server-sequential-thinking \
305.         @upstash/context7-mcp@latest \
306.         @playwright/mcp \
307.         codesight \
308.         token-optimizer-mcp 2>/dev/null || warn "Some MCP packages failed to pre-install (npx fallback will work but may be slower on first launch)"
309.     success "MCP server packages pre-installed"
310. else
311.     warn "npm not found — MCP servers will be installed on first launch (may timeout)"
312. fi
313. 
314. # =============================================================================
315. # Harness installer helper
316. # =============================================================================
317. install_harness() {
318.     local name="$1"
319.     local cmd="$2"
320.     local install_cmd="$3"
321. 
322.     if command -v "$cmd" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
323.         success "$name already installed: $(command -v "$cmd")"
324.         return 0
325.     fi
326. 
327.     if ! command -v npm >/dev/null 2>&1; then
328.         warn "npm not found — cannot auto-install $name"
329.         warn "Install manually, then re-run install.sh"
330.         return 1
331.     fi
332. 
333.     log "Installing $name..."
334.     if eval "$install_cmd"; then
335.         success "$name installed"
336.         return 0
337.     else
338.         warn "$name installation failed — install manually, then re-run"
339.         return 1
340.     fi
341. }
342. 
343. # =============================================================================
344. # Step 1: Skills Layer (ECC + shell wrappers)
345. # =============================================================================
346. 
347. should_run() {
348.     [ -z "$ONLY_TARGET" ] || [ "$ONLY_TARGET" = "$1" ]
349. }
350. 
351. if ! $SKIP_SKILLS && should_run "skills"; then
352.     section "Skills Layer"
353.     # safe-install.sh may exit non-zero when 'source <rc>' fails in a non-interactive
354.     # subshell — this is expected and harmless. Capture exit code to report real failures.
355.     safe_exit=0
356.     SAFE_ARGS=""
357.     $WITH_SCIENCE && SAFE_ARGS="$SAFE_ARGS --with-science"
358.     $WITH_CURATED && SAFE_ARGS="$SAFE_ARGS --with-curated"
359.     $WITH_BIO && SAFE_ARGS="$SAFE_ARGS --with-bio"
360.     bash "$REPO_DIR/safe-install.sh" $SAFE_ARGS || safe_exit=$?
361.     if [ "$safe_exit" -eq 0 ]; then
362.         success "ECC skills + shell wrappers installed (run: source $SHELL_RC to activate)"
363.     else
364.         warn "safe-install.sh exited with code $safe_exit — shell integration may need manual source $SHELL_RC"
365.     fi
366.     # Rebuild cache with full skill counts (safe-install.sh caps Codex at 100).
367.     # update-ecc.sh also restores ECC working tree to actual HEAD.
368.     log "Rebuilding skills cache with full counts..."
369.     bash "$REPO_DIR/scripts/update-ecc.sh" --force || true
370.     success "Skills cache rebuilt (all sources, no caps)"
371. else
372.     log "Skipping skills layer"
373. fi
374. 
375. # =============================================================================
376. # Step 1b: Ollama integration→model mapping
377. # =============================================================================
378. if should_run "ollama" || [ -z "$ONLY_TARGET" ]; then
379.     section "Ollama Integrations"
380.     bash "$REPO_DIR/scripts/setup-ollama-config.sh" && success "Ollama integrations configured" || warn "Ollama config skipped (Ollama not started yet)"
381. fi
382. 
383. # =============================================================================
384. # Step 2: Claude Code MCP + Global Instructions
385. # =============================================================================
386. if should_run "claude"; then
387.     section "Claude Code"
388.     install_harness "Claude Code" "claude" "npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code"
389.     if [ -f "$HOME/.claude.json" ]; then
390.         bash "$REPO_DIR/scripts/setup-mcp.sh" && success "Claude Code MCP configured" || warn "MCP setup skipped (run Claude Code once, then re-run)"
391.     else
392.         warn "~/.claude.json not found — run Claude Code once to initialize, then re-run install.sh"
393.     fi
394.     # Global CLAUDE.md: proactive MCP usage + conciseness + token discipline
395.     bash "$REPO_DIR/scripts/setup-claude-md.sh" && success "Global CLAUDE.md installed" || warn "CLAUDE.md setup skipped"
396.     # Pre-tool-use hooks: enforce codesight before broad Glob/Grep searches
397.     bash "$REPO_DIR/scripts/setup-hooks.sh" && success "Claude Code hooks installed" || warn "Hooks setup skipped"
398.     
399.     # BMO-style learning capture hooks (real-time correction detection)
400.     bash "$REPO_DIR/scripts/setup-learning-hook.sh" && success "BMO learning capture hooks configured" || warn "Learning hook setup skipped"
401. fi
402. 
403. # =============================================================================
404. # Step 3: OpenClaw
405. # =============================================================================
406. if should_run "openclaw"; then
407.     section "OpenClaw"
408.     bash "$REPO_DIR/scripts/setup-openclaw.sh" --model "$OLLAMA_MODEL" && success "OpenClaw configured" || warn "OpenClaw setup skipped (not installed yet)"
409. fi
410. 
411. # =============================================================================
412. # Step 4: Codex
413. # =============================================================================
414. if should_run "codex"; then
415.     section "Codex"
416.     install_harness "Codex CLI" "codex" "npm install -g @openai/codex"
417.     bash "$REPO_DIR/scripts/setup-codex.sh" --model "$OLLAMA_MODEL" && success "Codex configured" || warn "Codex setup skipped (not installed yet)"
418. fi
419. 
420. # =============================================================================
421. # Step 5: Pi
422. # =============================================================================
423. if should_run "pi"; then
424.     section "Pi"
425.     install_harness "Pi" "pi" "npm install -g @mariozechner/pi-coding-agent"
426.     bash "$REPO_DIR/scripts/setup-pi.sh" --model "$OLLAMA_MODEL" && success "Pi configured" || warn "Pi setup skipped (not installed yet)"
427. fi
428. 
429. # =============================================================================
430. # Step 6: Copilot CLI
431. # =============================================================================
432. if should_run "copilot"; then
433.     section "Copilot CLI"
434.     install_harness "Copilot CLI" "copilot" "npm install -g @github/copilot"
435.     bash "$REPO_DIR/scripts/setup-copilot.sh" && success "Copilot CLI MCP configured" || warn "Copilot setup skipped (not installed yet)"
436. fi
437. 
438. # =============================================================================
439. # =============================================================================
440. # Step 5: Cleanup stale/invalid skills from previous installs
441. # =============================================================================
442. if [ -f "$REPO_DIR/scripts/cleanup-invalid-skills.sh" ]; then
443.     log "Cleaning up invalid skills from previous installs..."
444.     bash "$REPO_DIR/scripts/cleanup-invalid-skills.sh" 2>/dev/null || warn "Skill cleanup had issues (non-fatal)"
445. fi
446. 
447. # Done
448. # =============================================================================
449. echo ""
450. echo "╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗"
```

</details>

<sub>⏱️ 1m 54s</sub>

### ✅ `view`

**view the file at /Users/chazh/Documents/GitHub/ai-skillweave/safe-install.sh.**

/Users/chazh/Documents/GitHub/ai-skillweave/safe-install.sh (lines 1-100)

<details>
<summary>100 lines</summary>

```
1. # =============================================================================
2. # SAFE Install - Everything Claude Code Skills
3. # =============================================================================
4. # Zero-RISK installation with automatic shell integration
5. # Installs ALL skills: ECC + Anthropic Official + Curated + your conversation-derived skills
6. # Works with: claude, openclaw, codex, pi, copilot, ollama (direct or via ollama launch)
7. # Supports: .zshrc, .bashrc, .profile
8. # Platforms: macOS, Linux, Windows (WSL/MSYS2/Cygwin)
9. # =============================================================================
10. set -e
11. 
12. CLAUDE_DIR="$HOME/.claude"
13. ECC_DIR="$HOME/.claude-everything-claude-code"
14. CURATED_DIR="$HOME/.claude-curated-skills"
15. SCIENCE_DIR="$HOME/.claude-scientific-skills"
16. CLAWBIO_DIR="$HOME/.claude-clawbio-skills"
17. SKILLS_CACHE_DIR="$HOME/.claude/skills-cache"
18. 
19. # Platform detection
20. case "$(uname -s)" in
21.     Darwin*)                 OS_TYPE="macOS" ;;
22.     Linux*)                  OS_TYPE="Linux" ;;
23.     MINGW*|MSYS*|CYGWIN*)   OS_TYPE="Windows" ;;  # untested; use WSL
24.     *)                       OS_TYPE="Unknown" ;;
25. esac
26. 
27. # Detect user's default shell (not the shell running this script)
28. _detect_user_shell() {
29.     local shell_name="${SHELL##*/}"
30.     case "$shell_name" in
31.         zsh)  echo "zsh" ;;
32.         bash) echo "bash" ;;
33.         fish) echo "fish" ;;
34.         *)    echo "bash" ;;  # safe default
35.     esac
36. }
37. USER_SHELL="$(_detect_user_shell)"
38. 
39. # Colors
40. BLUE='\033[0;34m'
41. GREEN='\033[0;32m'
42. YELLOW='\033[1;33m'
43. RED='\033[0;31m'
44. NC='\033[0m'
45. 
46. log() { echo -e "${BLUE}[INFO]${NC} $1"; }
47. success() { echo -e "${GREEN}[OK]${NC} $1"; }
48. warn() { echo -e "${YELLOW}[WARN]${NC} $1"; }
49. error() { echo -e "${RED}[ERROR]${NC} $1"; }
50. 
51. # Parse arguments
52. WITH_CURATED=false
53. CURATED_ONLY=false
54. WITH_SCIENCE=true
55. WITH_BIO=true
56. WITH_LEARN=true
57. 
58. for arg in "$@"; do
59.     case $arg in
60.         --with-curated)
61.             WITH_CURATED=true
62.             shift
63.             ;;
64.         --curated-only)
65.             CURATED_ONLY=true
66.             WITH_CURATED=true
67.             shift
68.             ;;
69.         --with-science)
70.             WITH_SCIENCE=true
71.             shift
72.             ;;
73.         --without-science)
74.             WITH_SCIENCE=false
75.             shift
76.             ;;
77.         --with-bio)
78.             WITH_BIO=true
79.             shift
80.             ;;
81.         --without-bio)
82.             WITH_BIO=false
83.             shift
84.             ;;
85.         --no-learn)
86.             WITH_LEARN=false
87.             shift
88.             ;;
89.         --uninstall)
90.             # Handled in main
91.             ;;
92.     esac
93. done
94. 
95. echo ""
96. echo "╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗"
97. echo "║   SAFE Install - Everything Claude Code Skills          ║"
98. if [ "$WITH_CURATED" = true ]; then
99.     echo "║   + Curated Skills (Anthropic Official + Codex)          ║"
100. fi
```

</details>

1. # Codex (OpenAI Codex CLI) configuration
2. # Installed to ~/.codex/config.toml by scripts/setup-codex.sh
3. #
4. # Placeholders:
5. #   {{OLLAMA_MODEL}} - replace with model name e.g. qwen3:32b
6. 
7. # Use the ollama-launch custom provider (OpenAI-compatible, localhost:11434/v1)
8. # Note: model_provider="oss" is not recognized in config.toml (only works as --oss CLI flag)
9. model_provider = "ollama-launch"
10. model = "{{OLLAMA_MODEL}}"
11. 
12. # Allow writing to any path (not just workspace dir).
13. # Default is workspace-write which blocks edits to files outside the working directory.
14. # Change to "workspace-write" if you want stricter sandboxing.
15. sandbox = "danger-full-access"
16. 
17. [projects."{{HOME}}"]
18. trust_level = "trusted"
19. 
20. # Disabled: requires network access to chatgpt.com which may be blocked by corporate proxy
21. [plugins."github@openai-curated"]
22. enabled = false
23. 
24. [profiles.ollama-launch]
25. openai_base_url = "http://127.0.0.1:11434/v1/"
26. forced_login_method = "api"
27. model_provider = "ollama-launch"
28. 
29. [model_providers.ollama-launch]
30. name = "Ollama"
31. base_url = "http://127.0.0.1:11434/v1/"
32.
```

85.     "alsoAllow": [
86.       "ollama_web_search",
87.       "ollama_web_fetch"
88.     ],
89.     "profile": "coding",
90.     "web": {
91.       "fetch": {
92.         "enabled": true
93.       },
94.       "search": {
95.         "enabled": true
96.       }
97.     }
98.   }
99. }
100.

<sub>⏱️ 2m 2s</sub>

### ✅ `view`

**view the file at /Users/chazh/Documents/GitHub/ai-skillweave/scripts/setup-mcp.sh.**

/Users/chazh/Documents/GitHub/ai-skillweave/scripts/setup-mcp.sh

<details>
<summary>112 lines</summary>

```
1. #!/bin/bash
2. # =============================================================================
3. # setup-mcp.sh — Inject MCP servers into Claude Code (~/.claude.json)
4. # =============================================================================
5. # Merges configs/claude-mcp-servers.json into the mcpServers key of
6. # ~/.claude.json. Safe to re-run (idempotent — won't overwrite existing entries).
7. #
8. # Usage:
9. #   scripts/setup-mcp.sh [--force]
10. #   --force : overwrite existing MCP server entries with template values
11. # =============================================================================
12. set -e
13. 
14. REPO_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")/.." && pwd)"
15. TEMPLATE="$REPO_DIR/configs/claude-mcp-servers.json"
16. TARGET="$HOME/.claude.json"
17. 
18. BLUE='\033[0;34m'; GREEN='\033[0;32m'; YELLOW='\033[1;33m'; RED='\033[0;31m'; NC='\033[0m'
19. log()     { echo -e "${BLUE}[MCP]${NC} $1"; }
20. success() { echo -e "${GREEN}[OK]${NC} $1"; }
21. warn()    { echo -e "${YELLOW}[WARN]${NC} $1"; }
22. error()   { echo -e "${RED}[ERROR]${NC} $1"; exit 1; }
23. 
24. FORCE=false
25. [[ "$*" == *"--force"* ]] && FORCE=true
26. 
27. # Determine CA cert path (Zscaler or system default)
28. CA_CERT=""
29. if [ -f "$HOME/.mamba_ca_bundle.pem" ]; then
30.     CA_CERT="$HOME/.mamba_ca_bundle.pem"
31. elif [ -f "/etc/ssl/certs/ca-certificates.crt" ]; then
32.     CA_CERT="/etc/ssl/certs/ca-certificates.crt"
33. fi
34. 
35. echo ""
36. echo "╔══════════════════════════════════════╗"
37. echo "║   MCP Setup for Claude Code          ║"
38. echo "╚══════════════════════════════════════╝"
39. echo ""
40. 
41. # Prereqs
42. command -v python3 >/dev/null 2>&1 || error "python3 not found"
43. [ -f "$TARGET" ] || error "~/.claude.json not found. Install Claude Code first."
44. [ -f "$TEMPLATE" ] || error "Template not found: $TEMPLATE"
45. 
46. # Backup
47. BACKUP="$TARGET.bak_mcp_$(date +%Y%m%d_%H%M%S)"
48. cp "$TARGET" "$BACKUP"
49. success "Backup: $BACKUP"
50. 
51. # Apply
52. python3 << PYEOF
53. import json, os, sys
54. 
55. target_path = os.path.expanduser("~/.claude.json")
56. template_path = "$TEMPLATE"
57. ca_cert = "$CA_CERT"
58. home = os.path.expanduser("~")
59. npx_path = "$(which npx 2>/dev/null || echo "npx")"
60. force = $([[ "$FORCE" == "true" ]] && echo "True" || echo "False")
61. 
62. with open(target_path) as f:
63.     config = json.load(f)
64. 
65. with open(template_path) as f:
66.     template = json.load(f)
67. 
68. if "mcpServers" not in config:
69.     config["mcpServers"] = {}
70. 
71. servers = template.get("mcpServers", {})
72. added, updated, skipped = [], [], []
73. 
74. for name, cfg in servers.items():
75.     # Substitute placeholders
76.     cfg_str = json.dumps(cfg)
77.     cfg_str = cfg_str.replace("{{HOME}}", home)
78.     cfg_str = cfg_str.replace("{{CA_CERT_PATH}}", ca_cert if ca_cert else "")
79.     cfg_str = cfg_str.replace("{{NPX_PATH}}", npx_path)
80.     cfg = json.loads(cfg_str)
81. 
82.     # Remove empty CA cert envs
83.     if "env" in cfg and cfg["env"].get("NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS") == "":
84.         del cfg["env"]["NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS"]
85.         if not cfg["env"]:
86.             del cfg["env"]
87. 
88.     if name in config["mcpServers"] and not force:
89.         skipped.append(name)
90.     else:
91.         action = "updated" if name in config["mcpServers"] else "added"
92.         config["mcpServers"][name] = cfg
93.         (updated if action == "updated" else added).append(name)
94. 
95. # Enable global prompt cache for system prompt (skills injection)
96. config.setdefault("cachedGrowthBookFeatures", {})["tengu_system_prompt_global_cache"] = True
97. 
98. with open(target_path, "w") as f:
99.     json.dump(config, f, indent=2)
100. 
101. print(f"Added:   {added}")
102. print(f"Updated: {updated}")
103. print(f"Skipped: {skipped}")
104. print(f"Total active MCP servers: {list(config['mcpServers'].keys())}")
105. print(f"tengu_system_prompt_global_cache: True (skills injection cached across sessions)")
106. PYEOF
107. 
108. success "MCP servers applied to $TARGET"
109. echo ""
110. log "Restart Claude Code (or run: claude) for changes to take effect."
111. echo ""
112.
```

</details>

<sub>⏱️ 2m 6s</sub>

### ✅ `view`

**view the file at /Users/chazh/Documents/GitHub/ai-skillweave/safe-install.sh.**

<details>
<summary>201 lines</summary>

```
100. fi
101. if [ "$WITH_SCIENCE" = true ]; then
102.     echo "║   + Scientific Skills (K-Dense Agent Skills)            ║"
103. fi
104. if [ "$WITH_BIO" = true ]; then
105.     echo "║   + Bioinformatics Skills (ClawBio)                    ║"
106. fi
107. echo "║   Zero-Risk • Auto Shell Integration                     ║"
108. echo "╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝"
109. echo ""
110. 
111. # =============================================================================
112. # Bootstrap prerequisites (lightweight — install.sh does the heavy lifting)
113. # =============================================================================
114. if ! command -v git >/dev/null 2>&1; then
115.     log "git not found — attempting install..."
116.     if [ "$OS_TYPE" = "macOS" ] && command -v brew >/dev/null 2>&1; then
117.         brew install git 2>/dev/null || warn "brew install git failed"
118.     elif command -v apt-get >/dev/null 2>&1; then
119.         sudo apt-get update -qq && sudo apt-get install -y -qq git 2>/dev/null || warn "apt install git failed"
120.     elif command -v dnf >/dev/null 2>&1; then
121.         sudo dnf install -y git 2>/dev/null || warn "dnf install git failed"
122.     elif command -v pacman >/dev/null 2>&1; then
123.         sudo pacman -S --noconfirm git 2>/dev/null || warn "pacman install git failed"
124.     fi
125. fi
126. command -v git >/dev/null 2>&1 || error "git is required but could not be installed"
127. 
128. if ! command -v python3 >/dev/null 2>&1; then
129.     log "python3 not found — attempting install..."
130.     if [ "$OS_TYPE" = "macOS" ] && command -v brew >/dev/null 2>&1; then
131.         brew install python3 2>/dev/null || warn "brew install python3 failed"
132.     elif command -v apt-get >/dev/null 2>&1; then
133.         sudo apt-get update -qq && sudo apt-get install -y -qq python3 python3-pip 2>/dev/null || warn "apt install python3 failed"
134.     elif command -v dnf >/dev/null 2>&1; then
135.         sudo dnf install -y python3 python3-pip 2>/dev/null || warn "dnf install python3 failed"
136.     elif command -v pacman >/dev/null 2>&1; then
137.         sudo pacman -S --noconfirm python python-pip 2>/dev/null || warn "pacman install python failed"
138.     fi
139. fi
140. command -v python3 >/dev/null 2>&1 || error "python3 is required but could not be installed"
141. 
142. # Pre-install Python dependencies for auto-learning pipeline
143. log "Installing Python dependencies for skill extraction..."
144. _install_python_deps() {
145.     local pkgs="sentence-transformers>=3.0.0 scikit-learn>=1.5.0"
146.     local in_venv=""
147.     if python3 -c "import sys; sys.exit(0 if (sys.prefix != sys.base_prefix) else 1)" 2>/dev/null; then
148.         in_venv=1
149.     fi
150.     if [ -n "$in_venv" ]; then
151.         log "Detected Python virtualenv — skipping --user flag"
152.         if python3 -m pip install --quiet $pkgs 2>&1; then
153.             success "Python deps installed (venv)"
154.             return 0
155.         fi
156.     else
157.         if python3 -m pip install --user --quiet $pkgs 2>&1; then
158.             success "Python deps installed (user site)"
159.             return 0
160.         fi
161.         warn "pip --user failed — trying without --user..."
162.         if python3 -m pip install --quiet $pkgs 2>&1; then
163.             success "Python deps installed (system site)"
164.             return 0
165.         fi
166.     fi
167.     if command -v pip3 >/dev/null 2>&1 && pip3 install --quiet $pkgs 2>&1; then
168.         success "Python deps installed via pip3"
169.         return 0
170.     fi
171.     warn "All pip install attempts failed (see errors above)"
172.     warn "Try manually: python3 -m pip install sentence-transformers scikit-learn"
173.     return 1
174. }
175. _install_python_deps || warn "Python deps missing — the learning pipeline will fail until fixed"
176. 
177. # Ensure node/npm available for harness installs
178. if ! command -v npm >/dev/null 2>&1; then
179.     log "npm not found — attempting install..."
180.     if [ "$OS_TYPE" = "macOS" ] && command -v brew >/dev/null 2>&1; then
181.         brew install node 2>/dev/null || warn "brew install node failed"
182.     elif command -v apt-get >/dev/null 2>&1; then
183.         curl -fsSL https://deb.nodesource.com/setup_22.x | bash - 2>/dev/null && \
184.             apt-get install -y nodejs 2>/dev/null || warn "NodeSource install failed"
185.     fi
186. fi
187. 
188. # =============================================================================
189. # Step 1: Fetch everything-claude-code to isolated directory
190. # =============================================================================
191. 
192. install_ecc_skills() {
193.     if [ "$CURATED_ONLY" = true ]; then
194.         log "Skipping ECC skills (curated-only mode)..."
195.         return 0
196.     fi
197. 
198.     log "Step 1: Installing ECC skills..."
199. 
200.     if [ -d "$ECC_DIR" ]; then
201.         warn "Existing ECC installation found: $ECC_DIR"
202.         # If stdin is a terminal, prompt interactively; otherwise keep existing
203.         if [ -t 0 ]; then
204.             read -p "Overwrite? [y/N] " -n 1 -r
205.             echo ""
206.             if [[ ! $REPLY =~ ^[Yy]$ ]]; then
207.                 success "Keeping existing ECC installation"
208.                 return 0
209.             else
210.                 rm -rf "$ECC_DIR"
211.             fi
212.         else
213.             success "Non-interactive mode — keeping existing ECC installation"
214.             return 0
215.         fi
216.     fi
217. 
218.     mkdir -p "$ECC_DIR"
219.     cd /tmp
220.     if git clone --depth 1 https://github.com/affaan-m/everything-claude-code.git ecc-temp 2>/dev/null; then
221.         mv ecc-temp/* "$ECC_DIR/" 2>/dev/null || true
222.         rm -rf ecc-temp
223.         success "ECC skills installed: $ECC_DIR ($(find "$ECC_DIR/skills" -name '*.md' 2>/dev/null | wc -l | tr -d ' ') skills)"
224.     else
225.         error "Failed to fetch ECC repository"
226.         return 1
227.     fi
228. 
229.     # Copy your learned skills from conversation history
230.     mkdir -p "$ECC_DIR/skills/learned"
231.     if [ -d "$CLAUDE_DIR/skills/learned" ]; then
232.         cp -r "$CLAUDE_DIR/skills/learned"/* "$ECC_DIR/skills/learned/" 2>/dev/null || true
233.         success "Your conversation skills copied"
234.     fi
235.     if [ -d "$CLAUDE_DIR/skills/research" ]; then
236.         cp -r "$CLAUDE_DIR/skills/research"/* "$ECC_DIR/skills/research/" 2>/dev/null || true
237.     fi
238. }
239. 
240. # =============================================================================
241. # Step 2: Fetch curated skills (Anthropic Official + Codex)
242. # =============================================================================
243. 
244. install_curated_skills() {
245.     if [ "$WITH_CURATED" = false ]; then
246.         log "Skipping curated skills (use --with-curated to add)"
247.         return 0
248.     fi
249. 
250.     log "Step 2: Installing curated skills..."
251. 
252.     # Remove existing curated installation
253.     if [ -d "$CURATED_DIR" ]; then
254.         rm -rf "$CURATED_DIR"
255.     fi
256.     mkdir -p "$CURATED_DIR"
257. 
258.     # 2a. Anthropic Official Skills
259.     log "Fetching Anthropic official skills..."
260.     mkdir -p "$CURATED_DIR/anthropic-official"
261.     cd /tmp
262.     if git clone --depth 1 https://github.com/anthropics/skills.git anthropic-temp 2>/dev/null; then
263.         mv anthropic-temp/* "$CURATED_DIR/anthropic-official/" 2>/dev/null || true
264.         rm -rf anthropic-temp
265.         ANTHROPIC_COUNT=$(find "$CURATED_DIR/anthropic-official" -name '*.md' 2>/dev/null | wc -l | tr -d ' ')
266.         success "Anthropic official skills: $ANTHROPIC_COUNT skills"
267.     else
268.         warn "Failed to fetch Anthropic skills (network issue?)"
269.     fi
270. 
271.     # 2c. OpenAI Codex Official Skills (for Codex harness)
272.     log "Fetching OpenAI Codex official skills..."
273.     mkdir -p "$CURATED_DIR/openai-codex"
274.     cd /tmp
275.     if git clone --depth 1 https://github.com/openai/skills.git codex-temp 2>/dev/null; then
276.         mv codex-temp/* "$CURATED_DIR/openai-codex/" 2>/dev/null || true
277.         rm -rf codex-temp
278.         CODEX_COUNT=$(find "$CURATED_DIR/openai-codex" -name '*.md' 2>/dev/null | wc -l | tr -d ' ')
279.         success "OpenAI Codex skills: $CODEX_COUNT skills"
280.     else
281.         warn "Failed to fetch Codex skills (network issue?)"
282.     fi
283. 
284.     # Summary
285.     TOTAL_CURATED=$(find "$CURATED_DIR" -name '*.md' 2>/dev/null | wc -l | tr -d ' ')
286.     success "Total curated skills: $TOTAL_CURATED"
287. }
288. 
289. # =============================================================================
290. # Step 2b: Install K-Dense Scientific Agent Skills
291. # =============================================================================
292. 
293. install_science_skills() {
294.     if [ "$WITH_SCIENCE" = false ]; then
295.         log "Skipping scientific skills (use --with-science to add)"
296.         return 0
297.     fi
298. 
299.     log "Step 2b: Installing K-Dense Scientific Agent Skills..."
300.
```

</details>

<sub>⏱️ 2m 6s</sub>

### ✅ `view`

**view the file at /Users/chazh/Documents/GitHub/ai-skillweave/extract-conversation-skills.py.**

/Users/chazh/Documents/GitHub/ai-skillweave/extract-conversation-skills.py (lines 1-100)

<details>
<summary>100 lines</summary>

```
1. #!/usr/bin/env python3
2. """
3. =============================================================================
4. Conversation History to Skills Extractor — ALMA-Inspired Pipeline
5. =============================================================================
6. 4-stage pipeline for extracting concise, generalizable skills from
7. conversation histories across multiple AI agent harnesses.
8. 
9. Stages:
10.   1. Ingestion  — Parse histories, classify utterances into memory types
11.   2. Learning   — Group similar patterns, require min evidence, score confidence
12.   3. Consolidation — Deduplicate, abstract, generalization filter
13.   4. Output     — Write concise SKILL.md files with condition+strategy form
14. 
15. Based on patterns from:
16.   - ALMA (RBKunnela/ALMA-memory): memory types, confidence scoring, decay
17.   - Reflexion (Shinn et al.): extract from failures, distill to plans
18.   - mem0: LLM-based extraction, entity linking
19.   - ai-linter: quality enforcement (≤500 lines, ≤5000 tokens)
20. 
21. Usage:
22.     python3 extract-conversation-skills.py [OPTIONS]
23. 
24. Options:
25.     --input DIR         Directory with conversation exports (auto-detect if not set)
26.     --output DIR        Directory to write generated skills (default: ~/.claude/skills/learned/)
27.     --harness H         Which harness to extract from: claude|codex|openclaw|all (default: all)
28.     --dry-run           Show what would be extracted without writing files
29.     --verbose           Show detailed analysis
30.     --stats             Show usage/decay statistics only (no extraction)
31.     --prune             Remove decayed/low-quality skills
32. =============================================================================
33. """
34. 
35. import os
36. # Prevent huggingface tokenizers from enabling parallelism before fork,
37. # which causes deadlock warnings and slowdowns when subprocess is used.
38. os.environ["TOKENIZERS_PARALLELISM"] = "false"
39. 
40. import re
41. import json
42. import hashlib
43. import math
44. from pathlib import Path
45. from datetime import datetime, timedelta
46. from collections import defaultdict
47. from dataclasses import dataclass, field
48. from typing import Dict, List, Set, Tuple, Optional
49. import time
50. from concurrent.futures import ThreadPoolExecutor, as_completed
51. 
52. 
53. # =============================================================================
54. # Dependency auto-install
55. # =============================================================================
56. 
57. def _ensure_deps(verbose: bool = False):
58.     """Ensure sentence-transformers and scikit-learn are available."""
59.     required = {
60.         "sentence-transformers": "sentence-transformers>=3.0.0",
61.         "scikit-learn": "scikit-learn>=1.5.0",
62.         "numpy": "numpy>=1.26.0",
63.     }
64.     missing = []
65.     for module, pkg in required.items():
66.         try:
67.             __import__(module.replace("-", "_"))
68.         except ImportError:
69.             missing.append(pkg)
70.     
71.     if missing:
72.         if verbose:
73.             print(f"  [DEPS] Installing: {', '.join(missing)}...")
74.         import subprocess, sys
75.         in_venv = sys.prefix != getattr(sys, "base_prefix", sys.prefix)
76.         cmd = [sys.executable, "-m", "pip", "install", "--quiet"] + missing
77.         if not in_venv:
78.             cmd.append("--user")
79.         result = subprocess.run(cmd, capture_output=True, text=True)
80.         if result.returncode != 0:
81.             print(f"  [WARN] Failed to auto-install dependencies: {result.stderr[:200]}")
82.             print(f"  [WARN] Please run: pip install {' '.join(missing)}")
83.             return False
84.         if verbose:
85.             print(f"  [DEPS] Installed: {', '.join(missing)}")
86.     return True
87. 
88. 
89. # =============================================================================
90. # Configuration
91. # =============================================================================
92. 
93. CLAUDE_HISTORY_PATHS = [
94.     Path.home() / ".claude" / "projects",   # Claude Code: projects/<project>/<session-id>.jsonl
95. ]
96. CODEX_HISTORY_PATHS = [
97.     Path.home() / ".codex" / "sessions",     # Codex: sessions/YYYY/MM/DD/rollout-*.jsonl
98. ]
99. OPENCLAW_HISTORY_PATHS = [
100.     Path.home() / ".openclaw" / "agents",    # OpenClaw: agents/main/sessions/*.jsonl
```
