```
100.     Path.home() / ".openclaw" / "agents",    # OpenClaw: agents/main/sessions/*.jsonl
101. ]
102. PI_HISTORY_PATHS = [
103.     Path.home() / ".pi" / "agent" / "sessions",  # Pi: agent/sessions/<project>/*.jsonl
104. ]
105. DEFAULT_OUTPUT_DIR = Path.home() / ".claude" / "skills" / "learned"
106. USAGE_FILE = DEFAULT_OUTPUT_DIR / ".usage.json"
107. 
108. # Model routing hierarchy: try in order until one works
109. # Note: Claude Code CLI (-p flag) requires ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, so we check first
110. MODEL_PRIORITY = [
111.     {"name": "ollama-cloud", "type": "ollama", "model": "qwen3.5:cloud", "timeout": 180},  # Ollama cloud (best quality, no local GPU needed)
112.     {"name": "ollama-local", "type": "ollama", "model": "qwen3.6:latest", "timeout": 240},  # Local qwen3.6 (fallback, slower but private)
113. ]
114. 
115. # Pipeline thresholds (ALMA-inspired)
116. MIN_OCCURRENCES = 3
117. MIN_CONFIDENCE = 0.5
118. GROUP_SIMILARITY = 0.5       # Jaccard threshold for grouping
119. DEDUP_SIMILARITY = 0.85      # Token overlap for deduplication
120. MAX_SKILL_LINES = 50
121. MAX_EXAMPLES_STORED = 5
122. DECAY_HALF_LIFE_DAYS = 90
123. PRUNE_THRESHOLD = 0.2
124. FEEDBACK_MIN_SAMPLES = 5
125. 
126. MEMORY_TYPES = ["heuristic", "anti_pattern", "preference", "domain_knowledge"]
127. GENERALIZABLE_TYPES = {"heuristic", "anti_pattern"}
128. 
129. 
130. def _try_get_completion(prompt: str, timeout: int = 60, verbose: bool = False) -> Optional[str]:
131.     """Try to get completion from models in priority order. Returns first successful response.
132.     
133.     Tries standard Ollama HTTP API first (reliable, fast), then falls back to subprocess.
134.     """
135.     import subprocess
136.     
137.     for model_config in MODEL_PRIORITY:
138.         model_name = model_config["name"]
139.         model_type = model_config["type"]
140.         model_id = model_config["model"]
141.         model_timeout = model_config["timeout"]
142.         
143.         if verbose:
144.             print(f"  [LLM-TRY] {model_name} ({model_id})...")
145.         
146.         try:
147.             if model_type == "ollama":
148.                 # --- PRIMARY: Standard Ollama HTTP API ---
149.                 try:
150.                     import urllib.request
151.                     import urllib.error
152.                     req = urllib.request.Request(
153.                         "http://localhost:11434/api/generate",
154.                         data=json.dumps({
155.                             "model": model_id,
156.                             "prompt": prompt,
157.                             "stream": False,
158.                             "options": {"temperature": 0.3, "num_predict": 512}
159.                         }).encode("utf-8"),
160.                         headers={"Content-Type": "application/json"},
161.                         method="POST"
162.                     )
163.                     with urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=min(timeout, model_timeout)) as resp:
164.                         data = json.loads(resp.read().decode("utf-8"))
165.                         response_text = data.get("response", "").strip()
166.                         if response_text:
167.                             if verbose:
168.                                 print(f"  [LLM-OK] {model_name} (HTTP API) returned {len(response_text)} chars")
169.                             return response_text
170.                 except (urllib.error.URLError, urllib.error.HTTPError, ConnectionRefusedError) as e:
171.                     if verbose:
172.                         print(f"  [LLM-FALLBACK] HTTP API unavailable ({type(e).__name__}), trying subprocess...")
173.                 except Exception as e:
174.                     if verbose:
175.                         print(f"  [LLM-FALLBACK] HTTP API error ({type(e).__name__}), trying subprocess...")
176.                 
177.                 # --- FALLBACK: Subprocess via ollama run ---
178.                 result = subprocess.run(
179.                     ["ollama", "run", model_id, prompt],
180.                     capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=min(timeout, model_timeout),
181.                     stdin=subprocess.DEVNULL,
182.                 )
183.                 if result.returncode == 0 and result.stdout.strip():
184.                     if verbose:
185.                         print(f"  [LLM-OK] {model_name} (subprocess) returned {len(result.stdout)} chars")
186.                     return result.stdout.strip()
187.                 elif verbose:
188.                     print(f"  [LLM-FAIL] {model_name}: {result.stderr[:100] if result.stderr else 'empty output'}")
189.         
190.         except subprocess.TimeoutExpired:
191.             if verbose:
192.                 print(f"  [LLM-TIMEOUT] {model_name} after {model_timeout}s")
193.         except Exception as e:
194.             if verbose:
195.                 print(f"  [LLM-ERROR] {model_name}: {type(e).__name__}")
196.     
197.     if verbose:
198.         print(f"  [LLM-ALL-FAIL] All models failed")
199.     return None
200. 
201. # Regex markers per memory type
202. # Frustration/escalation signals — strong evidence this is a real correction
203. FRUSTRATION_MARKERS = [
204.     r"\b(?:again|still|keep(?:ing)?|once more|repeatedly)\s+(?:telling|saying|asking|explaining)\b",
205.     r"\b(?:I (?:keep|already|just)\s+(?:told|said|asked|explained|mentioned))\b",
206.     r"\b(?:for the (?:last|nth)\s+time)\b",
207.     r"\b(?:I've (?:said|told|explained)\s+this\s+(?:before|already|multiple times))\b",
208.     r"\b(?:this is (?:the \d+|third|fourth|fifth|nth)\s+time)\b",
209.     r"\b(?:still (?:wrong|incorrect|not right|broken|failing))\b",
210.     r"\b(?:WRONG|INCORRECT|NO!|STOP)\b",
211.     r"\b(?:didn't I (?:just|already)\s+(?:say|tell|explain))\b",
212. ]
213. 
214. CLASSIFICATION_MARKERS = {
215.     "anti_pattern": [
216.         r"(?:don't|do not|never)\s+(?:do|say|write|generate|use|include|fabricat|hallucinat|invent|assume|claim|state|present)\s+",
217.         r"(?:stop|avoid)\s+(?:fabricat|hallucinat|inventing|making|generating|assuming|claiming|presenting)\s+",
218.         r"(?:that's\s+)?(?:wrong|incorrect|not\s+what)\s+(?:I\s+)?(?:asked|meant|wanted|said)\s+",
219.         r"\b(?:fabricat|hallucinat|made\s+up)\w+",
220.         r"(?:no,?\s+i\s+meant|actually,?\s+I\s+meant)\s+",
221.         r"(?:unreliable|unverified|unsourced|bogus|made-up)\s+(?:claim|info|data|source|result)\s*",
222.     ],
223.     "heuristic": [
224.         r"(?:always|usually|typically)\s+(?:do|use|include|check|verify|cite|reference|cross-?check)\s+",
225.         r"(?:the\s+(?:best|right|correct|proper)\s+way)\s+(?:is|to)\s+",
226.         r"(?:i\s+)?(?:found\s+that|learned\s+that|discovered\s+that)\s+",
227.         r"(?:works\s+(?:best|better|well))\s+",
228.         r"(?:prefer|recommend|suggest)\s+\w+\s+(?:over|instead\s+of|vs)\s+",
229.         r"(?:make\s+sure|ensure|always\s+verify)\s+",
230.         r"(?:you\s+(?:should|must|need\s+to)\s+(?:always|always\s+)?(?:cite|verify|check|cross-reference))\s+",
231.     ],
232.     "preference": [
233.         r"(?:i\s+(?:prefer|like|want|need))\s+",
234.         r"(?:my\s+(?:style|preference|convention))\s+(?:is|uses)\s+",
235.         r"(?:use\s+tabs|use\s+spaces|2-space|4-space)",
236.         r"(?:please\s+(?:always|never))\s+",
237.     ],
238.     "domain_knowledge": [
239.         r"(?:the\s+(?:api|endpoint|url|server|database)\s+(?:is|at|on))\s+",
240.         r"(?:the\s+(?:config|setting|variable|flag)\s+(?:is|should\s+be))\s+",
241.         r"(?:version\s+\d+\.\d+)",
242.         r"(?:the\s+password\s+(?:is|was))\s+",
243.     ],
244. }
245. 
246. # Patterns to SKIP — not corrections, just noise
247. SKIP_PATTERNS = [
248.     re.compile(r"^\s*(?:what|where|when|who|why|how|which|is|are|can|does|did)\b.*\?\s*$", re.IGNORECASE),  # pure questions
249.     re.compile(r"(?:Do\s+NOT|NEVER)\s+(?:use|run|call|execute)\s+(?:Read|Bash|Grep|Glob|Edit|Write|any\s+other\s+tool)", re.IGNORECASE),  # system tool restrictions
250.     re.compile(r"^\s*(?:yes|no|ok|okay|done|sure|right|correct|good)\s*[.!?]?\s*$", re.IGNORECASE),  # trivial responses
```

<details>
<summary>72 lines</summary>

```
1. #!/bin/bash
2. # Setup BMO-style learning capture hook in Claude Code settings
3. 
4. set -e
5. 
6. REPO_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")/.." && pwd)"
7. SETTINGS_FILE="$HOME/.claude/settings.json"
8. HOOK_PATH="$REPO_DIR/hooks/learning-capture.sh"
9. 
10. if [ ! -f "$SETTINGS_FILE" ]; then
11.   echo "[WARN] Claude Code settings not found: $SETTINGS_FILE"
12.   echo "Run Claude Code once to initialize, then re-run this script"
13.   exit 1
14. fi
15. 
16. if [ ! -f "$HOOK_PATH" ]; then
17.   echo "[ERROR] Learning capture hook not found: $HOOK_PATH"
18.   exit 1
19. fi
20. 
21. python3 << PYEOF
22. import json
23. from pathlib import Path
24. 
25. settings_file = Path.home() / ".claude" / "settings.json"
26. hook_path = "$HOOK_PATH"
27. 
28. try:
29.     with open(settings_file) as f:
30.         settings = json.load(f)
31.     
32.     # Validate and fix malformed hooks structure
33.     if "hooks" not in settings:
34.         settings["hooks"] = {}
35.     
36.     for hook_type in list(settings["hooks"].keys()):
37.         entries = settings["hooks"][hook_type]
38.         if not isinstance(entries, list):
39.             settings["hooks"][hook_type] = []
40.             continue
41.         cleaned = []
42.         for entry in entries:
43.             if not isinstance(entry, dict):
44.                 continue
45.             if "hooks" not in entry or not isinstance(entry["hooks"], list):
46.                 entry["hooks"] = []
47.             cleaned.append(entry)
48.         settings["hooks"][hook_type] = cleaned
49.     
50.     if "UserPromptSubmit" not in settings["hooks"]:
51.         settings["hooks"]["UserPromptSubmit"] = []
52.     
53.     # Add learning capture hook if not already present
54.     hook_exists = any(
55.         any(hook.get("command") == hook_path for hook in entry.get("hooks", []))
56.         for entry in settings["hooks"]["UserPromptSubmit"]
57.     )
58.     if not hook_exists:
59.         settings["hooks"]["UserPromptSubmit"].append({
60.             "matcher": "*",
61.             "hooks": [{"type": "command", "command": hook_path}]
62.         })
63.         with open(settings_file, "w") as f:
64.             json.dump(settings, f, indent=2)
65.         print(f"[OK] Added learning-capture hook to {settings_file}")
66.     else:
67.         print(f"[OK] Learning-capture hook already present in {settings_file}")
68. except Exception as e:
69.     print(f"[ERROR] Could not update settings.json: {e}")
70.     exit(1)
71. PYEOF
72.
```

</details>

<summary>112 lines</summary>

```
1. #!/bin/bash
2. # setup-copilot.sh — Configure MCP servers for GitHub Copilot CLI
3. # =============================================================================
4. # Writes MCP server config to ~/.copilot/mcp-config.json (read automatically
5. # by copilot on launch; augments built-in github-mcp-server).
6. #
7. # Usage:
8. #   scripts/setup-copilot.sh [--force]
9. #   --force : overwrite existing MCP server entries with template values
10. # =============================================================================
11. set -e
12. 
13. REPO_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")/.." && pwd)"
14. TEMPLATE="$REPO_DIR/configs/copilot-mcp-config.json"
15. TARGET="$HOME/.copilot/mcp-config.json"
16. 
17. log()     { echo -e "\033[1;34m[COPILOT]\033[0m $1"; }
18. success() { echo -e "\033[1;32m[OK]\033[0m $1"; }
19. warn()    { echo -e "\033[1;33m[WARN]\033[0m $1"; }
20. 
21. if [ ! -f "$TEMPLATE" ]; then
22.     warn "Template not found: $TEMPLATE"
23.     exit 1
24. fi
25. 
26. FORCE=false
27. [[ "$*" == *"--force"* ]] && FORCE=true
28. 
29. # Ensure Copilot config directory exists
30. mkdir -p "$(dirname "$TARGET")"
31. 
32. # Determine CA cert path (Zscaler or system default)
33. CA_CERT=""
34. if [ -f "$HOME/.mamba_ca_bundle.pem" ]; then
35.     CA_CERT="$HOME/.mamba_ca_bundle.pem"
36. elif [ -f "/etc/ssl/certs/ca-certificates.crt" ]; then
37.     CA_CERT="/etc/ssl/certs/ca-certificates.crt"
38. fi
39. 
40. log "Installing MCP servers for Copilot CLI..."
41. 
42. python3 << PYEOF
43. import json, os, sys
44. 
45. template_path = "$TEMPLATE"
46. target_path = "$TARGET"
47. ca_cert = "$CA_CERT"
48. home = os.path.expanduser("~")
49. npx_path = "$(which npx 2>/dev/null || echo "npx")"
50. force = $([[ "$FORCE" == "true" ]] && echo "True" || echo "False")
51. 
52. # Load or create target config
53. if os.path.exists(target_path):
54.     with open(target_path) as f:
55.         config = json.load(f)
56. else:
57.     config = {}
58. 
59. if "mcpServers" not in config:
60.     config["mcpServers"] = {}
61. 
62. with open(template_path) as f:
63.     template = json.load(f)
64. 
65. servers = template.get("mcpServers", {})
66. added = []
67. updated = []
68. skipped = []
69. 
70. for name, cfg in servers.items():
71.     # Substitute placeholders
72.     cfg_str = json.dumps(cfg)
73.     cfg_str = cfg_str.replace("{{HOME}}", home)
74.     cfg_str = cfg_str.replace("{{CA_CERT_PATH}}", ca_cert if ca_cert else "")
75.     cfg_str = cfg_str.replace("{{NPX_PATH}}", npx_path)
76.     cfg = json.loads(cfg_str)
77. 
78.     # Remove empty CA cert envs
79.     if "env" in cfg and cfg["env"].get("NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS") == "":
80.         del cfg["env"]["NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS"]
81.         if not cfg["env"]:
82.             del cfg["env"]
83. 
84.     if name in config["mcpServers"] and not force:
85.         skipped.append(name)
86.         continue
87. 
88.     action = "updated" if name in config["mcpServers"] else "added"
89.     config["mcpServers"][name] = cfg
90.     (updated if action == "updated" else added).append(name)
91. 
92. with open(target_path, "w") as f:
93.     json.dump(config, f, indent=2)
94.     f.write("\n")
95. 
96. if added:
97.     print(f"  Added:   {', '.join(added)}")
98. if updated:
99.     print(f"  Updated: {', '.join(updated)}")
100. if skipped:
101.     print(f"  Skipped (exists, use --force): {', '.join(skipped)}")
102. 
103. total = list(config["mcpServers"].keys())
104. print(f"Total MCP servers: {len(total)} — {total}")
105. PYEOF
106. 
107. success "MCP servers applied to Copilot CLI config ($TARGET)"
108. echo ""
109. echo "  Launch Copilot:  ollama launch copilot"
110. echo "  Or directly:     copilot"
111. echo ""
112.
```

</details>

<details>
<summary>55 lines</summary>

```
1. 
2. # =============================================================================
3. # Skills Layer — Everything Claude Code
4. # =============================================================================
5. # All harness commands get the ECC skills injected automatically.
6. # Injected by: ~/scripts/agent_harness_modifications/install.sh
7. # To remove:   ~/scripts/agent_harness_modifications/install.sh --uninstall
8. # =============================================================================
9. 
10. # Claude Code: inject skills via --append-system-prompt-file
11. _claude_with_skills() {
12.     local _skills_file="/tmp/claude-skills-$$.txt"
13.     cat ~/.claude/skills-cache/combined-skills.txt > "$_skills_file" 2>/dev/null
14.     if [ -s "$_skills_file" ]; then
15.         (unset SKILLS_CONTENT CODEX_SYSTEM_PROMPT OPENCLAW_SYSTEM_PROMPT; command claude --append-system-prompt-file "$_skills_file" "$@")
16.     else
17.         (unset SKILLS_CONTENT CODEX_SYSTEM_PROMPT OPENCLAW_SYSTEM_PROMPT; command claude "$@")
18.     fi
19.     rm -f "$_skills_file"
20. }
21. 
22. # OpenClaw: loads SKILL.md files natively from ~/.openclaw/workspace/skills/
23. _openclaw_with_skills() {
24.     (unset SKILLS_CONTENT CODEX_SYSTEM_PROMPT OPENCLAW_SYSTEM_PROMPT; command openclaw "$@")
25. }
26. 
27. # Codex: loads skills natively from ~/.codex/skills/ (ECC skills symlinked there)
28. _codex_with_skills() {
29.     (unset SKILLS_CONTENT OPENCLAW_SYSTEM_PROMPT; command codex "$@")
30. }
31. 
32. # Ollama: pass-through with env cleanup (openclaw/codex/pi load skills natively)
33. _ollama_with_skills() {
34.     (unset SKILLS_CONTENT CODEX_SYSTEM_PROMPT OPENCLAW_SYSTEM_PROMPT OLLAMA_SYSTEM_FILE; command ollama "$@")
35. }
36. 
37. # Pi: loads skills natively from ~/.pi/agent/skills/
38. _pi_with_skills() {
39.     (unset SKILLS_CONTENT CODEX_SYSTEM_PROMPT OPENCLAW_SYSTEM_PROMPT; command pi "$@")
40. }
41. 
42. # Cross-harness skill learner
43. alias learn-sync='~/scripts/agent_harness_modifications/sync-learned-skills.sh'
44. alias learn-sync-dry='~/scripts/agent_harness_modifications/sync-learned-skills.sh --dry-run --verbose'
45. alias learn-stats='~/scripts/agent_harness_modifications/sync-learned-skills.sh --stats'
46. alias learn-prune='~/scripts/agent_harness_modifications/sync-learned-skills.sh --prune'
47. 
48. # Wrapper aliases
49. alias claude='_claude_with_skills'
50. alias openclaw='_openclaw_with_skills'
51. alias codex='_codex_with_skills'
52. alias ollama='_ollama_with_skills'
53. alias pi='_pi_with_skills'
54. # End Skills Layer
55.
```

</details>

300. 
301.     if [ -d "$SCIENCE_DIR" ]; then
302.         warn "Existing scientific skills found: $SCIENCE_DIR"
303.         if [ -t 0 ]; then
304.             read -p "Overwrite? [y/N] " -n 1 -r
305.             echo ""
306.             if [[ ! $REPLY =~ ^[Yy]$ ]]; then
307.                 success "Keeping existing scientific skills"
308.                 return 0
309.             else
310.                 rm -rf "$SCIENCE_DIR"
311.             fi
312.         else
313.             success "Non-interactive mode — keeping existing scientific skills"
314.             return 0
315.         fi
316.     fi
317. 
318.     mkdir -p "$SCIENCE_DIR"
319.     cd /tmp
320.     if git clone --depth 1 https://github.com/K-Dense-AI/scientific-agent-skills.git science-temp 2>/dev/null; then
321.         # Copy skill directories (each contains SKILL.md + optional references/scripts/assets)
322.         mkdir -p "$SCIENCE_DIR/scientific-skills"
323.         cp -r science-temp/scientific-skills/* "$SCIENCE_DIR/scientific-skills/" 2>/dev/null || true
324.         SCIENCE_COUNT=$(find "$SCIENCE_DIR/scientific-skills" -name 'SKILL.md' 2>/dev/null | wc -l | tr -d ' ')
325.         rm -rf science-temp
326.         success "K-Dense scientific skills installed: $SCIENCE_DIR ($SCIENCE_COUNT skills)"
327.     else
328.         error "Failed to fetch K-Dense scientific-agent-skills repository"
329.         return 1
330.     fi
331. }
332. 
333. # =============================================================================
334. # Step 2c: Install ClawBio Bioinformatics Skills
335. # =============================================================================
336. 
337. install_bio_skills() {
338.     if [ "$WITH_BIO" = false ]; then
339.         log "Skipping ClawBio bioinformatics skills (use --with-bio to add)"
340.         return 0
341.     fi
342. 
343.     log "Step 2c: Installing ClawBio Bioinformatics Skills..."
344. 
345.     if [ -d "$CLAWBIO_DIR" ]; then
346.         warn "Existing ClawBio skills found: $CLAWBIO_DIR"
347.         if [ -t 0 ]; then
348.             read -p "Overwrite? [y/N] " -n 1 -r
349.             echo ""
350.             if [[ ! $REPLY =~ ^[Yy]$ ]]; then
351.                 success "Keeping existing ClawBio skills"
352.                 return 0
353.             else
354.                 rm -rf "$CLAWBIO_DIR"
355.             fi
356.         else
357.             success "Non-interactive mode — keeping existing ClawBio skills"
358.             return 0
359.         fi
360.     fi
361. 
362.     mkdir -p "$CLAWBIO_DIR"
363.     cd /tmp
364.     if git clone --depth 1 https://github.com/ClawBio/ClawBio.git clawbio-temp 2>/dev/null; then
365.         # Copy skill directories (each contains SKILL.md + optional Python scripts/examples/tests)
366.         mkdir -p "$CLAWBIO_DIR/skills"
367.         cp -r clawbio-temp/skills/* "$CLAWBIO_DIR/skills/" 2>/dev/null || true
368.         BIO_COUNT=$(find "$CLAWBIO_DIR/skills" -name 'SKILL.md' 2>/dev/null | wc -l | tr -d ' ')
369.         rm -rf clawbio-temp
370.         success "ClawBio bioinformatics skills installed: $CLAWBIO_DIR ($BIO_COUNT skills)"
371.     else
372.         error "Failed to fetch ClawBio repository"
373.         return 1
374.     fi
375. }
376. 
377. # =============================================================================
378. # Step 3: Create combined skills loader (single file with ALL skills)
379. # =============================================================================
380. 
381. create_loader() {
382.     log "Step 3: Creating skills loader (combining all skills)..."
383. 
384.     mkdir -p "$SKILLS_CACHE_DIR"
385. 
386.     # First, combine all skills into a single cached file
387.     COMBINED_FILE="$SKILLS_CACHE_DIR/combined-skills.txt"
388.     > "$COMBINED_FILE"  # Truncate/create file
389. 
390.     # Preamble: conciseness + MCP usage rules (injected at top of system prompt)
391.     cat >> "$COMBINED_FILE" << 'PREAMBLE'
392. # CRITICAL INSTRUCTIONS — READ FIRST
393. 
394. ## Conciseness
395. - Be terse. No trailing summaries, status tables, or "here's what I did" recaps.
396. - Show the change, not paragraphs explaining the change.
397. 
398. ## Use MCP tools PROACTIVELY
399. - Use codesight_get_summary BEFORE exploring a codebase with Grep/Glob/Read.
400. - Use smart_read (token-optimizer) instead of Read for large files.
401. - Use context7 query-docs BEFORE answering library/framework questions from training data.
402. - Use exa-web-search for anything that may have changed since training cutoff.
403. 
404. PREAMBLE
405. 
406.     # Add learned skills first (your personal skills - always included)
407.     if [ -d "$CLAUDE_DIR/skills/learned" ]; then
408.         for skill in "$CLAUDE_DIR/skills/learned"/*.md; do
409.             if [ -f "$skill" ]; then
410.                 echo "" >> "$COMBINED_FILE"
411.                 awk 'BEGIN{f=0} /^---$/{f++; next} f>=2' "$skill" >> "$COMBINED_FILE"
412.             fi
413.         done
414.     fi
415. 
416.     # Add curated skills with priority filtering
417.     # Priority 1: Anthropic Official (highest quality, vetted)
418.     if [ -d "$CURATED_DIR/anthropic-official" ]; then
419.         log "Merging Anthropic official skills (priority 1)..."
420.         find "$CURATED_DIR/anthropic-official" -name "*.md" -type f 2>/dev/null | while read -r skill; do
421.             echo "" >> "$COMBINED_FILE"
422.             awk 'BEGIN{f=0} /^---$/{f++; next} f>=2' "$skill" >> "$COMBINED_FILE"
423.         done
424.     fi
425. 
426.     # Priority 2: OpenAI Codex skills (for Codex harness)
427.     if [ -d "$CURATED_DIR/openai-codex" ]; then
428.         log "Merging OpenAI Codex skills (priority 2, top 100)..."
429.         # Filter to most relevant skills (avoid duplicates, focus on core patterns)
430.         find "$CURATED_DIR/openai-codex" -name "*.md" -type f 2>/dev/null | head -100 | while read -r skill; do
431.             echo "" >> "$COMBINED_FILE"
432.             awk 'BEGIN{f=0} /^---$/{f++; next} f>=2' "$skill" >> "$COMBINED_FILE"
433.         done
434.     fi
435. 
436.     # Priority 3: ECC skills (comprehensive base)
437.     if [ -d "$ECC_DIR/skills" ]; then
438.         log "Merging ECC skills (priority 3)..."
439.         find "$ECC_DIR/skills" -name "*.md" -type f ! -path "*/learned/*" 2>/dev/null | while read -r skill; do
440.             echo "" >> "$COMBINED_FILE"
441.             awk 'BEGIN{f=0} /^---$/{f++; next} f>=2' "$skill" >> "$COMBINED_FILE"
442.         done
443.     fi
444. 
445.     # Priority 4: K-Dense Scientific Agent Skills (SKILL.md in subdirectories)
446.     if [ -d "$SCIENCE_DIR/scientific-skills" ]; then
447.         log "Merging K-Dense scientific skills (priority 4)..."
448.         find "$SCIENCE_DIR/scientific-skills" -name "SKILL.md" -type f 2>/dev/null | while read -r skill; do
449.             echo "" >> "$COMBINED_FILE"
450.             awk 'BEGIN{f=0} /^---$/{f++; next} f>=2' "$skill" >> "$COMBINED_FILE"
451.         done
452.     fi
453. 
454.     # Priority 6: ClawBio Bioinformatics Skills (SKILL.md in subdirectories)
455.     if [ -d "$CLAWBIO_DIR/skills" ]; then
456.         log "Merging ClawBio bioinformatics skills (priority 6)..."
457.         find "$CLAWBIO_DIR/skills" -name "SKILL.md" -type f 2>/dev/null | while read -r skill; do
458.             echo "" >> "$COMBINED_FILE"
459.             awk 'BEGIN{f=0} /^---$/{f++; next} f>=2' "$skill" >> "$COMBINED_FILE"
460.         done
461.     fi
462. 
463.     SKILL_COUNT=$(wc -l < "$COMBINED_FILE" | tr -d ' ')
464.     success "Combined skills cache created ($SKILL_COUNT lines)"
465. 
466.     # Create the loader script (for backwards compatibility, but don't export large vars)
467.     cat > "$SKILLS_CACHE_DIR/load-skills.sh" << LOADER
468. #!/bin/bash
469. # Load combined skills - file-based approach to avoid environment limits
470. # SKILLS_CONTENT available via: \$(cat ~/.claude/skills-cache/combined-skills.txt)
471. COMBINED_SKILLS_FILE='$COMBINED_FILE'
472. LOADER
473. 
474.     chmod +x "$SKILLS_CACHE_DIR/load-skills.sh"
475.     success "Loader created: $SKILLS_CACHE_DIR/load-skills.sh"
476.     # Lean cache: personal learned skills only (injected into Anthropic Opus sessions)
477.     if ls "$CLAUDE_DIR/skills/learned"/*.md >/dev/null 2>&1; then
478.         cat "$CLAUDE_DIR/skills/learned"/*.md > "$SKILLS_CACHE_DIR/lean-skills.txt"
479.         success "Lean cache created: $SKILLS_CACHE_DIR/lean-skills.txt (personal skills only — 98% fewer tokens)"
480.     else
481.         > "$SKILLS_CACHE_DIR/lean-skills.txt"
482.         warn "No learned skills found — lean cache is empty"
483.     fi
484. }
485. 
486. # =============================================================================
487. # Step 4: Add aliases to shell rc files (idempotent)
488. # =============================================================================
489. 
490. # Generate the skills block to add to shell rc
491. generate_skills_block() {
492.     cat << 'SKILLS_BLOCK'
493. 
494. # Skills Layer - SAFE Install (Everything Claude Code)
495. # All default commands now include skills (ECC + Anthropic Official + Curated + conversation-derived)
496. # To disable: run ~/.claude/scripts/safe-install.sh --uninstall
497. 
498. # Helper function to inject skills as system prompt (uses file to avoid arg length limits)
499. _claude_with_skills() {
500.     local _skills_file="/tmp/claude-skills-$$.txt"
```

</details>

<sub>⏱️ 2m 23s</sub>

### ✅ `view`

**view the file at /Users/chazh/Documents/GitHub/ai-skillweave/README.md.**

/Users/chazh/Documents/GitHub/ai-skillweave/README.md (lines 1-80)

<details>
<summary>80 lines</summary>

````
1. # ai-skillweave
2. 
3. > Auto-learning agent harness: captures corrections live, mines session history for patterns, syncs ~450 skills across Claude/Codex/OpenClaw/Pi with MCP pre-configured.
4. 
5. One-command setup for all your Ollama agent harnesses: proper MCP servers, web tools, harness-specific configs — all portable and reproducible. Learns from your corrections automatically via real-time hooks and batch session analysis.
6. 
7. **~450 skills** on-disk across 5 sources — see [`docs/SKILLS-CATALOG.md`](docs/SKILLS-CATALOG.md) for a full categorized listing.
8. 
9. ---
10. 
11. ## Skill Sources
12. 
13. > **Skills in this repo are drawn from multiple open-source skill libraries**, each following the [Agent Skills](https://agentskills.io/) standard:
14. 
15. | Source | Skills | Notes |
16. |--------|--------|-------|
17. | [Everything Claude Code (ECC)](https://github.com/affaan-m/everything-claude-code) | 184 SKILL.md dirs | Community-maintained — testing, architecture, security, cloud, language patterns |
18. | [K-Dense scientific](https://github.com/K-Dense-AI/scientific-agent-skills) | 134 SKILL.md dirs | Bioinformatics, cheminformatics, drug discovery, clinical research, ML/AI |
19. | [ClawBio bioinformatics](https://github.com/ClawBio/ClawBio) | 56 SKILL.md dirs | Bioinformatics-native pipeline skills with executable Python scripts |
20. | [SkillGraph bioinformatics](https://github.com/variomeanalytics/bioinformatics-agent-skills) | MCP-served | Via MCP server with knowledge graph |
21. | Anthropic official | 17 SKILL.md dirs | Anthropic's official skills library |
22. | OpenAI Codex curated | 44 SKILL.md dirs | OpenAI's Codex skills collection |
23. | Personal learned | varies | BMO-style real-time capture (corrections detected live via hooks) + batch 4-stage pipeline |
24. 
25. `ai-skillweave`'s core contribution is **cross-harness delivery**: each skill library was originally designed for a single harness. This repo extends them so the same skills load natively into every `ollama launch` agent — OpenClaw, Pi, Codex, and Claude Code — each in the format that harness expects.
26. 
27. All sources are credited in the Skill Sources table above.
28. 
29. ---
30. 
31. ## Scientific Agent Skills — K-Dense
32. 
33. > **134 scientific and research skills from [K-Dense-AI/scientific-agent-skills](https://github.com/K-Dense-AI/scientific-agent-skills)** — covering bioinformatics, cheminformatics, drug discovery, clinical research, proteomics, medical imaging, ML/AI, materials science, physics, and 100+ scientific databases.
34. 
35. These skills follow the open [Agent Skills](https://agentskills.io/) standard and work with Claude Code, Cursor, Codex, and Gemini CLI. `ai-skillweave` extends them to all supported harnesses, just like ECC skills.
36. 
37. To install:
38. 
39. ```bash
40. ./safe-install.sh --with-science                    # ECC + K-Dense scientific skills
41. ```
42. 
43. 
44. 
45. ---
46. 
47. ## Bioinformatics Agent Skills — Variome Analytics
48. 
49. > **Bioinformatics pipeline skills served via MCP from [variomeanalytics/bioinformatics-agent-skills](https://github.com/variomeanalytics/bioinformatics-agent-skills)** — covering variant analysis, drug discovery, single-cell RNA-seq, genome-wide association studies, and 15+ database query skills (ClinVar, gnomAD, COSMIC, Ensembl, UniProt, and more).
50. 
51. Unlike ECC and K-Dense which ship on-disk `SKILL.md` files, these skills are served dynamically via an **MCP server** (`skillgraph`) that provides:
52. 
53. - `get_skill` — Full skill documentation for any skill in the graph
54. - `list_skills` — List all skills, optionally filtered by domain
55. - `search_skills` — Keyword search across skill IDs, triggers, and tool names
56. - `get_transitions` — Upstream/downstream skill edges with paper counts and data types
57. - `find_path` — Shortest pipeline path between two skills
58. - `get_graph_stats` — Graph statistics (skill count, edges, domain breakdown)
59. 
60. This knowledge graph approach means you can ask "what pipeline takes me from FASTQ to DEGs?" and get a real answer with evidence — something on-disk skill files alone can't provide.
61. 
62. **Installation:**
63. 
64. ```bash
65. # Add the SkillGraph MCP server (works out of the box after install.sh)
66. # It's already included in configs/claude-mcp-servers.json
67. ./scripts/setup-mcp.sh --force   # re-apply MCP config to add skillgraph
68. ```
69. 
70. ---
71. 
72. ## Bioinformatics Pipeline Skills — ClawBio
73. 
74. > **56 bioinformatics pipeline skills from [ClawBio/ClawBio](https://github.com/ClawBio/ClawBio)** — covering RNA-seq differential expression, VCF annotation, clinical variant reporting, single-cell orchestration, GWAS/PRS, HLA typing, methylation clocks, drug-target validation, and more.
75. 
76. Unlike ECC and K-Dense skills which are prompt-only `SKILL.md` files, ClawBio skills ship **executable Python scripts** alongside their skill definitions (172 `.py` files across 56 skills, plus 43 test directories). Each `SKILL.md` includes an `openclaw` metadata block with `uv` package requirements, so compatible harnesses can auto-install dependencies.
77. 
78. **Skill categories:**
79. 
80. | Category | Skills | Examples |
````

</details>

```
500.     local _skills_file="/tmp/claude-skills-$$.txt"
501.     cat ~/.claude/skills-cache/combined-skills.txt > "$_skills_file" 2>/dev/null
502.     if [ -s "$_skills_file" ]; then
503.         (unset SKILLS_CONTENT CODEX_SYSTEM_PROMPT OPENCLAW_SYSTEM_PROMPT; command claude --append-system-prompt-file "$_skills_file" "$@")
504.     else
505.         (unset SKILLS_CONTENT CODEX_SYSTEM_PROMPT OPENCLAW_SYSTEM_PROMPT; command claude "$@")
506.     fi
507.     rm -f "$_skills_file"
508. }
509. 
510. _openclaw_with_skills() {
511.     # OpenClaw natively loads workspace/skills/ SKILL.md files — no extra env var needed.
512.     # OPENCLAW_SYSTEM_PROMPT_FILE is NOT a real openclaw env var (not in source).
513.     (unset SKILLS_CONTENT CODEX_SYSTEM_PROMPT OPENCLAW_SYSTEM_PROMPT; command openclaw "$@")
514. }
515. 
516. _codex_with_skills() {
517.     # Codex loads skills natively from ~/.codex/skills/ (ECC skills symlinked there)
518.     (unset SKILLS_CONTENT OPENCLAW_SYSTEM_PROMPT; command codex "$@")
519. }
520. 
521. _ollama_with_skills() {
522.     # OLLAMA_SYSTEM_FILE is NOT set (breaks ollama launch codex/pi).
523.     # OPENCLAW_SYSTEM_PROMPT_FILE is not a real openclaw env var — removed.
524.     # OpenClaw loads ECC skills natively from ~/.openclaw/workspace/skills/ SKILL.md files.
525.     (unset SKILLS_CONTENT CODEX_SYSTEM_PROMPT OPENCLAW_SYSTEM_PROMPT OLLAMA_SYSTEM_FILE; command ollama "$@")
526. }
527. 
528. _pi_with_skills() {
529.     # Pi loads skills natively from ~/.pi/agent/skills/ (ECC skills symlinked there)
530.     (unset SKILLS_CONTENT CODEX_SYSTEM_PROMPT OPENCLAW_SYSTEM_PROMPT; command pi "$@")
531. }
532. 
533. _copilot_with_skills() {
534.     # Copilot CLI does NOT load ~/.claude/skills/ natively (different format).
535.     # It reads ~/.copilot/mcp-config.json for MCP servers and .github/copilot-instructions.md
536.     # for per-repo guidance. This wrapper ensures a clean environment.
537.     (unset SKILLS_CONTENT CODEX_SYSTEM_PROMPT OPENCLAW_SYSTEM_PROMPT; command copilot "$@")
538. }
539. 
540. # Wrapper aliases
541. alias claude='_claude_with_skills'
542. alias openclaw='_openclaw_with_skills'
543. alias codex='_codex_with_skills'
544. alias ollama='_ollama_with_skills'
545. alias pi='_pi_with_skills'
546. alias copilot='_copilot_with_skills'
547. 
548. # Learning pipeline commands
549. alias learn-sync='bash ~/.claude/scripts/sync-learned-skills.sh'
550. alias learn-sync-dry='bash ~/.claude/scripts/sync-learned-skills.sh --dry-run'
551. alias learn-stats='bash ~/.claude/scripts/sync-learned-skills.sh --stats'
552. alias learn-prune='bash ~/.claude/scripts/sync-learned-skills.sh --prune'
553. # End Skills Layer
554. SKILLS_BLOCK
555. }
556. 
557. setup_shell_integration() {
558.     log "Step 4: Setting up shell integration..."
559.     log "Detected user shell: $USER_SHELL (platform: $OS_TYPE)"
560. 
561.     # Find shell rc files to update
562.     local shell_rcs=()
563. 
564.     # Detect which shell rc files exist and should be updated.
565.     # Add ALL existing rc files so the user can switch shells without losing integration.
566.     if [ -f "$HOME/.zshrc" ]; then
567.         shell_rcs+=("$HOME/.zshrc")
568.         success "Found .zshrc"
569.     fi
570.     if [ -f "$HOME/.bashrc" ]; then
571.         shell_rcs+=("$HOME/.bashrc")
572.         success "Found .bashrc"
573.     fi
574.     if [ -f "$HOME/.bash_profile" ] && [ ${#shell_rcs[@]} -eq 0 ]; then
575.         # .bash_profile is used on macOS instead of .bashrc for login shells
576.         shell_rcs+=("$HOME/.bash_profile")
577.         success "Found .bash_profile"
578.     fi
579.     if [ -f "$HOME/.profile" ] && [ ${#shell_rcs[@]} -eq 0 ]; then
580.         shell_rcs+=("$HOME/.profile")
581.         success "Found .profile"
582.     fi
583. 
584.     # If no rc files found, create the appropriate one for the user's actual shell
585.     if [ ${#shell_rcs[@]} -eq 0 ]; then
586.         local default_rc
587.         case "$USER_SHELL" in
588.             zsh)  default_rc="$HOME/.zshrc" ;;
589.             bash) default_rc="$HOME/.bashrc" ;;
590.             *)    default_rc="$HOME/.bashrc" ;;
591.         esac
592.         shell_rcs+=("$default_rc")
593.         warn "No shell rc found, will create $(basename "$default_rc")"
594.     fi
595. 
596.     local skills_block
597.     skills_block=$(generate_skills_block)
598. 
599.     for shell_rc in "${shell_rcs[@]}"; do
600.         log "Processing $shell_rc..."
601. 
602.         # Check for existing entries and remove old block first (idempotent)
603.         if grep -q "# Skills Layer" "$shell_rc" 2>/dev/null; then
604.             warn "Shell integration already exists in $shell_rc — replacing"
605.             # sed -i with backup suffix works on both GNU (Linux) and BSD (macOS) sed
606.             sed -i.bak '/# Skills Layer/,/# End Skills Layer/d' "$shell_rc" 2>/dev/null || true
607.             success "Removed old integration from $shell_rc"
608.         fi
609. 
610.         # Add new entries
611.         echo "$skills_block" >> "$shell_rc"
612.         success "Shell integration added to $shell_rc"
613.     done
614. 
615.     # Determine which rc to tell the user to source
616.     local primary_rc
617.     case "$USER_SHELL" in
618.         zsh)  primary_rc="$HOME/.zshrc" ;;
619.         bash) primary_rc="$HOME/.bashrc" ;;
620.         *)    primary_rc="${shell_rcs[0]}" ;;
621.     esac
622. 
623.     # Source the current shell's rc file (may fail in non-interactive subshell — that's OK)
624.     # Use subshell to prevent set -e from catching failures inside sourced rc
625.     if [ -n "$ZSH_VERSION" ]; then
626.         (source "$HOME/.zshrc" 2>/dev/null) || true
627.         success "Shell reloaded (.zshrc)"
628.     elif [ -n "$BASH_VERSION" ]; then
629.         (source "$HOME/.bashrc" 2>/dev/null) || true
630.         success "Shell reloaded (.bashrc)"
631.     else
632.         warn "Run 'source $(basename "$primary_rc")' or restart terminal to activate"
633.     fi
634. }
635. 
636. # =============================================================================
637. # Step 4b: Symlink ECC skills into native skills dirs for codex and pi
638. # =============================================================================
639. 
640. _migrate_flat_to_dir_skills() {
641.     # Migrate flat .md files to <name>/SKILL.md directory format.
642.     # Claude Code's /skills command only discovers directory-format skills.
643.     local skills_dir="$HOME/.claude/skills"
644.     [ ! -d "$skills_dir" ] && return 0
645.     local migrated=0
646.     for flat_file in "$skills_dir"/*.md; do
647.         [ ! -f "$flat_file" ] && continue
648.         local skill_name
649.         skill_name=$(basename "$flat_file" .md)
650.         # Skip learned/ subdirectory (uses flat .md by convention)
651.         [ "$skill_name" = "learned" ] && continue
652.         # Skip if directory already exists (already migrated)
653.         if [ -d "$skills_dir/$skill_name" ]; then
654.             rm -f "$flat_file"
655.             continue
656.         fi
657.         mkdir -p "$skills_dir/$skill_name"
658.         mv "$flat_file" "$skills_dir/$skill_name/SKILL.md"
659.         migrated=$((migrated + 1))
660.     done
661.     [ "$migrated" -gt 0 ] && success "Migrated $migrated flat .md skills to directory format"
662.     return 0
663. }
664. 
665. link_native_skills() {
666.     log "Step 4b: Linking ECC skills into native harness skill directories..."
667. 
668.     if [ ! -d "$ECC_DIR/skills" ]; then
669.         warn "ECC skills not installed, skipping native linking"
670.         return 0
671.     fi
672. 
673.     # Claude Code: ~/.claude/skills/<skill-name>/SKILL.md (directory format)
674.     # Claude Code's /skills command ONLY discovers directory-format skills,
675.     # NOT flat .md files. Migrate any stale flat files first.
676.     local claude_skills_dir="$HOME/.claude/skills"
677.     mkdir -p "$claude_skills_dir"
678.     _migrate_flat_to_dir_skills
679.     local claude_count=0
680.     for dir in "$ECC_DIR/skills"/*/; do
681.         skill_name=$(basename "$dir")
682.         # Use SKILL.md if it exists, otherwise find any .md
683.         local src_file=""
684.         if [ -f "$dir/SKILL.md" ]; then
685.             src_file="$dir/SKILL.md"
686.         else
687.             src_file=$(find "$dir" -maxdepth 1 -name '*.md' -type f 2>/dev/null | head -1)
688.         fi
689.         [ -z "$src_file" ] && continue
690.         local dst_dir="$claude_skills_dir/$skill_name"
691.         local dst="$dst_dir/SKILL.md"
692.         if [ ! -f "$dst" ]; then
693.             mkdir -p "$dst_dir"
694.             cp "$src_file" "$dst"
695.             claude_count=$((claude_count + 1))
696.         fi
697.     done
698.     local total_count=$(find "$claude_skills_dir" -mindepth 1 -maxdepth 1 -type d ! -name 'learned' 2>/dev/null | wc -l | tr -d ' ')
699.     if [ "$claude_count" -eq 0 ] && [ "$total_count" -gt 0 ]; then
700.         success "Claude Code: all ECC skills already in ~/.claude/skills/ ($total_count total)"
```

</details>

Contents of https://github.com/gastownhall/beads/blob/main/docs/COPILOT_INTEGRATION.md:
## GitHub Copilot Integration Guide

[](#github-copilot-integration-guide)

This guide explains how to use beads with GitHub Copilot in VS Code.

## Overview

[](#overview)

Beads provides a persistent, structured memory for coding agents through the MCP (Model Context Protocol) server. With Copilot, you can use natural language to create, update, and track issues without leaving your editor.

**Important:** Beads is a system-wide CLI tool. You install it once and use it in any project. Do NOT clone the beads repository into your project.

## Prerequisites

[](#prerequisites)

*   VS Code 1.96+ with GitHub Copilot extension
*   GitHub Copilot subscription (Individual, Business, or Enterprise)
*   beads CLI installed (`brew install beads` or `npm install -g @beads/bd`)
*   Python 3.10+ OR uv package manager

## Quick Setup

[](#quick-setup)

### Step 1: Install beads-mcp

[](#step-1-install-beads-mcp)

# Using uv (recommended)
uv tool install beads-mcp

# Or using pip
pip install beads-mcp

# Or using pipx
pipx install beads-mcp

### Step 2: Configure VS Code MCP

[](#step-2-configure-vs-code-mcp)

Create or edit `.vscode/mcp.json` in your project:

{
  "servers": {
    "beads": {
      "command": "beads-mcp"
    }
  }
}

**For all projects:** Add to VS Code user-level MCP config:

Platform

Path

macOS

`~/Library/Application Support/Code/User/mcp.json`

Linux

`~/.config/Code/User/mcp.json`

Windows

`%APPDATA%\Code\User\mcp.json`

{
  "servers": {
    "beads": {
      "command": "beads-mcp",
      "args": \[\]
    }
  }
}

### Step 3: Initialize beads in your project

[](#step-3-initialize-beads-in-your-project)

cd your-project
bd init --quiet

This creates a `.beads/` directory with the issue database. The init wizard will ask about git hooks—these are optional and you can skip them if unfamiliar.

### Step 4: Add Copilot instructions (optional but recommended)

[](#step-4-add-copilot-instructions-optional-but-recommended)

Create `.github/copilot-instructions.md`:

\## Issue Tracking

This project uses \*\*bd (beads)\*\* for issue tracking.
Run \`bd prime\` for workflow context.

\*\*Quick reference:\*\*
\- \`bd ready\` - Find unblocked work
\- \`bd create "Title" --type task --priority 2\` - Create issue
\- \`bd close <id>\` - Complete work
\- \`bd dolt push\` - Push changes to remote (run at session end)

### Step 5: Restart VS Code

[](#step-5-restart-vs-code)

Reload the VS Code window for MCP configuration to take effect.

## Using Beads with Copilot

[](#using-beads-with-copilot)

### Natural Language Commands

[](#natural-language-commands)

With MCP configured, ask Copilot Chat:

You say

Copilot does

"What issues are ready to work on?"

Calls `beads_ready`

"Create a bug for the login timeout"

Calls `beads_create` with type=bug

"Show me issue bd-42"

Calls `beads_show`

"Mark bd-42 as complete"

Calls `beads_close`

"What's blocking bd-15?"

Calls `beads_dep_tree`

### MCP Tools Reference

[](#mcp-tools-reference)

Tool

Description

Example

`beads_ready`

List unblocked issues

"What can I work on?"

`beads_list`

List issues with filters

"Show all open bugs"

`beads_create`

Create new issue

"Create a task for refactoring"

`beads_show`

Show issue details

"Show bd-42 details"

`beads_update`

Update issue fields

"Set bd-42 to in progress"

`beads_close`

Close an issue

"Complete bd-42"

`beads_dolt_push`

Push changes to remote

"Push my changes"

`beads_dep_add`

Add dependency

"bd-99 blocks bd-42"

`beads_dep_tree`

Show dependency tree

"What depends on bd-42?"

### Example Workflow

[](#example-workflow)

```
You: What issues are ready to work on?

Copilot: [Calls beads_ready]
There are 3 issues ready:
1. [P1] bd-42: Fix authentication timeout
2. [P2] bd-99: Add password reset flow
3. [P3] bd-17: Update API documentation

You: Let me work on bd-42. Claim it.

Copilot: [Calls beads_claim]
Claimed bd-42 and started work.

You: [... work on the code ...]

You: I found a related bug - the session token isn't being refreshed.
     Create a bug for that, linked to bd-42.

Copilot: [Calls beads_create]
Created bd-103: Session token not refreshed
Linked as discovered-from bd-42.

You: Done with bd-42. Close it with reason "Fixed timeout handling"

Copilot: [Calls beads_close]
Closed bd-42: Fixed timeout handling

You: Push my changes to the remote

Copilot: [Calls beads_dolt_push]
Pushed: 2 issues updated, synced to Dolt remote.
```

## CLI vs MCP: When to Use Each

[](#cli-vs-mcp-when-to-use-each)

Approach

Best For

Trade-offs

**MCP (Copilot Chat)**

Natural language, discovery

Higher token overhead

**CLI (Terminal)**

Scripting, precision, speed

Requires terminal context

You can use both! MCP for conversational work, CLI for quick commands.

## Troubleshooting

[](#troubleshooting)

### MCP tools not appearing in Copilot

[](#mcp-tools-not-appearing-in-copilot)

1.  **Check VS Code version** - MCP requires VS Code 1.96+
2.  **Verify mcp.json syntax** - JSON must be valid
3.

## Detailed Agent Instructions for Beads Development

[](#detailed-agent-instructions-for-beads-development)

**For project overview and quick start, see [AGENTS.md](/gastownhall/beads/blob/main/AGENTS.md)**

This document contains detailed operational instructions for AI agents working on beads development, testing, and releases.

## Development Guidelines

[](#development-guidelines)

### Code Standards

[](#code-standards)

*   **Go version**: see `go.mod` for the required version (currently 1.26+)
*   **Linting**: `golangci-lint run ./...` (baseline warnings documented in [docs/LINTING.md](/gastownhall/beads/blob/main/docs/LINTING.md))
*   **Testing**: All new features need tests (`make test` for the normal local/CI path, `make test-icu-path` only when intentionally exercising the opt-in ICU regex path)
*   **Documentation**: Update relevant .md files

### File Organization

[](#file-organization)

```
beads/
├── cmd/bd/              # CLI commands
├── internal/
│   ├── types/           # Core data types
│   └── storage/         # Storage layer
│       └── dolt/        # Dolt implementation
├── examples/            # Integration examples
└── *.md                 # Documentation
```

### Testing Workflow

[](#testing-workflow)

**IMPORTANT:** Never pollute the production database with test issues!

**For manual testing**, use the `BEADS_DB` environment variable to point to a temporary database:

# Create test issues in isolated database
BEADS\_DB=/tmp/test.db bd init --quiet --prefix test
BEADS\_DB=/tmp/test.db bd create "Test issue" -p 1

# Or for quick testing
BEADS\_DB=/tmp/test.db bd create "Test feature" -p 1

**For automated tests**, use `t.TempDir()` in Go tests:

func TestMyFeature(t \*testing.T) {
    tmpDir := t.TempDir()
    testDB := filepath.Join(tmpDir, ".beads", "beads.db")
    s := newTestStore(t, testDB)
    // ... test code
}

**Git test isolation:** For tests that create temporary git repos, force repo-local hooks:

git config core.hooksPath .git/hooks

Do not rely on the developer's global git config. Global `core.hooksPath` can leak into temp repos and produce flaky test behavior.

**Warning:** bd will warn you when creating issues with "Test" prefix in the production database. Always use `BEADS_DB` for manual testing.

### Before Committing

[](#before-committing)

1.  **Run tests**: `make test` (or `./scripts/test.sh`)
    *   Only if intentionally exercising the ICU regex path: `make test-icu-path`
2.  **Run linter**: `golangci-lint run ./...` (ignore baseline warnings)
3.  **Update docs**: If you changed behavior, update README.md or other docs
4.  **Commit**: With git hooks installed (`bd hooks install`), Dolt changes are auto-committed

### Commit Message Convention

[](#commit-message-convention)

When committing work for an issue, include the issue ID in parentheses at the end:

git commit -m "Fix auth validation bug (bd-abc)"
git commit -m "Add retry logic for database locks (bd-xyz)"

This enables `bd doctor` to detect **orphaned issues** - work that was committed but the issue wasn't closed. The doctor check cross-references open issues against git history to find these orphans.

### Git Workflow

[](#git-workflow)

bd uses **Dolt** as its primary database. Changes are committed to Dolt history automatically (one Dolt commit per write command).

**Install git hooks** for automatic sync:

bd hooks install

### Git Integration

[](#git-integration)

**Dolt sync**: Dolt handles sync natively via `bd dolt push` / `bd dolt pull`. No JSONL export/import needed.

**Protected branches**: Dolt stores data under `refs/dolt/data`, separate from standard Git refs. See [docs/PROTECTED\_BRANCHES.md](/gastownhall/beads/blob/main/docs/PROTECTED_BRANCHES.md).

**Git worktrees**: Work directly with Dolt — no special flags needed. See [docs/ADVANCED.md](/gastownhall/beads/blob/main/docs/ADVANCED.md).

**Merge conflicts**: Rare with hash IDs. Dolt uses cell-level 3-way merge for conflict resolution.

## Git Workflow: PR by Default

[](#git-workflow-pr-by-default)

Crew workers use a PR-based workflow. Beads is a dependency of Gas City, so we defer to the standard PR flow to keep changes reviewable.

*   Work on a feature branch, push the branch, open a PR against `main`
*   `gh pr create` is the normal path to land work
*   Direct push to main is reserved for releases (tag + release commit) and narrow operational fixes; prefer a PR when unsure
*   When handling external contributor PRs, use fix-merge: checkout the PR branch locally, fix/rebase onto main, merge via PR, then close the PR

### External Contributor PRs: Check Before You Build

[](#external-contributor-prs-check-before-you-build)

**Read [CONTRIBUTING.md](/gastownhall/beads/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md)** — it contains promises we've made to contributors. Violating them damages trust and community.

Run the read-only preflight before implementing related work, opening a PR, or merging/closing a PR:

scripts/pr-preflight.sh --search "

<note>Content truncated. Call the fetch tool with a start_index of 5000 to get more content.</note>
````

Contents of https://raw.githubusercontent.com/gastownhall/beads/main/AGENTS.md:
# Agent Instructions

See [AGENT_INSTRUCTIONS.md](AGENT_INSTRUCTIONS.md) for full instructions.

This file exists for compatibility with tools that look for AGENTS.md.

## Key Sections

- **Issue Tracking** - How to use bd for work management
- **Development Guidelines** - Code standards and testing
- **Visual Design System** - Status icons, colors, and semantic styling for CLI output
- **Contributor Protection** - Read [CONTRIBUTING.md](CONTRIBUTING.md) before handling external PRs

## PR Safety for Agents

Before implementing work, opening a PR, or merging/closing a PR, run the PR
preflight:
```bash
scripts/pr-preflight.sh --search "<topic keywords>" --repo gastownhall/beads
scripts/pr-preflight.sh <pr-number> --repo gastownhall/beads
```

External contributor PRs have priority. Review and build on their branch when
possible, preserve their tests and attribution, and never close or supersede
their PR silently. If a rewrite is unavoidable, explain why on the original PR
and credit their design/tests.

## Visual Design Anti-Patterns

**NEVER use emoji-style icons** (🔴🟠🟡🔵⚪) in CLI output. They cause cognitive overload.

**ALWAYS use small Unicode symbols** with semantic colors:
- Status: `○ ◐ ● ✓ ❄`
- Priority: `● P0` (filled circle with color)

See [AGENT_INSTRUCTIONS.md](AGENT_INSTRUCTIONS.md) for full development guidelines.

## Agent Warning: Interactive Commands

**DO NOT use `bd edit`** - it opens an interactive editor ($EDITOR) which AI agents cannot use.

Use `bd update` with flags instead:
```bash
bd update <id> --description "new description"
bd update <id> --title "new title"
bd update <id> --design "design notes"
bd update <id> --notes "additional notes"
bd update <id> --acceptance "acceptance criteria"

# Use stdin for descriptions with special characters (backticks, !, nested quotes)
echo 'Description with `backticks` and "quotes"' | bd create "Title" --description=-
echo 'Updated text' | bd update <id> --description=-
```

## Testing Commands (No Ambiguity)

- Default local test command: `make test` (or `./scripts/test.sh`).
- Opt-in ICU regex path: `make test-icu-path` (or `./scripts/test-icu-path.sh ./...`).
- This ICU path is maintainer-only and not part of normal validation; `make test-full-cgo` and `./scripts/test-cgo.sh` are deprecated aliases.
- For package- or test-scoped shipped-config CGO runs, prefer:
```bash
CGO_ENABLED=1 go test -tags gms_pure_go ./cmd/bd/...
CGO_ENABLED=1 go test -tags gms_pure_go -run '^TestName$' ./cmd/bd/...
```

## Non-Interactive Shell Commands

**ALWAYS use non-interactive flags** with file operations to avoid hanging on confirmation prompts.

Shell commands like `cp`, `mv`, and `rm` may be aliased to include `-i` (interactive) mode on some systems, causing the agent to hang indefinitely waiting for y/n input.

**Use these forms instead:**
```bash
# Force overwrite without prompting
cp -f source dest           # NOT: cp source dest
mv -f source dest           # NOT: mv source dest
rm -f file                  # NOT: rm file

# For recursive operations
rm -rf directory            # NOT: rm -r directory
cp -rf source dest          # NOT: cp -r source dest
```

**Other commands that may prompt:**
- `scp` - use `-o BatchMode=yes` for non-interactive
- `ssh` - use `-o BatchMode=yes` to fail instead of prompting
- `apt-get` - use `-y` flag
- `brew` - use `HOMEBREW_NO_AUTO_UPDATE=1` env var

## Landing the Plane (Session Completion)

**When ending a work session**, you MUST complete ALL steps below. Work is NOT complete until `git push` succeeds.

**MANDATORY WORKFLOW:**

1. **File issues for remaining work** - Create issues for anything that needs follow-up
2. **Run quality gates** (if code changed) - Tests, linters, builds
3. **Update issue status** - Close finished work, update in-progress items
4. **PUSH TO REMOTE** - This is MANDATORY:
   ```bash
   git pull --rebase
   git push
   git status  # MUST show "up to date with origin"
   ```
5. **Clean up** - Clear stashes, prune 

<note>Content truncated. Call the fetch tool with a start_index of 4000 to get more content.</note>
````

</details>

Content type text/plain; charset=utf-8 cannot be simplified to markdown. Here is the raw content:
Contents of https://raw.githubusercontent.com/gastownhall/beads/main/CLAUDE.md:
# Instructions for AI Agents Working on Beads

> **Reading order**: Architecture → `docs/CLAUDE.md` | Workflow → `AGENT_INSTRUCTIONS.md` | Quick ref → `AGENTS.md`
>
> Run `bd prime` for AI-optimized context. Run `bd memories` to see persistent learnings.

## Project Overview

This is **beads** (command: `bd`), a Dolt-powered issue tracker designed for AI-supervised coding workflows. We dogfood our own tool!

## Issue Tracking

We use bd (beads) for issue tracking instead of Markdown TODOs or external tools.

### Quick Reference

```bash
# Find ready work (no blockers)
bd ready --json

# Find ready work including future deferred issues
bd ready --include-deferred --json

# Create new issue
bd create "Issue title" -t bug|feature|task -p 0-4 -d "Description" --json

# Create issue with due date and defer (GH#820)
bd create "Task" --due=+6h              # Due in 6 hours
bd create "Task" --defer=tomorrow       # Hidden from bd ready until tomorrow
bd create "Task" --due="next monday" --defer=+1h  # Both

# Update issue status
bd update <id> --status in_progress --json

# Update issue with due/defer dates
bd update <id> --due=+2d                # Set due date
bd update <id> --defer=""               # Clear defer (show immediately)

# Link discovered work
bd dep add <discovered-id> <parent-id> --type discovered-from

# Complete work
bd close <id> --reason "Done" --json

# Show dependency tree
bd dep tree <id>

# Get issue details
bd show <id> --json

# Query issues by time-based scheduling (GH#820)
bd list --deferred              # Show issues with defer_until set
bd list --defer-before=tomorrow # Deferred before tomorrow
bd list --defer-after=+1w       # Deferred after one week from now
bd list --due-before=+2d        # Due within 2 days
bd list --due-after="next monday" # Due after next Monday
bd list --overdue               # Due date in past (not closed)
```

### Workflow

1. **Check for ready work**: Run `bd ready` to see what's unblocked
2. **Claim your task**: `bd update <id> --claim` (atomic compare-and-swap)
3. **Work on it**: Implement, test, document
4. **Discover new work**: If you find bugs or TODOs, create issues:
   - `bd create "Found bug in auth" -t bug -p 1 --json`
   - Link it: `bd dep add <new-id> <current-id> --type discovered-from`
5. **Complete**: `bd close <id> --reason "Implemented"`

### Issue Types

- `bug` - Something broken that needs fixing
- `feature` - New functionality
- `task` - Work item (tests, docs, refactoring)
- `epic` - Large feature composed of multiple issues
- `chore` - Maintenance work (dependencies, tooling)

### Priorities

- `0` - Critical (security, data loss, broken builds)
- `1` - High (major features, important bugs)
- `2` - Medium (nice-to-have features, minor bugs)
- `3` - Low (polish, optimization)
- `4` - Backlog (future ideas)

### Dependency Types

- `blocks` - Hard dependency (issue X blocks issue Y)
- `related` - Soft relationship (issues are connected)
- `parent-child` - Epic/subtask relationship
- `discovered-from` - Track issues discovered during work

Only `blocks` dependencies affect the ready work queue.

## Development Guidelines

### Code Standards

- **Go version**: see `go.mod` for the required version (currently 1.26+)
- **Build tag**: `-tags gms_pure_go` is **MANDATORY** for all builds and tests
- **Build/Install**: `make build` / `make install` (never bare `go build` or `go install`)
- **Testing**: `make test` (never bare `go test ./...` — it misses required tags)
- **Linting**: `golangci-lint run ./...` (baseline warnings documented in docs/LINTING.md)
- **Documentation**: Update relevant .md files

### File Organization

```
beads/
├── cmd/bd/              # CLI commands (Cobra, one file per command)
├── internal/
│   ├── types/           # Core data types
│   ├── config/          # Configuration
│   ├── configfile/      # Config file handling
│   └── storage/         # Storage layer
│       ├── dolt/        # Dolt implementation (primary)
│       └── embeddedd

<note>Content truncated. Call the fetch tool with a start_index of 4000 to get more content.</note>
````

````
Contents of https://gastownhall.github.io/beads/:
**Beads** (`bd`) is a git-backed issue tracker designed for AI-supervised coding workflows.

## Why Beads?[​](#why-beads "Direct link to Why Beads?")

Traditional issue trackers (Jira, GitHub Issues) weren't designed for AI agents. Beads was built from the ground up for:

*   **AI-native workflows** - Hash-based IDs prevent collisions when multiple agents work concurrently
*   **Dolt-backed storage** - Issues stored in a version-controlled SQL database, enabling collaboration via Dolt-native replication
*   **Dependency-aware execution** - `bd ready` shows only unblocked work
*   **Formula system** - Declarative templates for repeatable workflows
*   **Multi-agent coordination** - Routing, gates, and molecules for complex workflows

## Quick Start[​](#quick-start "Direct link to Quick Start")

```
# Install via Homebrew (macOS/Linux)brew install beads# Or quick install (macOS/Linux/FreeBSD)curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/steveyegge/beads/main/scripts/install.sh | bash# Initialize in your projectcd your-projectbd init --quiet# Create your first issuebd create "Set up database" -p 1 -t task# See ready workbd ready
```

## Core Concepts[​](#core-concepts "Direct link to Core Concepts")

Concept

Description

**Issues**

Work items with priorities, types, labels, and dependencies

**Dependencies**

`blocks`, `parent-child`, `discovered-from`, `related`

**Dolt Server**

Database server for multi-writer access and performance

**Formulas**

Declarative workflow templates (TOML or JSON)

**Molecules**

Work graphs with parent-child relationships

**Gates**

Async coordination primitives (human, timer, GitHub)

## For AI Agents[​](#for-ai-agents "Direct link to For AI Agents")

Beads is optimized for AI coding agents:

```
# Always use --json for programmatic accessbd list --jsonbd show bd-42 --json# Track discovered work during implementationbd create "Found bug in auth" --description="Details..." \  --deps discovered-from:bd-100 --json# Sync at end of sessionbd sync
```

See the [Claude Code integration](/beads/integrations/claude-code) for detailed agent instructions.

## Architecture[​](#architecture "Direct link to Architecture")

```
Dolt DB (.beads/dolt/, gitignored)    ↕ dolt commitLocal Dolt history    ↕ dolt push/pullRemote Dolt repository (shared across machines)
```

The magic is automatic synchronization via Dolt's version-controlled database with built-in replication.

## Next Steps[​](#next-steps "Direct link to Next Steps")

*   [Installation](/beads/getting-started/installation) - Get bd installed
*   [Quick Start](/beads/getting-started/quickstart) - Create your first issues
*   [CLI Reference](/beads/cli-reference) - All available commands
*   [Workflows](/beads/workflows) - Formulas, molecules, and gates
````

</details>

<sub>⏱️ 5m 22s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

Found the root cause. **`combined-skills.txt` is 5.5 MB ≈ 1.37 million tokens — 6.9× Claude's 200K context window.** It's injected via `--append-system-prompt-file` every session.

With `--mode kimi-k2.6:cloud` (or other non-Anthropic backends via Ollama), Claude Code skips Anthropic's client-side context validation, so the overflow is silently handled instead of erroring.

The `lean-skills.txt` (6.7 KB, ~1.6K tokens) was already created for this exact purpose but is **never used** — a dormant bug.

---

Here's the full audit:

## 🔴 Critical

**1. Context limit crash — `_claude_with_skills()` injects `combined-skills.txt` (6.9× context limit)**
- `safe-install.sh:501` hardcodes `combined-skills.txt` in the generated shell function
- `lean-skills.txt` (personal learned skills only, ~1.6K tokens) exists but is never wired up
- Claude Code loads the full skill set natively from `~/.claude/skills/` already — there's no need to inject all 450+ skills on top

**Fix:** Use `lean-skills.txt` for the `--append-system-prompt-file` injection (or skip injection entirely since native loading handles the rest).

**2. `domain_knowledge` classifier captures passwords**
`extract-conversation-skills.py:243` has:
```python
r"(?:the\s+password\s+(?:is|was))\s+",
```
Any message like "the password is..." gets classified as a learnable skill and written to disk.

---

## 🟡 Significant

**3. `install.sh` has duplicate Step 5** (line 424 "Pi", line 440 "Cleanup") — Step 6/Copilot is also numbered wrong. Cosmetic but confusing.

**4. `configs/zshrc-skills-block.sh` aliases point to `~/scripts/agent_harness_modifications/...`** (old path) — the generated block in `safe-install.sh` correctly uses `~/.claude/scripts/...`. Anyone copying the config file manually gets broken `learn-sync` aliases.

**5. `shift` inside `for arg in "$@"` loops** (safe-install.sh) — no-op in bash, doesn't break anything but is a misleading pattern that suggests args are consumed when they aren't.

**6. `codesight-redirect.sh` uses `md5` (macOS) with `cksum` fallback** — works, but `cksum` output format varies by platform and could produce inconsistent state keys.

---

## 🟢 Minor / Design notes

**7. Sync is one-directional** — `sync-learned-skills.sh` pushes from `~/.claude/skills/learned` → codex/pi/openclaw but not back. By design, but could silently drop skills learned while using other harnesses until next `learn-sync`.

**8. `session-reflection.sh` uses `set -e` but calls a script that may not exist** — fails silently due to no guard on `python3 "$CONSOLIDATE_SCRIPT"` when the script is missing (though it does check with `-f` first). Fine.

---

## Beads (`bd`) — Harness Support

There's no `bd setup` command — the closest is `bd init`. Looking at the repo structure, beads has:
- `.claude-plugin` — native Claude Code plugin integration  
- `COPILOT_INTEGRATION.md` — GitHub Copilot setup (MCP via `beads-mcp`)
- `AGENTS.md` / `AGENT_INSTRUCTIONS.md` — instructions for any agent that reads AGENTS.md (Codex, OpenClaw, Claude Code, etc.)

**It works with every harness** that has bash access (all of them) or MCP support (Claude Code, Copilot, Cursor, OpenClaw). The `.claude-plugin` file just makes Claude Code integration tighter (auto-discovered vs manually configured MCP).

**Worth integrating?** Yes, with caveats:
- Genuine value: replaces messy markdown todo tracking with a dependency-aware, version-controlled graph — agents that currently use `todos` table or ad-hoc notes would benefit
- Works natively with every harness you already support
- `bd prime` gives an AI-optimized context dump — complements the skills layer well

**Suggested approach:** Add beads install as an optional step in `install.sh --with-beads`, write `bd init` + `beads-mcp` config for each harness's MCP config (codex/openclaw/copilot already get MCP setup), and add `bd prime` to the global CLAUDE.md instructions.

---

Want me to fix the context limit bug (`lean-skills.txt` swap) and the password classifier issue now?

<sub>⏱️ 6m 23s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

Kimi K2.6 is **256K** — Claude Sonnet 4.6 is 200K so kimi is actually slightly larger. But both are far below 1.37M tokens.

The real reason kimi doesn't crash: **Ollama silently truncates** when input exceeds `num_ctx`. The Anthropic API instead returns an explicit error. So with kimi you're getting most of the combined-skills.txt silently dropped and the session continues. With Claude direct, you get the hard error.

This is actually worse than it looks — with every Ollama-backed model, almost all 450 skills are being silently discarded at the API layer. The injection isn't working as intended for any model.

The fix is clear: use `lean-skills.txt` (~1.6K tokens, personal learned skills) for `--append-system-prompt-file`. The full skill library loads natively via Claude Code's `/skills` system — it doesn't need to be pre-injected into every session's context. Want me to apply the fix?

<details>
<summary>75 lines</summary>

```
486. # =============================================================================
487. # Step 4: Add aliases to shell rc files (idempotent)
488. # =============================================================================
489. 
490. # Generate the skills block to add to shell rc
491. generate_skills_block() {
492.     cat << 'SKILLS_BLOCK'
493. 
494. # Skills Layer - SAFE Install (Everything Claude Code)
495. # All default commands now include skills (ECC + Anthropic Official + Curated + conversation-derived)
496. # To disable: run ~/.claude/scripts/safe-install.sh --uninstall
497. 
498. # Helper function to inject skills as system prompt (uses file to avoid arg length limits)
499. _claude_with_skills() {
500.     local _skills_file="/tmp/claude-skills-$$.txt"
501.     cat ~/.claude/skills-cache/combined-skills.txt > "$_skills_file" 2>/dev/null
502.     if [ -s "$_skills_file" ]; then
503.         (unset SKILLS_CONTENT CODEX_SYSTEM_PROMPT OPENCLAW_SYSTEM_PROMPT; command claude --append-system-prompt-file "$_skills_file" "$@")
504.     else
505.         (unset SKILLS_CONTENT CODEX_SYSTEM_PROMPT OPENCLAW_SYSTEM_PROMPT; command claude "$@")
506.     fi
507.     rm -f "$_skills_file"
508. }
509. 
510. _openclaw_with_skills() {
511.     # OpenClaw natively loads workspace/skills/ SKILL.md files — no extra env var needed.
512.     # OPENCLAW_SYSTEM_PROMPT_FILE is NOT a real openclaw env var (not in source).
513.     (unset SKILLS_CONTENT CODEX_SYSTEM_PROMPT OPENCLAW_SYSTEM_PROMPT; command openclaw "$@")
514. }
515. 
516. _codex_with_skills() {
517.     # Codex loads skills natively from ~/.codex/skills/ (ECC skills symlinked there)
518.     (unset SKILLS_CONTENT OPENCLAW_SYSTEM_PROMPT; command codex "$@")
519. }
520. 
521. _ollama_with_skills() {
522.     # OLLAMA_SYSTEM_FILE is NOT set (breaks ollama launch codex/pi).
523.     # OPENCLAW_SYSTEM_PROMPT_FILE is not a real openclaw env var — removed.
524.     # OpenClaw loads ECC skills natively from ~/.openclaw/workspace/skills/ SKILL.md files.
525.     (unset SKILLS_CONTENT CODEX_SYSTEM_PROMPT OPENCLAW_SYSTEM_PROMPT OLLAMA_SYSTEM_FILE; command ollama "$@")
526. }
527. 
528. _pi_with_skills() {
529.     # Pi loads skills natively from ~/.pi/agent/skills/ (ECC skills symlinked there)
530.     (unset SKILLS_CONTENT CODEX_SYSTEM_PROMPT OPENCLAW_SYSTEM_PROMPT; command pi "$@")
531. }
532. 
533. _copilot_with_skills() {
534.     # Copilot CLI does NOT load ~/.claude/skills/ natively (different format).
535.     # It reads ~/.copilot/mcp-config.json for MCP servers and .github/copilot-instructions.md
536.     # for per-repo guidance. This wrapper ensures a clean environment.
537.     (unset SKILLS_CONTENT CODEX_SYSTEM_PROMPT OPENCLAW_SYSTEM_PROMPT; command copilot "$@")
538. }
539. 
540. # Wrapper aliases
541. alias claude='_claude_with_skills'
542. alias openclaw='_openclaw_with_skills'
543. alias codex='_codex_with_skills'
544. alias ollama='_ollama_with_skills'
545. alias pi='_pi_with_skills'
546. alias copilot='_copilot_with_skills'
547. 
548. # Learning pipeline commands
549. alias learn-sync='bash ~/.claude/scripts/sync-learned-skills.sh'
550. alias learn-sync-dry='bash ~/.claude/scripts/sync-learned-skills.sh --dry-run'
551. alias learn-stats='bash ~/.claude/scripts/sync-learned-skills.sh --stats'
552. alias learn-prune='bash ~/.claude/scripts/sync-learned-skills.sh --prune'
553. # End Skills Layer
554. SKILLS_BLOCK
555. }
556. 
557. setup_shell_integration() {
558.     log "Step 4: Setting up shell integration..."
559.     log "Detected user shell: $USER_SHELL (platform: $OS_TYPE)"
560.
```

97. ```
98. 
99. ClawBio is installed by default (`--with-bio` is on). To update, re-run with `--with-bio` to re-clone the latest from GitHub.
100. 
101. 
102. 
103. ---
104. 
105. ## Quick Start (New Machine)
106. 
107. ```bash
108. # 1. Clone this repo
109. git clone https://github.com/chazhyseni/ai-skillweave
110. cd ai-skillweave
111. 
112. # 2. Install everything (auto-detects macOS/Linux/WSL)
113. ./install.sh
114. 
115. # 3. Reload shell (install.sh tells you which file)
116. source ~/.bashrc   # Linux/WSL
117. source ~/.zshrc    # macOS
118. 
119. # 4. Launch any harness
120. ollama launch claude      # Claude Code + MCP tools
121. ollama launch openclaw    # OpenClaw + subagents + web
122. ollama launch pi          # Pi + subagents
123. ollama launch codex       # Codex via Ollama backend
124. ollama launch copilot     # Copilot CLI + MCP servers
125. ```
126. 
127. ---
128. 
129. ## What This Repo Does
130. 
131. | Component | What it configures |
132. |-----------|-------------------|
133. | **Claude Code MCP** | Adds 8 auto-configured servers to `~/.claude.json`: memory, sequential-thinking, context7, playwright, google-docs-editor, token-optimizer, codesight, skillgraph |
134. | **OpenClaw** | Enables web tools + Ollama plugin in `~/.openclaw/openclaw.json` |
135. | **Pi** | Sets Ollama as provider + installs `pi-subagents` package |
136. | **Codex** | Configures `ollama-launch` provider + `danger-full-access` sandbox in `~/.codex/config.toml` |
137. | **Copilot CLI** | Configures MCP servers in `~/.copilot/mcp-config.json` (run `scripts/setup-copilot.sh` to apply) |
138. | **Ollama integrations** | Sets per-harness model mapping in `~/.ollama/config.json` (qwen3.6 default for all harnesses) |
139. | **Shell wrappers** | Adds `_*_with_skills` functions + aliases in `~/.bashrc` and/or `~/.zshrc` |
140. | **Claude Code skills** | Copies ECC SKILL.md files to `~/.claude/skills/` — visible via `/skills`, works with any launch method |
141. | **ECC Skills cache** | Combined skills cache at `~/.claude/skills-cache/combined-skills.txt` for system prompt injection |
142. 
143. ---
144. 
145. ## Repository Structure
146. 
147. ```
148. ai-skillweave/
149. ├── install.sh                    ← Master installer (run this)
150. ├── safe-install.sh               ← ECC skills installer
151. ├── extract-conversation-skills.py ← 4-stage learning pipeline (Ingestion→Learning→Consolidation→Output)
152. ├── sync-learned-skills.sh        ← Sync learned skills + run pipeline (--stats, --prune, --sync-only)
153. │
154. ├── hooks/                        ← Claude Code hooks (auto-installed by install.sh)
155. │   ├── codesight-redirect.sh     ← PreToolUse: redirect broad searches to codesight
156. │   ├── learning-capture.sh       ← UserPromptSubmit: BMO-style real-time correction capture
157. │   └── session-reflection.sh     ← Session end: consolidate captured events into skills
158. │
159. ├── configs/                      ← Portable config templates
160. │   ├── claude-mcp-servers.json   ← MCP servers for Claude Code CLI
161. │   ├── claude-desktop-mcp-servers.json  ← MCP servers for Claude Desktop GUI
162. │   ├── global-claude-md.md       ← Global CLAUDE.md template (MCP rules + conciseness)
163. │   ├── openclaw.json             ← OpenClaw config (web tools enabled)
164. │   ├── codex-config.toml         ← Codex ollama-launch provider config
165. │   ├── pi-settings.json          ← Pi agent settings
166. │   ├── ollama-integrations.json  ← Ollama integration→model mapping
167. │   └── zshrc-skills-block.sh     ← Shell skills layer block
168. │
169. ├── scripts/                      ← Individual setup scripts
170. │   ├── setup-mcp.sh              ← Inject MCP into ~/.claude.json (CLI)
171. │   ├── setup-claude-md.sh        ← Install global CLAUDE.md (MCP rules + conciseness)
172. │   ├── setup-hooks.sh            ← Install PreToolUse hook (codesight-redirect)
173. │   ├── setup-learning-hook.sh    ← Install UserPromptSubmit hook (BMO learning capture)
174. │   ├── consolidate-learnings.py  ← Consolidate captured events into SKILL.md files
175. │   ├── setup-claude-desktop.sh   ← Standalone: MCP + skills for Claude Desktop GUI
176. │   ├── build-desktop-skills.sh   ← Package .skill files for Desktop upload
177. │   ├── setup-openclaw.sh         ← Apply OpenClaw config
178. │   ├── setup-codex.sh            ← Apply Codex config
179. │   ├── setup-pi.sh               ← Apply Pi settings
180. │   ├── setup-ollama-config.sh    ← Apply Ollama integration→model mapping
181. │   ├── update-ecc.sh             ← Pull latest ECC + rebuild cache + learn-sync + re-sync harnesses
182. │   ├── disable-zscaler.sh        ← Disable Zscaler proxy
183. │   └── verify.sh                 ← Health check all components
184. │
185. ├── docs/
186. │   ├── AUDIT.md                  ← MCP/subagent audit (what was fixed + why)
187. │   └── TROUBLESHOOTING.md        ← Common issues and fixes
188. │
189. └── shared-learnings/
190.     └── learnings.md              ← Cross-harness learned patterns log
191. ```
192. 
193. ---
194. 
195. ## Platform Support
196. 
197. | Platform | Status | Shell | Notes |
198. |----------|--------|-------|-------|
199. | **macOS** | ✅ Tested | zsh (default) | Homebrew for dependencies |
200. | **Linux** | ✅ Tested | bash (default) | apt/dnf/pacman auto-detected |
````

</details>

<details>
