```
700.         success "Claude Code: all ECC skills already in ~/.claude/skills/ ($total_count total)"
701.     else
702.         success "Claude Code: $claude_count ECC skills installed to ~/.claude/skills/ ($total_count total)"
703.     fi
704. 
705.     # Codex: handled by update-ecc.sh which properly sanitizes YAML (symlinks
706.     # for clean skills, sanitized copies for skills with block scalars/extra fields).
707.     # safe-install.sh used to skip invalid skills entirely — update-ecc.sh fixes them.
708.     if [ -d "$HOME/.codex/skills" ]; then
709.         log "Codex skills will be synced by update-ecc.sh (proper YAML sanitization)"
710.     else
711.         warn "~/.codex/skills not found — install Codex CLI first"
712.     fi
713. 
714.     # OpenClaw: ~/.openclaw/workspace/skills/<skill-name>/ (real copies, NOT symlinks)
715.     # Must be real files inside workspace root - OpenClaw rejects symlinks escaping the workspace
716.     # Skills appear with source="openclaw-workspace" and status="✓ ready"
717.     if [ -d "$HOME/.openclaw/workspace" ]; then
718.         local openclaw_count=0
719.         local ws_skills="$HOME/.openclaw/workspace/skills"
720.         mkdir -p "$ws_skills"
721.         for dir in "$ECC_DIR/skills"/*/; do
722.             skill_name=$(basename "$dir")
723.             [ ! -f "$dir/SKILL.md" ] && continue
724.             grep -q '^description: *[>|]' "$dir/SKILL.md" 2>/dev/null && continue
725.             if grep -E '^[a-z_]+:' "$dir/SKILL.md" 2>/dev/null | grep -qvE '^(name|description|origin|tools):'; then
726.                 continue
727.             fi
728.             dst="$ws_skills/$skill_name"
729.             if [ ! -d "$dst" ]; then
730.                 mkdir -p "$dst"
731.                 cp "$dir/SKILL.md" "$dst/SKILL.md"
732.                 openclaw_count=$((openclaw_count + 1))
733.             fi
734.         done
735.         success "OpenClaw: $openclaw_count ECC skills copied to workspace ($(ls "$ws_skills" | wc -l | tr -d ' ') total)"
736.     else
737.         warn "~/.openclaw/workspace not found — install openclaw first"
738.     fi
739. 
740.     # Pi: ~/.pi/agent/skills/<skill-name>/ (each MUST have SKILL.md)
741.     if [ -d "$HOME/.pi/agent" ]; then
742.         mkdir -p "$HOME/.pi/agent/skills"
743.         local pi_count=0
744.         for dir in "$ECC_DIR/skills"/*/; do
745.             skill_name=$(basename "$dir")
746.             # Skip dirs without SKILL.md - they break pi's skill scanner
747.             [ ! -f "$dir/SKILL.md" ] && continue
748.             if [ ! -e "$HOME/.pi/agent/skills/$skill_name" ]; then
749.                 ln -s "$dir" "$HOME/.pi/agent/skills/$skill_name"
750.                 pi_count=$((pi_count + 1))
751.             fi
752.         done
753.         success "Pi: $pi_count ECC skills linked ($(ls "$HOME/.pi/agent/skills/" | wc -l | tr -d ' ') total)"
754.     else
755.         warn "~/.pi/agent not found — install pi first"
756.     fi
757. 
758.     # K-Dense Scientific Skills: install into Claude Code native skills dir
759.     # These are directory-based skills (SKILL.md + references/ + scripts/ + assets/)
760.     # Claude Code ONLY discovers <name>/SKILL.md directory format
761.     if [ -d "$SCIENCE_DIR/scientific-skills" ]; then
762.         local science_count=0
763.         for dir in "$SCIENCE_DIR/scientific-skills"/*/; do
764.             skill_name=$(basename "$dir")
765.             [ ! -f "$dir/SKILL.md" ] && continue
766.             # Claude Code: directory format (<name>/SKILL.md)
767.             local dst_dir="$claude_skills_dir/$skill_name"
768.             local dst="$dst_dir/SKILL.md"
769.             if [ ! -f "$dst" ]; then
770.                 mkdir -p "$dst_dir"
771.                 cp "$dir/SKILL.md" "$dst"
772.                 science_count=$((science_count + 1))
773.             fi
774.         done
775.         success "Claude Code: $science_count K-Dense scientific skills installed to ~/.claude/skills/"
776. 
777.         # OpenClaw: real copies for K-Dense skills (sanitize YAML)
778.         if [ -d "$HOME/.openclaw/workspace" ]; then
779.             local oc_science_count=0
780.             for dir in "$SCIENCE_DIR/scientific-skills"/*/; do
781.                 skill_name=$(basename "$dir")
782.                 [ ! -f "$dir/SKILL.md" ] && continue
783.                 dst="$ws_skills/$skill_name"
784.                 if [ ! -d "$dst" ]; then
785.                     mkdir -p "$dst"
786.                     # Copy SKILL.md (sanitize if needed)
787.                     if grep -q '^description: *[>|]' "$dir/SKILL.md" 2>/dev/null || \
788.                        grep -E '^[a-z_]+:' "$dir/SKILL.md" 2>/dev/null | grep -qvE '^(name|description|origin|tools|license|allowed-tools|metadata|compatibility):'; then
789.                         # Needs sanitization — copy only SKILL.md with sanitized content
790.                         python3 -c "
791. import re, sys
792. with open('$dir/SKILL.md') as f:
793.     content = f.read()
794. parts = content.split('---', 2)
795. if len(parts) >= 3:
796.     print('---' + parts[1] + '---' + parts[2])
797. else:
798.     print(content)
799. " > "$dst/SKILL.md" 2>/dev/null || cp "$dir/SKILL.md" "$dst/SKILL.md"
800.                     else

```
1. # =============================================================================
2. # SAFE Install - Everything Claude Code Skills
3. # =============================================================================
4. # Zero-RISK installation with automatic shell integration
5. # Installs ALL skills: ECC + Anthropic Official + Curated + your conversation-derived skills
6. # Works with: claude, openclaw, codex, pi, copilot, ollama (direct or via ollama launch)
7. # Supports: .zshrc, .bashrc, .profile
8. # Platforms: macOS, Linux, Windows (WSL/MSYS2/Cygwin)
9. # =============================================================================
10. set -e
11. 
12. CLAUDE_DIR="$HOME/.claude"
13. ECC_DIR="$HOME/.claude-everything-claude-code"
14. CURATED_DIR="$HOME/.claude-curated-skills"
15. SCIENCE_DIR="$HOME/.claude-scientific-skills"
16. CLAWBIO_DIR="$HOME/.claude-clawbio-skills"
17. SKILLS_CACHE_DIR="$HOME/.claude/skills-cache"
18. 
19. # Platform detection
20. case "$(uname -s)" in
21.     Darwin*)                 OS_TYPE="macOS" ;;
22.     Linux*)                  OS_TYPE="Linux" ;;
23.     MINGW*|MSYS*|CYGWIN*)   OS_TYPE="Windows" ;;  # untested; use WSL
24.     *)                       OS_TYPE="Unknown" ;;
25. esac
26. 
27. # Detect user's default shell (not the shell running this script)
28. _detect_user_shell() {
29.     local shell_name="${SHELL##*/}"
30.     case "$shell_name" in
31.         zsh)  echo "zsh" ;;
32.         bash) echo "bash" ;;
33.         fish) echo "fish" ;;
34.         *)    echo "bash" ;;  # safe default
35.     esac
36. }
37. USER_SHELL="$(_detect_user_shell)"
38. 
39. # Colors
40. BLUE='\033[0;34m'
41. GREEN='\033[0;32m'
42. YELLOW='\033[1;33m'
43. RED='\033[0;31m'
44. NC='\033[0m'
45. 
46. log() { echo -e "${BLUE}[INFO]${NC} $1"; }
47. success() { echo -e "${GREEN}[OK]${NC} $1"; }
48. warn() { echo -e "${YELLOW}[WARN]${NC} $1"; }
49. error() { echo -e "${RED}[ERROR]${NC} $1"; }
50. 
51. # Parse arguments
52. WITH_CURATED=false
53. CURATED_ONLY=false
54. WITH_SCIENCE=true
55. WITH_BIO=true
56. WITH_LEARN=true
57. 
58. for arg in "$@"; do
59.     case $arg in
60.         --with-curated)
61.             WITH_CURATED=true
62.             shift
63.             ;;
64.         --curated-only)
65.             CURATED_ONLY=true
66.             WITH_CURATED=true
67.             shift
68.             ;;
69.         --with-science)
70.             WITH_SCIENCE=true
71.             shift
72.             ;;
73.         --without-science)
74.             WITH_SCIENCE=false
75.             shift
76.             ;;
77.         --with-bio)
78.             WITH_BIO=true
79.             shift
80.             ;;
81.         --without-bio)
82.             WITH_BIO=false
83.             shift
84.             ;;
85.         --no-learn)
86.             WITH_LEARN=false
87.             shift
88.             ;;
89.         --uninstall)
90.             # Handled in main
91.             ;;
92.     esac
93. done
94. 
95. echo ""
96. echo "╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗"
97. echo "║   SAFE Install - Everything Claude Code Skills          ║"
98. if [ "$WITH_CURATED" = true ]; then
99.     echo "║   + Curated Skills (Anthropic Official + Codex)          ║"
100. fi
101. if [ "$WITH_SCIENCE" = true ]; then
102.     echo "║   + Scientific Skills (K-Dense Agent Skills)            ║"
103. fi
104. if [ "$WITH_BIO" = true ]; then
105.     echo "║   + Bioinformatics Skills (ClawBio)                    ║"
106. fi
107. echo "║   Zero-Risk • Auto Shell Integration                     ║"
108. echo "╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝"
109. echo ""
110. 
111. # =============================================================================
112. # Bootstrap prerequisites (lightweight — install.sh does the heavy lifting)
113. # =============================================================================
114. if ! command -v git >/dev/null 2>&1; then
115.     log "git not found — attempting install..."
116.     if [ "$OS_TYPE" = "macOS" ] && command -v brew >/dev/null 2>&1; then
117.         brew install git 2>/dev/null || warn "brew install git failed"
118.     elif command -v apt-get >/dev/null 2>&1; then
119.         sudo apt-get update -qq && sudo apt-get install -y -qq git 2>/dev/null || warn "apt install git failed"
120.     elif command -v dnf >/dev/null 2>&1; then
121.         sudo dnf install -y git 2>/dev/null || warn "dnf install git failed"
122.     elif command -v pacman >/dev/null 2>&1; then
123.         sudo pacman -S --noconfirm git 2>/dev/null || warn "pacman install git failed"
124.     fi
125. fi
126. command -v git >/dev/null 2>&1 || error "git is required but could not be installed"
127. 
128. if ! command -v python3 >/dev/null 2>&1; then
129.     log "python3 not found — attempting install..."
130.     if [ "$OS_TYPE" = "macOS" ] && command -v brew >/dev/null 2>&1; then
131.         brew install python3 2>/dev/null || warn "brew install python3 failed"
132.     elif command -v apt-get >/dev/null 2>&1; then
133.         sudo apt-get update -qq && sudo apt-get install -y -qq python3 python3-pip 2>/dev/null || warn "apt install python3 failed"
134.     elif command -v dnf >/dev/null 2>&1; then
135.         sudo dnf install -y python3 python3-pip 2>/dev/null || warn "dnf install python3 failed"
136.     elif command -v pacman >/dev/null 2>&1; then
137.         sudo pacman -S --noconfirm python python-pip 2>/dev/null || warn "pacman install python failed"
138.     fi
139. fi
140. command -v python3 >/dev/null 2>&1 || error "python3 is required but could not be installed"
```

470. # SKILLS_CONTENT available via: \$(cat ~/.claude/skills-cache/combined-skills.txt)
471. COMBINED_SKILLS_FILE='$COMBINED_FILE'
472. LOADER
473. 
474.     chmod +x "$SKILLS_CACHE_DIR/load-skills.sh"
475.     success "Loader created: $SKILLS_CACHE_DIR/load-skills.sh"
476.     # Lean cache: personal learned skills only (injected into Anthropic Opus sessions)
477.     if ls "$CLAUDE_DIR/skills/learned"/*.md >/dev/null 2>&1; then
478.         cat "$CLAUDE_DIR/skills/learned"/*.md > "$SKILLS_CACHE_DIR/lean-skills.txt"
479.         success "Lean cache created: $SKILLS_CACHE_DIR/lean-skills.txt (personal skills only — 98% fewer tokens)"
480.     else
481.         > "$SKILLS_CACHE_DIR/lean-skills.txt"
482.         warn "No learned skills found — lean cache is empty"
483.     fi
484. }
485. 
486. # =============================================================================
487. # Step 4: Add aliases to shell rc files (idempotent)
488. # =============================================================================
489. 
490. # Generate the skills block to add to shell rc
491. generate_skills_block() {
492.     cat << 'SKILLS_BLOCK'
493. 
494. # Skills Layer - SAFE Install (Everything Claude Code)
495. # All default commands now include skills (ECC + Anthropic Official + Curated + conversation-derived)
496. # To disable: run ~/.claude/scripts/safe-install.sh --uninstall
497. 
498. # Helper function to inject skills as system prompt (uses file to avoid arg length limits)
499. _claude_with_skills() {
500.     local _skills_file="/tmp/claude-skills-$$.txt"
501.     cat ~/.claude/skills-cache/combined-skills.txt > "$_skills_file" 2>/dev/null
502.     if [ -s "$_skills_file" ]; then
503.         (unset SKILLS_CONTENT CODEX_SYSTEM_PROMPT OPENCLAW_SYSTEM_PROMPT; command claude --append-system-prompt-file "$_skills_file" "$@")
504.     else
505.         (unset SKILLS_CONTENT CODEX_SYSTEM_PROMPT OPENCLAW_SYSTEM_PROMPT; command claude "$@")
506.     fi
507.     rm -f "$_skills_file"
508. }
509. 
510. _openclaw_with_skills() {
511.     # OpenClaw natively loads workspace/skills/ SKILL.md files — no extra env var needed.
512.     # OPENCLAW_SYSTEM_PROMPT_FILE is NOT a real openclaw env var (not in source).
513.     (unset SKILLS_CONTENT CODEX_SYSTEM_PROMPT OPENCLAW_SYSTEM_PROMPT; command openclaw "$@")
514. }
515. 
516. _codex_with_skills() {
517.     # Codex loads skills natively from ~/.codex/skills/ (ECC skills symlinked there)
518.     (unset SKILLS_CONTENT OPENCLAW_SYSTEM_PROMPT; command codex "$@")
519. }
520. 
521. _ollama_with_skills() {
522.     # OLLAMA_SYSTEM_FILE is NOT set (breaks ollama launch codex/pi).
523.     # OPENCLAW_SYSTEM_PROMPT_FILE is not a real openclaw env var — removed.
524.     # OpenClaw loads ECC skills natively from ~/.openclaw/workspace/skills/ SKILL.md files.
525.     (unset SKILLS_CONTENT CODEX_SYSTEM_PROMPT OPENCLAW_SYSTEM_PROMPT OLLAMA_SYSTEM_FILE; command ollama "$@")
526. }
527. 
528. _pi_with_skills() {
529.     # Pi loads skills natively from ~/.pi/agent/skills/ (ECC skills symlinked there)
530.     (unset SKILLS_CONTENT CODEX_SYSTEM_PROMPT OPENCLAW_SYSTEM_PROMPT; command pi "$@")
531. }
532. 
533. _copilot_with_skills() {
534.     # Copilot CLI does NOT load ~/.claude/skills/ natively (different format).
535.     # It reads ~/.copilot/mcp-config.json for MCP servers and .github/copilot-instructions.md
536.     # for per-repo guidance. This wrapper ensures a clean environment.
537.     (unset SKILLS_CONTENT CODEX_SYSTEM_PROMPT OPENCLAW_SYSTEM_PROMPT; command copilot "$@")
538. }
539. 
540. # Wrapper aliases
541. alias claude='_claude_with_skills'
542. alias openclaw='_openclaw_with_skills'
543. alias codex='_codex_with_skills'
544. alias ollama='_ollama_with_skills'
545. alias pi='_pi_with_skills'
546. alias copilot='_copilot_with_skills'
547. 
548. # Learning pipeline commands
549. alias learn-sync='bash ~/.claude/scripts/sync-learned-skills.sh'
550. alias learn-sync-dry='bash ~/.claude/scripts/sync-learned-skills.sh --dry-run'
551. alias learn-stats='bash ~/.claude/scripts/sync-learned-skills.sh --stats'
552. alias learn-prune='bash ~/.claude/scripts/sync-learned-skills.sh --prune'
553. # End Skills Layer
554. SKILLS_BLOCK
555. }
556. 
557. setup_shell_integration() {
558.     log "Step 4: Setting up shell integration..."
559.     log "Detected user shell: $USER_SHELL (platform: $OS_TYPE)"
560.
```

```
1. #!/usr/bin/env python3
2. """
3. =============================================================================
4. Conversation History to Skills Extractor — ALMA-Inspired Pipeline
5. =============================================================================
6. 4-stage pipeline for extracting concise, generalizable skills from
7. conversation histories across multiple AI agent harnesses.
8. 
9. Stages:
10.   1. Ingestion  — Parse histories, classify utterances into memory types
11.   2. Learning   — Group similar patterns, require min evidence, score confidence
12.   3. Consolidation — Deduplicate, abstract, generalization filter
13.   4. Output     — Write concise SKILL.md files with condition+strategy form
14. 
15. Based on patterns from:
16.   - ALMA (RBKunnela/ALMA-memory): memory types, confidence scoring, decay
17.   - Reflexion (Shinn et al.): extract from failures, distill to plans
18.   - mem0: LLM-based extraction, entity linking
19.   - ai-linter: quality enforcement (≤500 lines, ≤5000 tokens)
20. 
21. Usage:
22.     python3 extract-conversation-skills.py [OPTIONS]
23. 
24. Options:
25.     --input DIR         Directory with conversation exports (auto-detect if not set)
26.     --output DIR        Directory to write generated skills (default: ~/.claude/skills/learned/)
27.     --harness H         Which harness to extract from: claude|codex|openclaw|all (default: all)
28.     --dry-run           Show what would be extracted without writing files
29.     --verbose           Show detailed analysis
30.     --stats             Show usage/decay statistics only (no extraction)
31.     --prune             Remove decayed/low-quality skills
32. =============================================================================
33. """
34. 
35. import os
36. # Prevent huggingface tokenizers from enabling parallelism before fork,
37. # which causes deadlock warnings and slowdowns when subprocess is used.
38. os.environ["TOKENIZERS_PARALLELISM"] = "false"
39. 
40. import re
41. import json
42. import hashlib
43. import math
44. from pathlib import Path
45. from datetime import datetime, timedelta
46. from collections import defaultdict
47. from dataclasses import dataclass, field
48. from typing import Dict, List, Set, Tuple, Optional
49. import time
50. from concurrent.futures import ThreadPoolExecutor, as_completed
51. 
52. 
53. # =============================================================================
54. # Dependency auto-install
55. # =============================================================================
56. 
57. def _ensure_deps(verbose: bool = False):
58.     """Ensure sentence-transformers and scikit-learn are available."""
59.     required = {
60.         "sentence-transformers": "sentence-transformers>=3.0.0",
61.         "scikit-learn": "scikit-learn>=1.5.0",
62.         "numpy": "numpy>=1.26.0",
63.     }
64.     missing = []
65.     for module, pkg in required.items():
66.         try:
67.             __import__(module.replace("-", "_"))
68.         except ImportError:
69.             missing.append(pkg)
70.     
71.     if missing:
72.         if verbose:
73.             print(f"  [DEPS] Installing: {', '.join(missing)}...")
74.         import subprocess, sys
75.         in_venv = sys.prefix != getattr(sys, "base_prefix", sys.prefix)
76.         cmd = [sys.executable, "-m", "pip", "install", "--quiet"] + missing
77.         if not in_venv:
78.             cmd.append("--user")
79.         result = subprocess.run(cmd, capture_output=True, text=True)
80.         if result.returncode != 0:
81.             print(f"  [WARN] Failed to auto-install dependencies: {result.stderr[:200]}")
82.             print(f"  [WARN] Please run: pip install {' '.join(missing)}")
83.             return False
84.         if verbose:
85.             print(f"  [DEPS] Installed: {', '.join(missing)}")
86.     return True
87. 
88. 
89. # =============================================================================
90. # Configuration
91. # =============================================================================
92. 
93. CLAUDE_HISTORY_PATHS = [
94.     Path.home() / ".claude" / "projects",   # Claude Code: projects/<project>/<session-id>.jsonl
95. ]
96. CODEX_HISTORY_PATHS = [
97.     Path.home() / ".codex" / "sessions",     # Codex: sessions/YYYY/MM/DD/rollout-*.jsonl
98. ]
99. OPENCLAW_HISTORY_PATHS = [
100.     Path.home() / ".openclaw" / "agents",    # OpenClaw: agents/main/sessions/*.jsonl
101. ]
102. PI_HISTORY_PATHS = [
103.     Path.home() / ".pi" / "agent" / "sessions",  # Pi: agent/sessions/<project>/*.jsonl
104. ]
105. DEFAULT_OUTPUT_DIR = Path.home() / ".claude" / "skills" / "learned"
106. USAGE_FILE = DEFAULT_OUTPUT_DIR / ".usage.json"
107. 
108. # Model routing hierarchy: try in order until one works
109. # Note: Claude Code CLI (-p flag) requires ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, so we check first
110. MODEL_PRIORITY = [
111.     {"name": "ollama-cloud", "type": "ollama", "model": "qwen3.5:cloud", "timeout": 180},  # Ollama cloud (best quality, no local GPU needed)
112.     {"name": "ollama-local", "type": "ollama", "model": "qwen3.6:latest", "timeout": 240},  # Local qwen3.6 (fallback, slower but private)
113. ]
114. 
115. # Pipeline thresholds (ALMA-inspired)
116. MIN_OCCURRENCES = 3
117. MIN_CONFIDENCE = 0.5
118. GROUP_SIMILARITY = 0.5       # Jaccard threshold for grouping
119. DEDUP_SIMILARITY = 0.85      # Token overlap for deduplication
120. MAX_SKILL_LINES = 50
121. MAX_EXAMPLES_STORED = 5
122. DECAY_HALF_LIFE_DAYS = 90
123. PRUNE_THRESHOLD = 0.2
124. FEEDBACK_MIN_SAMPLES = 5
125. 
126. MEMORY_TYPES = ["heuristic", "anti_pattern", "preference", "domain_knowledge"]
127. GENERALIZABLE_TYPES = {"heuristic", "anti_pattern"}
128. 
129. 
130. def _try_get_completion(prompt: str, timeout: int = 60, verbose: bool = False) -> Optional[str]:
131.     """Try to get completion from models in priority order. Returns first successful response.
132.     
133.     Tries standard Ollama HTTP API first (reliable, fast), then falls back to subprocess.
134.     """
135.     import subprocess
136.     
137.     for model_config in MODEL_PRIORITY:
138.         model_name = model_config["name"]
139.         model_type = model_config["type"]
140.         model_id = model_config["model"]
```

</details>

/Users/chazh/Documents/GitHub/ai-skillweave/install.sh (lines 1-220)

<details>
<summary>220 lines</summary>

```
1. #!/bin/bash
2. # =============================================================================
3. # install.sh — Master setup for Ollama Agent Harness
4. # =============================================================================
5. # One-command setup for all AI agent harnesses on a new machine.
6. # Configures: Claude Code (MCP), OpenClaw, Pi, Codex, Copilot CLI, shell skills layer.
7. #
8. # Requirements:
9. #   - Ollama installed and running (https://ollama.com)
10. #   - At least one model: ollama pull <model-name>
11. #   - Claude Code, OpenClaw, Pi, Codex, and/or Copilot installed as needed
12. #
13. # Usage:
14. #   ./install.sh                           # Full setup (all harnesses + all skills)
15. #   ./install.sh --without-science         # Skip K-Dense scientific skills
16. #   ./install.sh --model llama3.2:3b       # Use a specific Ollama model
17. #   ./install.sh --skip-skills             # Skip ECC skills installation
18. #   ./install.sh --only claude             # Only configure claude
19. #   ./install.sh --only openclaw           # Only configure openclaw
20. #   ./install.sh --uninstall               # Remove shell integrations
21. #   ./install.sh --verify                  # Run health check only
22. # =============================================================================
23. set -e
24. 
25. REPO_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
26. 
27. # =============================================================================
28. # Platform detection
29. # =============================================================================
30. case "$(uname -s)" in
31.     Darwin*)                 OS_TYPE="macOS" ;;
32.     Linux*)                  OS_TYPE="Linux" ;;
33.     MINGW*|MSYS*|CYGWIN*)   OS_TYPE="Windows" ;;  # untested; use WSL
34.     *)                       OS_TYPE="Unknown" ;;
35. esac
36. 
37. # Detect user's login shell for RC file references
38. case "${SHELL##*/}" in
39.     zsh)  USER_SHELL="zsh";  SHELL_RC="~/.zshrc" ;;
40.     bash) USER_SHELL="bash"; SHELL_RC="~/.bashrc" ;;
41.     *)    USER_SHELL="bash"; SHELL_RC="~/.bashrc" ;;
42. esac
43. 
44. # Platform-aware package install hint
45. case "$OS_TYPE" in
46.     macOS)   PKG_HINT="brew install" ;;
47.     Linux)
48.         if command -v apt-get >/dev/null 2>&1; then
49.             PKG_HINT="apt-get install"
50.         elif command -v dnf >/dev/null 2>&1; then
51.             PKG_HINT="dnf install"
52.         elif command -v pacman >/dev/null 2>&1; then
53.             PKG_HINT="pacman -S"
54.         else
55.             PKG_HINT="your package manager to install"
56.         fi
57.         ;;
58.     Windows) PKG_HINT="your package manager to install" ;;
59.     *)       PKG_HINT="your package manager to install" ;;
60. esac
61. 
62. BLUE='\033[0;34m'; GREEN='\033[0;32m'; YELLOW='\033[1;33m'; RED='\033[0;31m'; CYAN='\033[0;36m'; NC='\033[0m'
63. log()     { echo -e "${BLUE}[INSTALL]${NC} $1"; }
64. success() { echo -e "${GREEN}[OK]${NC} $1"; }
65. warn()    { echo -e "${YELLOW}[WARN]${NC} $1"; }
66. error()   { echo -e "${RED}[ERROR]${NC} $1"; exit 1; }
67. section() { echo ""; echo -e "${CYAN}═══════════════════════════════════════${NC}"; echo -e "${CYAN}  $1${NC}"; echo -e "${CYAN}═══════════════════════════════════════${NC}"; }
68. 
69. # =============================================================================
70. # Parse arguments
71. # =============================================================================
72. OLLAMA_MODEL="qwen3.6"
73. SKIP_SKILLS=false
74. WITH_SCIENCE=true
75. WITH_CURATED=true
76. WITH_BIO=true
77. WITH_LEARN=true
78. ONLY_TARGET=""
79. 
80. while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
81.     case "$1" in
82.         --model)            OLLAMA_MODEL="$2"; shift 2 ;;
83.         --skip-skills)      SKIP_SKILLS=true; shift ;;
84.         --with-science)     WITH_SCIENCE=true; shift ;;
85.         --without-science)  WITH_SCIENCE=false; shift ;;
86.         --with-curated)     WITH_CURATED=true; shift ;;
87.         --without-curated)  WITH_CURATED=false; shift ;;
88.         --with-bio)         WITH_BIO=true; shift ;;
89.         --without-bio)      WITH_BIO=false; shift ;;
90.         --learn)            WITH_LEARN=true; shift ;;
91.         --no-learn)         WITH_LEARN=false; shift ;;
92.         --only)             ONLY_TARGET="$2"; shift 2 ;;
93.         --uninstall)
94.             "$REPO_DIR/safe-install.sh" --uninstall
95.             exit 0
96.             ;;
97.         --verify)
98.             bash "$REPO_DIR/scripts/verify.sh"
99.             exit 0
100.             ;;
101.         --help|-h)
102.             echo "Usage: ./install.sh [--model MODEL] [--skip-skills] [--with-science] [--without-science] [--with-bio] [--without-bio] [--only TARGET] [--uninstall] [--verify]"
103.             echo ""
104.             echo "  --with-science     Include K-Dense scientific skills (default)"
105.             echo "  --without-science  Skip K-Dense scientific skills"
106.             echo "  --with-bio         Include ClawBio bioinformatics skills (default)"
107.             echo "  --without-bio      Skip ClawBio bioinformatics skills"
108.             exit 0
109.             ;;
110.         *) shift ;;
111.     esac
112. done
113. 
114. echo ""
115. echo "╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗"
116. echo "║   ai-skillweave — Full Setup                           ║"
117. echo "╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝"
118. echo ""
119. log "Platform: $OS_TYPE ($USER_SHELL)"
120. log "Model:    $OLLAMA_MODEL"
121. log "Repo:     $REPO_DIR"
122. echo ""
123. 
124. if [ "$OS_TYPE" = "Windows" ]; then
125.     warn "Native Windows detected. This installer targets bash-based systems."
126.     warn "For best results, run inside WSL (Windows Subsystem for Linux)."
127.     warn "Continuing anyway — some steps may not apply."
128.     echo ""
129. fi
130. 
131. # =============================================================================
132. # Step 0a: Bootstrap prerequisites (git, node/npm, ollama, python3)
133. # =============================================================================
134. section "Bootstrap"
135. 
136. _install_if_missing() {
137.     local cmd="$1"
138.     local name="$2"
139.     local install_cmd="$3"
140.     if command -v "$cmd" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
141.         success "$name already installed: $(command -v "$cmd")"
142.         return 0
143.     fi
144.     log "Installing $name..."
145.     if eval "$install_cmd"; then
146.         success "$name installed"
147.         return 0
148.     else
149.         warn "$name installation failed — install manually, then re-run"
150.         return 1
151.     fi
152. }
153. 
154. # --- git ---
155. if [ "$OS_TYPE" = "macOS" ]; then
156.     _install_if_missing git "git" "brew install git"
157. else
158.     if command -v apt-get >/dev/null 2>&1; then
159.         _install_if_missing git "git" "sudo apt-get update -qq && sudo apt-get install -y -qq git"
160.     elif command -v dnf >/dev/null 2>&1; then
161.         _install_if_missing git "git" "sudo dnf install -y git"
162.     elif command -v pacman >/dev/null 2>&1; then
163.         _install_if_missing git "git" "sudo pacman -S --noconfirm git"
164.     fi
165. fi
166. 
167. # --- node + npm ---
168. if ! command -v node >/dev/null 2>&1 || ! command -v npm >/dev/null 2>&1; then
169.     log "Node.js/npm not found — installing..."
170.     if [ "$OS_TYPE" = "macOS" ]; then
171.         brew install node 2>/dev/null || warn "brew install node failed"
172.     else
173.         # Use NodeSource setup for LTS Node 22
174.         curl -fsSL https://deb.nodesource.com/setup_22.x | bash - 2>/dev/null && \
175.             apt-get install -y nodejs 2>/dev/null || \
176.             warn "NodeSource install failed — install node manually"
177.     fi
178.     # Verify
179.     if command -v node >/dev/null 2>&1 && command -v npm >/dev/null 2>&1; then
180.         success "Node.js $(node --version) + npm $(npm --version) installed"
181.     else
182.         warn "Node.js/npm still not available — npx-based MCP servers won't work"
183.     fi
184. else
185.     success "Node.js $(node --version) + npm $(npm --version) already installed"
186. fi
187. 
188. # --- ollama ---
189. if ! command -v ollama >/dev/null 2>&1; then
190.     log "Ollama not found — installing..."
191.     curl -fsSL https://ollama.com/install.sh | sh && \
192.         success "Ollama installed" || \
193.         warn "Ollama install failed — install manually from https://ollama.com"
194. else
195.     success "Ollama already installed: $(command -v ollama)"
196. fi
197. 
198. # --- python3 + pip ---
199. if ! command -v python3 >/dev/null 2>&1; then
200.     log "python3 not found — installing..."
201.     if [ "$OS_TYPE" = "macOS" ]; then
202.         brew install python3 2>/dev/null || warn "brew install python3 failed"
203.     else
204.         if command -v apt-get >/dev/null 2>&1; then
205.             sudo apt-get update -qq && sudo apt-get install -y -qq python3 python3-pip python3-venv
206.         elif command -v dnf >/dev/null 2>&1; then
207.             sudo dnf install -y python3 python3-pip
208.         elif command -v pacman >/dev/null 2>&1; then
209.             sudo pacman -S --noconfirm python python-pip
210.         fi
211.     fi
212. fi
213. if command -v python3 >/dev/null 2>&1; then
214.     success "python3: $(command -v python3)"
215. else
216.     error "python3 is required but could not be installed automatically"
217. fi
218. 
219. # =============================================================================
220. # Step 0: Preflight checks
```

</details>

/Users/chazh/Documents/GitHub/ai-skillweave/configs/zshrc-skills-block.sh (lines 1-120)

<details>
<summary>55 lines</summary>

```
1. 
2. # =============================================================================
3. # Skills Layer — Everything Claude Code
4. # =============================================================================
5. # All harness commands get the ECC skills injected automatically.
6. # Injected by: ~/scripts/agent_harness_modifications/install.sh
7. # To remove:   ~/scripts/agent_harness_modifications/install.sh --uninstall
8. # =============================================================================
9. 
10. # Claude Code: inject skills via --append-system-prompt-file
11. _claude_with_skills() {
12.     local _skills_file="/tmp/claude-skills-$$.txt"
13.     cat ~/.claude/skills-cache/combined-skills.txt > "$_skills_file" 2>/dev/null
14.     if [ -s "$_skills_file" ]; then
15.         (unset SKILLS_CONTENT CODEX_SYSTEM_PROMPT OPENCLAW_SYSTEM_PROMPT; command claude --append-system-prompt-file "$_skills_file" "$@")
16.     else
17.         (unset SKILLS_CONTENT CODEX_SYSTEM_PROMPT OPENCLAW_SYSTEM_PROMPT; command claude "$@")
18.     fi
19.     rm -f "$_skills_file"
20. }
21. 
22. # OpenClaw: loads SKILL.md files natively from ~/.openclaw/workspace/skills/
23. _openclaw_with_skills() {
24.     (unset SKILLS_CONTENT CODEX_SYSTEM_PROMPT OPENCLAW_SYSTEM_PROMPT; command openclaw "$@")
25. }
26. 
27. # Codex: loads skills natively from ~/.codex/skills/ (ECC skills symlinked there)
28. _codex_with_skills() {
29.     (unset SKILLS_CONTENT OPENCLAW_SYSTEM_PROMPT; command codex "$@")
30. }
31. 
32. # Ollama: pass-through with env cleanup (openclaw/codex/pi load skills natively)
33. _ollama_with_skills() {
34.     (unset SKILLS_CONTENT CODEX_SYSTEM_PROMPT OPENCLAW_SYSTEM_PROMPT OLLAMA_SYSTEM_FILE; command ollama "$@")
35. }
36. 
37. # Pi: loads skills natively from ~/.pi/agent/skills/
38. _pi_with_skills() {
39.     (unset SKILLS_CONTENT CODEX_SYSTEM_PROMPT OPENCLAW_SYSTEM_PROMPT; command pi "$@")
40. }
41. 
42. # Cross-harness skill learner
43. alias learn-sync='~/scripts/agent_harness_modifications/sync-learned-skills.sh'
44. alias learn-sync-dry='~/scripts/agent_harness_modifications/sync-learned-skills.sh --dry-run --verbose'
45. alias learn-stats='~/scripts/agent_harness_modifications/sync-learned-skills.sh --stats'
46. alias learn-prune='~/scripts/agent_harness_modifications/sync-learned-skills.sh --prune'
47. 
48. # Wrapper aliases
49. alias claude='_claude_with_skills'
50. alias openclaw='_openclaw_with_skills'
51. alias codex='_codex_with_skills'
52. alias ollama='_ollama_with_skills'
53. alias pi='_pi_with_skills'
54. # End Skills Layer
55.
```

</details>

/Users/chazh/Documents/GitHub/ai-skillweave/configs/global-claude-md.md (lines 1-220)

<details>
<summary>45 lines</summary>

```
1. # Global Instructions
2. 
3. ## Output Style
4. - Be concise. No summaries of what you just did — the user can see the diff.
5. - No trailing recaps, status tables, or "here's what happened" blocks unless asked.
6. - When editing files, show the change, not a paragraph explaining the change.
7. - Prefer 1-2 sentences over bullet lists. Skip pleasantries.
8. 
9. ## MCP Tool Usage — USE THESE PROACTIVELY
10. 
11. You have MCP servers available. Use them INSTEAD of raw built-in tools when applicable:
12. 
13. ### codesight (USE FIRST when exploring any repo)
14. - BEFORE using Grep/Glob/Read to explore a codebase, call `codesight_get_summary` or `codesight_scan` to get a compact map of routes, schema, components, and dependencies.
15. - Use `codesight_get_routes` instead of grepping for API endpoints.
16. - Use `codesight_get_schema` instead of reading model files one by one.
17. - Use `codesight_get_hot_files` to find high-impact files before exploring.
18. - This saves tokens: one codesight call (~500 tokens) replaces 10+ file reads (~5000+ tokens).
19. 
20. ### token-optimizer (USE for large contexts)
21. - When context is growing large (long conversations, many file reads), use `optimize_session` or `compress_text` to reduce token usage.
22. - Use `smart_read` instead of raw Read for large files — it returns only the relevant parts.
23. - Use `smart_grep` instead of raw Grep — it deduplicates and compresses results.
24. - Use `smart_diff` instead of raw git diff — it summarizes changes compactly.
25. 
26. ### context7 (USE for library/framework questions)
27. - When the user asks about ANY library, framework, or API, call `resolve-library-id` then `query-docs` BEFORE answering from training data.
28. - Your training data may be outdated. Context7 has current docs.
29. 
30. ### exa-web-search (USE for current information)
31. - When the user asks about anything that may have changed since your training cutoff, search first.
32. 
33. ### memory (USE for cross-session continuity)
34. - Save important context that should persist across conversations.
35. 
36. ### sequential-thinking (USE for complex reasoning)
37. - For multi-step problems, architecture decisions, or debugging — use this to structure your thinking.
38. 
39. ## Token Discipline
40. - Do NOT read entire files when you only need a few lines. Use line ranges.
41. - Do NOT re-read files you just edited — the harness tracks file state.
42. - Do NOT run exploratory commands (find, ls -R, cat) when codesight or smart_read can answer faster.
43. - Prefer targeted Grep over broad file scanning.
44. - When multiple independent searches are needed, run them in parallel (single message, multiple tool calls).
45.
```

</details>

/Users/chazh/Documents/GitHub/ai-skillweave/hooks/codesight-redirect.sh (lines 1-220)

<details>
<summary>85 lines</summary>

```
1. #!/bin/bash
2. # =============================================================================
3. # codesight-redirect.sh — PreToolUse hook
4. # =============================================================================
5. # Intercepts broad Glob/Grep codebase searches when codesight is configured.
6. # Reminds Claude to call codesight_get_summary first (once per session/project).
7. #
8. # Exit 2 = block tool + send message to Claude
9. # Exit 0 = allow tool to proceed
10. # =============================================================================
11. 
12. input=$(cat)
13. 
14. # Extract tool name, pattern, and path from hook payload
15. tool=$(echo "$input" | python3 -c "
16. import json, sys
17. d = json.load(sys.stdin)
18. print(d.get('tool_name', ''))
19. " 2>/dev/null)
20. 
21. session_id=$(echo "$input" | python3 -c "
22. import json, sys
23. d = json.load(sys.stdin)
24. print(d.get('session_id', 'default')[:24])
25. " 2>/dev/null || echo "default")
26. 
27. pattern=$(echo "$input" | python3 -c "
28. import json, sys
29. inp = json.load(sys.stdin).get('tool_input', {})
30. print(inp.get('pattern', '') or inp.get('glob', ''))
31. " 2>/dev/null)
32. 
33. path=$(echo "$input" | python3 -c "
34. import json, sys
35. inp = json.load(sys.stdin).get('tool_input', {})
36. print(inp.get('path', '') or inp.get('file_path', ''))
37. " 2>/dev/null)
38. 
39. # Only intercept broad searches (** glob patterns indicating codebase exploration)
40. is_broad=false
41. [[ "$pattern" == *"**"* ]] && is_broad=true
42. [[ -z "$path" && "$tool" == "Glob" ]] && is_broad=true
43. $is_broad || exit 0
44. 
45. # Walk up from search path to find nearest .codesight/ directory
46. check_dir="${path:-$(pwd)}"
47. [[ -f "$check_dir" ]] && check_dir=$(dirname "$check_dir")
48. codesight_dir=""
49. depth=0
50. while [[ "$check_dir" != "/" && $depth -lt 10 ]]; do
51.     if [[ -d "$check_dir/.codesight" ]]; then
52.         codesight_dir="$check_dir"
53.         break
54.     fi
55.     check_dir=$(dirname "$check_dir")
56.     ((depth++))
57. done
58. 
59. # No codesight configured — allow through
60. [[ -n "$codesight_dir" ]] || exit 0
61. 
62. # Only remind once per session per project (state tracked in /tmp)
63. state_dir="/tmp/claude-hooks-${session_id}"
64. mkdir -p "$state_dir"
65. # Hash the project dir for a short key
66. state_key=$(echo "$codesight_dir" | md5 2>/dev/null || echo "$codesight_dir" | cksum | cut -d' ' -f1)
67. state_key="${state_key:0:8}"
68. reminded_file="$state_dir/codesight-${state_key}.done"
69. 
70. # Already reminded this session — allow through
71. [[ -f "$reminded_file" ]] && exit 0
72. 
73. touch "$reminded_file"
74. 
75. cat << EOF
76. CODESIGHT REDIRECT: $codesight_dir has codesight configured.
77. 
78. Before running broad $tool searches, call codesight_get_summary first:
79.   mcp__codesight__codesight_get_summary(directory="$codesight_dir")
80. 
81. This returns a compact project map (~500 tokens) — routes, schema, hot files, env vars.
82. After calling codesight_get_summary, run targeted searches as needed (this reminder won't repeat).
83. EOF
84. exit 2
85.
```

</details>

### ✅ `view`

**view the file at /Users/chazh/Documents/GitHub/ai-skillweave/README.md.**

/Users/chazh/Documents/GitHub/ai-skillweave/README.md (lines 1-260)

<details>
<summary>260 lines</summary>

````
1. # ai-skillweave
2. 
3. > Auto-learning agent harness: captures corrections live, mines session history for patterns, syncs ~450 skills across Claude/Codex/OpenClaw/Pi with MCP pre-configured.
4. 
5. One-command setup for all your Ollama agent harnesses: proper MCP servers, web tools, harness-specific configs — all portable and reproducible. Learns from your corrections automatically via real-time hooks and batch session analysis.
6. 
7. **~450 skills** on-disk across 5 sources — see [`docs/SKILLS-CATALOG.md`](docs/SKILLS-CATALOG.md) for a full categorized listing.
8. 
9. ---
10. 
11. ## Skill Sources
12. 
13. > **Skills in this repo are drawn from multiple open-source skill libraries**, each following the [Agent Skills](https://agentskills.io/) standard:
14. 
15. | Source | Skills | Notes |
16. |--------|--------|-------|
17. | [Everything Claude Code (ECC)](https://github.com/affaan-m/everything-claude-code) | 184 SKILL.md dirs | Community-maintained — testing, architecture, security, cloud, language patterns |
18. | [K-Dense scientific](https://github.com/K-Dense-AI/scientific-agent-skills) | 134 SKILL.md dirs | Bioinformatics, cheminformatics, drug discovery, clinical research, ML/AI |
19. | [ClawBio bioinformatics](https://github.com/ClawBio/ClawBio) | 56 SKILL.md dirs | Bioinformatics-native pipeline skills with executable Python scripts |
20. | [SkillGraph bioinformatics](https://github.com/variomeanalytics/bioinformatics-agent-skills) | MCP-served | Via MCP server with knowledge graph |
21. | Anthropic official | 17 SKILL.md dirs | Anthropic's official skills library |
22. | OpenAI Codex curated | 44 SKILL.md dirs | OpenAI's Codex skills collection |
23. | Personal learned | varies | BMO-style real-time capture (corrections detected live via hooks) + batch 4-stage pipeline |
24. 
25. `ai-skillweave`'s core contribution is **cross-harness delivery**: each skill library was originally designed for a single harness. This repo extends them so the same skills load natively into every `ollama launch` agent — OpenClaw, Pi, Codex, and Claude Code — each in the format that harness expects.
26. 
27. All sources are credited in the Skill Sources table above.
28. 
29. ---
30. 
31. ## Scientific Agent Skills — K-Dense
32. 
33. > **134 scientific and research skills from [K-Dense-AI/scientific-agent-skills](https://github.com/K-Dense-AI/scientific-agent-skills)** — covering bioinformatics, cheminformatics, drug discovery, clinical research, proteomics, medical imaging, ML/AI, materials science, physics, and 100+ scientific databases.
34. 
35. These skills follow the open [Agent Skills](https://agentskills.io/) standard and work with Claude Code, Cursor, Codex, and Gemini CLI. `ai-skillweave` extends them to all supported harnesses, just like ECC skills.
36. 
37. To install:
38. 
39. ```bash
40. ./safe-install.sh --with-science                    # ECC + K-Dense scientific skills
41. ```
42. 
43. 
44. 
45. ---
46. 
47. ## Bioinformatics Agent Skills — Variome Analytics
48. 
49. > **Bioinformatics pipeline skills served via MCP from [variomeanalytics/bioinformatics-agent-skills](https://github.com/variomeanalytics/bioinformatics-agent-skills)** — covering variant analysis, drug discovery, single-cell RNA-seq, genome-wide association studies, and 15+ database query skills (ClinVar, gnomAD, COSMIC, Ensembl, UniProt, and more).
50. 
51. Unlike ECC and K-Dense which ship on-disk `SKILL.md` files, these skills are served dynamically via an **MCP server** (`skillgraph`) that provides:
52. 
53. - `get_skill` — Full skill documentation for any skill in the graph
54. - `list_skills` — List all skills, optionally filtered by domain
55. - `search_skills` — Keyword search across skill IDs, triggers, and tool names
56. - `get_transitions` — Upstream/downstream skill edges with paper counts and data types
57. - `find_path` — Shortest pipeline path between two skills
58. - `get_graph_stats` — Graph statistics (skill count, edges, domain breakdown)
59. 
60. This knowledge graph approach means you can ask "what pipeline takes me from FASTQ to DEGs?" and get a real answer with evidence — something on-disk skill files alone can't provide.
61. 
62. **Installation:**
63. 
64. ```bash
65. # Add the SkillGraph MCP server (works out of the box after install.sh)
66. # It's already included in configs/claude-mcp-servers.json
67. ./scripts/setup-mcp.sh --force   # re-apply MCP config to add skillgraph
68. ```
69. 
70. ---
71. 
72. ## Bioinformatics Pipeline Skills — ClawBio
73. 
74. > **56 bioinformatics pipeline skills from [ClawBio/ClawBio](https://github.com/ClawBio/ClawBio)** — covering RNA-seq differential expression, VCF annotation, clinical variant reporting, single-cell orchestration, GWAS/PRS, HLA typing, methylation clocks, drug-target validation, and more.
75. 
76. Unlike ECC and K-Dense skills which are prompt-only `SKILL.md` files, ClawBio skills ship **executable Python scripts** alongside their skill definitions (172 `.py` files across 56 skills, plus 43 test directories). Each `SKILL.md` includes an `openclaw` metadata block with `uv` package requirements, so compatible harnesses can auto-install dependencies.
77. 
78. **Skill categories:**
79. 
80. | Category | Skills | Examples |
81. |----------|--------|---------|
82. | Genomics & Variants | 7 | `variant-annotation`, `vcf-annotator`, `fine-mapping`, `hla-typing`, `archaic-introgression` |
83. | Clinical & Pharma | 8 | `clinical-variant-reporter`, `clinpgx`, `pharmgx-reporter`, `nutrigx_advisor`, `drug-photo` |
84. | Transcriptomics | 6 | `rnaseq-de`, `scrna-orchestrator`, `scrna-embedding`, `de-summary`, `proteomics-de` |
85. | GWAS & Population | 6 | `gwas-lookup`, `gwas-prs`, `claw-ancestry-pca`, `mendelian-randomisation` |
86. | Data Integration | 6 | `bio-orchestrator`, `bioconductor-bridge`, `galaxy-bridge`, `bigquery-public` |
87. | Literature & Protocols | 5 | `pubmed-summariser`, `lit-synthesizer`, `bgpt-mcp`, `protocols-io` |
88. | Epigenomics | 1 | `methylation-clock` |
89. | Other | 17 | `seq-wrangler`, `equity-scorer`, `struct-predictor`, `ukb-navigator`, ... |
90. 
91. **Installation:**
92. 
93. ```bash
94. ./safe-install.sh --with-bio                         # ECC + ClawBio bioinformatics (56 skills)
95. ./safe-install.sh --with-science --with-bio          # ECC + K-Dense + ClawBio
96. ./safe-install.sh --with-science --with-bio --with-anthropic --with-codex  # Full: all sources
97. ```
98. 
99. ClawBio is installed by default (`--with-bio` is on). To update, re-run with `--with-bio` to re-clone the latest from GitHub.
100. 
101. 
102. 
103. ---
104. 
105. ## Quick Start (New Machine)
106. 
107. ```bash
108. # 1. Clone this repo
109. git clone https://github.com/chazhyseni/ai-skillweave
110. cd ai-skillweave
111. 
112. # 2. Install everything (auto-detects macOS/Linux/WSL)
113. ./install.sh
114. 
115. # 3. Reload shell (install.sh tells you which file)
116. source ~/.bashrc   # Linux/WSL
117. source ~/.zshrc    # macOS
118. 
119. # 4. Launch any harness
120. ollama launch claude      # Claude Code + MCP tools
121. ollama launch openclaw    # OpenClaw + subagents + web
122. ollama launch pi          # Pi + subagents
123. ollama launch codex       # Codex via Ollama backend
124. ollama launch copilot     # Copilot CLI + MCP servers
125. ```
126. 
127. ---
128. 
129. ## What This Repo Does
130. 
131. | Component | What it configures |
132. |-----------|-------------------|
133. | **Claude Code MCP** | Adds 8 auto-configured servers to `~/.claude.json`: memory, sequential-thinking, context7, playwright, google-docs-editor, token-optimizer, codesight, skillgraph |
134. | **OpenClaw** | Enables web tools + Ollama plugin in `~/.openclaw/openclaw.json` |
135. | **Pi** | Sets Ollama as provider + installs `pi-subagents` package |
136. | **Codex** | Configures `ollama-launch` provider + `danger-full-access` sandbox in `~/.codex/config.toml` |
137. | **Copilot CLI** | Configures MCP servers in `~/.copilot/mcp-config.json` (run `scripts/setup-copilot.sh` to apply) |
138. | **Ollama integrations** | Sets per-harness model mapping in `~/.ollama/config.json` (qwen3.6 default for all harnesses) |
139. | **Shell wrappers** | Adds `_*_with_skills` functions + aliases in `~/.bashrc` and/or `~/.zshrc` |
140. | **Claude Code skills** | Copies ECC SKILL.md files to `~/.claude/skills/` — visible via `/skills`, works with any launch method |
141. | **ECC Skills cache** | Combined skills cache at `~/.claude/skills-cache/combined-skills.txt` for system prompt injection |
142. 
143. ---
144. 
145. ## Repository Structure
146. 
147. ```
148. ai-skillweave/
149. ├── install.sh                    ← Master installer (run this)
150. ├── safe-install.sh               ← ECC skills installer
151. ├── extract-conversation-skills.py ← 4-stage learning pipeline (Ingestion→Learning→Consolidation→Output)
152. ├── sync-learned-skills.sh        ← Sync learned skills + run pipeline (--stats, --prune, --sync-only)
153. │
154. ├── hooks/                        ← Claude Code hooks (auto-installed by install.sh)
155. │   ├── codesight-redirect.sh     ← PreToolUse: redirect broad searches to codesight
156. │   ├── learning-capture.sh       ← UserPromptSubmit: BMO-style real-time correction capture
157. │   └── session-reflection.sh     ← Session end: consolidate captured events into skills
158. │
159. ├── configs/                      ← Portable config templates
160. │   ├── claude-mcp-servers.json   ← MCP servers for Claude Code CLI
161. │   ├── claude-desktop-mcp-servers.json  ← MCP servers for Claude Desktop GUI
162. │   ├── global-claude-md.md       ← Global CLAUDE.md template (MCP rules + conciseness)
163. │   ├── openclaw.json             ← OpenClaw config (web tools enabled)
164. │   ├── codex-config.toml         ← Codex ollama-launch provider config
165. │   ├── pi-settings.json          ← Pi agent settings
166. │   ├── ollama-integrations.json  ← Ollama integration→model mapping
167. │   └── zshrc-skills-block.sh     ← Shell skills layer block
168. │
169. ├── scripts/                      ← Individual setup scripts
170. │   ├── setup-mcp.sh              ← Inject MCP into ~/.claude.json (CLI)
171. │   ├── setup-claude-md.sh        ← Install global CLAUDE.md (MCP rules + conciseness)
172. │   ├── setup-hooks.sh            ← Install PreToolUse hook (codesight-redirect)
173. │   ├── setup-learning-hook.sh    ← Install UserPromptSubmit hook (BMO learning capture)
174. │   ├── consolidate-learnings.py  ← Consolidate captured events into SKILL.md files
175. │   ├── setup-claude-desktop.sh   ← Standalone: MCP + skills for Claude Desktop GUI
176. │   ├── build-desktop-skills.sh   ← Package .skill files for Desktop upload
177. │   ├── setup-openclaw.sh         ← Apply OpenClaw config
178. │   ├── setup-codex.sh            ← Apply Codex config
179. │   ├── setup-pi.sh               ← Apply Pi settings
180. │   ├── setup-ollama-config.sh    ← Apply Ollama integration→model mapping
181. │   ├── update-ecc.sh             ← Pull latest ECC + rebuild cache + learn-sync + re-sync harnesses
182. │   ├── disable-zscaler.sh        ← Disable Zscaler proxy
183. │   └── verify.sh                 ← Health check all components
184. │
185. ├── docs/
186. │   ├── AUDIT.md                  ← MCP/subagent audit (what was fixed + why)
187. │   └── TROUBLESHOOTING.md        ← Common issues and fixes
188. │
189. └── shared-learnings/
190.     └── learnings.md              ← Cross-harness learned patterns log
191. ```
192. 
193. ---
194. 
195. ## Platform Support
196. 
197. | Platform | Status | Shell | Notes |
198. |----------|--------|-------|-------|
199. | **macOS** | ✅ Tested | zsh (default) | Homebrew for dependencies |
200. | **Linux** | ✅ Tested | bash (default) | apt/dnf/pacman auto-detected |
201. | **Windows (WSL)** | ✅ Supported | bash | Run inside WSL — native Windows is not supported |
202. 
203. `install.sh` auto-detects the platform and user shell, installs skills into the correct RC file (`~/.bashrc` or `~/.zshrc`), and shows platform-appropriate messages.
204. 
205. ---
206. 
207. ## Before You Begin — First-Time Setup
208. 
209. Run these steps **once** on a new machine before cloning this repo.
210. 
211. ### 1. Install Ollama
212. 
213. ```bash
214. # macOS:
215. brew install ollama
216. # or download from https://ollama.com
217. 
218. # Linux (Debian/Ubuntu):
219. curl -fsSL https://ollama.com/install.sh | sh
220. 
221. # Start the server:
222. ollama serve          # Linux (or use systemd)
223. # macOS: open /Applications/Ollama.app (runs as menubar app)
224. ```
225. 
226. ### 2. Pull a model (or use cloud models)
227. 
228. ```bash
229. # Cloud models — no download needed, stream from Ollama's servers at inference time:
230. # Just use them directly: ollama run qwen3.5:cloud
231. 
232. # Local model — runs on your machine (~23GB download):
233. ollama pull qwen3.6                    # recommended local model
234. ollama pull gemma4:e4b                 # lightweight, good for subagents
235. ```
236. 
237. ### 3. Install Node.js (for MCP servers)
238. 
239. ```bash
240. # macOS:
241. brew install node
242. 
243. # Linux (Debian/Ubuntu):
244. curl -fsSL https://deb.nodesource.com/setup_lts.x | sudo -E bash -
245. sudo apt-get install -y nodejs
246. 
247. # Or use nvm (any platform):
248. curl -o- https://raw.githubusercontent.com/nvm-sh/nvm/v0.40.0/install.sh | bash
249. nvm install --lts
250. 
251. # Verify: node --version  (needs v18+)
252. ```
253. 
254. ### 4. Install Claude Code
255. 
256. ```bash
257. npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code
258. 
259. # Authenticate (requires Claude Pro/Max/Team account):
260. claude auth login
````

</details>

200. 
201. # Regex markers per memory type
202. # Frustration/escalation signals — strong evidence this is a real correction
203. FRUSTRATION_MARKERS = [
204.     r"\b(?:again|still|keep(?:ing)?|once more|repeatedly)\s+(?:telling|saying|asking|explaining)\b",
205.     r"\b(?:I (?:keep|already|just)\s+(?:told|said|asked|explained|mentioned))\b",
206.     r"\b(?:for the (?:last|nth)\s+time)\b",
207.     r"\b(?:I've (?:said|told|explained)\s+this\s+(?:before|already|multiple times))\b",
208.     r"\b(?:this is (?:the \d+|third|fourth|fifth|nth)\s+time)\b",
209.     r"\b(?:still (?:wrong|incorrect|not right|broken|failing))\b",
210.     r"\b(?:WRONG|INCORRECT|NO!|STOP)\b",
211.     r"\b(?:didn't I (?:just|already)\s+(?:say|tell|explain))\b",
212. ]
213. 
214. CLASSIFICATION_MARKERS = {
215.     "anti_pattern": [
216.         r"(?:don't|do not|never)\s+(?:do|say|write|generate|use|include|fabricat|hallucinat|invent|assume|claim|state|present)\s+",
217.         r"(?:stop|avoid)\s+(?:fabricat|hallucinat|inventing|making|generating|assuming|claiming|presenting)\s+",
218.         r"(?:that's\s+)?(?:wrong|incorrect|not\s+what)\s+(?:I\s+)?(?:asked|meant|wanted|said)\s+",
219.         r"\b(?:fabricat|hallucinat|made\s+up)\w+",
220.         r"(?:no,?\s+i\s+meant|actually,?\s+I\s+meant)\s+",
221.         r"(?:unreliable|unverified|unsourced|bogus|made-up)\s+(?:claim|info|data|source|result)\s*",
222.     ],
223.     "heuristic": [
224.         r"(?:always|usually|typically)\s+(?:do|use|include|check|verify|cite|reference|cross-?check)\s+",
225.         r"(?:the\s+(?:best|right|correct|proper)\s+way)\s+(?:is|to)\s+",
226.         r"(?:i\s+)?(?:found\s+that|learned\s+that|discovered\s+that)\s+",
227.         r"(?:works\s+(?:best|better|well))\s+",
228.         r"(?:prefer|recommend|suggest)\s+\w+\s+(?:over|instead\s+of|vs)\s+",
229.         r"(?:make\s+sure|ensure|always\s+verify)\s+",
230.         r"(?:you\s+(?:should|must|need\s+to)\s+(?:always|always\s+)?(?:cite|verify|check|cross-reference))\s+",
231.     ],
232.     "preference": [
233.         r"(?:i\s+(?:prefer|like|want|need))\s+",
234.         r"(?:my\s+(?:style|preference|convention))\s+(?:is|uses)\s+",
235.         r"(?:use\s+tabs|use\s+spaces|2-space|4-space)",
236.         r"(?:please\s+(?:always|never))\s+",
237.     ],
238.     "domain_knowledge": [
239.         r"(?:the\s+(?:api|endpoint|url|server|database)\s+(?:is|at|on))\s+",
240.         r"(?:the\s+(?:config|setting|variable|flag)\s+(?:is|should\s+be))\s+",
241.         r"(?:version\s+\d+\.\d+)",
242.         r"(?:the\s+password\s+(?:is|was))\s+",
243.     ],
244. }
245. 
246. # Patterns to SKIP — not corrections, just noise
247. SKIP_PATTERNS = [
248.     re.compile(r"^\s*(?:what|where|when|who|why|how|which|is|are|can|does|did)\b.*\?\s*$", re.IGNORECASE),  # pure questions
249.     re.compile(r"(?:Do\s+NOT|NEVER)\s+(?:use|run|call|execute)\s+(?:Read|Bash|Grep|Glob|Edit|Write|any\s+other\s+tool)", re.IGNORECASE),  # system tool restrictions
250.     re.compile(r"^\s*(?:yes|no|ok|okay|done|sure|right|correct|good)\s*[.!?]?\s*$", re.IGNORECASE),  # trivial responses
251.     re.compile(r"^\s*[\*\-\#]+\s*"),  # markdown bullets/headers leaked from compacted context
252.     re.compile(r"\(From compacted session\)", re.IGNORECASE),  # compacted session artifacts
253.     # System prompt instructions that leak into user messages (Claude Code compaction artifacts)
254.     re.compile(r"^Before providing your final summary", re.IGNORECASE),
255.     re.compile(r"^IMPORTANT:\s*ensure that this step", re.IGNORECASE),
256.     re.compile(r"^This should be verbatim", re.IGNORECASE),
257.     re.compile(r"^wrap your analysis in\s+<", re.IGNORECASE),
258.     re.compile(r"ensure you'?ve?\s+covered all necessary points", re.IGNORECASE),
259.     re.compile(r"DIRECTLY in line with the user'?s most recent", re.IGNORECASE),
260.     re.compile(r"no drift in task interpretation", re.IGNORECASE),
261.     # Generic agent instructions not from user
262.     re.compile(r"^(?:First|Then|Next|Finally),?\s+(?:think|analyze|review|check|verify)", re.IGNORECASE),
263.     re.compile(r"^<analysis>", re.IGNORECASE),
264.     re.compile(r"^Step \d+:", re.IGNORECASE),
265. ]
266. 
267. # Quality gate: conditions that indicate we failed to abstract properly
268. GENERIC_CONDITIONS = {
269.     "when the relevant context arises",
270.     "when appropriate",
```

### ✅ `view`

**view the file at /Users/chazh/Documents/GitHub/ai-skillweave/install.sh.**

/Users/chazh/Documents/GitHub/ai-skillweave/install.sh (lines 220-520)

<details>
<summary>254 lines</summary>

```
220. # Step 0: Preflight checks
221. # =============================================================================
222. section "Preflight"
223. 
224. command -v python3 >/dev/null 2>&1 || error "python3 required ($PKG_HINT python3)"
225. 
226. # Install Python dependencies for auto-learning pipeline
227. log "Installing Python dependencies for skill extraction (sentence-transformers, scikit-learn)..."
228. _install_python_deps() {
229.     local pkgs="sentence-transformers>=3.0.0 scikit-learn>=1.5.0"
230.     # Detect virtualenv: --user is forbidden inside venv/conda
231.     local in_venv=""
232.     if python3 -c "import sys; sys.exit(0 if (sys.prefix != sys.base_prefix) else 1)" 2>/dev/null; then
233.         in_venv=1
234.     fi
235.     if [ -n "$in_venv" ]; then
236.         log "Detected Python virtualenv — skipping --user flag"
237.         if python3 -m pip install --quiet $pkgs 2>&1; then
238.             success "Python deps installed (venv)"
239.             return 0
240.         fi
241.     else
242.         # Try multiple pip invocation strategies; show errors so user can diagnose
243.         if python3 -m pip install --user --quiet $pkgs 2>&1; then
244.             success "Python deps installed (user site)"
245.             return 0
246.         fi
247.         warn "pip --user failed — trying without --user..."
248.         if python3 -m pip install --quiet $pkgs 2>&1; then
249.             success "Python deps installed (system site)"
250.             return 0
251.         fi
252.     fi
253.     if command -v pip3 >/dev/null 2>&1 && pip3 install --quiet $pkgs 2>&1; then
254.         success "Python deps installed via pip3"
255.         return 0
256.     fi
257.     warn "All pip install attempts failed (see errors above)"
258.     warn "Try manually: python3 -m pip install sentence-transformers scikit-learn"
259.     return 1
260. }
261. _install_python_deps || warn "Python deps missing — the learning pipeline will fail until fixed"
262. 
263. command -v node >/dev/null 2>&1 || warn "node not found — npx-based MCP servers won't work ($PKG_HINT node)"
264. command -v git >/dev/null 2>&1 || warn "git not found — ECC skills install will be skipped"
265. 
266. if command -v ollama >/dev/null 2>&1; then
267.     success "ollama: $(command -v ollama)"
268.     if curl -s --max-time 3 http://localhost:11434/api/status >/dev/null 2>&1; then
269.         success "Ollama server: running"
270. 
271.         # Check if requested model is available
272.         if ollama list 2>/dev/null | grep -q "^${OLLAMA_MODEL}"; then
273.             success "Model available: $OLLAMA_MODEL"
274.         else
275.             warn "Model '$OLLAMA_MODEL' not in local list (may be cloud model — OK)"
276.             warn "If local model needed: ollama pull $OLLAMA_MODEL"
277.         fi
278.     else
279.         if [ "$OS_TYPE" = "macOS" ]; then
280.             warn "Ollama server not running — start it: open /Applications/Ollama.app"
281.         else
282.             warn "Ollama server not running — start it: ollama serve"
283.         fi
284.         warn "Continuing setup; start Ollama before using harnesses"
285.     fi
286. else
287.     warn "ollama not installed — install from https://ollama.com"
288.     warn "Continuing setup without Ollama; harnesses that need it will fail at runtime"
289. fi
290. 
291. # Disable Zscaler tray if running (macOS only, no sudo needed)
292. if [ "$OS_TYPE" = "macOS" ] && launchctl list 2>/dev/null | grep -q "zscaler"; then
293.     log "Detected Zscaler — disabling tray (no sudo needed)..."
294.     bash "$REPO_DIR/scripts/disable-zscaler.sh" --tray 2>/dev/null || true
295. fi
296. 
297. # =============================================================================
298. # Step 0b: Pre-install npx-based MCP servers globally (prevents timeout on first launch)
299. # =============================================================================
300. if command -v npm >/dev/null 2>&1; then
301.     log "Pre-installing MCP server packages globally (prevents first-launch timeouts)..."
302.     # Install silently in background; failures are non-fatal (npx will fallback)
303.     npm install -g @modelcontextprotocol/server-memory \
304.         @modelcontextprotocol/server-sequential-thinking \
305.         @upstash/context7-mcp@latest \
306.         @playwright/mcp \
307.         codesight \
308.         token-optimizer-mcp 2>/dev/null || warn "Some MCP packages failed to pre-install (npx fallback will work but may be slower on first launch)"
309.     success "MCP server packages pre-installed"
310. else
311.     warn "npm not found — MCP servers will be installed on first launch (may timeout)"
312. fi
313. 
314. # =============================================================================
315. # Harness installer helper
316. # =============================================================================
317. install_harness() {
318.     local name="$1"
319.     local cmd="$2"
320.     local install_cmd="$3"
321. 
322.     if command -v "$cmd" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
323.         success "$name already installed: $(command -v "$cmd")"
324.         return 0
325.     fi
326. 
327.     if ! command -v npm >/dev/null 2>&1; then
328.         warn "npm not found — cannot auto-install $name"
329.         warn "Install manually, then re-run install.sh"
330.         return 1
331.     fi
332. 
333.     log "Installing $name..."
334.     if eval "$install_cmd"; then
335.         success "$name installed"
336.         return 0
337.     else
338.         warn "$name installation failed — install manually, then re-run"
339.         return 1
340.     fi
341. }
342. 
343. # =============================================================================
344. # Step 1: Skills Layer (ECC + shell wrappers)
345. # =============================================================================
346. 
347. should_run() {
348.     [ -z "$ONLY_TARGET" ] || [ "$ONLY_TARGET" = "$1" ]
349. }
350. 
351. if ! $SKIP_SKILLS && should_run "skills"; then
352.     section "Skills Layer"
353.     # safe-install.sh may exit non-zero when 'source <rc>' fails in a non-interactive
354.     # subshell — this is expected and harmless. Capture exit code to report real failures.
355.     safe_exit=0
356.     SAFE_ARGS=""
357.     $WITH_SCIENCE && SAFE_ARGS="$SAFE_ARGS --with-science"
358.     $WITH_CURATED && SAFE_ARGS="$SAFE_ARGS --with-curated"
359.     $WITH_BIO && SAFE_ARGS="$SAFE_ARGS --with-bio"
360.     bash "$REPO_DIR/safe-install.sh" $SAFE_ARGS || safe_exit=$?
361.     if [ "$safe_exit" -eq 0 ]; then
362.         success "ECC skills + shell wrappers installed (run: source $SHELL_RC to activate)"
363.     else
364.         warn "safe-install.sh exited with code $safe_exit — shell integration may need manual source $SHELL_RC"
365.     fi
366.     # Rebuild cache with full skill counts (safe-install.sh caps Codex at 100).
367.     # update-ecc.sh also restores ECC working tree to actual HEAD.
368.     log "Rebuilding skills cache with full counts..."
369.     bash "$REPO_DIR/scripts/update-ecc.sh" --force || true
370.     success "Skills cache rebuilt (all sources, no caps)"
371. else
372.     log "Skipping skills layer"
373. fi
374. 
375. # =============================================================================
376. # Step 1b: Ollama integration→model mapping
377. # =============================================================================
378. if should_run "ollama" || [ -z "$ONLY_TARGET" ]; then
379.     section "Ollama Integrations"
380.     bash "$REPO_DIR/scripts/setup-ollama-config.sh" && success "Ollama integrations configured" || warn "Ollama config skipped (Ollama not started yet)"
381. fi
382. 
383. # =============================================================================
384. # Step 2: Claude Code MCP + Global Instructions
385. # =============================================================================
386. if should_run "claude"; then
387.     section "Claude Code"
388.     install_harness "Claude Code" "claude" "npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code"
389.     if [ -f "$HOME/.claude.json" ]; then
390.         bash "$REPO_DIR/scripts/setup-mcp.sh" && success "Claude Code MCP configured" || warn "MCP setup skipped (run Claude Code once, then re-run)"
391.     else
392.         warn "~/.claude.json not found — run Claude Code once to initialize, then re-run install.sh"
393.     fi
394.     # Global CLAUDE.md: proactive MCP usage + conciseness + token discipline
395.     bash "$REPO_DIR/scripts/setup-claude-md.sh" && success "Global CLAUDE.md installed" || warn "CLAUDE.md setup skipped"
396.     # Pre-tool-use hooks: enforce codesight before broad Glob/Grep searches
397.     bash "$REPO_DIR/scripts/setup-hooks.sh" && success "Claude Code hooks installed" || warn "Hooks setup skipped"
398.     
399.     # BMO-style learning capture hooks (real-time correction detection)
400.     bash "$REPO_DIR/scripts/setup-learning-hook.sh" && success "BMO learning capture hooks configured" || warn "Learning hook setup skipped"
401. fi
402. 
403. # =============================================================================
404. # Step 3: OpenClaw
405. # =============================================================================
406. if should_run "openclaw"; then
407.     section "OpenClaw"
408.     bash "$REPO_DIR/scripts/setup-openclaw.sh" --model "$OLLAMA_MODEL" && success "OpenClaw configured" || warn "OpenClaw setup skipped (not installed yet)"
409. fi
410. 
411. # =============================================================================
412. # Step 4: Codex
413. # =============================================================================
414. if should_run "codex"; then
415.     section "Codex"
416.     install_harness "Codex CLI" "codex" "npm install -g @openai/codex"
417.     bash "$REPO_DIR/scripts/setup-codex.sh" --model "$OLLAMA_MODEL" && success "Codex configured" || warn "Codex setup skipped (not installed yet)"
418. fi
419. 
420. # =============================================================================
421. # Step 5: Pi
422. # =============================================================================
423. if should_run "pi"; then
424.     section "Pi"
425.     install_harness "Pi" "pi" "npm install -g @mariozechner/pi-coding-agent"
426.     bash "$REPO_DIR/scripts/setup-pi.sh" --model "$OLLAMA_MODEL" && success "Pi configured" || warn "Pi setup skipped (not installed yet)"
427. fi
428. 
429. # =============================================================================
430. # Step 6: Copilot CLI
431. # =============================================================================
432. if should_run "copilot"; then
433.     section "Copilot CLI"
434.     install_harness "Copilot CLI" "copilot" "npm install -g @github/copilot"
435.     bash "$REPO_DIR/scripts/setup-copilot.sh" && success "Copilot CLI MCP configured" || warn "Copilot setup skipped (not installed yet)"
436. fi
437. 
438. # =============================================================================
439. # =============================================================================
440. # Step 5: Cleanup stale/invalid skills from previous installs
441. # =============================================================================
442. if [ -f "$REPO_DIR/scripts/cleanup-invalid-skills.sh" ]; then
443.     log "Cleaning up invalid skills from previous installs..."
444.     bash "$REPO_DIR/scripts/cleanup-invalid-skills.sh" 2>/dev/null || warn "Skill cleanup had issues (non-fatal)"
445. fi
446. 
447. # Done
448. # =============================================================================
449. echo ""
450. echo "╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗"
451. echo "║   Setup Complete ($OS_TYPE / $USER_SHELL)"
452. echo "╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝"
453. echo ""
454. echo "  Reload shell:   source $SHELL_RC"
455. echo ""
456. echo "  Launch harnesses:"
457. echo "    ollama launch claude      → Claude Code with MCP + skills"
458. echo "    ollama launch openclaw    → OpenClaw with web tools + subagents"
459. echo "    ollama launch pi          → Pi with subagents"
460. echo "    ollama launch codex       → Codex via ollama-launch provider"
461. echo "    ollama launch copilot     → Copilot CLI with MCP servers"
462. echo ""
463. echo "  Health check:   ./install.sh --verify"
464. echo ""
465. if [ "$OS_TYPE" = "macOS" ]; then
466.     echo "  Zscaler (if needed after reboot):"
467.     echo "    scripts/disable-zscaler.sh"
468.     echo "    # Then (in Terminal with sudo):"
469.     echo "    sudo launchctl unload /Library/LaunchDaemons/com.zscaler.service.plist"
470.     echo "    sudo launchctl unload /Library/LaunchDaemons/com.zscaler.tunnel.plist"
471.     echo ""
472. fi
473.
```
