275.     fi
276. 
277.     # Summary
278.     TOTAL_CURATED=$(find "$CURATED_DIR" -name '*.md' 2>/dev/null | wc -l | tr -d ' ')
279.     success "Total curated skills: $TOTAL_CURATED"
280. }
281. 
282. # =============================================================================
283. # Step 2b: Install K-Dense Scientific Agent Skills
284. # =============================================================================
285. 
286. install_science_skills() {
287.     if [ "$WITH_SCIENCE" = false ]; then
288.         log "Skipping scientific skills (use --with-science to add)"
289.         return 0
290.     fi
291. 
292.     log "Step 2b: Installing K-Dense Scientific Agent Skills..."
293. 
294.     if [ -d "$SCIENCE_DIR" ]; then
295.         warn "Existing scientific skills found: $SCIENCE_DIR"
296.         if [ -t 0 ]; then
297.             read -p "Overwrite? [y/N] " -n 1 -r
298.             echo ""
299.             if [[ ! $REPLY =~ ^[Yy]$ ]]; then
300.                 success "Keeping existing scientific skills"
301.                 return 0
302.             else
303.                 rm -rf "$SCIENCE_DIR"
304.             fi
305.         else
306.             success "Non-interactive mode — keeping existing scientific skills"
307.             return 0
308.         fi
309.     fi
310. 
311.     mkdir -p "$SCIENCE_DIR"
312.     cd /tmp
313.     if git clone --depth 1 https://github.com/K-Dense-AI/scientific-agent-skills.git science-temp 2>/dev/null; then
314.         # Copy skill directories (each contains SKILL.md + optional references/scripts/assets)
315.         mkdir -p "$SCIENCE_DIR/scientific-skills"
316.         cp -r science-temp/scientific-skills/* "$SCIENCE_DIR/scientific-skills/" 2>/dev/null || true
317.         SCIENCE_COUNT=$(find "$SCIENCE_DIR/scientific-skills" -name 'SKILL.md' 2>/dev/null | wc -l | tr -d ' ')
318.         rm -rf science-temp
319.         success "K-Dense scientific skills installed: $SCIENCE_DIR ($SCIENCE_COUNT skills)"
320.     else
321.         error "Failed to fetch K-Dense scientific-agent-skills repository"
322.         return 1
323.     fi
324. }
325. 
326. # =============================================================================
327. # Step 2c: Install ClawBio Bioinformatics Skills
328. # =============================================================================
329. 
330. install_bio_skills() {
331.     if [ "$WITH_BIO" = false ]; then
332.         log "Skipping ClawBio bioinformatics skills (use --with-bio to add)"
333.         return 0
334.     fi
335. 
336.     log "Step 2c: Installing ClawBio Bioinformatics Skills..."
337. 
338.     if [ -d "$CLAWBIO_DIR" ]; then
339.         warn "Existing ClawBio skills found: $CLAWBIO_DIR"
340.         if [ -t 0 ]; then
341.             read -p "Overwrite? [y/N] " -n 1 -r
342.             echo ""
343.             if [[ ! $REPLY =~ ^[Yy]$ ]]; then
344.                 success "Keeping existing ClawBio skills"
345.                 return 0
346.             else
347.                 rm -rf "$CLAWBIO_DIR"
348.             fi
349.         else
350.             success "Non-interactive mode — keeping existing ClawBio skills"
351.             return 0
352.         fi
353.     fi
354. 
355.     mkdir -p "$CLAWBIO_DIR"
356.     cd /tmp
357.     if git clone --depth 1 https://github.com/ClawBio/ClawBio.git clawbio-temp 2>/dev/null; then
358.         # Copy skill directories (each contains SKILL.md + optional Python scripts/examples/tests)
359.         mkdir -p "$CLAWBIO_DIR/skills"
360.         cp -r clawbio-temp/skills/* "$CLAWBIO_DIR/skills/" 2>/dev/null || true
361.         BIO_COUNT=$(find "$CLAWBIO_DIR/skills" -name 'SKILL.md' 2>/dev/null | wc -l | tr -d ' ')
362.         rm -rf clawbio-temp
363.         success "ClawBio bioinformatics skills installed: $CLAWBIO_DIR ($BIO_COUNT skills)"
364.     else
365.         error "Failed to fetch ClawBio repository"
366.         return 1
367.     fi
368. }
369. 
370. # =============================================================================
371. # Step 3: Create combined skills loader (single file with ALL skills)
372. # =============================================================================
373. 
374. create_loader() {
375.     log "Step 3: Creating skills loader (combining all skills)..."
376. 
377.     mkdir -p "$SKILLS_CACHE_DIR"
378. 
379.     # First, combine all skills into a single cached file
380.     COMBINED_FILE="$SKILLS_CACHE_DIR/combined-skills.txt"
381.     > "$COMBINED_FILE"  # Truncate/create file
382. 
383.     # Preamble: conciseness + MCP usage rules (injected at top of system prompt)
384.     cat >> "$COMBINED_FILE" << 'PREAMBLE'
385. # CRITICAL INSTRUCTIONS — READ FIRST
386. 
387. ## Conciseness
388. - Be terse. No trailing summaries, status tables, or "here's what I did" recaps.
389. - Show the change, not paragraphs explaining the change.
390. 
391. ## Use MCP tools PROACTIVELY
392. - Use codesight_get_summary BEFORE exploring a codebase with Grep/Glob/Read.
393. - Use smart_read (token-optimizer) instead of Read for large files.
394. - Use context7 query-docs BEFORE answering library/framework questions from training data.
395. - Use exa-web-search for anything that may have changed since training cutoff.
396. 
397. PREAMBLE
398. 
399.     # Add learned skills first (your personal skills - always included)
400.     if [ -d "$CLAUDE_DIR/skills/learned" ]; then
401.         for skill in "$CLAUDE_DIR/skills/learned"/*.md; do
402.             if [ -f "$skill" ]; then
403.                 echo "" >> "$COMBINED_FILE"
404.                 awk 'BEGIN{f=0} /^---$/{f++; next} f>=2' "$skill" >> "$COMBINED_FILE"
405.             fi
406.         done
407.     fi
408. 
409.     # Add curated skills with priority filtering
410.     # Priority 1: Anthropic Official (highest quality, vetted)
411.     if [ -d "$CURATED_DIR/anthropic-official" ]; then
412.         log "Merging Anthropic official skills (priority 1)..."
413.         find "$CURATED_DIR/anthropic-official" -name "*.md" -type f 2>/dev/null | while read -r skill; do
414.             echo "" >> "$COMBINED_FILE"
415.             awk 'BEGIN{f=0} /^---$/{f++; next} f>=2' "$skill" >> "$COMBINED_FILE"
416.         done
417.     fi
418. 
419.     # Priority 2: OpenAI Codex skills (for Codex harness)
420.     if [ -d "$CURATED_DIR/openai-codex" ]; then
421.         log "Merging OpenAI Codex skills (priority 2, top 100)..."
422.         # Filter to most relevant skills (avoid duplicates, focus on core patterns)
423.         find "$CURATED_DIR/openai-codex" -name "*.md" -type f 2>/dev/null | head -100 | while read -r skill; do
424.             echo "" >> "$COMBINED_FILE"
425.             awk 'BEGIN{f=0} /^---$/{f++; next} f>=2' "$skill" >> "$COMBINED_FILE"
426.         done
427.     fi
428. 
429.     # Priority 3: ECC skills (comprehensive base)
430.     if [ -d "$ECC_DIR/skills" ]; then
431.         log "Merging ECC skills (priority 3)..."
432.         find "$ECC_DIR/skills" -name "*.md" -type f ! -path "*/learned/*" 2>/dev/null | while read -r skill; do
433.             echo "" >> "$COMBINED_FILE"
434.             awk 'BEGIN{f=0} /^---$/{f++; next} f>=2' "$skill" >> "$COMBINED_FILE"
435.         done
436.     fi
437. 
438.     # Priority 4: K-Dense Scientific Agent Skills (SKILL.md in subdirectories)
439.     if [ -d "$SCIENCE_DIR/scientific-skills" ]; then
440.         log "Merging K-Dense scientific skills (priority 4)..."
441.         find "$SCIENCE_DIR/scientific-skills" -name "SKILL.md" -type f 2>/dev/null | while read -r skill; do
442.             echo "" >> "$COMBINED_FILE"
443.             awk 'BEGIN{f=0} /^---$/{f++; next} f>=2' "$skill" >> "$COMBINED_FILE"
444.         done
445.     fi
446. 
447.     # Priority 6: ClawBio Bioinformatics Skills (SKILL.md in subdirectories)
448.     if [ -d "$CLAWBIO_DIR/skills" ]; then
449.         log "Merging ClawBio bioinformatics skills (priority 6)..."
450.         find "$CLAWBIO_DIR/skills" -name "SKILL.md" -type f 2>/dev/null | while read -r skill; do
451.             echo "" >> "$COMBINED_FILE"
452.             awk 'BEGIN{f=0} /^---$/{f++; next} f>=2' "$skill" >> "$COMBINED_FILE"
453.         done
454.     fi
455. 
456.     SKILL_COUNT=$(wc -l < "$COMBINED_FILE" | tr -d ' ')
457.     success "Combined skills cache created ($SKILL_COUNT lines)"
458. 
459.     # Create the loader script (for backwards compatibility, but don't export large vars)
460.     cat > "$SKILLS_CACHE_DIR/load-skills.sh" << LOADER
461. #!/bin/bash
462. # Load combined skills - file-based approach to avoid environment limits
463. # SKILLS_CONTENT available via: \$(cat ~/.claude/skills-cache/combined-skills.txt)
464. COMBINED_SKILLS_FILE='$COMBINED_FILE'
465. LOADER
466. 
467.     chmod +x "$SKILLS_CACHE_DIR/load-skills.sh"
468.     success "Loader created: $SKILLS_CACHE_DIR/load-skills.sh"
469.     # Lean cache: personal learned skills only (injected into Anthropic Opus sessions)
470.     if ls "$CLAUDE_DIR/skills/learned"/*.md >/dev/null 2>&1; then
471.         cat "$CLAUDE_DIR/skills/learned"/*.md > "$SKILLS_CACHE_DIR/lean-skills.txt"
472.         success "Lean cache created: $SKILLS_CACHE_DIR/lean-skills.txt (personal skills only — 98% fewer tokens)"
473.     else
474.         > "$SKILLS_CACHE_DIR/lean-skills.txt"
475.         warn "No learned skills found — lean cache is empty"
476.     fi
477. }
478. 
479. # =============================================================================
480. # Step 4: Add aliases to shell rc files (idempotent)
481. # =============================================================================
482. 
483. # Generate the skills block to add to shell rc
484. generate_skills_block() {
485.     cat << 'SKILLS_BLOCK'
486. 
487. # Skills Layer - SAFE Install (Everything Claude Code)
488. # All default commands now include skills (ECC + Anthropic Official + Curated + conversation-derived)
489. # To disable: run ~/.claude/scripts/safe-install.sh --uninstall
490. 
491. # Helper function to inject skills as system prompt (uses file to avoid arg length limits)
492. # Uses lean-skills.txt (personal learned skills only, ~1-2K tokens) instead of
493. # combined-skills.txt (~1.4M tokens — exceeds Claude's 200K context window).
494. # The full 450+ skill library loads natively via ~/.claude/skills/ (/skills command).
495. _claude_with_skills() {
496.     local _skills_file="/tmp/claude-skills-$$.txt"
497.     cat ~/.claude/skills-cache/lean-skills.txt > "$_skills_file" 2>/dev/null
498.     if [ -s "$_skills_file" ]; then
499.         (unset SKILLS_CONTENT CODEX_SYSTEM_PROMPT OPENCLAW_SYSTEM_PROMPT; command claude --append-system-prompt-file "$_skills_file" "$@")
500.     else
501.         (unset SKILLS_CONTENT CODEX_SYSTEM_PROMPT OPENCLAW_SYSTEM_PROMPT; command claude "$@")
502.     fi
503.     rm -f "$_skills_file"
504. }
505. 
506. _openclaw_with_skills() {
507.     # OpenClaw natively loads workspace/skills/ SKILL.md files — no extra env var needed.
508.     # OPENCLAW_SYSTEM_PROMPT_FILE is NOT a real openclaw env var (not in source).
509.     (unset SKILLS_CONTENT CODEX_SYSTEM_PROMPT OPENCLAW_SYSTEM_PROMPT; command openclaw "$@")
510. }
511. 
512. _codex_with_skills() {
513.     # Codex loads skills natively from ~/.codex/skills/ (ECC skills symlinked there)
514.     (unset SKILLS_CONTENT OPENCLAW_SYSTEM_PROMPT; command codex "$@")
515. }
516. 
517. _ollama_with_skills() {
518.     # OLLAMA_SYSTEM_FILE is NOT set (breaks ollama launch codex/pi).
519.     # OPENCLAW_SYSTEM_PROMPT_FILE is not a real openclaw env var — removed.
520.     # OpenClaw loads ECC skills natively from ~/.openclaw/workspace/skills/ SKILL.md files.
521.     (unset SKILLS_CONTENT CODEX_SYSTEM_PROMPT OPENCLAW_SYSTEM_PROMPT OLLAMA_SYSTEM_FILE; command ollama "$@")
522. }
523. 
524. _pi_with_skills() {
525.     # Pi loads skills natively from ~/.pi/agent/skills/ (ECC skills symlinked there)
526.     (unset SKILLS_CONTENT CODEX_SYSTEM_PROMPT OPENCLAW_SYSTEM_PROMPT; command pi "$@")
527. }
528. 
529. _copilot_with_skills() {
530.     # Copilot CLI does NOT load ~/.claude/skills/ natively (different format).
531.     # It reads ~/.copilot/mcp-config.json for MCP servers and .github/copilot-instructions.md
532.     # for per-repo guidance. This wrapper ensures a clean environment.
533.     (unset SKILLS_CONTENT CODEX_SYSTEM_PROMPT OPENCLAW_SYSTEM_PROMPT; command copilot "$@")
534. }
535. 
536. # Wrapper aliases
537. alias claude='_claude_with_skills'
538. alias openclaw='_openclaw_with_skills'
539. alias codex='_codex_with_skills'
540. alias ollama='_ollama_with_skills'
541. alias pi='_pi_with_skills'
542. alias copilot='_copilot_with_skills'
543. 
544. # Learning pipeline commands
545. alias learn-sync='bash ~/.claude/scripts/sync-learned-skills.sh'
546. alias learn-sync-dry='bash ~/.claude/scripts/sync-learned-skills.sh --dry-run'
547. alias learn-stats='bash ~/.claude/scripts/sync-learned-skills.sh --stats'
548. alias learn-prune='bash ~/.claude/scripts/sync-learned-skills.sh --prune'
549. # End Skills Layer
550. SKILLS_BLOCK
551. }
552. 
553. setup_shell_integration() {
554.     log "Step 4: Setting up shell integration..."
555.     log "Detected user shell: $USER_SHELL (platform: $OS_TYPE)"
556. 
557.     # Find shell rc files to update
558.     local shell_rcs=()
559. 
560.     # Detect which shell rc files exist and should be updated.
561.     # Add ALL existing rc files so the user can switch shells without losing integration.
562.     if [ -f "$HOME/.zshrc" ]; then
563.         shell_rcs+=("$HOME/.zshrc")
564.         success "Found .zshrc"
565.     fi
566.     if [ -f "$HOME/.bashrc" ]; then
567.         shell_rcs+=("$HOME/.bashrc")
568.         success "Found .bashrc"
569.     fi
570.     if [ -f "$HOME/.bash_profile" ] && [ ${#shell_rcs[@]} -eq 0 ]; then
571.         # .bash_profile is used on macOS instead of .bashrc for login shells
572.         shell_rcs+=("$HOME/.bash_profile")
573.         success "Found .bash_profile"
574.     fi
575.     if [ -f "$HOME/.profile" ] && [ ${#shell_rcs[@]} -eq 0 ]; then
576.         shell_rcs+=("$HOME/.profile")
577.         success "Found .profile"
578.     fi
579. 
580.     # If no rc files found, create the appropriate one for the user's actual shell
581.     if [ ${#shell_rcs[@]} -eq 0 ]; then
582.         local default_rc
583.         case "$USER_SHELL" in
584.             zsh)  default_rc="$HOME/.zshrc" ;;
585.             bash) default_rc="$HOME/.bashrc" ;;
586.             *)    default_rc="$HOME/.bashrc" ;;
587.         esac
588.         shell_rcs+=("$default_rc")
589.         warn "No shell rc found, will create $(basename "$default_rc")"
590.     fi
591. 
592.     local skills_block
593.     skills_block=$(generate_skills_block)
594. 
595.     for shell_rc in "${shell_rcs[@]}"; do
596.         log "Processing $shell_rc..."
597. 
598.         # Check for existing entries and remove old block first (idempotent)
599.         if grep -q "# Skills Layer" "$shell_rc" 2>/dev/null; then
600.             warn "Shell integration already exists in $shell_rc — replacing"
601.             # sed -i with backup suffix works on both GNU (Linux) and BSD (macOS) sed
602.             sed -i.bak '/# Skills Layer/,/# End Skills Layer/d' "$shell_rc" 2>/dev/null || true
603.             success "Removed old integration from $shell_rc"
604.         fi
605. 
606.         # Add new entries
607.         echo "$skills_block" >> "$shell_rc"
608.         success "Shell integration added to $shell_rc"
609.     done
610. 
611.     # Determine which rc to tell the user to source
612.     local primary_rc
613.     case "$USER_SHELL" in
614.         zsh)  primary_rc="$HOME/.zshrc" ;;
615.         bash) primary_rc="$HOME/.bashrc" ;;
616.         *)    primary_rc="${shell_rcs[0]}" ;;
617.     esac
618. 
619.     # Source the current shell's rc file (may fail in non-interactive subshell — that's OK)
620.     # Use subshell to prevent set -e from catching failures inside sourced rc
621.     if [ -n "$ZSH_VERSION" ]; then
622.         (source "$HOME/.zshrc" 2>/dev/null) || true
623.         success "Shell reloaded (.zshrc)"
624.     elif [ -n "$BASH_VERSION" ]; then
625.         (source "$HOME/.bashrc" 2>/dev/null) || true
626.         success "Shell reloaded (.bashrc)"
627.     else
628.         warn "Run 'source $(basename "$primary_rc")' or restart terminal to activate"
629.     fi
630. }
631. 
632. # =============================================================================
633. # Step 4b: Copy learning pipeline scripts to ~/.claude/scripts/
634. # =============================================================================
635. # The generated shell aliases (learn-sync, learn-stats, etc.) reference
636. # ~/.claude/scripts/sync-learned-skills.sh. This step ensures those files exist.
637. 
638. copy_scripts_to_claude_dir() {
639.     local scripts_dest="$HOME/.claude/scripts"
640.     mkdir -p "$scripts_dest"
641. 
642.     local scripts_to_copy=(
643.         "sync-learned-skills.sh"
644.         "extract-conversation-skills.py"
645.         "safe-install.sh"
646.     )
647.     local copied=0
648.     for script in "${scripts_to_copy[@]}"; do
649.         local src="$REPO_DIR/$script"
650.         if [ -f "$src" ]; then
651.             cp "$src" "$scripts_dest/$script"
652.             chmod +x "$scripts_dest/$script" 2>/dev/null || true
653.             copied=$((copied + 1))
654.         fi
655.     done
656.     success "Copied $copied learning pipeline scripts to $scripts_dest/"
657. }
658. 
659. # =============================================================================
660. # Step 4c: Symlink ECC skills into native skills dirs for codex and pi
661. # =============================================================================
662. 
663. _migrate_flat_to_dir_skills() {
664.     # Migrate flat .md files to <name>/SKILL.md directory format.
665.     # Claude Code's /skills command only discovers directory-format skills.
666.     local skills_dir="$HOME/.claude/skills"
667.     [ ! -d "$skills_dir" ] && return 0
668.     local migrated=0
669.     for flat_file in "$skills_dir"/*.md; do
670.         [ ! -f "$flat_file" ] && continue
671.         local skill_name
672.         skill_name=$(basename "$flat_file" .md)
673.         # Skip learned/ subdirectory (uses flat .md by convention)
674.         [ "$skill_name" = "learned" ] && continue
675.         # Skip if directory already exists (already migrated)
676.         if [ -d "$skills_dir/$skill_name" ]; then
677.             rm -f "$flat_file"
678.             continue
679.         fi
680.         mkdir -p "$skills_dir/$skill_name"
681.         mv "$flat_file" "$skills_dir/$skill_name/SKILL.md"
682.         migrated=$((migrated + 1))
683.     done
684.     [ "$migrated" -gt 0 ] && success "Migrated $migrated flat .md skills to directory format"
685.     return 0
686. }
687. 
688. link_native_skills() {
689.     log "Step 4b: Linking ECC skills into native harness skill directories..."
690. 
691.     if [ ! -d "$ECC_DIR/skills" ]; then
692.         warn "ECC skills not installed, skipping native linking"
693.         return 0
694.     fi
695. 
696.     # Claude Code: ~/.claude/skills/<skill-name>/SKILL.md (directory format)
697.     # Claude Code's /skills command ONLY discovers directory-format skills,
698.     # NOT flat .md files. Migrate any stale flat files first.
699.     local claude_skills_dir="$HOME/.claude/skills"
700.     mkdir -p "$claude_skills_dir"
701.     _migrate_flat_to_dir_skills
702.     local claude_count=0
703.     for dir in "$ECC_DIR/skills"/*/; do
704.         skill_name=$(basename "$dir")
705.         # Use SKILL.md if it exists, otherwise find any .md
706.         local src_file=""
707.         if [ -f "$dir/SKILL.md" ]; then
708.             src_file="$dir/SKILL.md"
709.         else
710.             src_file=$(find "$dir" -maxdepth 1 -name '*.md' -type f 2>/dev/null | head -1)
711.         fi
712.         [ -z "$src_file" ] && continue
713.         local dst_dir="$claude_skills_dir/$skill_name"
714.         local dst="$dst_dir/SKILL.md"
715.         if [ ! -f "$dst" ]; then
716.             mkdir -p "$dst_dir"
717.             cp "$src_file" "$dst"
718.             claude_count=$((claude_count + 1))
719.         fi
720.     done
721.     local total_count=$(find "$claude_skills_dir" -mindepth 1 -maxdepth 1 -type d ! -name 'learned' 2>/dev/null | wc -l | tr -d ' ')
722.     if [ "$claude_count" -eq 0 ] && [ "$total_count" -gt 0 ]; then
723.         success "Claude Code: all ECC skills already in ~/.claude/skills/ ($total_count total)"
724.     else
725.         success "Claude Code: $claude_count ECC skills installed to ~/.claude/skills/ ($total_count total)"
726.     fi
727. 
728.     # Codex: handled by update-ecc.sh which properly sanitizes YAML (symlinks
729.     # for clean skills, sanitized copies for skills with block scalars/extra fields).
730.     # safe-install.sh used to skip invalid skills entirely — update-ecc.sh fixes them.
731.     if [ -d "$HOME/.codex/skills" ]; then
732.         log "Codex skills will be synced by update-ecc.sh (proper YAML sanitization)"
733.     else
734.         warn "~/.codex/skills not found — install Codex CLI first"
735.     fi
736. 
737.     # OpenClaw: ~/.openclaw/workspace/skills/<skill-name>/ (real copies, NOT symlinks)
738.     # Must be real files inside workspace root - OpenClaw rejects symlinks escaping the workspace
739.     # Skills appear with source="openclaw-workspace" and status="✓ ready"
740.     if [ -d "$HOME/.openclaw/workspace" ]; then
741.         local openclaw_count=0
742.         local ws_skills="$HOME/.openclaw/workspace/skills"
743.         mkdir -p "$ws_skills"
744.         for dir in "$ECC_DIR/skills"/*/; do
745.             skill_name=$(basename "$dir")
746.             [ ! -f "$dir/SKILL.md" ] && continue
747.             grep -q '^description: *[>|]' "$dir/SKILL.md" 2>/dev/null && continue
748.             if grep -E '^[a-z_]+:' "$dir/SKILL.md" 2>/dev/null | grep -qvE '^(name|description|origin|tools):'; then
749.                 continue

[Output truncated. Use view_range=[750, ...] to continue reading. In your next response, you may batch this with other view calls. File has at least 1065 lines.]

1. #!/bin/bash
2. # =============================================================================
3. # setup-beads.sh — Install and configure beads (bd) for all harnesses
4. # =============================================================================
5. # Installs the beads CLI + beads-mcp Python package, then injects the beads
6. # MCP server into ~/.claude.json (Claude Code) and ~/.copilot/mcp-config.json
7. # (Copilot CLI). Initialises a .beads/ workspace if none exists.
8. #
9. # On macOS: installs via Homebrew (auto-installs Homebrew if missing).
10. # On Linux: installs via the official beads install script.
11. #
12. # Usage:
13. #   scripts/setup-beads.sh
14. #   scripts/setup-beads.sh --skip-init    # skip bd init
15. #   scripts/setup-beads.sh --force        # reinstall + overwrite existing MCP entry
16. # =============================================================================
17. set -e
18. 
19. REPO_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")/.." && pwd)"
20. 
21. # Platform detection
22. case "$(uname -s)" in
23.     Darwin*)                OS_TYPE="macOS" ;;
24.     Linux*)                 OS_TYPE="Linux" ;;
25.     MINGW*|MSYS*|CYGWIN*)  OS_TYPE="Windows" ;;
26.     *)                      OS_TYPE="Unknown" ;;
27. esac
28. 
29. BLUE='\033[0;34m'; GREEN='\033[0;32m'; YELLOW='\033[1;33m'; RED='\033[0;31m'; NC='\033[0m'
30. log()     { echo -e "${BLUE}[BEADS]${NC} $1"; }
31. success() { echo -e "${GREEN}[OK]${NC} $1"; }
32. warn()    { echo -e "${YELLOW}[WARN]${NC} $1"; }
33. error()   { echo -e "${RED}[ERROR]${NC} $1"; exit 1; }
34. 
35. SKIP_INIT=false
36. FORCE=false
37. for arg in "$@"; do
38.     case $arg in
39.         --skip-init) SKIP_INIT=true ;;
40.         --force)     FORCE=true ;;
41.     esac
42. done
43. 
44. echo ""
45. echo "╔══════════════════════════════════════╗"
46. echo "║   Beads Integration Setup            ║"
47. echo "╚══════════════════════════════════════╝"
48. echo ""
49. 
50. # =============================================================================
51. # Step 1: Ensure Homebrew is installed (macOS only)
52. # =============================================================================
53. if [ "$OS_TYPE" = "macOS" ]; then
54.     if ! command -v brew >/dev/null 2>&1; then
55.         log "Homebrew not found — installing..."
56.         log "This will run the official Homebrew installer from brew.sh"
57.         /bin/bash -c "$(curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Homebrew/install/HEAD/install.sh)"
58.         # Homebrew may install to /opt/homebrew (Apple Silicon) or /usr/local (Intel)
59.         if [ -f /opt/homebrew/bin/brew ]; then
60.             eval "$(/opt/homebrew/bin/brew shellenv)"
61.         elif [ -f /usr/local/bin/brew ]; then
62.             eval "$(/usr/local/bin/brew shellenv)"
63.         fi
64.         if command -v brew >/dev/null 2>&1; then
65.             success "Homebrew installed: $(brew --version | head -1)"
66.         else
67.             error "Homebrew install failed. Install from https://brew.sh and re-run."
68.         fi
69.     else
70.         success "Homebrew found: $(brew --version | head -1)"
71.     fi
72. fi
73. 
74. # =============================================================================
75. # Step 2: Install beads CLI
76. # =============================================================================
77. if command -v bd >/dev/null 2>&1 && [ "$FORCE" != "true" ]; then
78.     success "beads (bd) already installed: $(bd --version 2>/dev/null || echo 'version unknown')"
79. else
80.     log "Installing beads CLI..."
81.     if [ "$OS_TYPE" = "macOS" ]; then
82.         brew install beads && success "beads installed via Homebrew" || warn "brew install beads failed — check https://github.com/gastownhall/beads"
83.     else
84.         # Linux: official install script
85.         if curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/gastownhall/beads/main/scripts/install.sh | bash; then
86.             success "beads installed via official install script"
87.         else
88.             warn "beads install failed — install manually from https://github.com/gastownhall/beads"
89.         fi
90.     fi
91. fi
92. 
93. # =============================================================================
94. # Step 3: Install beads-mcp (Python package — harness-agnostic MCP server)
95. # =============================================================================
96. BEADS_MCP_CMD=""
97. 
98. # Prefer uv tool install (isolated, no PEP 668 issues)
99. if command -v uv >/dev/null 2>&1; then
100.     log "Installing beads-mcp via uv..."
101.     if uv tool install beads-mcp 2>/dev/null; then
102.         success "beads-mcp installed via uv tool"
103.         BEADS_MCP_CMD="beads-mcp"
104.     else
105.         warn "uv tool install beads-mcp failed — trying pip3..."
106.     fi
107. fi
108. 
109. # Fallback: pip3
110. if [ -z "$BEADS_MCP_CMD" ] && command -v pip3 >/dev/null 2>&1; then
111.     log "Installing beads-mcp via pip3..."
112.     if pip3 install --quiet beads-mcp 2>/dev/null; then
113.         success "beads-mcp installed via pip3"
114.         BEADS_MCP_CMD="beads-mcp"
115.     else
116.         warn "pip3 install beads-mcp failed — trying with --user flag..."
117.         if pip3 install --quiet --user beads-mcp 2>/dev/null; then
118.             success "beads-mcp installed via pip3 --user"
119.             BEADS_MCP_CMD="beads-mcp"
120.         else
121.             warn "beads-mcp could not be installed — MCP integration will be skipped"
122.         fi
123.     fi
124. fi
125. 
126. if [ -n "$BEADS_MCP_CMD" ] && command -v beads-mcp >/dev/null 2>&1; then
127.     success "beads-mcp binary confirmed: $(command -v beads-mcp)"
128. elif [ -n "$BEADS_MCP_CMD" ]; then
129.     warn "beads-mcp installed but not in PATH yet — MCP entries will point to 'beads-mcp'"
130.     warn "Ensure ~/.local/bin or uv tool bin dir is in your PATH"
131. fi
132. 
133. # =============================================================================
134. # Step 4: Inject beads MCP entry into ~/.claude.json (Claude Code)
135. # =============================================================================
136. inject_mcp_entry() {
137.     local config_path="$1"
138.     local harness_name="$2"
139. 
140.     if [ ! -f "$config_path" ]; then
141.         warn "$config_path not found — skipping $harness_name MCP injection"
142.         return 0
143.     fi
144. 
145.     python3 << PYEOF
146. import json, os, sys
147. 
148. config_path = "$config_path"
149. force = $([[ "$FORCE" == "true" ]] && echo "True" || echo "False")
150. 
151. with open(config_path) as f:
152.     config = json.load(f)
153. 
154. key = "mcpServers"
155. if key not in config:
156.     config[key] = {}
157. 
158. if "beads" in config[key] and not force:
159.     print("beads MCP entry already present (skip — use --force to overwrite)")
160.     sys.exit(0)
161. 
162. config[key]["beads"] = {
163.     "command": "beads-mcp",
164.     "args": []
165. }
166. 
167. with open(config_path, "w") as f:
168.     json.dump(config, f, indent=2)
169. 
170. action = "Updated" if "beads" in config[key] else "Added"
171. print(f"{action} beads MCP entry in {config_path}")
172. PYEOF
173. }
174. 
175. if [ -n "$BEADS_MCP_CMD" ]; then
176.     log "Injecting beads MCP entry into Claude Code config..."
177.     inject_mcp_entry "$HOME/.claude.json" "Claude Code" && success "Claude Code: beads MCP configured" || warn "Claude Code MCP injection failed"
178. 
179.     COPILOT_CFG="$HOME/.copilot/mcp-config.json"
180.     if [ -f "$COPILOT_CFG" ]; then
181.         log "Injecting beads MCP entry into Copilot CLI config..."
182.         inject_mcp_entry "$COPILOT_CFG" "Copilot CLI" && success "Copilot CLI: beads MCP configured" || warn "Copilot CLI MCP injection failed"
183.     else
184.         warn "~/.copilot/mcp-config.json not found — run scripts/setup-copilot.sh first"
185.     fi
186. else
187.     warn "beads-mcp not available — skipping MCP injection"
188.     warn "Install beads-mcp manually: uv tool install beads-mcp"
189. fi
190. 
191. # =============================================================================
192. # Step 5: Initialise beads workspace in repo (stealth mode — no git ops)
193. # =============================================================================
194. if [ "$SKIP_INIT" = "true" ]; then
195.     log "Skipping bd init (--skip-init passed)"
196. elif ! command -v bd >/dev/null 2>&1; then
197.     warn "bd command not found — skipping bd init"
198. else
199.     if [ -d "$REPO_DIR/.beads" ]; then
200.         success "Beads workspace already initialised at $REPO_DIR/.beads/"
201.     else
202.         log "Initialising beads workspace (stealth mode)..."
203.         cd "$REPO_DIR"
204.         bd init --quiet --stealth 2>/dev/null && success "Beads workspace initialised (.beads/)" || warn "bd init failed — run 'bd init' manually in the repo"
205.     fi
206. fi
207. 
208. # =============================================================================
209. # Done
210. # =============================================================================
211. echo ""
212. echo "  beads CLI:   $(command -v bd 2>/dev/null || echo 'not in PATH')"
213. echo "  beads-mcp:   $(command -v beads-mcp 2>/dev/null || echo 'not in PATH')"
214. echo ""
215. echo "  Key commands:"
216. echo "    bd prime     → AI-optimised project context dump (use at session start)"
217. echo "    bd ready     → List open work items"
218. echo "    bd create 'Title' -p 2  → Create work item (priority 1=high, 3=low)"
219. echo "    bd close <id>           → Close completed item"
220. echo ""
221. log "Restart Claude Code or Copilot CLI for MCP changes to take effect"
222. echo ""
223.
```

</details>

/Users/chazh/Documents/GitHub/ai-skillweave/scripts/verify.sh

<details>
<summary>266 lines</summary>

```
1. #!/bin/bash
2. # =============================================================================
3. # verify.sh — Verify the full agent harness setup is working
4. # =============================================================================
5. # Checks all components: Ollama, Claude Code MCP, OpenClaw, Pi, Codex,
6. # skills layer, and network/proxy status.
7. #
8. # Usage:
9. #   scripts/verify.sh [--fix]
10. #   --fix : attempt to auto-fix issues found
11. # =============================================================================
12. 
13. REPO_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")/.." && pwd)"
14. 
15. # Platform detection
16. case "$(uname -s)" in
17.     Darwin*)                 OS_TYPE="macOS" ;;
18.     Linux*)                  OS_TYPE="Linux" ;;
19.     MINGW*|MSYS*|CYGWIN*)   OS_TYPE="Windows" ;;
20.     *)                       OS_TYPE="Unknown" ;;
21. esac
22. 
23. # Detect user's default shell
24. case "${SHELL##*/}" in
25.     zsh)  USER_SHELL="zsh";  PRIMARY_RC="$HOME/.zshrc" ;;
26.     bash) USER_SHELL="bash"; PRIMARY_RC="$HOME/.bashrc" ;;
27.     *)    USER_SHELL="bash"; PRIMARY_RC="$HOME/.bashrc" ;;
28. esac
29. 
30. BLUE='\033[0;34m'; GREEN='\033[0;32m'; YELLOW='\033[1;33m'; RED='\033[0;31m'; CYAN='\033[0;36m'; NC='\033[0m'
31. ok()   { echo -e "  ${GREEN}✓${NC} $1"; }
32. fail() { echo -e "  ${RED}✗${NC} $1"; FAIL_COUNT=$((FAIL_COUNT+1)); }
33. warn() { echo -e "  ${YELLOW}⚠${NC} $1"; WARN_COUNT=$((WARN_COUNT+1)); }
34. section() { echo ""; echo -e "${CYAN}── $1 ──${NC}"; }
35. 
36. FAIL_COUNT=0; WARN_COUNT=0
37. FIX=false
38. [[ "$*" == *"--fix"* ]] && FIX=true
39. 
40. echo ""
41. echo "╔══════════════════════════════════════╗"
42. echo "║   Agent Harness Verification         ║"
43. echo "╚══════════════════════════════════════╝"
44. 
45. # ── Ollama ──
46. section "Ollama"
47. if command -v ollama >/dev/null 2>&1; then
48.     ok "ollama binary found: $(command -v ollama)"
49. else
50.     fail "ollama not found — install from https://ollama.com"
51. fi
52. 
53. if curl -s --max-time 3 http://localhost:11434/api/status >/dev/null 2>&1; then
54.     ok "Ollama server running (localhost:11434)"
55.     MODELS=$(ollama list 2>/dev/null | tail -n +2 | awk '{print $1}' | tr '\n' ' ')
56.     ok "Models: $MODELS"
57. else
58.     fail "Ollama server not running — start it: ollama serve"
59. fi
60. 
61. # Check config
62. if [ -f "$HOME/.ollama/config.json" ]; then
63.     ok "~/.ollama/config.json present"
64.     LAST_SEL=$(python3 -c "import json; d=json.load(open('$HOME/.ollama/config.json')); print(d.get('last_selection','?'))" 2>/dev/null)
65.     ok "  Last integration: $LAST_SEL"
66. else
67.     warn "~/.ollama/config.json not found"
68. fi
69. 
70. # ── Claude Code ──
71. section "Claude Code (ollama launch claude)"
72. if command -v claude >/dev/null 2>&1; then
73.     ok "claude binary found"
74. else
75.     fail "claude not found — install Claude Code"
76. fi
77. 
78. if [ -f "$HOME/.claude.json" ]; then
79.     ok "~/.claude.json present"
80.     MCP_COUNT=$(python3 -c "import json; d=json.load(open('$HOME/.claude.json')); print(len(d.get('mcpServers',{})))" 2>/dev/null)
81.     if [ "$MCP_COUNT" -gt 0 ] 2>/dev/null; then
82.         ok "MCP servers configured: $MCP_COUNT"
83.         MCP_NAMES=$(python3 -c "import json; d=json.load(open('$HOME/.claude.json')); print(' '.join(d.get('mcpServers',{}).keys()))" 2>/dev/null)
84.         ok "  Servers: $MCP_NAMES"
85.     else
86.         fail "No MCP servers configured in ~/.claude.json"
87.         if $FIX; then
88.             echo "    → Running setup-mcp.sh..."
89.             "$REPO_DIR/scripts/setup-mcp.sh" && ok "Fixed: MCP servers applied"
90.         else
91.             echo "    → Fix: scripts/setup-mcp.sh"
92.         fi
93.     fi
94. else
95.     fail "~/.claude.json not found — run Claude Code once to initialize"
96. fi
97. 
98. if [ -d "$HOME/.claude/skills-cache" ] && [ -f "$HOME/.claude/skills-cache/combined-skills.txt" ]; then
99.     SKILL_SIZE=$(wc -c < "$HOME/.claude/skills-cache/combined-skills.txt" | tr -d ' ')
100.     ok "Skills cache: $SKILL_SIZE bytes"
101. else
102.     warn "Skills cache missing — run: safe-install.sh"
103. fi
104. 
105. # ── OpenClaw ──
106. section "OpenClaw (ollama launch openclaw)"
107. if command -v openclaw >/dev/null 2>&1; then
108.     ok "openclaw binary found"
109. else
110.     warn "openclaw not found — install: ollama launch openclaw --config"
111. fi
112. 
113. if [ -f "$HOME/.openclaw/openclaw.json" ]; then
114.     ok "~/.openclaw/openclaw.json present"
115.     python3 << 'PYEOF'
116. import json, os
117. cfg = json.load(open(os.path.expanduser("~/.openclaw/openclaw.json")))
118. web_fetch = cfg.get("tools",{}).get("web",{}).get("fetch",{}).get("enabled", False)
119. web_search = cfg.get("tools",{}).get("web",{}).get("search",{}).get("enabled", False)
120. ollama_plugin = cfg.get("plugins",{}).get("entries",{}).get("ollama",{}).get("enabled", False)
121. model = cfg.get("agents",{}).get("defaults",{}).get("model",{}).get("primary","?")
122. gw_port = cfg.get("gateway",{}).get("port", "?")
123. print(f"  Model:         {model}")
124. print(f"  Web fetch:     {'✓ enabled' if web_fetch else '✗ disabled'}")
125. print(f"  Web search:    {'✓ enabled' if web_search else '✗ disabled'}")
126. print(f"  Ollama plugin: {'✓ enabled' if ollama_plugin else '✗ disabled'}")
127. print(f"  Gateway port:  {gw_port}")
128. PYEOF
129. else
130.     warn "~/.openclaw/openclaw.json not found"
131.     if $FIX; then
132.         echo "    → Running setup-openclaw.sh..."
133.         "$REPO_DIR/scripts/setup-openclaw.sh" && ok "Fixed: OpenClaw config applied"
134.     else
135.         echo "    → Fix: scripts/setup-openclaw.sh"
136.     fi
137. fi
138. 
139. # Check gateway running
140. if curl -s --max-time 2 http://localhost:18789/ 2>/dev/null | grep -q "OpenClaw"; then
141.     ok "OpenClaw gateway: running (port 18789)"
142. else
143.     warn "OpenClaw gateway not running (start: ollama launch openclaw)"
144. fi
145. 
146. # ── Pi ──
147. section "Pi (ollama launch pi)"
148. if command -v pi >/dev/null 2>&1; then
149.     ok "pi binary found"
150. else
151.     warn "pi not found — install Pi agent"
152. fi
153. 
154. if [ -f "$HOME/.pi/agent/settings.json" ]; then
155.     ok "~/.pi/agent/settings.json present"
156.     python3 << 'PYEOF'
157. import json, os
158. s = json.load(open(os.path.expanduser("~/.pi/agent/settings.json")))
159. print(f"  Model:    {s.get('defaultModel','?')}")
160. print(f"  Provider: {s.get('defaultProvider','?')}")
161. print(f"  Packages: {s.get('packages',[])}")
162. PYEOF
163.     SKILL_COUNT=$(ls "$HOME/.pi/agent/skills/" 2>/dev/null | wc -l | tr -d ' ')
164.     ok "Pi skills directory: $SKILL_COUNT skills linked"
165. else
166.     warn "~/.pi/agent/settings.json not found"
167. fi
168. 
169. # ── Codex ──
170. section "Codex (ollama launch codex)"
171. if command -v codex >/dev/null 2>&1; then
172.     ok "codex binary found"
173. else
174.     warn "codex not found — install Codex CLI"
175. fi
176. 
177. if [ -f "$HOME/.codex/config.toml" ]; then
178.     ok "~/.codex/config.toml present"
179.     PROVIDER=$(grep "model_provider" "$HOME/.codex/config.toml" | head -1 | cut -d'"' -f2)
180.     MODEL=$(grep "^model = " "$HOME/.codex/config.toml" | cut -d'"' -f2)
181.     ok "  Provider: $PROVIDER | Model: $MODEL"
182. else
183.     warn "~/.codex/config.toml not found"
184. fi
185. 
186. # ── Skills Layer ──
187. section "Shell Skills Layer"
188. 
189. # Check all possible RC files, but primary is based on user's shell
190. skills_found=false
191. for rc_file in "$PRIMARY_RC" "$HOME/.zshrc" "$HOME/.bashrc" "$HOME/.bash_profile" "$HOME/.profile"; do
192.     if [ -f "$rc_file" ] && grep -q "_claude_with_skills\|# Skills Layer" "$rc_file" 2>/dev/null; then
193.         ok "Skills layer in $rc_file"
194.         skills_found=true
195.     fi
196. done
197. if ! $skills_found; then
198.     fail "Skills layer not installed in any shell rc file"
199.     if $FIX; then
200.         echo "    → Running safe-install.sh..."
201.         "$REPO_DIR/safe-install.sh" && ok "Fixed: Skills layer installed"
202.     else
203.         echo "    → Fix: ./safe-install.sh"
204.     fi
205. fi
206. 
207. # Check aliases in the primary rc file
208. for alias_name in claude openclaw codex ollama pi; do
209.     if grep -q "alias $alias_name=" "$PRIMARY_RC" 2>/dev/null; then
210.         ok "alias '$alias_name' configured in $(basename "$PRIMARY_RC")"
211.     elif grep -q "alias $alias_name=" "$HOME/.zshrc" "$HOME/.bashrc" 2>/dev/null; then
212.         ok "alias '$alias_name' configured (in alternate rc)"
213.     else
214.         warn "alias '$alias_name' not found in shell rc files"
215.     fi
216. done
217. 
218. # ── Network / Proxy ──
219. section "Network & Proxy"
220. 
221. if [ "$OS_TYPE" = "macOS" ]; then
222.     PROXY_HTTP=$(scutil --proxy 2>/dev/null | grep "HTTPEnable" | awk '{print $3}')
223.     PROXY_HTTPS=$(scutil --proxy 2>/dev/null | grep "HTTPSEnable" | awk '{print $3}')
224. 
225.     if [ "$PROXY_HTTP" = "1" ] || [ "$PROXY_HTTPS" = "1" ]; then
226.         warn "System HTTP/HTTPS proxy is ENABLED — may intercept Ollama streams"
227.         echo "    → Fix: scripts/disable-zscaler.sh"
228.     else
229.         ok "No system HTTP/HTTPS proxy (Zscaler off)"
230.     fi
231. 
232.     if launchctl list 2>/dev/null | grep -q "zscaler"; then
233.         warn "Zscaler tray still running"
234.         echo "    → Fix: scripts/disable-zscaler.sh --tray"
235.     else
236.         ok "Zscaler tray: not running"
237.     fi
238. else
239.     # Linux/Windows: check environment proxy variables
240.     if [ -n "$http_proxy" ] || [ -n "$https_proxy" ] || [ -n "$HTTP_PROXY" ] || [ -n "$HTTPS_PROXY" ]; then
241.         warn "HTTP proxy env vars set — may intercept Ollama streams"
242.         [ -n "$http_proxy" ] && echo "    http_proxy=$http_proxy"
243.         [ -n "$https_proxy" ] && echo "    https_proxy=$https_proxy"
244.     else
245.         ok "No proxy env vars set"
246.     fi
247. fi
248. 
249. if curl -s --max-time 5 -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" https://ollama.com 2>/dev/null | grep -q "200\|301\|302"; then
250.     ok "ollama.com reachable"
251. else
252.     warn "ollama.com not reachable — cloud models may fail"
253. fi
254. 
255. # ── Summary ──
256. echo ""
257. echo "════════════════════════════════════════"
258. if [ $FAIL_COUNT -eq 0 ] && [ $WARN_COUNT -eq 0 ]; then
259.     echo -e "${GREEN}✓ All checks passed!${NC}"
260. elif [ $FAIL_COUNT -eq 0 ]; then
261.     echo -e "${YELLOW}✓ Passed with $WARN_COUNT warning(s) — review above${NC}"
262. else
263.     echo -e "${RED}✗ $FAIL_COUNT failure(s), $WARN_COUNT warning(s) — run with --fix or check above${NC}"
264. fi
265. echo ""
266.
```

<details>
<summary>59 lines</summary>

```
1. 
2. # =============================================================================
3. # Skills Layer — Everything Claude Code
4. # =============================================================================
5. # All harness commands get the ECC skills injected automatically.
6. # Injected by: ai-skillweave/install.sh
7. # To remove:   ai-skillweave/safe-install.sh --uninstall
8. # =============================================================================
9. 
10. # Claude Code: inject personal learned skills as system prompt supplement.
11. # Uses lean-skills.txt (~personal skills only, ~1-2K tokens) NOT combined-skills.txt
12. # (~1.4M tokens which would exceed Claude's 200K context window and crash the session).
13. # The full 450+ skill library is already natively available via Claude Code's /skills
14. # command from ~/.claude/skills/ — no injection needed for those.
15. _claude_with_skills() {
16.     local _skills_file="/tmp/claude-skills-$$.txt"
17.     cat ~/.claude/skills-cache/lean-skills.txt > "$_skills_file" 2>/dev/null
18.     if [ -s "$_skills_file" ]; then
19.         (unset SKILLS_CONTENT CODEX_SYSTEM_PROMPT OPENCLAW_SYSTEM_PROMPT; command claude --append-system-prompt-file "$_skills_file" "$@")
20.     else
21.         (unset SKILLS_CONTENT CODEX_SYSTEM_PROMPT OPENCLAW_SYSTEM_PROMPT; command claude "$@")
22.     fi
23.     rm -f "$_skills_file"
24. }
25. 
26. # OpenClaw: loads SKILL.md files natively from ~/.openclaw/workspace/skills/
27. _openclaw_with_skills() {
28.     (unset SKILLS_CONTENT CODEX_SYSTEM_PROMPT OPENCLAW_SYSTEM_PROMPT; command openclaw "$@")
29. }
30. 
31. # Codex: loads skills natively from ~/.codex/skills/ (ECC skills symlinked there)
32. _codex_with_skills() {
33.     (unset SKILLS_CONTENT OPENCLAW_SYSTEM_PROMPT; command codex "$@")
34. }
35. 
36. # Ollama: pass-through with env cleanup (openclaw/codex/pi load skills natively)
37. _ollama_with_skills() {
38.     (unset SKILLS_CONTENT CODEX_SYSTEM_PROMPT OPENCLAW_SYSTEM_PROMPT OLLAMA_SYSTEM_FILE; command ollama "$@")
39. }
40. 
41. # Pi: loads skills natively from ~/.pi/agent/skills/
42. _pi_with_skills() {
43.     (unset SKILLS_CONTENT CODEX_SYSTEM_PROMPT OPENCLAW_SYSTEM_PROMPT; command pi "$@")
44. }
45. 
46. # Cross-harness skill learner
47. alias learn-sync='bash ~/.claude/scripts/sync-learned-skills.sh'
48. alias learn-sync-dry='bash ~/.claude/scripts/sync-learned-skills.sh --dry-run --verbose'
49. alias learn-stats='bash ~/.claude/scripts/sync-learned-skills.sh --stats'
50. alias learn-prune='bash ~/.claude/scripts/sync-learned-skills.sh --prune'
51. 
52. # Wrapper aliases
53. alias claude='_claude_with_skills'
54. alias openclaw='_openclaw_with_skills'
55. alias codex='_codex_with_skills'
56. alias ollama='_ollama_with_skills'
57. alias pi='_pi_with_skills'
58. # End Skills Layer
59.
```

</details>

<details>
<summary>85 lines</summary>

```
1. #!/bin/bash
2. # =============================================================================
3. # codesight-redirect.sh — PreToolUse hook
4. # =============================================================================
5. # Intercepts broad Glob/Grep codebase searches when codesight is configured.
6. # Reminds Claude to call codesight_get_summary first (once per session/project).
7. #
8. # Exit 2 = block tool + send message to Claude
9. # Exit 0 = allow tool to proceed
10. # =============================================================================
11. 
12. input=$(cat)
13. 
14. # Extract tool name, pattern, and path from hook payload
15. tool=$(echo "$input" | python3 -c "
16. import json, sys
17. d = json.load(sys.stdin)
18. print(d.get('tool_name', ''))
19. " 2>/dev/null)
20. 
21. session_id=$(echo "$input" | python3 -c "
22. import json, sys
23. d = json.load(sys.stdin)
24. print(d.get('session_id', 'default')[:24])
25. " 2>/dev/null || echo "default")
26. 
27. pattern=$(echo "$input" | python3 -c "
28. import json, sys
29. inp = json.load(sys.stdin).get('tool_input', {})
30. print(inp.get('pattern', '') or inp.get('glob', ''))
31. " 2>/dev/null)
32. 
33. path=$(echo "$input" | python3 -c "
34. import json, sys
35. inp = json.load(sys.stdin).get('tool_input', {})
36. print(inp.get('path', '') or inp.get('file_path', ''))
37. " 2>/dev/null)
38. 
39. # Only intercept broad searches (** glob patterns indicating codebase exploration)
40. is_broad=false
41. [[ "$pattern" == *"**"* ]] && is_broad=true
42. [[ -z "$path" && "$tool" == "Glob" ]] && is_broad=true
43. $is_broad || exit 0
44. 
45. # Walk up from search path to find nearest .codesight/ directory
46. check_dir="${path:-$(pwd)}"
47. [[ -f "$check_dir" ]] && check_dir=$(dirname "$check_dir")
48. codesight_dir=""
49. depth=0
50. while [[ "$check_dir" != "/" && $depth -lt 10 ]]; do
51.     if [[ -d "$check_dir/.codesight" ]]; then
52.         codesight_dir="$check_dir"
53.         break
54.     fi
55.     check_dir=$(dirname "$check_dir")
56.     ((depth++))
57. done
58. 
59. # No codesight configured — allow through
60. [[ -n "$codesight_dir" ]] || exit 0
61. 
62. # Only remind once per session per project (state tracked in /tmp)
63. state_dir="/tmp/claude-hooks-${session_id}"
64. mkdir -p "$state_dir"
65. # Hash the project dir for a short key
66. state_key=$(echo "$codesight_dir" | md5 2>/dev/null || echo "$codesight_dir" | cksum | cut -d' ' -f1)
67. state_key="${state_key:0:8}"
68. reminded_file="$state_dir/codesight-${state_key}.done"
69. 
70. # Already reminded this session — allow through
71. [[ -f "$reminded_file" ]] && exit 0
72. 
73. touch "$reminded_file"
74. 
75. cat << EOF
76. CODESIGHT REDIRECT: $codesight_dir has codesight configured.
77. 
78. Before running broad $tool searches, call codesight_get_summary first:
79.   mcp__codesight__codesight_get_summary(directory="$codesight_dir")
80. 
81. This returns a compact project map (~500 tokens) — routes, schema, hot files, env vars.
82. After calling codesight_get_summary, run targeted searches as needed (this reminder won't repeat).
83. EOF
84. exit 2
85.
```

</details>

1. #!/bin/bash
2. # =============================================================================
3. # install.sh — Master setup for Ollama Agent Harness
4. # =============================================================================
5. # One-command setup for all AI agent harnesses on a new machine.
6. # Configures: Claude Code (MCP), OpenClaw, Pi, Codex, Copilot CLI, shell skills layer.
7. #
8. # Requirements:
9. #   - Ollama installed and running (https://ollama.com)
10. #   - At least one model: ollama pull <model-name>
11. #   - Claude Code, OpenClaw, Pi, Codex, and/or Copilot installed as needed
12. #
13. # Usage:
14. #   ./install.sh                           # Full setup (all harnesses + all skills)
15. #   ./install.sh --without-science         # Skip K-Dense scientific skills
16. #   ./install.sh --model llama3.2:3b       # Use a specific Ollama model
17. #   ./install.sh --skip-skills             # Skip ECC skills installation
18. #   ./install.sh --only claude             # Only configure claude
19. #   ./install.sh --only openclaw           # Only configure openclaw
20. #   ./install.sh --uninstall               # Remove shell integrations
21. #   ./install.sh --verify                  # Run health check only
22. # =============================================================================
23. set -e
24. 
25. REPO_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
26. 
27. # =============================================================================
28. # Platform detection
29. # =============================================================================
30. case "$(uname -s)" in
31.     Darwin*)                 OS_TYPE="macOS" ;;
32.     Linux*)                  OS_TYPE="Linux" ;;
33.     MINGW*|MSYS*|CYGWIN*)   OS_TYPE="Windows" ;;  # untested; use WSL
34.     *)                       OS_TYPE="Unknown" ;;
35. esac
36. 
37. # Detect user's login shell for RC file references
38. case "${SHELL##*/}" in
39.     zsh)  USER_SHELL="zsh";  SHELL_RC="~/.zshrc" ;;
40.     bash) USER_SHELL="bash"; SHELL_RC="~/.bashrc" ;;
41.     *)    USER_SHELL="bash"; SHELL_RC="~/.bashrc" ;;
42. esac
43. 
44. # Platform-aware package install hint
45. case "$OS_TYPE" in
46.     macOS)   PKG_HINT="brew install" ;;
47.     Linux)
48.         if command -v apt-get >/dev/null 2>&1; then
49.             PKG_HINT="apt-get install"
50.         elif command -v dnf >/dev/null 2>&1; then
51.             PKG_HINT="dnf install"
52.         elif command -v pacman >/dev/null 2>&1; then
53.             PKG_HINT="pacman -S"
54.         else
55.             PKG_HINT="your package manager to install"
56.         fi
57.         ;;
58.     Windows) PKG_HINT="your package manager to install" ;;
59.     *)       PKG_HINT="your package manager to install" ;;
60. esac
61. 
62. BLUE='\033[0;34m'; GREEN='\033[0;32m'; YELLOW='\033[1;33m'; RED='\033[0;31m'; CYAN='\033[0;36m'; NC='\033[0m'
63. log()     { echo -e "${BLUE}[INSTALL]${NC} $1"; }
64. success() { echo -e "${GREEN}[OK]${NC} $1"; }
65. warn()    { echo -e "${YELLOW}[WARN]${NC} $1"; }
66. error()   { echo -e "${RED}[ERROR]${NC} $1"; exit 1; }
67. section() { echo ""; echo -e "${CYAN}═══════════════════════════════════════${NC}"; echo -e "${CYAN}  $1${NC}"; echo -e "${CYAN}═══════════════════════════════════════${NC}"; }
68. 
69. # =============================================================================
70. # Parse arguments
71. # =============================================================================
72. OLLAMA_MODEL="qwen3.6"
73. SKIP_SKILLS=false
74. WITH_SCIENCE=true
75. WITH_CURATED=true
76. WITH_BIO=true
77. WITH_LEARN=true
78. ONLY_TARGET=""
79. 
80. while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
81.     case "$1" in
82.         --model)            OLLAMA_MODEL="$2"; shift 2 ;;
83.         --skip-skills)      SKIP_SKILLS=true; shift ;;
84.         --with-science)     WITH_SCIENCE=true; shift ;;
85.         --without-science)  WITH_SCIENCE=false; shift ;;
86.         --with-curated)     WITH_CURATED=true; shift ;;
87.         --without-curated)  WITH_CURATED=false; shift ;;
88.         --with-bio)         WITH_BIO=true; shift ;;
89.         --without-bio)      WITH_BIO=false; shift ;;
90.         --learn)            WITH_LEARN=true; shift ;;
91.         --no-learn)         WITH_LEARN=false; shift ;;
92.         --only)             ONLY_TARGET="$2"; shift 2 ;;
93.         --uninstall)
94.             "$REPO_DIR/safe-install.sh" --uninstall
95.             exit 0
96.             ;;
97.         --verify)
98.             bash "$REPO_DIR/scripts/verify.sh"
99.             exit 0
100.             ;;
101.         --help|-h)
102.             echo "Usage: ./install.sh [--model MODEL] [--skip-skills] [--with-science] [--without-science] [--with-bio] [--without-bio] [--only TARGET] [--uninstall] [--verify]"
103.             echo ""
104.             echo "  --with-science     Include K-Dense scientific skills (default)"
105.             echo "  --without-science  Skip K-Dense scientific skills"
106.             echo "  --with-bio         Include ClawBio bioinformatics skills (default)"
107.             echo "  --without-bio      Skip ClawBio bioinformatics skills"
108.             exit 0
109.             ;;
110.         *) shift ;;
111.     esac
112. done
113. 
114. echo ""
115. echo "╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗"
116. echo "║   ai-skillweave — Full Setup                           ║"
117. echo "╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝"
118. echo ""
119. log "Platform: $OS_TYPE ($USER_SHELL)"
120. log "Model:    $OLLAMA_MODEL"
121. log "Repo:     $REPO_DIR"
122. echo ""
123. 
124. if [ "$OS_TYPE" = "Windows" ]; then
125.     warn "Native Windows detected. This installer targets bash-based systems."
126.     warn "For best results, run inside WSL (Windows Subsystem for Linux)."
127.     warn "Continuing anyway — some steps may not apply."
128.     echo ""
129. fi
130. 
131. # =============================================================================
132. # Step 0a: Bootstrap prerequisites (git, node/npm, ollama, python3)
133. # =============================================================================
134. section "Bootstrap"
135. 
136. _install_if_missing() {
137.     local cmd="$1"
138.     local name="$2"
139.     local install_cmd="$3"
140.     if command -v "$cmd" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
141.         success "$name already installed: $(command -v "$cmd")"
142.         return 0
143.     fi
144.     log "Installing $name..."
145.     if eval "$install_cmd"; then
146.         success "$name installed"
147.         return 0
148.     else
149.         warn "$name installation failed — install manually, then re-run"
150.         return 1
151.     fi
152. }
153. 
154. # --- git ---
155. if [ "$OS_TYPE" = "macOS" ]; then
156.     _install_if_missing git "git" "brew install git"
157. else
158.     if command -v apt-get >/dev/null 2>&1; then
159.         _install_if_missing git "git" "sudo apt-get update -qq && sudo apt-get install -y -qq git"
160.     elif command -v dnf >/dev/null 2>&1; then
161.         _install_if_missing git "git" "sudo dnf install -y git"
162.     elif command -v pacman >/dev/null 2>&1; then
163.         _install_if_missing git "git" "sudo pacman -S --noconfirm git"
164.     fi
165. fi
166. 
167. # --- node + npm ---
168. if ! command -v node >/dev/null 2>&1 || ! command -v npm >/dev/null 2>&1; then
169.     log "Node.js/npm not found — installing..."
170.     if [ "$OS_TYPE" = "macOS" ]; then
171.         brew install node 2>/dev/null || warn "brew install node failed"
172.     else
173.         # Use NodeSource setup for LTS Node 22
174.         curl -fsSL https://deb.nodesource.com/setup_22.x | bash - 2>/dev/null && \
175.             apt-get install -y nodejs 2>/dev/null || \
176.             warn "NodeSource install failed — install node manually"
177.     fi
178.     # Verify
179.     if command -v node >/dev/null 2>&1 && command -v npm >/dev/null 2>&1; then
180.         success "Node.js $(node --version) + npm $(npm --version) installed"
181.     else
182.         warn "Node.js/npm still not available — npx-based MCP servers won't work"
183.     fi
184. else
185.     success "Node.js $(node --version) + npm $(npm --version) already installed"
186. fi
187. 
188. # --- ollama ---
189. if ! command -v ollama >/dev/null 2>&1; then
190.     log "Ollama not found — installing..."
191.     curl -fsSL https://ollama.com/install.sh | sh && \
192.         success "Ollama installed" || \
193.         warn "Ollama install failed — install manually from https://ollama.com"
194. else
195.     success "Ollama already installed: $(command -v ollama)"
196. fi
197. 
198. # --- python3 + pip ---
199. if ! command -v python3 >/dev/null 2>&1; then
200.     log "python3 not found — installing..."
201.     if [ "$OS_TYPE" = "macOS" ]; then
202.         brew install python3 2>/dev/null || warn "brew install python3 failed"
203.     else
204.         if command -v apt-get >/dev/null 2>&1; then
205.             sudo apt-get update -qq && sudo apt-get install -y -qq python3 python3-pip python3-venv
206.         elif command -v dnf >/dev/null 2>&1; then
207.             sudo dnf install -y python3 python3-pip
208.         elif command -v pacman >/dev/null 2>&1; then
209.             sudo pacman -S --noconfirm python python-pip
210.         fi
211.     fi
212. fi
213. if command -v python3 >/dev/null 2>&1; then
214.     success "python3: $(command -v python3)"
215. else
216.     error "python3 is required but could not be installed automatically"
217. fi
218. 
219. # =============================================================================
220. # Step 0: Preflight checks
```

</details>

```
220. # Step 0: Preflight checks
221. # =============================================================================
222. section "Preflight"
223. 
224. command -v python3 >/dev/null 2>&1 || error "python3 required ($PKG_HINT python3)"
225. 
226. # Install Python dependencies for auto-learning pipeline
227. log "Installing Python dependencies for skill extraction (sentence-transformers, scikit-learn)..."
228. _install_python_deps() {
229.     local pkgs="sentence-transformers>=3.0.0 scikit-learn>=1.5.0"
230.     # Detect virtualenv: --user is forbidden inside venv/conda
231.     local in_venv=""
232.     if python3 -c "import sys; sys.exit(0 if (sys.prefix != sys.base_prefix) else 1)" 2>/dev/null; then
233.         in_venv=1
234.     fi
235.     if [ -n "$in_venv" ]; then
236.         log "Detected Python virtualenv — skipping --user flag"
237.         if python3 -m pip install --quiet $pkgs 2>&1; then
238.             success "Python deps installed (venv)"
239.             return 0
240.         fi
241.     else
242.         # Try multiple pip invocation strategies; show errors so user can diagnose
243.         if python3 -m pip install --user --quiet $pkgs 2>&1; then
244.             success "Python deps installed (user site)"
245.             return 0
246.         fi
247.         warn "pip --user failed — trying without --user..."
248.         if python3 -m pip install --quiet $pkgs 2>&1; then
249.             success "Python deps installed (system site)"
250.             return 0
251.         fi
252.     fi
253.     if command -v pip3 >/dev/null 2>&1 && pip3 install --quiet $pkgs 2>&1; then
254.         success "Python deps installed via pip3"
255.         return 0
256.     fi
257.     warn "All pip install attempts failed (see errors above)"
258.     warn "Try manually: python3 -m pip install sentence-transformers scikit-learn"
259.     return 1
260. }
261. _install_python_deps || warn "Python deps missing — the learning pipeline will fail until fixed"
262. 
263. command -v node >/dev/null 2>&1 || warn "node not found — npx-based MCP servers won't work ($PKG_HINT node)"
264. command -v git >/dev/null 2>&1 || warn "git not found — ECC skills install will be skipped"
265. 
266. if command -v ollama >/dev/null 2>&1; then
267.     success "ollama: $(command -v ollama)"
268.     if curl -s --max-time 3 http://localhost:11434/api/status >/dev/null 2>&1; then
269.         success "Ollama server: running"
270. 
271.         # Check if requested model is available
272.         if ollama list 2>/dev/null | grep -q "^${OLLAMA_MODEL}"; then
273.             success "Model available: $OLLAMA_MODEL"
274.         else
275.             warn "Model '$OLLAMA_MODEL' not in local list (may be cloud model — OK)"
276.             warn "If local model needed: ollama pull $OLLAMA_MODEL"
277.         fi
278.     else
279.         if [ "$OS_TYPE" = "macOS" ]; then
280.             warn "Ollama server not running — start it: open /Applications/Ollama.app"
281.         else
282.             warn "Ollama server not running — start it: ollama serve"
283.         fi
284.         warn "Continuing setup; start Ollama before using harnesses"
285.     fi
286. else
287.     warn "ollama not installed — install from https://ollama.com"
288.     warn "Continuing setup without Ollama; harnesses that need it will fail at runtime"
289. fi
290. 
291. # Disable Zscaler tray if running (macOS only, no sudo needed)
292. if [ "$OS_TYPE" = "macOS" ] && launchctl list 2>/dev/null | grep -q "zscaler"; then
293.     log "Detected Zscaler — disabling tray (no sudo needed)..."
294.     bash "$REPO_DIR/scripts/disable-zscaler.sh" --tray 2>/dev/null || true
295. fi
296. 
297. # =============================================================================
298. # Step 0b: Pre-install npx-based MCP servers globally (prevents timeout on first launch)
299. # =============================================================================
300. if command -v npm >/dev/null 2>&1; then
301.     log "Pre-installing MCP server packages globally (prevents first-launch timeouts)..."
302.     # Install silently in background; failures are non-fatal (npx will fallback)
303.     npm install -g @modelcontextprotocol/server-memory \
304.         @modelcontextprotocol/server-sequential-thinking \
305.         @upstash/context7-mcp@latest \
306.         @playwright/mcp \
307.         codesight \
308.         token-optimizer-mcp 2>/dev/null || warn "Some MCP packages failed to pre-install (npx fallback will work but may be slower on first launch)"
309.     success "MCP server packages pre-installed"
310. else
311.     warn "npm not found — MCP servers will be installed on first launch (may timeout)"
312. fi
313. 
314. # =============================================================================
315. # Harness installer helper
316. # =============================================================================
317. install_harness() {
318.     local name="$1"
319.     local cmd="$2"
320.     local install_cmd="$3"
321. 
322.     if command -v "$cmd" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
323.         success "$name already installed: $(command -v "$cmd")"
324.         return 0
325.     fi
326. 
327.     if ! command -v npm >/dev/null 2>&1; then
328.         warn "npm not found — cannot auto-install $name"
329.         warn "Install manually, then re-run install.sh"
330.         return 1
331.     fi
332. 
333.     log "Installing $name..."
334.     if eval "$install_cmd"; then
335.         success "$name installed"
336.         return 0
337.     else
338.         warn "$name installation failed — install manually, then re-run"
339.         return 1
340.     fi
341. }
342. 
343. # =============================================================================
344. # Step 1: Skills Layer (ECC + shell wrappers)
345. # =============================================================================
346. 
347. should_run() {
348.     [ -z "$ONLY_TARGET" ] || [ "$ONLY_TARGET" = "$1" ]
349. }
350. 
351. if ! $SKIP_SKILLS && should_run "skills"; then
352.     section "Skills Layer"
353.     # safe-install.sh may exit non-zero when 'source <rc>' fails in a non-interactive
354.     # subshell — this is expected and harmless. Capture exit code to report real failures.
355.     safe_exit=0
356.     SAFE_ARGS=""
357.     $WITH_SCIENCE && SAFE_ARGS="$SAFE_ARGS --with-science"
358.     $WITH_CURATED && SAFE_ARGS="$SAFE_ARGS --with-curated"
359.     $WITH_BIO && SAFE_ARGS="$SAFE_ARGS --with-bio"
360.     bash "$REPO_DIR/safe-install.sh" $SAFE_ARGS || safe_exit=$?
361.     if [ "$safe_exit" -eq 0 ]; then
362.         success "ECC skills + shell wrappers installed (run: source $SHELL_RC to activate)"
363.     else
364.         warn "safe-install.sh exited with code $safe_exit — shell integration may need manual source $SHELL_RC"
365.     fi
366.     # Rebuild cache with full skill counts (safe-install.sh caps Codex at 100).
367.     # update-ecc.sh also restores ECC working tree to actual HEAD.
368.     log "Rebuilding skills cache with full counts..."
369.     bash "$REPO_DIR/scripts/update-ecc.sh" --force || true
370.     success "Skills cache rebuilt (all sources, no caps)"
371. else
372.     log "Skipping skills layer"
373. fi
374. 
375. # =============================================================================
376. # Step 1b: Ollama integration→model mapping
377. # =============================================================================
378. if should_run "ollama" || [ -z "$ONLY_TARGET" ]; then
379.     section "Ollama Integrations"
380.     bash "$REPO_DIR/scripts/setup-ollama-config.sh" && success "Ollama integrations configured" || warn "Ollama config skipped (Ollama not started yet)"
381. fi
382. 
383. # =============================================================================
384. # Step 2: Claude Code MCP + Global Instructions
385. # =============================================================================
386. if should_run "claude"; then
387.     section "Claude Code"
388.     install_harness "Claude Code" "claude" "npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code"
389.     if [ -f "$HOME/.claude.json" ]; then
390.         bash "$REPO_DIR/scripts/setup-mcp.sh" && success "Claude Code MCP configured" || warn "MCP setup skipped (run Claude Code once, then re-run)"
391.     else
392.         warn "~/.claude.json not found — run Claude Code once to initialize, then re-run install.sh"
393.     fi
394.     # Global CLAUDE.md: proactive MCP usage + conciseness + token discipline
395.     bash "$REPO_DIR/scripts/setup-claude-md.sh" && success "Global CLAUDE.md installed" || warn "CLAUDE.md setup skipped"
396.     # Pre-tool-use hooks: enforce codesight before broad Glob/Grep searches
397.     bash "$REPO_DIR/scripts/setup-hooks.sh" && success "Claude Code hooks installed" || warn "Hooks setup skipped"
398.     
399.     # BMO-style learning capture hooks (real-time correction detection)
400.     bash "$REPO_DIR/scripts/setup-learning-hook.sh" && success "BMO learning capture hooks configured" || warn "Learning hook setup skipped"
401. fi
402. 
403. # =============================================================================
404. # Step 3: OpenClaw
405. # =============================================================================
406. if should_run "openclaw"; then
407.     section "OpenClaw"
408.     bash "$REPO_DIR/scripts/setup-openclaw.sh" --model "$OLLAMA_MODEL" && success "OpenClaw configured" || warn "OpenClaw setup skipped (not installed yet)"
409. fi
410. 
411. # =============================================================================
412. # Step 4: Codex
413. # =============================================================================
414. if should_run "codex"; then
415.     section "Codex"
416.     install_harness "Codex CLI" "codex" "npm install -g @openai/codex"
417.     bash "$REPO_DIR/scripts/setup-codex.sh" --model "$OLLAMA_MODEL" && success "Codex configured" || warn "Codex setup skipped (not installed yet)"
418. fi
419. 
420. # =============================================================================
421. # Step 5: Pi
422. # =============================================================================
423. if should_run "pi"; then
424.     section "Pi"
425.     install_harness "Pi" "pi" "npm install -g @mariozechner/pi-coding-agent"
426.     bash "$REPO_DIR/scripts/setup-pi.sh" --model "$OLLAMA_MODEL" && success "Pi configured" || warn "Pi setup skipped (not installed yet)"
427. fi
428. 
429. # =============================================================================
430. # Step 6: Copilot CLI
431. # =============================================================================
432. if should_run "copilot"; then
433.     section "Copilot CLI"
434.     install_harness "Copilot CLI" "copilot" "npm install -g @github/copilot"
435.     bash "$REPO_DIR/scripts/setup-copilot.sh" && success "Copilot CLI MCP configured" || warn "Copilot setup skipped (not installed yet)"
436. fi
437. 
438. # =============================================================================
439. # Step 7: Beads integration (bd CLI + beads-mcp + bd init)
440. # =============================================================================
441. if should_run "beads"; then
442.     section "Beads"
443.     bash "$REPO_DIR/scripts/setup-beads.sh" && success "Beads configured" || warn "Beads setup skipped (non-fatal)"
444. fi
445. 
446. # =============================================================================
447. # Step 8: Cleanup stale/invalid skills from previous installs
448. # =============================================================================
449. if [ -f "$REPO_DIR/scripts/cleanup-invalid-skills.sh" ]; then
450.     log "Cleaning up invalid skills from previous installs..."
451.     bash "$REPO_DIR/scripts/cleanup-invalid-skills.sh" 2>/dev/null || warn "Skill cleanup had issues (non-fatal)"
452. fi
453. 
454. # Done
455. # =============================================================================
456. echo ""
457. echo "╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗"
458. echo "║   Setup Complete ($OS_TYPE / $USER_SHELL)"
459. echo "╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝"
460. echo ""
461. echo "  Reload shell:   source $SHELL_RC"
462. echo ""
463. echo "  Launch harnesses:"
464. echo "    ollama launch claude      → Claude Code with MCP + skills"
465. echo "    ollama launch openclaw    → OpenClaw with web tools + subagents"
466. echo "    ollama launch pi          → Pi with subagents"
467. echo "    ollama launch codex       → Codex via ollama-launch provider"
468. echo "    ollama launch copilot     → Copilot CLI with MCP servers"
469. echo ""
470. echo "  Health check:   ./install.sh --verify"
471. echo ""
472. if [ "$OS_TYPE" = "macOS" ]; then
473.     echo "  Zscaler (if needed after reboot):"
474.     echo "    scripts/disable-zscaler.sh"
475.     echo "    # Then (in Terminal with sudo):"
476.     echo "    sudo launchctl unload /Library/LaunchDaemons/com.zscaler.service.plist"
477.     echo "    sudo launchctl unload /Library/LaunchDaemons/com.zscaler.tunnel.plist"
478.     echo ""
479. fi
480.
```

</details>

927. 
928. # =============================================================================
929. # Step 5: Uninstall
930. # =============================================================================
931. 
932. uninstall() {
933.     log "Uninstalling skills layer..."
934. 
935.     # Remove shell integration from all possible rc files
936.     for rc_file in "$HOME/.zshrc" "$HOME/.bashrc" "$HOME/.profile"; do
937.         if [ -f "$rc_file" ]; then
938.             if grep -q "# Skills Layer" "$rc_file" 2>/dev/null; then
939.                 sed -i.bak '/# Skills Layer/,/# End Skills Layer/d' "$rc_file" 2>/dev/null || true
940.                 success "Removed shell integration from $rc_file"
941.             fi
942.         fi
943.     done
944. 
945.     # Remove installed files
946.     rm -rf "$ECC_DIR"
947.     rm -rf "$CURATED_DIR"
948.     rm -rf "$SCIENCE_DIR"
949.     rm -rf "$CLAWBIO_DIR"
950.     rm -rf "$SKILLS_CACHE_DIR"
951.     # Remove Claude Code native skills (only ECC-originated ones)
952.     if [ -d "$HOME/.claude/skills" ]; then
953.         rm -rf "$HOME/.claude/skills"
954.         success "Removed ~/.claude/skills/"
955.     fi
956. 
957.     success "Uninstall complete"
958.     echo ""
959.     echo "Note: Your original ~/.claude/ config was never modified."
960.     echo "Run 'source ~/.zshrc' or 'source ~/.bashrc' or restart terminal to fully clean up."
961. }
962. 
963. # =============================================================================
964. # Step 6: Show usage
965. # =============================================================================
966. 
967. show_usage() {
968.     # Count skills
969.     ECC_COUNT=0
970.     ANTHROPIC_COUNT=0
971.     CODEX_COUNT=0
972.     SCIENCE_COUNT=0
973.     BIO_COUNT=0
974.     if [ -d "$ECC_DIR/skills" ]; then
975.         ECC_COUNT=$(find "$ECC_DIR/skills" -maxdepth 2 -name 'SKILL.md' 2>/dev/null | wc -l | tr -d ' ')
976.     fi
977.     if [ -d "$CURATED_DIR/anthropic-official" ]; then
978.         ANTHROPIC_COUNT=$(find "$CURATED_DIR/anthropic-official" -name 'SKILL.md' 2>/dev/null | wc -l | tr -d ' ')
979.     fi
980.     if [ -d "$CURATED_DIR/openai-codex" ]; then
981.         CODEX_COUNT=$(find "$CURATED_DIR/openai-codex" -name 'SKILL.md' 2>/dev/null | wc -l | tr -d ' ')
982.     fi
983.     if [ -d "$SCIENCE_DIR/scientific-skills" ]; then
984.         SCIENCE_COUNT=$(find "$SCIENCE_DIR/scientific-skills" -name 'SKILL.md' 2>/dev/null | wc -l | tr -d ' ')
985.     fi
986.     if [ -d "$CLAWBIO_DIR/skills" ]; then
987.         BIO_COUNT=$(find "$CLAWBIO_DIR/skills" -name 'SKILL.md' 2>/dev/null | wc -l | tr -d ' ')
988.     fi
989.     TOTAL_COUNT=$((ECC_COUNT + ANTHROPIC_COUNT + CODEX_COUNT + SCIENCE_COUNT + BIO_COUNT))
990. 
991.     # Determine which rc file to tell the user to source
992.     local reload_rc
993.     case "$USER_SHELL" in
994.         zsh)  reload_rc="~/.zshrc" ;;
995.         bash) reload_rc="~/.bashrc" ;;
996.         *)    reload_rc="~/.bashrc" ;;
997.     esac
998. 
999.     cat << USAGE
1000. 
1001. ╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗
1002. ║   Installation Complete ($OS_TYPE / $USER_SHELL)
1003. ╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝
1004. 
1005. Skills loaded:
1006.   - ECC skills:           $ECC_COUNT
1007.   - Anthropic skills:    $ANTHROPIC_COUNT
1008.   - Codex skills:        $CODEX_COUNT
1009.   - Scientific skills:    $SCIENCE_COUNT (K-Dense Agent Skills)
1010.   - Bioinformatics skills: $BIO_COUNT (ClawBio)
1011.   - Total:                $TOTAL_COUNT
1012. 
1013. Activate:  source $reload_rc   (or restart terminal)
1014. 
1015. All default commands now have skills:
1016.   claude                    ollama launch claude
1017.   openclaw                  ollama launch openclaw
1018.   codex                     ollama launch codex
1019.   pi                        ollama launch pi
1020.   copilot                   ollama launch copilot
1021.   ollama
1022. 
1023. To bypass skills (raw commands):
1024.   command claude [args]
1025.   command codex [args]
1026.   command openclaw [args]
1027.   command pi [args]
1028.   command copilot [args]
1029.   OLLAMA_SYSTEM="" ollama [args]
1030. 
1031. To list skills:
1032.   ls ~/.claude-everything-claude-code/skills/   # ECC skills
1033.   ls ~/.claude-curated-skills/                   # Curated skills
1034.   ls ~/.claude-scientific-skills/scientific-skills/  # K-Dense scientific skills
1035.   ls ~/.claude/skills-cache/                     # Combined cache
1036. 
1037. To reinstall with curated + scientific skills:
1038.   ~/.claude/scripts/safe-install.sh --with-curated --with-science
1039. 
1040. To uninstall:
1041.   ~/.claude/scripts/safe-install.sh --uninstall
1042. 
1043. USAGE
1044. }
1045. 
1046. # =============================================================================
1047. # Main
1048. # =============================================================================
1049. 
1050. if [[ "$*" == *"--uninstall"* ]]; then
1051.     uninstall
1052.     exit 0
1053. fi
1054. 
1055. install_ecc_skills
1056. install_curated_skills
1057. install_science_skills
1058. install_bio_skills
1059. create_loader
1060. link_native_skills
1061. setup_shell_integration || true
1062. copy_scripts_to_claude_dir || true
1063. 
1064. show_usage
1065.
```

</details>
