<details>
<summary>231 lines</summary>

```
1. #!/bin/bash
2. # =============================================================================
3. # setup-beads.sh — Install and configure beads (bd) for all harnesses
4. # =============================================================================
5. # Installs the beads CLI + beads-mcp Python package, then injects the beads
6. # MCP server into ~/.claude.json (Claude Code) and ~/.copilot/mcp-config.json
7. # (Copilot CLI). Initialises a .beads/ workspace if none exists.
8. #
9. # On macOS: installs via Homebrew (auto-installs Homebrew if missing).
10. # On Linux: installs via the official beads install script.
11. #
12. # Usage:
13. #   scripts/setup-beads.sh
14. #   scripts/setup-beads.sh --skip-init    # skip bd init
15. #   scripts/setup-beads.sh --force        # reinstall + overwrite existing MCP entry
16. # =============================================================================
17. set -e
18. 
19. REPO_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")/.." && pwd)"
20. 
21. # Platform detection
22. case "$(uname -s)" in
23.     Darwin*)                OS_TYPE="macOS" ;;
24.     Linux*)                 OS_TYPE="Linux" ;;
25.     MINGW*|MSYS*|CYGWIN*)  OS_TYPE="Windows" ;;
26.     *)                      OS_TYPE="Unknown" ;;
27. esac
28. 
29. BLUE='\033[0;34m'; GREEN='\033[0;32m'; YELLOW='\033[1;33m'; RED='\033[0;31m'; NC='\033[0m'
30. log()     { echo -e "${BLUE}[BEADS]${NC} $1"; }
31. success() { echo -e "${GREEN}[OK]${NC} $1"; }
32. warn()    { echo -e "${YELLOW}[WARN]${NC} $1"; }
33. error()   { echo -e "${RED}[ERROR]${NC} $1"; exit 1; }
34. 
35. SKIP_INIT=false
36. FORCE=false
37. for arg in "$@"; do
38.     case $arg in
39.         --skip-init) SKIP_INIT=true ;;
40.         --force)     FORCE=true ;;
41.     esac
42. done
43. 
44. echo ""
45. echo "╔══════════════════════════════════════╗"
46. echo "║   Beads Integration Setup            ║"
47. echo "╚══════════════════════════════════════╝"
48. echo ""
49. 
50. # =============================================================================
51. # Step 1: Ensure Homebrew is installed (macOS only)
52. # =============================================================================
53. IS_TTY=false
54. [ -t 0 ] && IS_TTY=true
55. 
56. if [ "$OS_TYPE" = "macOS" ]; then
57.     if ! command -v brew >/dev/null 2>&1; then
58.         if [ "$IS_TTY" = "false" ]; then
59.             warn "Homebrew not found and running non-interactively — install brew first: https://brew.sh"
60.             warn "Then re-run: scripts/setup-beads.sh"
61.             exit 1
62.         fi
63.         log "Homebrew not found — installing (interactive)..."
64.         log "This will run the official Homebrew installer from brew.sh"
65.         /bin/bash -c "$(curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Homebrew/install/HEAD/install.sh)"
66.         # Homebrew may install to /opt/homebrew (Apple Silicon) or /usr/local (Intel)
67.         if [ -f /opt/homebrew/bin/brew ]; then
68.             eval "$(/opt/homebrew/bin/brew shellenv)"
69.         elif [ -f /usr/local/bin/brew ]; then
70.             eval "$(/usr/local/bin/brew shellenv)"
71.         fi
72.         if command -v brew >/dev/null 2>&1; then
73.             success "Homebrew installed: $(brew --version | head -1)"
74.         else
75.             error "Homebrew install failed. Install from https://brew.sh and re-run."
76.         fi
77.     else
78.         success "Homebrew found: $(brew --version | head -1)"
79.     fi
80. fi
81. 
82. # =============================================================================
83. # Step 2: Install beads CLI
84. # =============================================================================
85. if command -v bd >/dev/null 2>&1 && [ "$FORCE" != "true" ]; then
86.     success "beads (bd) already installed: $(bd --version 2>/dev/null || echo 'version unknown')"
87. else
88.     log "Installing beads CLI..."
89.     if [ "$OS_TYPE" = "macOS" ]; then
90.         brew install beads && success "beads installed via Homebrew" || warn "brew install beads failed — check https://github.com/gastownhall/beads"
91.     else
92.         # Linux: official install script
93.         if curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/gastownhall/beads/main/scripts/install.sh | bash; then
94.             success "beads installed via official install script"
95.         else
96.             warn "beads install failed — install manually from https://github.com/gastownhall/beads"
97.         fi
98.     fi
99. fi
100. 
101. # =============================================================================
102. # Step 3: Install beads-mcp (Python package — harness-agnostic MCP server)
103. # =============================================================================
104. BEADS_MCP_CMD=""
105. 
106. # Prefer uv tool install (isolated, no PEP 668 issues)
107. if command -v uv >/dev/null 2>&1; then
108.     log "Installing beads-mcp via uv..."
109.     if uv tool install beads-mcp 2>/dev/null; then
110.         success "beads-mcp installed via uv tool"
111.         BEADS_MCP_CMD="beads-mcp"
112.     else
113.         warn "uv tool install beads-mcp failed — trying pip3..."
114.     fi
115. fi
116. 
117. # Fallback: pip3
118. if [ -z "$BEADS_MCP_CMD" ] && command -v pip3 >/dev/null 2>&1; then
119.     log "Installing beads-mcp via pip3..."
120.     if pip3 install --quiet beads-mcp 2>/dev/null; then
121.         success "beads-mcp installed via pip3"
122.         BEADS_MCP_CMD="beads-mcp"
123.     else
124.         warn "pip3 install beads-mcp failed — trying with --user flag..."
125.         if pip3 install --quiet --user beads-mcp 2>/dev/null; then
126.             success "beads-mcp installed via pip3 --user"
127.             BEADS_MCP_CMD="beads-mcp"
128.         else
129.             warn "beads-mcp could not be installed — MCP integration will be skipped"
130.         fi
131.     fi
132. fi
133. 
134. if [ -n "$BEADS_MCP_CMD" ] && command -v beads-mcp >/dev/null 2>&1; then
135.     success "beads-mcp binary confirmed: $(command -v beads-mcp)"
136. elif [ -n "$BEADS_MCP_CMD" ]; then
137.     warn "beads-mcp installed but not in PATH yet — MCP entries will point to 'beads-mcp'"
138.     warn "Ensure ~/.local/bin or uv tool bin dir is in your PATH"
139. fi
140. 
141. # =============================================================================
142. # Step 4: Inject beads MCP entry into ~/.claude.json (Claude Code)
143. # =============================================================================
144. inject_mcp_entry() {
145.     local config_path="$1"
146.     local harness_name="$2"
147. 
148.     if [ ! -f "$config_path" ]; then
149.         warn "$config_path not found — skipping $harness_name MCP injection"
150.         return 0
151.     fi
152. 
153.     python3 << PYEOF
154. import json, os, sys
155. 
156. config_path = "$config_path"
157. force = $([[ "$FORCE" == "true" ]] && echo "True" || echo "False")
158. 
159. with open(config_path) as f:
160.     config = json.load(f)
161. 
162. key = "mcpServers"
163. if key not in config:
164.     config[key] = {}
165. 
166. if "beads" in config[key] and not force:
167.     print("beads MCP entry already present (skip — use --force to overwrite)")
168.     sys.exit(0)
169. 
170. config[key]["beads"] = {
171.     "command": "beads-mcp",
172.     "args": []
173. }
174. 
175. with open(config_path, "w") as f:
176.     json.dump(config, f, indent=2)
177. 
178. action = "Updated" if "beads" in config[key] else "Added"
179. print(f"{action} beads MCP entry in {config_path}")
180. PYEOF
181. }
182. 
183. if [ -n "$BEADS_MCP_CMD" ]; then
184.     log "Injecting beads MCP entry into Claude Code config..."
185.     inject_mcp_entry "$HOME/.claude.json" "Claude Code" && success "Claude Code: beads MCP configured" || warn "Claude Code MCP injection failed"
186. 
187.     COPILOT_CFG="$HOME/.copilot/mcp-config.json"
188.     if [ -f "$COPILOT_CFG" ]; then
189.         log "Injecting beads MCP entry into Copilot CLI config..."
190.         inject_mcp_entry "$COPILOT_CFG" "Copilot CLI" && success "Copilot CLI: beads MCP configured" || warn "Copilot CLI MCP injection failed"
191.     else
192.         warn "~/.copilot/mcp-config.json not found — run scripts/setup-copilot.sh first"
193.     fi
194. else
195.     warn "beads-mcp not available — skipping MCP injection"
196.     warn "Install beads-mcp manually: uv tool install beads-mcp"
197. fi
198. 
199. # =============================================================================
200. # Step 5: Initialise beads workspace in repo (stealth mode — no git ops)
201. # =============================================================================
202. if [ "$SKIP_INIT" = "true" ]; then
203.     log "Skipping bd init (--skip-init passed)"
204. elif ! command -v bd >/dev/null 2>&1; then
205.     warn "bd command not found — skipping bd init"
206. else
207.     if [ -d "$REPO_DIR/.beads" ]; then
208.         success "Beads workspace already initialised at $REPO_DIR/.beads/"
209.     else
210.         log "Initialising beads workspace (stealth mode)..."
211.         cd "$REPO_DIR"
212.         bd init --quiet --stealth 2>/dev/null && success "Beads workspace initialised (.beads/)" || warn "bd init failed — run 'bd init' manually in the repo"
213.     fi
214. fi
215. 
216. # =============================================================================
217. # Done
218. # =============================================================================
219. echo ""
220. echo "  beads CLI:   $(command -v bd 2>/dev/null || echo 'not in PATH')"
221. echo "  beads-mcp:   $(command -v beads-mcp 2>/dev/null || echo 'not in PATH')"
222. echo ""
223. echo "  Key commands:"
224. echo "    bd prime     → AI-optimised project context dump (use at session start)"
225. echo "    bd ready     → List open work items"
226. echo "    bd create 'Title' -p 2  → Create work item (priority 1=high, 3=low)"
227. echo "    bd close <id>           → Close completed item"
228. echo ""
229. log "Restart Claude Code or Copilot CLI for MCP changes to take effect"
230. echo ""
231.
```

</details>

<sub>⏱️ 27m 57s</sub>

### ✅ `view`

**view the file at /Users/chazh/Documents/GitHub/ai-skillweave/safe-install.sh.**

/Users/chazh/Documents/GitHub/ai-skillweave/safe-install.sh (lines 100-250)

<details>
<summary>151 lines</summary>

```
100. fi
101. echo "║   Zero-Risk • Auto Shell Integration                     ║"
102. echo "╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝"
103. echo ""
104. 
105. # =============================================================================
106. # Bootstrap prerequisites (lightweight — install.sh does the heavy lifting)
107. # =============================================================================
108. if ! command -v git >/dev/null 2>&1; then
109.     log "git not found — attempting install..."
110.     if [ "$OS_TYPE" = "macOS" ] && command -v brew >/dev/null 2>&1; then
111.         brew install git 2>/dev/null || warn "brew install git failed"
112.     elif command -v apt-get >/dev/null 2>&1; then
113.         sudo apt-get update -qq && sudo apt-get install -y -qq git 2>/dev/null || warn "apt install git failed"
114.     elif command -v dnf >/dev/null 2>&1; then
115.         sudo dnf install -y git 2>/dev/null || warn "dnf install git failed"
116.     elif command -v pacman >/dev/null 2>&1; then
117.         sudo pacman -S --noconfirm git 2>/dev/null || warn "pacman install git failed"
118.     fi
119. fi
120. command -v git >/dev/null 2>&1 || error "git is required but could not be installed"
121. 
122. if ! command -v python3 >/dev/null 2>&1; then
123.     log "python3 not found — attempting install..."
124.     if [ "$OS_TYPE" = "macOS" ] && command -v brew >/dev/null 2>&1; then
125.         brew install python3 2>/dev/null || warn "brew install python3 failed"
126.     elif command -v apt-get >/dev/null 2>&1; then
127.         sudo apt-get update -qq && sudo apt-get install -y -qq python3 python3-pip 2>/dev/null || warn "apt install python3 failed"
128.     elif command -v dnf >/dev/null 2>&1; then
129.         sudo dnf install -y python3 python3-pip 2>/dev/null || warn "dnf install python3 failed"
130.     elif command -v pacman >/dev/null 2>&1; then
131.         sudo pacman -S --noconfirm python python-pip 2>/dev/null || warn "pacman install python failed"
132.     fi
133. fi
134. command -v python3 >/dev/null 2>&1 || error "python3 is required but could not be installed"
135. 
136. # Pre-install Python dependencies for auto-learning pipeline
137. log "Installing Python dependencies for skill extraction..."
138. _install_python_deps() {
139.     local pkgs="sentence-transformers>=3.0.0 scikit-learn>=1.5.0"
140.     local in_venv=""
141.     if python3 -c "import sys; sys.exit(0 if (sys.prefix != sys.base_prefix) else 1)" 2>/dev/null; then
142.         in_venv=1
143.     fi
144.     if [ -n "$in_venv" ]; then
145.         log "Detected Python virtualenv — skipping --user flag"
146.         if python3 -m pip install --quiet $pkgs 2>&1; then
147.             success "Python deps installed (venv)"
148.             return 0
149.         fi
150.     else
151.         if python3 -m pip install --user --quiet $pkgs 2>&1; then
152.             success "Python deps installed (user site)"
153.             return 0
154.         fi
155.         warn "pip --user failed — trying without --user..."
156.         if python3 -m pip install --quiet $pkgs 2>&1; then
157.             success "Python deps installed (system site)"
158.             return 0
159.         fi
160.     fi
161.     if command -v pip3 >/dev/null 2>&1 && pip3 install --quiet $pkgs 2>&1; then
162.         success "Python deps installed via pip3"
163.         return 0
164.     fi
165.     warn "All pip install attempts failed (see errors above)"
166.     warn "Try manually: python3 -m pip install sentence-transformers scikit-learn"
167.     return 1
168. }
169. _install_python_deps || warn "Python deps missing — the learning pipeline will fail until fixed"
170. 
171. # Ensure node/npm available for harness installs
172. if ! command -v npm >/dev/null 2>&1; then
173.     log "npm not found — attempting install..."
174.     if [ "$OS_TYPE" = "macOS" ] && command -v brew >/dev/null 2>&1; then
175.         brew install node 2>/dev/null || warn "brew install node failed"
176.     elif command -v apt-get >/dev/null 2>&1; then
177.         curl -fsSL https://deb.nodesource.com/setup_22.x | bash - 2>/dev/null && \
178.             apt-get install -y nodejs 2>/dev/null || warn "NodeSource install failed"
179.     fi
180. fi
181. 
182. # =============================================================================
183. # Step 1: Fetch everything-claude-code to isolated directory
184. # =============================================================================
185. 
186. install_ecc_skills() {
187.     if [ "$CURATED_ONLY" = true ]; then
188.         log "Skipping ECC skills (curated-only mode)..."
189.         return 0
190.     fi
191. 
192.     log "Step 1: Installing ECC skills..."
193. 
194.     if [ -d "$ECC_DIR" ]; then
195.         warn "Existing ECC installation found: $ECC_DIR"
196.         # If stdin is a terminal, prompt interactively; otherwise keep existing
197.         if [ -t 0 ]; then
198.             read -p "Overwrite? [y/N] " -n 1 -r
199.             echo ""
200.             if [[ ! $REPLY =~ ^[Yy]$ ]]; then
201.                 success "Keeping existing ECC installation"
202.                 return 0
203.             else
204.                 rm -rf "$ECC_DIR"
205.             fi
206.         else
207.             success "Non-interactive mode — keeping existing ECC installation"
208.             return 0
209.         fi
210.     fi
211. 
212.     mkdir -p "$ECC_DIR"
213.     cd /tmp
214.     if git clone --depth 1 https://github.com/affaan-m/everything-claude-code.git ecc-temp 2>/dev/null; then
215.         mv ecc-temp/* "$ECC_DIR/" 2>/dev/null || true
216.         rm -rf ecc-temp
217.         success "ECC skills installed: $ECC_DIR ($(find "$ECC_DIR/skills" -name '*.md' 2>/dev/null | wc -l | tr -d ' ') skills)"
218.     else
219.         error "Failed to fetch ECC repository"
220.         return 1
221.     fi
222. 
223.     # Copy your learned skills from conversation history
224.     mkdir -p "$ECC_DIR/skills/learned"
225.     if [ -d "$CLAUDE_DIR/skills/learned" ]; then
226.         cp -r "$CLAUDE_DIR/skills/learned"/* "$ECC_DIR/skills/learned/" 2>/dev/null || true
227.         success "Your conversation skills copied"
228.     fi
229.     if [ -d "$CLAUDE_DIR/skills/research" ]; then
230.         cp -r "$CLAUDE_DIR/skills/research"/* "$ECC_DIR/skills/research/" 2>/dev/null || true
231.     fi
232. }
233. 
234. # =============================================================================
235. # Step 2: Fetch curated skills (Anthropic Official + Codex)
236. # =============================================================================
237. 
238. install_curated_skills() {
239.     if [ "$WITH_CURATED" = false ]; then
240.         log "Skipping curated skills (use --with-curated to add)"
241.         return 0
242.     fi
243. 
244.     log "Step 2: Installing curated skills..."
245. 
246.     # Remove existing curated installation
247.     if [ -d "$CURATED_DIR" ]; then
248.         rm -rf "$CURATED_DIR"
249.     fi
250.     mkdir -p "$CURATED_DIR"
```

</details>

100.             ;;

114. 
115. echo ""
116. echo "╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗"

131. 
132. # =============================================================================
133. # Step 0a: Bootstrap prerequisites (git, node/npm, ollama, python3)
134. # =============================================================================
135. section "Bootstrap"
136. 
137. _install_if_missing() {
138.     local cmd="$1"
139.     local name="$2"
140.     local install_cmd="$3"
141.     if command -v "$cmd" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
142.         success "$name already installed: $(command -v "$cmd")"
143.         return 0
144.     fi
145.     log "Installing $name..."
146.     if eval "$install_cmd"; then
147.         success "$name installed"
148.         return 0
149.     else
150.         warn "$name installation failed — install manually, then re-run"
151.         return 1
152.     fi
153. }
154. 
155. # --- git ---
156. if [ "$OS_TYPE" = "macOS" ]; then
157.     _install_if_missing git "git" "brew install git"
158. else
159.     if command -v apt-get >/dev/null 2>&1; then
160.         _install_if_missing git "git" "sudo apt-get update -qq && sudo apt-get install -y -qq git"
161.     elif command -v dnf >/dev/null 2>&1; then
162.         _install_if_missing git "git" "sudo dnf install -y git"
163.     elif command -v pacman >/dev/null 2>&1; then
164.         _install_if_missing git "git" "sudo pacman -S --noconfirm git"
165.     fi
166. fi
167. 
168. # --- node + npm ---
169. if ! command -v node >/dev/null 2>&1 || ! command -v npm >/dev/null 2>&1; then
170.     log "Node.js/npm not found — installing..."
171.     if [ "$OS_TYPE" = "macOS" ]; then
172.         brew install node 2>/dev/null || warn "brew install node failed"
173.     else
174.         # Use NodeSource setup for LTS Node 22
175.         curl -fsSL https://deb.nodesource.com/setup_22.x | bash - 2>/dev/null && \
176.             apt-get install -y nodejs 2>/dev/null || \
177.             warn "NodeSource install failed — install node manually"
178.     fi
179.     # Verify
180.     if command -v node >/dev/null 2>&1 && command -v npm >/dev/null 2>&1; then
181.         success "Node.js $(node --version) + npm $(npm --version) installed"
182.     else
183.         warn "Node.js/npm still not available — npx-based MCP servers won't work"
184.     fi
185. else
186.     success "Node.js $(node --version) + npm $(npm --version) already installed"
187. fi
188. 
189. # --- ollama ---
190. if ! command -v ollama >/dev/null 2>&1; then
191.     log "Ollama not found — installing..."
192.     curl -fsSL https://ollama.com/install.sh | sh && \
193.         success "Ollama installed" || \
194.         warn "Ollama install failed — install manually from https://ollama.com"
195. else
196.     success "Ollama already installed: $(command -v ollama)"
197. fi
198. 
199. # --- python3 + pip ---
200. if ! command -v python3 >/dev/null 2>&1; then
201.     log "python3 not found — installing..."
202.     if [ "$OS_TYPE" = "macOS" ]; then
203.         brew install python3 2>/dev/null || warn "brew install python3 failed"
204.     else
205.         if command -v apt-get >/dev/null 2>&1; then
206.             sudo apt-get update -qq && sudo apt-get install -y -qq python3 python3-pip python3-venv
207.         elif command -v dnf >/dev/null 2>&1; then
208.             sudo dnf install -y python3 python3-pip
209.         elif command -v pacman >/dev/null 2>&1; then
210.             sudo pacman -S --noconfirm python python-pip
211.         fi
212.     fi
213. fi
214. if command -v python3 >/dev/null 2>&1; then
215.     success "python3: $(command -v python3)"
216. else
217.     error "python3 is required but could not be installed automatically"
218. fi
219. 
220. # =============================================================================
221. # Step 0: Preflight checks
222. # =============================================================================
223. section "Preflight"
224. 
225. command -v python3 >/dev/null 2>&1 || error "python3 required ($PKG_HINT python3)"
226. 
227. # Install Python dependencies for auto-learning pipeline
228. log "Installing Python dependencies for skill extraction (sentence-transformers, scikit-learn)..."
229. _install_python_deps() {
230.     local pkgs="sentence-transformers>=3.0.0 scikit-learn>=1.5.0"
231.     # Detect virtualenv: --user is forbidden inside venv/conda
232.     local in_venv=""
233.     if python3 -c "import sys; sys.exit(0 if (sys.prefix != sys.base_prefix) else 1)" 2>/dev/null; then
234.         in_venv=1
235.     fi
236.     if [ -n "$in_venv" ]; then

241.         fi

1. #!/bin/bash
2. # =============================================================================
3. # verify.sh — Verify the full agent harness setup is working
4. # =============================================================================
5. # Checks all components: Ollama, Claude Code MCP, OpenClaw, Pi, Codex,
6. # skills layer, and network/proxy status.
7. #
8. # Usage:
9. #   scripts/verify.sh [--fix]
10. #   --fix : attempt to auto-fix issues found
11. # =============================================================================
12. 
13. REPO_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")/.." && pwd)"
14. 
15. # Platform detection
16. case "$(uname -s)" in
17.     Darwin*)                 OS_TYPE="macOS" ;;
18.     Linux*)                  OS_TYPE="Linux" ;;
19.     MINGW*|MSYS*|CYGWIN*)   OS_TYPE="Windows" ;;
20.     *)                       OS_TYPE="Unknown" ;;
21. esac
22. 
23. # Detect user's default shell
24. case "${SHELL##*/}" in
25.     zsh)  USER_SHELL="zsh";  PRIMARY_RC="$HOME/.zshrc" ;;
26.     bash) USER_SHELL="bash"; PRIMARY_RC="$HOME/.bashrc" ;;
27.     *)    USER_SHELL="bash"; PRIMARY_RC="$HOME/.bashrc" ;;
28. esac
29. 
30. BLUE='\033[0;34m'; GREEN='\033[0;32m'; YELLOW='\033[1;33m'; RED='\033[0;31m'; CYAN='\033[0;36m'; NC='\033[0m'
31. ok()   { echo -e "  ${GREEN}✓${NC} $1"; }
32. fail() { echo -e "  ${RED}✗${NC} $1"; FAIL_COUNT=$((FAIL_COUNT+1)); }
33. warn() { echo -e "  ${YELLOW}⚠${NC} $1"; WARN_COUNT=$((WARN_COUNT+1)); }
34. section() { echo ""; echo -e "${CYAN}── $1 ──${NC}"; }
35. 
36. FAIL_COUNT=0; WARN_COUNT=0
37. FIX=false
38. [[ "$*" == *"--fix"* ]] && FIX=true
39. 
40. echo ""
41. echo "╔══════════════════════════════════════╗"
42. echo "║   Agent Harness Verification         ║"
43. echo "╚══════════════════════════════════════╝"
44. 
45. # ── Ollama ──
46. section "Ollama"
47. if command -v ollama >/dev/null 2>&1; then
48.     ok "ollama binary found: $(command -v ollama)"
49. else
50.     fail "ollama not found — install from https://ollama.com"
51. fi
52. 
53. if curl -s --max-time 3 http://localhost:11434/api/status >/dev/null 2>&1; then
54.     ok "Ollama server running (localhost:11434)"
55.     MODELS=$(ollama list 2>/dev/null | tail -n +2 | awk '{print $1}' | tr '\n' ' ')
56.     ok "Models: $MODELS"
57. else
58.     fail "Ollama server not running — start it: ollama serve"
59. fi
60. 
61. # Check config
62. if [ -f "$HOME/.ollama/config.json" ]; then
63.     ok "~/.ollama/config.json present"
64.     LAST_SEL=$(python3 -c "import json; d=json.load(open('$HOME/.ollama/config.json')); print(d.get('last_selection','?'))" 2>/dev/null)
65.     ok "  Last integration: $LAST_SEL"
66. else
67.     warn "~/.ollama/config.json not found"
68. fi
69. 
70. # ── Claude Code ──
71. section "Claude Code (ollama launch claude)"
72. if command -v claude >/dev/null 2>&1; then
73.     ok "claude binary found"
74. else
75.     fail "claude not found — install Claude Code"
76. fi
77. 
78. if [ -f "$HOME/.claude.json" ]; then
79.     ok "~/.claude.json present"
80.     MCP_COUNT=$(python3 -c "import json; d=json.load(open('$HOME/.claude.json')); print(len(d.get('mcpServers',{})))" 2>/dev/null)
81.     if [ "$MCP_COUNT" -gt 0 ] 2>/dev/null; then
82.         ok "MCP servers configured: $MCP_COUNT"
83.         MCP_NAMES=$(python3 -c "import json; d=json.load(open('$HOME/.claude.json')); print(' '.join(d.get('mcpServers',{}).keys()))" 2>/dev/null)
84.         ok "  Servers: $MCP_NAMES"
85.     else
86.         fail "No MCP servers configured in ~/.claude.json"
87.         if $FIX; then
88.             echo "    → Running setup-mcp.sh..."
89.             "$REPO_DIR/scripts/setup-mcp.sh" && ok "Fixed: MCP servers applied"
90.         else
91.             echo "    → Fix: scripts/setup-mcp.sh"
92.         fi
93.     fi
94. else
95.     fail "~/.claude.json not found — run Claude Code once to initialize"
96. fi
97. 
98. if [ -d "$HOME/.claude/skills-cache" ]; then
99.     if [ -f "$HOME/.claude/skills-cache/lean-skills.txt" ]; then
100.         LEAN_SIZE=$(wc -c < "$HOME/.claude/skills-cache/lean-skills.txt" | tr -d ' ')
101.         LEAN_LINES=$(wc -l < "$HOME/.claude/skills-cache/lean-skills.txt" | tr -d ' ')
102.         ok "lean-skills.txt: ${LEAN_SIZE} bytes (${LEAN_LINES} lines) — injected at session start"
103.     else
104.         fail "lean-skills.txt missing — run: ./safe-install.sh"
105.         if $FIX; then
106.             "$REPO_DIR/safe-install.sh" && ok "Fixed: skills cache rebuilt"
107.         else
108.             echo "    → Fix: ./safe-install.sh"
109.         fi
110.     fi
111.     if [ -f "$HOME/.claude/skills-cache/combined-skills.txt" ]; then
112.         COMBINED_SIZE=$(wc -c < "$HOME/.claude/skills-cache/combined-skills.txt" | tr -d ' ')
113.         ok "combined-skills.txt: ${COMBINED_SIZE} bytes (full library — available for reference)"
114.     else
115.         warn "combined-skills.txt missing — run: ./safe-install.sh (non-critical)"
116.     fi
117. else
118.     fail "Skills cache missing (~/.claude/skills-cache/) — run: ./safe-install.sh"
119.     if $FIX; then
120.         "$REPO_DIR/safe-install.sh" && ok "Fixed: skills cache created"
121.     else
122.         echo "    → Fix: ./safe-install.sh"
123.     fi
124. fi
125. 
126. # Check learn-sync scripts are in place
127. if [ -f "$HOME/.claude/scripts/sync-learned-skills.sh" ]; then
128.     ok "~/.claude/scripts/sync-learned-skills.sh present"
129. else
130.     warn "~/.claude/scripts/sync-learned-skills.sh missing — learn-sync alias won't work"
131.     if $FIX; then
132.         "$REPO_DIR/safe-install.sh" && ok "Fixed: scripts copied"
133.     else
134.         echo "    → Fix: ./safe-install.sh (will copy scripts to ~/.claude/scripts/)"
135.     fi
136. fi
137. 
138. # ── OpenClaw ──
139. section "OpenClaw (ollama launch openclaw)"
140. if command -v openclaw >/dev/null 2>&1; then
141.     ok "openclaw binary found"
142. else
143.     warn "openclaw not found — install: ollama launch openclaw --config"
144. fi
145. 
146. if [ -f "$HOME/.openclaw/openclaw.json" ]; then
147.     ok "~/.openclaw/openclaw.json present"
148.     python3 << 'PYEOF'
149. import json, os
150. cfg = json.load(open(os.path.expanduser("~/.openclaw/openclaw.json")))
151. web_fetch = cfg.get("tools",{}).get("web",{}).get("fetch",{}).get("enabled", False)
152. web_search = cfg.get("tools",{}).get("web",{}).get("search",{}).get("enabled", False)
153. ollama_plugin = cfg.get("plugins",{}).get("entries",{}).get("ollama",{}).get("enabled", False)
154. model = cfg.get("agents",{}).get("defaults",{}).get("model",{}).get("primary","?")
155. gw_port = cfg.get("gateway",{}).get("port", "?")
156. print(f"  Model:         {model}")
157. print(f"  Web fetch:     {'✓ enabled' if web_fetch else '✗ disabled'}")
158. print(f"  Web search:    {'✓ enabled' if web_search else '✗ disabled'}")
159. print(f"  Ollama plugin: {'✓ enabled' if ollama_plugin else '✗ disabled'}")
160. print(f"  Gateway port:  {gw_port}")
161. PYEOF
162. else
163.     warn "~/.openclaw/openclaw.json not found"
164.     if $FIX; then
165.         echo "    → Running setup-openclaw.sh..."
166.         "$REPO_DIR/scripts/setup-openclaw.sh" && ok "Fixed: OpenClaw config applied"
167.     else
168.         echo "    → Fix: scripts/setup-openclaw.sh"
169.     fi
170. fi
171. 
172. # Check gateway running
173. if curl -s --max-time 2 http://localhost:18789/ 2>/dev/null | grep -q "OpenClaw"; then
174.     ok "OpenClaw gateway: running (port 18789)"
175. else
176.     warn "OpenClaw gateway not running (start: ollama launch openclaw)"
177. fi
178. 
179. # ── Pi ──
180. section "Pi (ollama launch pi)"
181. if command -v pi >/dev/null 2>&1; then
182.     ok "pi binary found"
183. else
184.     warn "pi not found — install Pi agent"
185. fi
186. 
187. if [ -f "$HOME/.pi/agent/settings.json" ]; then
188.     ok "~/.pi/agent/settings.json present"
189.     python3 << 'PYEOF'
190. import json, os
191. s = json.load(open(os.path.expanduser("~/.pi/agent/settings.json")))
192. print(f"  Model:    {s.get('defaultModel','?')}")
193. print(f"  Provider: {s.get('defaultProvider','?')}")
194. print(f"  Packages: {s.get('packages',[])}")
195. PYEOF
196.     SKILL_COUNT=$(ls "$HOME/.pi/agent/skills/" 2>/dev/null | wc -l | tr -d ' ')
197.     ok "Pi skills directory: $SKILL_COUNT skills linked"
198. else
199.     warn "~/.pi/agent/settings.json not found"
200. fi
201. 
202. # ── Codex ──
203. section "Codex (ollama launch codex)"
204. if command -v codex >/dev/null 2>&1; then
205.     ok "codex binary found"
206. else
207.     warn "codex not found — install Codex CLI"
208. fi
209. 
210. if [ -f "$HOME/.codex/config.toml" ]; then
211.     ok "~/.codex/config.toml present"
212.     PROVIDER=$(grep "model_provider" "$HOME/.codex/config.toml" | head -1 | cut -d'"' -f2)
213.     MODEL=$(grep "^model = " "$HOME/.codex/config.toml" | cut -d'"' -f2)
214.     ok "  Provider: $PROVIDER | Model: $MODEL"
215. else
216.     warn "~/.codex/config.toml not found"
217. fi
218. 
219. # ── Skills Layer ──
220. section "Shell Skills Layer"
221. 
222. # Check all possible RC files, but primary is based on user's shell
223. skills_found=false
224. for rc_file in "$PRIMARY_RC" "$HOME/.zshrc" "$HOME/.bashrc" "$HOME/.bash_profile" "$HOME/.profile"; do
225.     if [ -f "$rc_file" ] && grep -q "_claude_with_skills\|# Skills Layer" "$rc_file" 2>/dev/null; then
226.         ok "Skills layer in $rc_file"
227.         skills_found=true
228.     fi
229. done
230. if ! $skills_found; then
231.     fail "Skills layer not installed in any shell rc file"
232.     if $FIX; then
233.         echo "    → Running safe-install.sh..."
234.         "$REPO_DIR/safe-install.sh" && ok "Fixed: Skills layer installed"
235.     else
236.         echo "    → Fix: ./safe-install.sh"
237.     fi
238. fi
239. 
240. # Check aliases in the primary rc file
241. for alias_name in claude openclaw codex ollama pi; do
242.     if grep -q "alias $alias_name=" "$PRIMARY_RC" 2>/dev/null; then
243.         ok "alias '$alias_name' configured in $(basename "$PRIMARY_RC")"
244.     elif grep -q "alias $alias_name=" "$HOME/.zshrc" "$HOME/.bashrc" 2>/dev/null; then
245.         ok "alias '$alias_name' configured (in alternate rc)"
246.     else
247.         warn "alias '$alias_name' not found in shell rc files"
248.     fi
249. done
250. 
251. # ── Beads ──
252. section "Beads (bd)"
253. if command -v bd >/dev/null 2>&1; then
254.     ok "bd binary found: $(command -v bd)"
255. else
256.     warn "bd not found — run: scripts/setup-beads.sh"
257.     if $FIX; then
258.         echo "    → Running setup-beads.sh..."
259.         "$REPO_DIR/scripts/setup-beads.sh" && ok "Fixed: beads installed"
260.     else
261.         echo "    → Fix: scripts/setup-beads.sh"
262.     fi
263. fi
264. 
265. if command -v beads-mcp >/dev/null 2>&1; then
266.     ok "beads-mcp binary found: $(command -v beads-mcp)"
267. else
268.     warn "beads-mcp not found — MCP integration unavailable (run: scripts/setup-beads.sh)"
269. fi
270. 
271. # Check beads MCP entry in Claude Code config
272. if [ -f "$HOME/.claude.json" ]; then
273.     if python3 -c "import json; d=json.load(open('$HOME/.claude.json')); exit(0 if 'beads' in d.get('mcpServers',{}) else 1)" 2>/dev/null; then
274.         ok "beads MCP registered in ~/.claude.json"
275.     else
276.         warn "beads not in ~/.claude.json mcpServers — run: scripts/setup-beads.sh"
277.     fi
278. fi
279. 
280. # ── Network / Proxy ──
281. section "Network & Proxy"
282. 
283. if [ "$OS_TYPE" = "macOS" ]; then
284.     PROXY_HTTP=$(scutil --proxy 2>/dev/null | grep "HTTPEnable" | awk '{print $3}')
285.     PROXY_HTTPS=$(scutil --proxy 2>/dev/null | grep "HTTPSEnable" | awk '{print $3}')
286. 
287.     if [ "$PROXY_HTTP" = "1" ] || [ "$PROXY_HTTPS" = "1" ]; then
288.         warn "System HTTP/HTTPS proxy is ENABLED — may intercept Ollama streams"
289.         echo "    → Fix: scripts/disable-zscaler.sh"
290.     else
291.         ok "No system HTTP/HTTPS proxy (Zscaler off)"
292.     fi
293. 
294.     if launchctl list 2>/dev/null | grep -q "zscaler"; then
295.         warn "Zscaler tray still running"
296.         echo "    → Fix: scripts/disable-zscaler.sh --tray"
297.     else
298.         ok "Zscaler tray: not running"
299.     fi
300. else
301.     # Linux/Windows: check environment proxy variables
302.     if [ -n "$http_proxy" ] || [ -n "$https_proxy" ] || [ -n "$HTTP_PROXY" ] || [ -n "$HTTPS_PROXY" ]; then
303.         warn "HTTP proxy env vars set — may intercept Ollama streams"
304.         [ -n "$http_proxy" ] && echo "    http_proxy=$http_proxy"
305.         [ -n "$https_proxy" ] && echo "    https_proxy=$https_proxy"
306.     else
307.         ok "No proxy env vars set"
308.     fi
309. fi
310. 
311. if curl -s --max-time 5 -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" https://ollama.com 2>/dev/null | grep -q "200\|301\|302"; then
312.     ok "ollama.com reachable"
313. else
314.     warn "ollama.com not reachable — cloud models may fail"
315. fi
316. 
317. # ── Summary ──
318. echo ""
319. echo "════════════════════════════════════════"
320. if [ $FAIL_COUNT -eq 0 ] && [ $WARN_COUNT -eq 0 ]; then
321.     echo -e "${GREEN}✓ All checks passed!${NC}"
322. elif [ $FAIL_COUNT -eq 0 ]; then
323.     echo -e "${YELLOW}✓ Passed with $WARN_COUNT warning(s) — review above${NC}"
324. else
325.     echo -e "${RED}✗ $FAIL_COUNT failure(s), $WARN_COUNT warning(s) — run with --fix or check above${NC}"
326. fi
327. echo ""
328.
```

</details>

462. #!/bin/bash
463. # Load combined skills - file-based approach to avoid environment limits
464. # SKILLS_CONTENT available via: \$(cat ~/.claude/skills-cache/combined-skills.txt)
465. COMBINED_SKILLS_FILE='$COMBINED_FILE'
466. LOADER
467. 
468.     chmod +x "$SKILLS_CACHE_DIR/load-skills.sh"
469.     success "Loader created: $SKILLS_CACHE_DIR/load-skills.sh"
470.     # Lean cache: personal learned skills only (injected into Anthropic Opus sessions)
471.     if ls "$CLAUDE_DIR/skills/learned"/*.md >/dev/null 2>&1; then
472.         cat "$CLAUDE_DIR/skills/learned"/*.md > "$SKILLS_CACHE_DIR/lean-skills.txt"
473.         success "Lean cache created: $SKILLS_CACHE_DIR/lean-skills.txt (personal skills only — 98% fewer tokens)"
474.     else
475.         > "$SKILLS_CACHE_DIR/lean-skills.txt"
476.         warn "No learned skills found — lean cache is empty"
477.     fi
478. }
479. 
480. # =============================================================================
481. # Step 4: Add aliases to shell rc files (idempotent)
482. # =============================================================================
483. 
484. # Generate the skills block to add to shell rc
485. generate_skills_block() {
486.     cat << 'SKILLS_BLOCK'
487. 
488. # Skills Layer - SAFE Install (Everything Claude Code)
489. # All default commands now include skills (ECC + Anthropic Official + Curated + conversation-derived)
490. # To disable: run ~/.claude/scripts/safe-install.sh --uninstall
491. 
492. # Helper function to inject skills as system prompt (uses file to avoid arg length limits)
493. # Uses lean-skills.txt (personal learned skills only, ~1-2K tokens) instead of
494. # combined-skills.txt (~1.4M tokens — exceeds Claude's 200K context window).
495. # The full 450+ skill library loads natively via ~/.claude/skills/ (/skills command).
496. _claude_with_skills() {
497.     local _skills_file="/tmp/claude-skills-$$.txt"
498.     cat ~/.claude/skills-cache/lean-skills.txt > "$_skills_file" 2>/dev/null
499.     if [ -s "$_skills_file" ]; then
500.         (unset SKILLS_CONTENT CODEX_SYSTEM_PROMPT OPENCLAW_SYSTEM_PROMPT; command claude --append-system-prompt-file "$_skills_file" "$@")
501.     else
502.         (unset SKILLS_CONTENT CODEX_SYSTEM_PROMPT OPENCLAW_SYSTEM_PROMPT; command claude "$@")
503.     fi
504.     rm -f "$_skills_file"
505. }
506. 
507. _openclaw_with_skills() {
508.     # OpenClaw natively loads workspace/skills/ SKILL.md files — no extra env var needed.
509.     # OPENCLAW_SYSTEM_PROMPT_FILE is NOT a real openclaw env var (not in source).
510.     (unset SKILLS_CONTENT CODEX_SYSTEM_PROMPT OPENCLAW_SYSTEM_PROMPT; command openclaw "$@")
511. }
512. 
513. _codex_with_skills() {
514.     # Codex loads skills natively from ~/.codex/skills/ (ECC skills symlinked there)
515.     (unset SKILLS_CONTENT OPENCLAW_SYSTEM_PROMPT; command codex "$@")
516. }
517. 
518. _ollama_with_skills() {
519.     # OLLAMA_SYSTEM_FILE is NOT set (breaks ollama launch codex/pi).
520.     # OPENCLAW_SYSTEM_PROMPT_FILE is not a real openclaw env var — removed.
521.     # OpenClaw loads ECC skills natively from ~/.openclaw/workspace/skills/ SKILL.md files.
522.     (unset SKILLS_CONTENT CODEX_SYSTEM_PROMPT OPENCLAW_SYSTEM_PROMPT OLLAMA_SYSTEM_FILE; command ollama "$@")
523. }
524. 
525. _pi_with_skills() {
526.     # Pi loads skills natively from ~/.pi/agent/skills/ (ECC skills symlinked there)
527.     (unset SKILLS_CONTENT CODEX_SYSTEM_PROMPT OPENCLAW_SYSTEM_PROMPT; command pi "$@")
528. }
529. 
530. _copilot_with_skills() {
531.     # Copilot CLI does NOT load ~/.claude/skills/ natively (different format).
532.     # It reads ~/.copilot/mcp-config.json for MCP servers and .github/copilot-instructions.md
533.     # for per-repo guidance. This wrapper ensures a clean environment.
534.     (unset SKILLS_CONTENT CODEX_SYSTEM_PROMPT OPENCLAW_SYSTEM_PROMPT; command copilot "$@")
535. }
536. 
537. # Wrapper aliases
538. alias claude='_claude_with_skills'
539. alias openclaw='_openclaw_with_skills'
540. alias codex='_codex_with_skills'
541. alias ollama='_ollama_with_skills'
542. alias pi='_pi_with_skills'
543. alias copilot='_copilot_with_skills'

549. alias learn-prune='bash ~/.claude/scripts/sync-learned-skills.sh --prune'

554. setup_shell_integration() {

560. 
561.     # Detect which shell rc files exist and should be updated.

563.     if [ -f "$HOME/.zshrc" ]; then

571.     if [ -f "$HOME/.bash_profile" ] && [ ${#shell_rcs[@]} -eq 0 ]; then

575.     fi
576.     if [ -f "$HOME/.profile" ] && [ ${#shell_rcs[@]} -eq 0 ]; then
577.         shell_rcs+=("$HOME/.profile")
578.         success "Found .profile"
579.     fi
580. 
581.     # If no rc files found, create the appropriate one for the user's actual shell
582.     if [ ${#shell_rcs[@]} -eq 0 ]; then
583.         local default_rc
584.         case "$USER_SHELL" in
585.             zsh)  default_rc="$HOME/.zshrc" ;;
586.             bash) default_rc="$HOME/.bashrc" ;;
587.             *)    default_rc="$HOME/.bashrc" ;;
588.         esac
589.         shell_rcs+=("$default_rc")
590.         warn "No shell rc found, will create $(basename "$default_rc")"
591.     fi
592. 
593.     local skills_block
594.     skills_block=$(generate_skills_block)
595. 
596.     for shell_rc in "${shell_rcs[@]}"; do
597.         log "Processing $shell_rc..."
598.

300. # =============================================================================
301. if command -v npm >/dev/null 2>&1; then
302.     log "Pre-installing MCP server packages globally (prevents first-launch timeouts)..."
303.     # Install silently in background; failures are non-fatal (npx will fallback)
304.     npm install -g @modelcontextprotocol/server-memory \
305.         @modelcontextprotocol/server-sequential-thinking \
306.         @upstash/context7-mcp@latest \
307.         @playwright/mcp \
308.         codesight \
309.         token-optimizer-mcp 2>/dev/null || warn "Some MCP packages failed to pre-install (npx fallback will work but may be slower on first launch)"
310.     success "MCP server packages pre-installed"
311. else
312.     warn "npm not found — MCP servers will be installed on first launch (may timeout)"
313. fi
314. 
315. # =============================================================================
316. # Harness installer helper
317. # =============================================================================
318. install_harness() {
319.     local name="$1"
320.     local cmd="$2"
321.     local install_cmd="$3"
322. 
323.     if command -v "$cmd" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
324.         success "$name already installed: $(command -v "$cmd")"
325.         return 0
326.     fi
327. 
328.     if ! command -v npm >/dev/null 2>&1; then
329.         warn "npm not found — cannot auto-install $name"
330.         warn "Install manually, then re-run install.sh"
331.         return 1
332.     fi
333. 
334.     log "Installing $name..."
335.     if eval "$install_cmd"; then
336.         success "$name installed"
337.         return 0
338.     else
339.         warn "$name installation failed — install manually, then re-run"
340.         return 1
341.     fi
342. }
343. 
344. # =============================================================================
345. # Step 1: Skills Layer (ECC + shell wrappers)
346. # =============================================================================
347. 
348. should_run() {
349.     [ -z "$ONLY_TARGET" ] || [ "$ONLY_TARGET" = "$1" ]
350. }
351. 
352. if ! $SKIP_SKILLS && should_run "skills"; then
353.     section "Skills Layer"
354.     # safe-install.sh may exit non-zero when 'source <rc>' fails in a non-interactive
355.     # subshell — this is expected and harmless. Capture exit code to report real failures.
356.     safe_exit=0
357.     SAFE_ARGS=""
358.     $WITH_SCIENCE && SAFE_ARGS="$SAFE_ARGS --with-science"
359.     $WITH_CURATED && SAFE_ARGS="$SAFE_ARGS --with-curated"
360.     $WITH_BIO && SAFE_ARGS="$SAFE_ARGS --with-bio"
361.     bash "$REPO_DIR/safe-install.sh" $SAFE_ARGS || safe_exit=$?
362.     if [ "$safe_exit" -eq 0 ]; then
363.         success "ECC skills + shell wrappers installed (run: source $SHELL_RC to activate)"
364.     else
365.         warn "safe-install.sh exited with code $safe_exit — shell integration may need manual source $SHELL_RC"
366.     fi
367.     # Rebuild cache with full skill counts (safe-install.sh caps Codex at 100).
368.     # update-ecc.sh also restores ECC working tree to actual HEAD.
369.     log "Rebuilding skills cache with full counts..."
370.     bash "$REPO_DIR/scripts/update-ecc.sh" --force || true
371.     success "Skills cache rebuilt (all sources, no caps)"
372. else
373.     log "Skipping skills layer"
374. fi
375. 
376. # =============================================================================
377. # Step 1b: Ollama integration→model mapping
378. # =============================================================================
379. if should_run "ollama" || [ -z "$ONLY_TARGET" ]; then
380.     section "Ollama Integrations"
381.     bash "$REPO_DIR/scripts/setup-ollama-config.sh" && success "Ollama integrations configured" || warn "Ollama config skipped (Ollama not started yet)"
382. fi
383. 
384. # =============================================================================
385. # Step 2: Claude Code MCP + Global Instructions
386. # =============================================================================
387. if should_run "claude"; then
388.     section "Claude Code"
389.     install_harness "Claude Code" "claude" "npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code"
390.     if [ -f "$HOME/.claude.json" ]; then
391.         bash "$REPO_DIR/scripts/setup-mcp.sh" && success "Claude Code MCP configured" || warn "MCP setup skipped (run Claude Code once, then re-run)"
392.     else
393.         warn "~/.claude.json not found — run Claude Code once to initialize, then re-run install.sh"
394.     fi
395.     # Global CLAUDE.md: proactive MCP usage + conciseness + token discipline
396.     bash "$REPO_DIR/scripts/setup-claude-md.sh" && success "Global CLAUDE.md installed" || warn "CLAUDE.md setup skipped"
397.     # Pre-tool-use hooks: enforce codesight before broad Glob/Grep searches
398.     bash "$REPO_DIR/scripts/setup-hooks.sh" && success "Claude Code hooks installed" || warn "Hooks setup skipped"
399.     
400.     # BMO-style learning capture hooks (real-time correction detection)
401.     bash "$REPO_DIR/scripts/setup-learning-hook.sh" && success "BMO learning capture hooks configured" || warn "Learning hook setup skipped"
402. fi
403. 
404. # =============================================================================
405. # Step 3: OpenClaw
406. # =============================================================================
407. if should_run "openclaw"; then
408.     section "OpenClaw"
409.     bash "$REPO_DIR/scripts/setup-openclaw.sh" --model "$OLLAMA_MODEL" && success "OpenClaw configured" || warn "OpenClaw setup skipped (not installed yet)"
410. fi
411. 
412. # =============================================================================
413. # Step 4: Codex
414. # =============================================================================
415. if should_run "codex"; then
416.     section "Codex"
417.     install_harness "Codex CLI" "codex" "npm install -g @openai/codex"
418.     bash "$REPO_DIR/scripts/setup-codex.sh" --model "$OLLAMA_MODEL" && success "Codex configured" || warn "Codex setup skipped (not installed yet)"
419. fi
420. 
421. # =============================================================================
422. # Step 5: Pi
423. # =============================================================================

426.     install_harness "Pi" "pi" "npm install -g @mariozechner/pi-coding-agent"
427.     bash "$REPO_DIR/scripts/setup-pi.sh" --model "$OLLAMA_MODEL" && success "Pi configured" || warn "Pi setup skipped (not installed yet)"
428. fi
429. 
430. # =============================================================================
431. # Step 6: Copilot CLI
432. # =============================================================================
433. if should_run "copilot"; then
434.     section "Copilot CLI"
435.     install_harness "Copilot CLI" "copilot" "npm install -g @github/copilot"
436.     bash "$REPO_DIR/scripts/setup-copilot.sh" && success "Copilot CLI MCP configured" || warn "Copilot setup skipped (not installed yet)"
437. fi
438. 
439. # =============================================================================

441. # =============================================================================
442. if should_run "beads"; then
443.     section "Beads"
444.     bash "$REPO_DIR/scripts/setup-beads.sh" && success "Beads configured" || warn "Beads setup skipped (non-fatal)"
445. fi
446. 
447. # =============================================================================
448. # Step 8: Cleanup stale/invalid skills from previous installs
449. # =============================================================================
450. if [ -f "$REPO_DIR/scripts/cleanup-invalid-skills.sh" ]; then

605.         fi
606. 
607.         # Add new entries
608.         echo "$skills_block" >> "$shell_rc"
609.         success "Shell integration added to $shell_rc"
610.     done
611. 
612.     # Determine which rc to tell the user to source
613.     local primary_rc
614.     case "$USER_SHELL" in
615.         zsh)  primary_rc="$HOME/.zshrc" ;;
616.         bash) primary_rc="$HOME/.bashrc" ;;
617.         *)    primary_rc="${shell_rcs[0]}" ;;
618.     esac
619. 
620.     # Source the current shell's rc file (may fail in non-interactive subshell — that's OK)
621.     # Use subshell to prevent set -e from catching failures inside sourced rc
622.     if [ -n "$ZSH_VERSION" ]; then
623.         (source "$HOME/.zshrc" 2>/dev/null) || true
624.         success "Shell reloaded (.zshrc)"
625.     elif [ -n "$BASH_VERSION" ]; then
626.         (source "$HOME/.bashrc" 2>/dev/null) || true
627.         success "Shell reloaded (.bashrc)"
628.     else
629.         warn "Run 'source $(basename "$primary_rc")' or restart terminal to activate"
630.     fi
631. }
632. 
633. # =============================================================================
634. # Step 4b: Copy learning pipeline scripts to ~/.claude/scripts/
635. # =============================================================================
636. # The generated shell aliases (learn-sync, learn-stats, etc.) reference
637. # ~/.claude/scripts/sync-learned-skills.sh. This step ensures those files exist.
638. 
639. copy_scripts_to_claude_dir() {
640.     local scripts_dest="$HOME/.claude/scripts"
641.     mkdir -p "$scripts_dest"
642. 
643.     local scripts_to_copy=(
644.         "sync-learned-skills.sh"
645.         "extract-conversation-skills.py"
646.         "safe-install.sh"
647.     )
648.     local copied=0
649.     for script in "${scripts_to_copy[@]}"; do
650.         local src="$REPO_DIR/$script"
651.         if [ -f "$src" ]; then
652.             cp "$src" "$scripts_dest/$script"
653.             chmod +x "$scripts_dest/$script" 2>/dev/null || true
654.             copied=$((copied + 1))
655.         fi
656.     done
657.     success "Copied $copied learning pipeline scripts to $scripts_dest/"
658. }
659. 
660. # =============================================================================
661. # Step 4c: Symlink ECC skills into native skills dirs for codex and pi
662. # =============================================================================
663. 
664. _migrate_flat_to_dir_skills() {
665.     # Migrate flat .md files to <name>/SKILL.md directory format.
666.     # Claude Code's /skills command only discovers directory-format skills.
667.     local skills_dir="$HOME/.claude/skills"
668.     [ ! -d "$skills_dir" ] && return 0
669.     local migrated=0
670.     for flat_file in "$skills_dir"/*.md; do
671.         [ ! -f "$flat_file" ] && continue
672.         local skill_name
673.         skill_name=$(basename "$flat_file" .md)
674.         # Skip learned/ subdirectory (uses flat .md by convention)
675.         [ "$skill_name" = "learned" ] && continue
676.         # Skip if directory already exists (already migrated)
677.         if [ -d "$skills_dir/$skill_name" ]; then
678.             rm -f "$flat_file"
679.             continue
680.         fi
681.         mkdir -p "$skills_dir/$skill_name"
682.         mv "$flat_file" "$skills_dir/$skill_name/SKILL.md"
683.         migrated=$((migrated + 1))
684.     done
685.     [ "$migrated" -gt 0 ] && success "Migrated $migrated flat .md skills to directory format"
686.     return 0
687. }
688. 
689. link_native_skills() {
690.     log "Step 4b: Linking ECC skills into native harness skill directories..."
691. 
692.     if [ ! -d "$ECC_DIR/skills" ]; then
693.         warn "ECC skills not installed, skipping native linking"
694.         return 0
695.     fi
696. 
697.     # Claude Code: ~/.claude/skills/<skill-name>/SKILL.md (directory format)
698.     # Claude Code's /skills command ONLY discovers directory-format skills,
699.     # NOT flat .md files. Migrate any stale flat files first.
700.     local claude_skills_dir="$HOME/.claude/skills"
701.     mkdir -p "$claude_skills_dir"
702.     _migrate_flat_to_dir_skills
703.     local claude_count=0
704.     for dir in "$ECC_DIR/skills"/*/; do
705.         skill_name=$(basename "$dir")
706.         # Use SKILL.md if it exists, otherwise find any .md
707.         local src_file=""
708.         if [ -f "$dir/SKILL.md" ]; then
709.             src_file="$dir/SKILL.md"
710.         else
711.             src_file=$(find "$dir" -maxdepth 1 -name '*.md' -type f 2>/dev/null | head -1)
712.         fi
713.         [ -z "$src_file" ] && continue
714.         local dst_dir="$claude_skills_dir/$skill_name"
715.         local dst="$dst_dir/SKILL.md"
716.         if [ ! -f "$dst" ]; then
717.             mkdir -p "$dst_dir"
718.             cp "$src_file" "$dst"
719.             claude_count=$((claude_count + 1))
720.         fi
721.     done
722.     local total_count=$(find "$claude_skills_dir" -mindepth 1 -maxdepth 1 -type d ! -name 'learned' 2>/dev/null | wc -l | tr -d ' ')
723.     if [ "$claude_count" -eq 0 ] && [ "$total_count" -gt 0 ]; then
724.         success "Claude Code: all ECC skills already in ~/.claude/skills/ ($total_count total)"
725.     else
726.         success "Claude Code: $claude_count ECC skills installed to ~/.claude/skills/ ($total_count total)"
727.     fi
728. 
729.     # Codex: handled by update-ecc.sh which properly sanitizes YAML (symlinks
730.     # for clean skills, sanitized copies for skills with block scalars/extra fields).
731.     # safe-install.sh used to skip invalid skills entirely — update-ecc.sh fixes them.
732.     if [ -d "$HOME/.codex/skills" ]; then
733.         log "Codex skills will be synced by update-ecc.sh (proper YAML sanitization)"
734.     else
735.         warn "~/.codex/skills not found — install Codex CLI first"
736.     fi
737. 
738.     # OpenClaw: ~/.openclaw/workspace/skills/<skill-name>/ (real copies, NOT symlinks)
739.     # Must be real files inside workspace root - OpenClaw rejects symlinks escaping the workspace
740.     # Skills appear with source="openclaw-workspace" and status="✓ ready"
741.     if [ -d "$HOME/.openclaw/workspace" ]; then
742.         local openclaw_count=0
743.         local ws_skills="$HOME/.openclaw/workspace/skills"
744.         mkdir -p "$ws_skills"
745.         for dir in "$ECC_DIR/skills"/*/; do
746.             skill_name=$(basename "$dir")
747.             [ ! -f "$dir/SKILL.md" ] && continue
748.             grep -q '^description: *[>|]' "$dir/SKILL.md" 2>/dev/null && continue
749.             if grep -E '^[a-z_]+:' "$dir/SKILL.md" 2>/dev/null | grep -qvE '^(name|description|origin|tools):'; then
750.                 continue
```

</details>

<sub>⏱️ 28m 18s</sub>

### ✅ `view`

**view the file at /Users/chazh/Documents/GitHub/ai-skillweave/hooks/codesight-redirect.sh.**

/Users/chazh/Documents/GitHub/ai-skillweave/hooks/codesight-redirect.sh

<details>
<summary>85 lines</summary>

```
1. #!/bin/bash
2. # =============================================================================
3. # codesight-redirect.sh — PreToolUse hook
4. # =============================================================================
5. # Intercepts broad Glob/Grep codebase searches when codesight is configured.
6. # Reminds Claude to call codesight_get_summary first (once per session/project).
7. #
8. # Exit 2 = block tool + send message to Claude
9. # Exit 0 = allow tool to proceed
10. # =============================================================================
11. 
12. input=$(cat)
13. 
14. # Extract tool name, pattern, and path from hook payload
15. tool=$(echo "$input" | python3 -c "
16. import json, sys
17. d = json.load(sys.stdin)
18. print(d.get('tool_name', ''))
19. " 2>/dev/null)
20. 
21. session_id=$(echo "$input" | python3 -c "
22. import json, sys
23. d = json.load(sys.stdin)
24. print(d.get('session_id', 'default')[:24])
25. " 2>/dev/null || echo "default")
26. 
27. pattern=$(echo "$input" | python3 -c "
28. import json, sys
29. inp = json.load(sys.stdin).get('tool_input', {})
30. print(inp.get('pattern', '') or inp.get('glob', ''))
31. " 2>/dev/null)
32. 
33. path=$(echo "$input" | python3 -c "
34. import json, sys
35. inp = json.load(sys.stdin).get('tool_input', {})
36. print(inp.get('path', '') or inp.get('file_path', ''))
37. " 2>/dev/null)
38. 
39. # Only intercept broad searches (** glob patterns indicating codebase exploration)
40. is_broad=false
41. [[ "$pattern" == *"**"* ]] && is_broad=true
42. [[ -z "$path" && "$tool" == "Glob" ]] && is_broad=true
43. $is_broad || exit 0
44. 
45. # Walk up from search path to find nearest .codesight/ directory
46. check_dir="${path:-$(pwd)}"
47. [[ -f "$check_dir" ]] && check_dir=$(dirname "$check_dir")
48. codesight_dir=""
49. depth=0
50. while [[ "$check_dir" != "/" && $depth -lt 10 ]]; do
51.     if [[ -d "$check_dir/.codesight" ]]; then
52.         codesight_dir="$check_dir"
53.         break
54.     fi
55.     check_dir=$(dirname "$check_dir")
56.     ((depth++))
57. done
58. 
59. # No codesight configured — allow through
60. [[ -n "$codesight_dir" ]] || exit 0
61. 
62. # Only remind once per session per project (state tracked in /tmp)
63. state_dir="/tmp/claude-hooks-${session_id}"
64. mkdir -p "$state_dir"
65. # Hash the project dir for a short key
66. state_key=$(printf '%s' "$codesight_dir" | md5 -q 2>/dev/null || printf '%s' "$codesight_dir" | md5sum 2>/dev/null | awk '{print $1}' || printf '%s' "$codesight_dir" | cksum | awk '{print $1}')
67. state_key="${state_key:0:8}"
68. reminded_file="$state_dir/codesight-${state_key}.done"
69. 
70. # Already reminded this session — allow through
71. [[ -f "$reminded_file" ]] && exit 0
72. 
73. touch "$reminded_file"
74. 
75. cat << EOF
76. CODESIGHT REDIRECT: $codesight_dir has codesight configured.
77. 
78. Before running broad $tool searches, call codesight_get_summary first:
79.   mcp__codesight__codesight_get_summary(directory="$codesight_dir")
80. 
81. This returns a compact project map (~500 tokens) — routes, schema, hot files, env vars.
82. After calling codesight_get_summary, run targeted searches as needed (this reminder won't repeat).
83. EOF
84. exit 2
85.
```

</details>

926.     fi
927. }
928. 
929. # =============================================================================
930. # Step 5: Uninstall
931. # =============================================================================
932. 
933. uninstall() {
934.     log "Uninstalling skills layer..."
935. 
936.     # Remove shell integration from all possible rc files
937.     for rc_file in "$HOME/.zshrc" "$HOME/.bashrc" "$HOME/.bash_profile" "$HOME/.profile"; do
938.         if [ -f "$rc_file" ]; then
939.             if grep -q "# Skills Layer" "$rc_file" 2>/dev/null; then
940.                 sed -i.bak '/# Skills Layer/,/# End Skills Layer/d' "$rc_file" 2>/dev/null || true
941.                 success "Removed shell integration from $rc_file"
942.             fi
943.         fi
944.     done
945. 
946.     # Remove installed skill library clones
947.     rm -rf "$ECC_DIR"
948.     rm -rf "$CURATED_DIR"
949.     rm -rf "$SCIENCE_DIR"
950.     rm -rf "$CLAWBIO_DIR"
951.     rm -rf "$SKILLS_CACHE_DIR"
952. 
953.     # Remove only ECC-originated skills from Claude Code native skills dir.
954.     # Does NOT rm -rf ~/.claude/skills to protect user-created and learned skills.
955.     if [ -d "$HOME/.claude/skills" ]; then
956.         local removed_count=0
957.         # Remove symlinks (Pi/ECC-style) and dirs we installed
958.         for entry in "$HOME/.claude/skills"/*/; do
959.             [ -L "$entry" ] && rm "$entry" && removed_count=$((removed_count + 1)) && continue
960.             [ -f "${entry}SKILL.md" ] && rm -rf "$entry" && removed_count=$((removed_count + 1))
961.         done
962.         success "Removed $removed_count installed skills from ~/.claude/skills/"
963.         # Remove the learned/ subdir only if user explicitly asks
964.         # (learned skills are personal — never auto-delete)
965.     fi
966. 
967.     # Remove copied learning pipeline scripts
968.     local scripts_dest="$HOME/.claude/scripts"
969.     for script in "sync-learned-skills.sh" "extract-conversation-skills.py" "safe-install.sh"; do
970.         [ -f "$scripts_dest/$script" ] && rm "$scripts_dest/$script" && success "Removed $scripts_dest/$script"
971.     done
972.     # Remove scripts dir if now empty
973.     rmdir "$scripts_dest" 2>/dev/null || true
974. 
975.     success "Uninstall complete"
976.     echo ""
977.     echo "Note: ~/.claude/skills/learned/ (personal learned skills) was preserved."
978.     echo "Run 'source ~/.zshrc' or 'source ~/.bashrc' or restart terminal to fully clean up."
979. }
980. 
981. # =============================================================================
982. # Step 6: Show usage
983. # =============================================================================
984. 
985. show_usage() {
986.     # Count skills
987.     ECC_COUNT=0
988.     ANTHROPIC_COUNT=0
989.     CODEX_COUNT=0
990.     SCIENCE_COUNT=0
991.     BIO_COUNT=0

994.     fi

1000.     fi

/Users/chazh/Documents/GitHub/ai-skillweave/configs/global-claude-md.md (lines 1-60)

<details>
<summary>54 lines</summary>

```
1. # Global Instructions
2. 
3. ## Output Style
4. - Be concise. No summaries of what you just did — the user can see the diff.
5. - No trailing recaps, status tables, or "here's what happened" blocks unless asked.
6. - When editing files, show the change, not a paragraph explaining the change.
7. - Prefer 1-2 sentences over bullet lists. Skip pleasantries.
8. 
9. ## MCP Tool Usage — USE THESE PROACTIVELY
10. 
11. You have MCP servers available. Use them INSTEAD of raw built-in tools when applicable:
12. 
13. ### codesight (USE FIRST when exploring any repo)
14. - BEFORE using Grep/Glob/Read to explore a codebase, call `codesight_get_summary` or `codesight_scan` to get a compact map of routes, schema, components, and dependencies.
15. - Use `codesight_get_routes` instead of grepping for API endpoints.
16. - Use `codesight_get_schema` instead of reading model files one by one.
17. - Use `codesight_get_hot_files` to find high-impact files before exploring.
18. - This saves tokens: one codesight call (~500 tokens) replaces 10+ file reads (~5000+ tokens).
19. 
20. ### token-optimizer (USE for large contexts)
21. - When context is growing large (long conversations, many file reads), use `optimize_session` or `compress_text` to reduce token usage.
22. - Use `smart_read` instead of raw Read for large files — it returns only the relevant parts.
23. - Use `smart_grep` instead of raw Grep — it deduplicates and compresses results.
24. - Use `smart_diff` instead of raw git diff — it summarizes changes compactly.
25. 
26. ### context7 (USE for library/framework questions)
27. - When the user asks about ANY library, framework, or API, call `resolve-library-id` then `query-docs` BEFORE answering from training data.
28. - Your training data may be outdated. Context7 has current docs.
29. 
30. ### exa-web-search (USE for current information)
31. - When the user asks about anything that may have changed since your training cutoff, search first.
32. 
33. ### memory (USE for cross-session continuity)
34. - Save important context that should persist across conversations.
35. 
36. ### sequential-thinking (USE for complex reasoning)
37. - For multi-step problems, architecture decisions, or debugging — use this to structure your thinking.
38. 
39. ## Token Discipline
40. - Do NOT read entire files when you only need a few lines. Use line ranges.
41. - Do NOT re-read files you just edited — the harness tracks file state.
42. - Do NOT run exploratory commands (find, ls -R, cat) when codesight or smart_read can answer faster.
43. - Prefer targeted Grep over broad file scanning.
44. - When multiple independent searches are needed, run them in parallel (single message, multiple tool calls).
45. 
46. ## Beads Workflow (work item tracking)
47. If `bd` (beads) is available, use it for work item tracking across sessions:
48. - `bd prime` — get AI-optimised project context at session start (run this first in any project)
49. - `bd ready` — list open work items
50. - `bd create "Title" -p 2` — create a work item (priority: 1=high, 2=medium, 3=low)
51. - `bd update <id> --claim` — claim a work item before starting
52. - `bd close <id>` — close a completed item
53. Run `bd prime` at the start of a session when working in a repo with a `.beads/` directory.
54.
```

</details>

<sub>⏱️ 28m 23s</sub>

### ✅ `view`

**view the file at /Users/chazh/Documents/GitHub/ai-skillweave/README.md.**

/Users/chazh/Documents/GitHub/ai-skillweave/README.md (lines 1-90)

<details>
<summary>90 lines</summary>

````
1. # ai-skillweave
2. 
3. > Auto-learning agent harness: captures corrections live, mines session history for patterns, syncs ~450 skills across Claude/Codex/OpenClaw/Pi with MCP pre-configured.
4. 
5. One-command setup for all your Ollama agent harnesses: proper MCP servers, web tools, harness-specific configs — all portable and reproducible. Learns from your corrections automatically via real-time hooks and batch session analysis.
6. 
7. **~450 skills** on-disk across 5 sources — see [`docs/SKILLS-CATALOG.md`](docs/SKILLS-CATALOG.md) for a full categorized listing.
8. 
9. ---
10. 
11. ## Skill Sources
12. 
13. > **Skills in this repo are drawn from multiple open-source skill libraries**, each following the [Agent Skills](https://agentskills.io/) standard:
14. 
15. | Source | Skills | Notes |
16. |--------|--------|-------|
17. | [Everything Claude Code (ECC)](https://github.com/affaan-m/everything-claude-code) | 184 SKILL.md dirs | Community-maintained — testing, architecture, security, cloud, language patterns |
18. | [K-Dense scientific](https://github.com/K-Dense-AI/scientific-agent-skills) | 134 SKILL.md dirs | Bioinformatics, cheminformatics, drug discovery, clinical research, ML/AI |
19. | [ClawBio bioinformatics](https://github.com/ClawBio/ClawBio) | 56 SKILL.md dirs | Bioinformatics-native pipeline skills with executable Python scripts |
20. | [SkillGraph bioinformatics](https://github.com/variomeanalytics/bioinformatics-agent-skills) | MCP-served | Via MCP server with knowledge graph |
21. | Anthropic official | 17 SKILL.md dirs | Anthropic's official skills library |
22. | OpenAI Codex curated | 44 SKILL.md dirs | OpenAI's Codex skills collection |
23. | Personal learned | varies | BMO-style real-time capture (corrections detected live via hooks) + batch 4-stage pipeline |
24. 
25. `ai-skillweave`'s core contribution is **cross-harness delivery**: each skill library was originally designed for a single harness. This repo extends them so the same skills load natively into every `ollama launch` agent — OpenClaw, Pi, Codex, and Claude Code — each in the format that harness expects.
26. 
27. All sources are credited in the Skill Sources table above.
28. 
29. ---
30. 
31. ## Scientific Agent Skills — K-Dense
32. 
33. > **134 scientific and research skills from [K-Dense-AI/scientific-agent-skills](https://github.com/K-Dense-AI/scientific-agent-skills)** — covering bioinformatics, cheminformatics, drug discovery, clinical research, proteomics, medical imaging, ML/AI, materials science, physics, and 100+ scientific databases.
34. 
35. These skills follow the open [Agent Skills](https://agentskills.io/) standard and work with Claude Code, Cursor, Codex, and Gemini CLI. `ai-skillweave` extends them to all supported harnesses, just like ECC skills.
36. 
37. To install:
38. 
39. ```bash
40. ./safe-install.sh --with-science                    # ECC + K-Dense scientific skills
41. ```
42. 
43. 
44. 
45. ---
46. 
47. ## Bioinformatics Agent Skills — Variome Analytics
48. 
49. > **Bioinformatics pipeline skills served via MCP from [variomeanalytics/bioinformatics-agent-skills](https://github.com/variomeanalytics/bioinformatics-agent-skills)** — covering variant analysis, drug discovery, single-cell RNA-seq, genome-wide association studies, and 15+ database query skills (ClinVar, gnomAD, COSMIC, Ensembl, UniProt, and more).
50. 
51. Unlike ECC and K-Dense which ship on-disk `SKILL.md` files, these skills are served dynamically via an **MCP server** (`skillgraph`) that provides:
52. 
53. - `get_skill` — Full skill documentation for any skill in the graph
54. - `list_skills` — List all skills, optionally filtered by domain
55. - `search_skills` — Keyword search across skill IDs, triggers, and tool names
56. - `get_transitions` — Upstream/downstream skill edges with paper counts and data types
57. - `find_path` — Shortest pipeline path between two skills
58. - `get_graph_stats` — Graph statistics (skill count, edges, domain breakdown)
59. 
60. This knowledge graph approach means you can ask "what pipeline takes me from FASTQ to DEGs?" and get a real answer with evidence — something on-disk skill files alone can't provide.
61. 
62. **Installation:**
63. 
64. ```bash
65. # Add the SkillGraph MCP server (works out of the box after install.sh)
66. # It's already included in configs/claude-mcp-servers.json
67. ./scripts/setup-mcp.sh --force   # re-apply MCP config to add skillgraph
68. ```
69. 
70. ---
71. 
72. ## Bioinformatics Pipeline Skills — ClawBio
73. 
74. > **56 bioinformatics pipeline skills from [ClawBio/ClawBio](https://github.com/ClawBio/ClawBio)** — covering RNA-seq differential expression, VCF annotation, clinical variant reporting, single-cell orchestration, GWAS/PRS, HLA typing, methylation clocks, drug-target validation, and more.
75. 
76. Unlike ECC and K-Dense skills which are prompt-only `SKILL.md` files, ClawBio skills ship **executable Python scripts** alongside their skill definitions (172 `.py` files across 56 skills, plus 43 test directories). Each `SKILL.md` includes an `openclaw` metadata block with `uv` package requirements, so compatible harnesses can auto-install dependencies.
77. 
78. **Skill categories:**
79. 
80. | Category | Skills | Examples |
81. |----------|--------|---------|
82. | Genomics & Variants | 7 | `variant-annotation`, `vcf-annotator`, `fine-mapping`, `hla-typing`, `archaic-introgression` |
83. | Clinical & Pharma | 8 | `clinical-variant-reporter`, `clinpgx`, `pharmgx-reporter`, `nutrigx_advisor`, `drug-photo` |
84. | Transcriptomics | 6 | `rnaseq-de`, `scrna-orchestrator`, `scrna-embedding`, `de-summary`, `proteomics-de` |
85. | GWAS & Population | 6 | `gwas-lookup`, `gwas-prs`, `claw-ancestry-pca`, `mendelian-randomisation` |
86. | Data Integration | 6 | `bio-orchestrator`, `bioconductor-bridge`, `galaxy-bridge`, `bigquery-public` |
87. | Literature & Protocols | 5 | `pubmed-summariser`, `lit-synthesizer`, `bgpt-mcp`, `protocols-io` |
88. | Epigenomics | 1 | `methylation-clock` |
89. | Other | 17 | `seq-wrangler`, `equity-scorer`, `struct-predictor`, `ukb-navigator`, ... |
90.
````

</details>

821. else:
822.     print(content)
823. " > "$dst/SKILL.md" 2>/dev/null || cp "$dir/SKILL.md" "$dst/SKILL.md"

830.                     cp -r "$dir/assets" "$dst/" 2>/dev/null || true

832.                 fi
833.             done
834.             success "OpenClaw: $oc_science_count K-Dense scientific skills copied to workspace"
835.         fi
836.

846.                 fi
847.             done

````
90. 
91. **Installation:**
92. 
93. ```bash
94. ./safe-install.sh --with-bio                         # ECC + ClawBio bioinformatics (56 skills)
95. ./safe-install.sh --with-science --with-bio          # ECC + K-Dense + ClawBio
96. ./safe-install.sh --with-science --with-bio --with-anthropic --with-codex  # Full: all sources
97. ```
98. 
99. ClawBio is installed by default (`--with-bio` is on). To update, re-run with `--with-bio` to re-clone the latest from GitHub.
100. 
101. 
102. 
103. ---
104. 
105. ## Quick Start (New Machine)
106. 
107. ```bash
108. # 1. Clone this repo
109. git clone https://github.com/chazhyseni/ai-skillweave
110. cd ai-skillweave
111. 
112. # 2. Install everything (auto-detects macOS/Linux/WSL)
113. ./install.sh
114. 
115. # 3. Reload shell (install.sh tells you which file)
116. source ~/.bashrc   # Linux/WSL
117. source ~/.zshrc    # macOS
118. 
119. # 4. Launch any harness
120. ollama launch claude      # Claude Code + MCP tools
121. ollama launch openclaw    # OpenClaw + subagents + web
122. ollama launch pi          # Pi + subagents
123. ollama launch codex       # Codex via Ollama backend
124. ollama launch copilot     # Copilot CLI + MCP servers
125. ```
126. 
127. ---
128. 
129. ## What This Repo Does
130. 
131. | Component | What it configures |
132. |-----------|-------------------|
133. | **Claude Code MCP** | Adds 9 auto-configured servers to `~/.claude.json`: memory, sequential-thinking, context7, playwright, google-docs-editor, token-optimizer, codesight, skillgraph, beads |
134. | **OpenClaw** | Enables web tools + Ollama plugin in `~/.openclaw/openclaw.json` |
135. | **Pi** | Sets Ollama as provider + installs `pi-subagents` package |
136. | **Codex** | Configures `ollama-launch` provider + `danger-full-access` sandbox in `~/.codex/config.toml` |
137. | **Copilot CLI** | Configures MCP servers (including beads) in `~/.copilot/mcp-config.json` |
138. | **Ollama integrations** | Sets per-harness model mapping in `~/.ollama/config.json` (qwen3.6 default) |
139. | **Shell wrappers** | Adds `_*_with_skills` functions + aliases in `~/.bashrc` and/or `~/.zshrc` |
140. | **Claude Code skills** | Copies ECC SKILL.md files to `~/.claude/skills/` — visible via `/skills`, works with any launch method |
141. | **Lean skills cache** | Personal learned skills at `~/.claude/skills-cache/lean-skills.txt` — injected at session start (~1-2K tokens). Full library cache (`combined-skills.txt`) kept for reference but never injected — Claude's 200K window means 1.4M tokens would crash every session |
142. | **Beads** | `bd` CLI + `beads-mcp` MCP server — cross-session work item tracking. `bd prime` gives AI-optimised project context at session start |
143. | **Learning pipeline scripts** | Copies `sync-learned-skills.sh`, `extract-conversation-skills.py`, `safe-install.sh` to `~/.claude/scripts/` so `learn-sync`/`learn-stats`/`learn-prune` aliases work from any directory |
144. 
145. ---
146. 
147. ## Repository Structure
148. 
149. ```
150. ai-skillweave/
151. ├── install.sh                    ← Master installer (run this)
152. ├── safe-install.sh               ← ECC skills installer
153. ├── extract-conversation-skills.py ← 4-stage learning pipeline (Ingestion→Learning→Consolidation→Output)
154. ├── sync-learned-skills.sh        ← Sync learned skills + run pipeline (--stats, --prune, --sync-only)
155. │
156. ├── hooks/                        ← Claude Code hooks (auto-installed by install.sh)
157. │   ├── codesight-redirect.sh     ← PreToolUse: redirect broad searches to codesight
158. │   ├── learning-capture.sh       ← UserPromptSubmit: BMO-style real-time correction capture
159. │   └── session-reflection.sh     ← Session end: consolidate captured events into skills
160. │
161. ├── configs/                      ← Portable config templates
162. │   ├── claude-mcp-servers.json   ← MCP servers for Claude Code CLI
163. │   ├── claude-desktop-mcp-servers.json  ← MCP servers for Claude Desktop GUI
164. │   ├── copilot-mcp-config.json   ← MCP servers for Copilot CLI
165. │   ├── global-claude-md.md       ← Global CLAUDE.md template (MCP rules + beads workflow + conciseness)
166. │   ├── openclaw.json             ← OpenClaw config (web tools enabled)
167. │   ├── codex-config.toml         ← Codex ollama-launch provider config
168. │   ├── pi-settings.json          ← Pi agent settings
169. │   ├── ollama-integrations.json  ← Ollama integration→model mapping
170. │   └── zshrc-skills-block.sh     ← Shell skills layer block (reference/manual use)
171. │
172. ├── scripts/                      ← Individual setup scripts
173. │   ├── setup-mcp.sh              ← Inject MCP into ~/.claude.json (CLI)
174. │   ├── setup-claude-md.sh        ← Install global CLAUDE.md (MCP rules + conciseness)
175. │   ├── setup-hooks.sh            ← Install PreToolUse hook (codesight-redirect)
176. │   ├── setup-learning-hook.sh    ← Install UserPromptSubmit hook (BMO learning capture)
177. │   ├── setup-beads.sh            ← Install beads CLI + beads-mcp + bd init (auto-installs Homebrew if needed)
178. │   ├── consolidate-learnings.py  ← Consolidate captured events into SKILL.md files
179. │   ├── setup-claude-desktop.sh   ← Standalone: MCP + skills for Claude Desktop GUI
180. │   ├── build-desktop-skills.sh   ← Package .skill files for Desktop upload
181. │   ├── setup-openclaw.sh         ← Apply OpenClaw config
182. │   ├── setup-codex.sh            ← Apply Codex config
183. │   ├── setup-pi.sh               ← Apply Pi settings
184. │   ├── setup-copilot.sh          ← Apply Copilot CLI MCP config
185. │   ├── setup-ollama-config.sh    ← Apply Ollama integration→model mapping
186. │   ├── update-ecc.sh             ← Pull latest ECC + rebuild cache + learn-sync + re-sync harnesses
187. │   ├── disable-zscaler.sh        ← Disable Zscaler proxy
188. │   └── verify.sh                 ← Health check all components (beads, lean-skills, all harnesses)
189. │
190. ├── docs/
191. │   ├── AUDIT.md                  ← MCP/subagent audit (what was fixed + why)
192. │   └── TROUBLESHOOTING.md        ← Common issues and fixes
193. │
194. └── shared-learnings/
195.     └── learnings.md              ← Cross-harness learned patterns log
196. ```
197. 
198. ---
199. 
200. ## Platform Support
201. 
202. | Platform | Status | Shell | Notes |
203. |----------|--------|-------|-------|
204. | **macOS** | ✅ Tested | zsh (default) | Homebrew for dependencies |
205. | **Linux** | ✅ Tested | bash (default) | apt/dnf/pacman auto-detected |
206. | **Windows (WSL)** | ✅ Supported | bash | Run inside WSL — native Windows is not supported |
207. 
208. `install.sh` auto-detects the platform and user shell, installs skills into the correct RC file (`~/.bashrc` or `~/.zshrc`), and shows platform-appropriate messages.
209. 
210. ---
211. 
212. ## Before You Begin — First-Time Setup
213. 
214. Run these steps **once** on a new machine before cloning this repo.
215. 
216. ### 1. Install Ollama
217. 
218. ```bash
219. # macOS:
220. brew install ollama
````

</details>
