251. 
252.     # 2a. Anthropic Official Skills
253.     log "Fetching Anthropic official skills..."
254.     mkdir -p "$CURATED_DIR/anthropic-official"
255.     cd /tmp
256.     if git clone --depth 1 https://github.com/anthropics/skills.git anthropic-temp 2>/dev/null; then
257.         mv anthropic-temp/* "$CURATED_DIR/anthropic-official/" 2>/dev/null || true
258.         rm -rf anthropic-temp
259.         ANTHROPIC_COUNT=$(find "$CURATED_DIR/anthropic-official" -name '*.md' 2>/dev/null | wc -l | tr -d ' ')
260.         success "Anthropic official skills: $ANTHROPIC_COUNT skills"
261.     else
262.         warn "Failed to fetch Anthropic skills (network issue?)"
263.     fi
264. 
265.     # 2c. OpenAI Codex Official Skills (for Codex harness)
266.     log "Fetching OpenAI Codex official skills..."
267.     mkdir -p "$CURATED_DIR/openai-codex"
268.     cd /tmp
269.     if git clone --depth 1 https://github.com/openai/skills.git codex-temp 2>/dev/null; then
270.         mv codex-temp/* "$CURATED_DIR/openai-codex/" 2>/dev/null || true
271.         rm -rf codex-temp
272.         CODEX_COUNT=$(find "$CURATED_DIR/openai-codex" -name '*.md' 2>/dev/null | wc -l | tr -d ' ')
273.         success "OpenAI Codex skills: $CODEX_COUNT skills"
274.     else
275.         warn "Failed to fetch Codex skills (network issue?)"
276.     fi
277. 
278.     # Summary
279.     TOTAL_CURATED=$(find "$CURATED_DIR" -name '*.md' 2>/dev/null | wc -l | tr -d ' ')
280.     success "Total curated skills: $TOTAL_CURATED"
281. }
282. 
283. # =============================================================================
284. # Step 2b: Install K-Dense Scientific Agent Skills
285. # =============================================================================
286. 
287. install_science_skills() {
288.     if [ "$WITH_SCIENCE" = false ]; then
289.         log "Skipping scientific skills (use --with-science to add)"
290.         return 0
291.     fi
292. 
293.     log "Step 2b: Installing K-Dense Scientific Agent Skills..."
294. 
295.     if [ -d "$SCIENCE_DIR" ]; then
296.         warn "Existing scientific skills found: $SCIENCE_DIR"
297.         if [ -t 0 ]; then
298.             read -p "Overwrite? [y/N] " -n 1 -r
299.             echo ""
300.             if [[ ! $REPLY =~ ^[Yy]$ ]]; then
301.                 success "Keeping existing scientific skills"
302.                 return 0
303.             else
304.                 rm -rf "$SCIENCE_DIR"
305.             fi
306.         else
307.             success "Non-interactive mode — keeping existing scientific skills"
308.             return 0
309.         fi
310.     fi
311. 
312.     mkdir -p "$SCIENCE_DIR"
313.     cd /tmp
314.     if git clone --depth 1 https://github.com/K-Dense-AI/scientific-agent-skills.git science-temp 2>/dev/null; then
315.         # Copy skill directories (each contains SKILL.md + optional references/scripts/assets)
316.         mkdir -p "$SCIENCE_DIR/scientific-skills"
317.         cp -r science-temp/scientific-skills/* "$SCIENCE_DIR/scientific-skills/" 2>/dev/null || true
318.         SCIENCE_COUNT=$(find "$SCIENCE_DIR/scientific-skills" -name 'SKILL.md' 2>/dev/null | wc -l | tr -d ' ')
319.         rm -rf science-temp
320.         success "K-Dense scientific skills installed: $SCIENCE_DIR ($SCIENCE_COUNT skills)"
321.     else
322.         error "Failed to fetch K-Dense scientific-agent-skills repository"
323.         return 1
324.     fi
325. }
326. 
327. # =============================================================================
328. # Step 2c: Install ClawBio Bioinformatics Skills
329. # =============================================================================
330. 
331. install_bio_skills() {
332.     if [ "$WITH_BIO" = false ]; then
333.         log "Skipping ClawBio bioinformatics skills (use --with-bio to add)"
334.         return 0
335.     fi
336. 
337.     log "Step 2c: Installing ClawBio Bioinformatics Skills..."
338. 
339.     if [ -d "$CLAWBIO_DIR" ]; then
340.         warn "Existing ClawBio skills found: $CLAWBIO_DIR"
341.         if [ -t 0 ]; then
342.             read -p "Overwrite? [y/N] " -n 1 -r
343.             echo ""
344.             if [[ ! $REPLY =~ ^[Yy]$ ]]; then
345.                 success "Keeping existing ClawBio skills"
346.                 return 0
347.             else
348.                 rm -rf "$CLAWBIO_DIR"
349.             fi
350.         else
351.             success "Non-interactive mode — keeping existing ClawBio skills"
352.             return 0
353.         fi
354.     fi
355. 
356.     mkdir -p "$CLAWBIO_DIR"
357.     cd /tmp
358.     if git clone --depth 1 https://github.com/ClawBio/ClawBio.git clawbio-temp 2>/dev/null; then
359.         # Copy skill directories (each contains SKILL.md + optional Python scripts/examples/tests)
360.         mkdir -p "$CLAWBIO_DIR/skills"
361.         cp -r clawbio-temp/skills/* "$CLAWBIO_DIR/skills/" 2>/dev/null || true
362.         BIO_COUNT=$(find "$CLAWBIO_DIR/skills" -name 'SKILL.md' 2>/dev/null | wc -l | tr -d ' ')
363.         rm -rf clawbio-temp
364.         success "ClawBio bioinformatics skills installed: $CLAWBIO_DIR ($BIO_COUNT skills)"
365.     else
366.         error "Failed to fetch ClawBio repository"
367.         return 1
368.     fi
369. }
370. 
371. # =============================================================================
372. # Step 3: Create combined skills loader (single file with ALL skills)
373. # =============================================================================
374. 
375. create_loader() {
376.     log "Step 3: Creating skills loader (combining all skills)..."
377. 
378.     mkdir -p "$SKILLS_CACHE_DIR"
379. 
380.     # First, combine all skills into a single cached file
381.     COMBINED_FILE="$SKILLS_CACHE_DIR/combined-skills.txt"
382.     > "$COMBINED_FILE"  # Truncate/create file
383. 
384.     # Preamble: conciseness + MCP usage rules (injected at top of system prompt)
385.     cat >> "$COMBINED_FILE" << 'PREAMBLE'
386. # CRITICAL INSTRUCTIONS — READ FIRST
387. 
388. ## Conciseness
389. - Be terse. No trailing summaries, status tables, or "here's what I did" recaps.
390. - Show the change, not paragraphs explaining the change.
391. 
392. ## Use MCP tools PROACTIVELY
393. - Use codesight_get_summary BEFORE exploring a codebase with Grep/Glob/Read.
394. - Use smart_read (token-optimizer) instead of Read for large files.
395. - Use context7 query-docs BEFORE answering library/framework questions from training data.
396. - Use exa-web-search for anything that may have changed since training cutoff.
397. 
398. PREAMBLE
399. 
400.     # Add learned skills first (your personal skills - always included)
401.     if [ -d "$CLAUDE_DIR/skills/learned" ]; then
402.         for skill in "$CLAUDE_DIR/skills/learned"/*.md; do
403.             if [ -f "$skill" ]; then
404.                 echo "" >> "$COMBINED_FILE"
405.                 awk 'BEGIN{f=0} /^---$/{f++; next} f>=2' "$skill" >> "$COMBINED_FILE"
406.             fi
407.         done
408.     fi
409. 
410.     # Add curated skills with priority filtering
411.     # Priority 1: Anthropic Official (highest quality, vetted)
412.     if [ -d "$CURATED_DIR/anthropic-official" ]; then
413.         log "Merging Anthropic official skills (priority 1)..."
414.         find "$CURATED_DIR/anthropic-official" -name "*.md" -type f 2>/dev/null | while read -r skill; do
415.             echo "" >> "$COMBINED_FILE"
416.             awk 'BEGIN{f=0} /^---$/{f++; next} f>=2' "$skill" >> "$COMBINED_FILE"
417.         done
418.     fi
419. 
420.     # Priority 2: OpenAI Codex skills (for Codex harness)
```

</details>


### ✅ `view`

**view the file at /Users/chazh/Documents/GitHub/ai-skillweave/README.md.**

/Users/chazh/Documents/GitHub/ai-skillweave/README.md (lines 320-520)

<details>
<summary>201 lines</summary>

````
320. 
321. ---
322. 
323. ## Install Options
324. 
325. ```bash
326. # Full setup — all harnesses, all skills (ECC + K-Dense scientific), default model
327. ./install.sh
328. 
329. # Skip K-Dense scientific skills (faster, fewer skills)
330. ./install.sh --without-science
331. 
332. # Use a local model instead (faster, no cloud dependency)
333. ./install.sh --model qwen3.6
334. ./install.sh --model gemma4:26b
335. 
336. # Configure only specific harnesses
337. ./install.sh --only claude
338. ./install.sh --only openclaw
339. ./install.sh --only pi
340. ./install.sh --only codex
341. ./install.sh --only copilot
342. ./install.sh --only beads
343. 
344. # Skip ECC skills installation (faster, if skills already installed)
345. ./install.sh --skip-skills
346. 
347. # Run health check
348. ./install.sh --verify
349. # or: scripts/verify.sh
350. 
351. # Uninstall shell layer only (configs preserved)
352. ./install.sh --uninstall
353. ```
354. 
355. ---
356. 
357. ## MCP Servers (Claude Code)
358. 
359. ### Automatically applied by `./install.sh` (no API keys needed)
360. 
361. `scripts/setup-mcp.sh` reads `configs/claude-mcp-servers.json` and merges these into `~/.claude.json`:
362. 
363. | Server | What it does |
364. |--------|-------------|
365. | `memory` | Persistent memory across Claude Code sessions |
366. | `sequential-thinking` | Chain-of-thought reasoning tool |
367. | `context7` | Live docs lookup for any library/framework |
368. | `playwright` | Browser automation from within Claude Code |
369. | `google-docs-editor` | Read/write Google Docs (local server, pre-built) |
370. | `token-optimizer` | 95%+ context reduction via deduplication — saves Opus tokens |
371. | `codesight` | Maps codebase routes, schema, components, dependencies — AI context for any project |
372. | `skillgraph` | Bioinformatics pipeline skills + knowledge graph via MCP — variant analysis, drug discovery, single-cell, 15+ databases |
373. | `beads` | Cross-session work item tracking — `bd prime` gives AI-optimised context at session start (injected after `setup-beads.sh` confirms `beads-mcp` is installed) |
374. 
375. **No tokens or API keys needed for any of the above.** `install.sh` applies them automatically on a new machine.
376. 
377. ### Manually added (require API keys)
378. 
379. The `_api_key_servers_commented` block in `configs/claude-mcp-servers.json` contains templates that are **never auto-applied** — you fill them in and add them yourself:
380. 
381. ```bash
382. # GitHub (needs a PAT from github.com/settings/tokens)
383. claude mcp add github \
384.   -e GITHUB_PERSONAL_ACCESS_TOKEN=ghp_YOUR_TOKEN \
385.   --scope user \
386.   -- npx -y @modelcontextprotocol/server-github
387. 
388. # Exa web search (needs key from exa.ai)
389. claude mcp add exa \
390.   -e EXA_API_KEY=exa_xxx \
391.   --scope user \
392.   -- npx -y exa-mcp-server
393. ```
394. 
395. To add a server from the template: copy it from `_api_key_servers_commented` → move it to `mcpServers` → fill in the real key → run `scripts/setup-mcp.sh --force`.
396. 
397. ---
398. 
399. ## OpenClaw Subagents
400. 
401. OpenClaw's native subagent system works via `~/.openclaw/subagents/`. After setup:
402. 
403. - Web tools (`ollama_web_search`, `ollama_web_fetch`) are enabled via the Ollama plugin
404. - Gateway runs on `localhost:18789`
405. - Skills are loaded from `~/.openclaw/workspace/skills/` (ECC skills copied there)
406. 
407. ---
408. 
409. ## ECC Skills — Everything Claude Code
410. 
411. Skills from **[Everything Claude Code (ECC)](https://github.com/affaan-m/everything-claude-code)** are the largest single source (184 SKILL.md dirs). ECC is a community-maintained library of production-ready AI agent skills covering testing, architecture, security, cloud deployment, language-specific patterns, and more.
412. 
413. ECC skills are structured Markdown prompts (`.md` files) that tell AI agents *how to think* about specific tasks — when to activate, how to approach the problem, examples, and pitfalls.
414. 
415. ### Skill Sources
416. 
417. | Source | Skills | Notes |
418. |--------|--------|-------|
419. | ECC core | 184 SKILL.md dirs | From `affaan-m/everything-claude-code` |
420. | K-Dense scientific | 134 SKILL.md dirs | From `K-Dense-AI/scientific-agent-skills` |
421. | ClawBio bioinformatics | 56 SKILL.md dirs | From `ClawBio/ClawBio` — pipeline skills with Python scripts |
422. | SkillGraph bioinformatics | Bioinformatics pipeline skills | From `variomeanalytics/bioinformatics-agent-skills` via MCP server |
423. | Anthropic official | 17 SKILL.md dirs (85 total .md) | From Anthropic's official skills library |
424. | OpenAI Codex curated | 44 SKILL.md dirs (534 total .md) | From OpenAI's Codex skills collection |
425. | Personal learned | varies | BMO-style real-time capture (corrections detected live via hooks) + batch 4-stage pipeline |
426. 
427. ### What Each Harness Gets
428. 
429. | Harness | Skills | How they load |
430. |---------|--------|--------------|
431. | `claude` / `ollama launch claude` | **~450 native** + lean skills cache | SKILL.md → `~/.claude/skills/` (native `/skills`) + personal learned skills via `lean-skills.txt` (~1-2K tokens, via `--append-system-prompt-file`) + MCP servers |
432. | `ollama launch openclaw` | **~450 skill dirs** | Real SKILL.md copies in `~/.openclaw/workspace/skills/`, YAML-sanitized |
433. | `ollama launch pi` | **~450 skill dirs** | Symlinks in `~/.pi/agent/skills/` |
434. | `ollama launch codex` | **~450 + 5 built-in** | YAML-sanitized copies in `~/.codex/skills/` + Codex system skills |
435. 
436. Native `~/.claude/skills/` installation means skills are visible via Claude Code's `/skills` command and load **regardless of launch method** (direct CLI, `ollama launch`, VSCode extension).
437. 
438. > **YAML sanitization:** Skills with block-scalar descriptions, extra metadata fields (author, version, tags), or nested YAML mappings are automatically sanitized without modifying source files.
439. 
440. ### Cross-Harness Skill Sync
441. 
442. When you learn something useful in one session, sync it everywhere:
443. ```bash
444. learn-sync          # Extract patterns + sync to all harnesses
445. learn-sync-dry      # Preview what would sync
446. learn-stats         # Show skill counts, feedback scores, decay status
447. learn-prune         # Archive low-signal skills (feedback × decay < 0.2)
448. ```
449. 
450. ---
451. 
452. ## Harness Evolution — Self-Improving Skills
453. 
454. > Your harnesses get better over time. Corrections and preferences you state during sessions are captured in real-time and automatically distilled into concise, generalizable skills that load in every future session across every harness.
455. 
456. Two complementary learning approaches run in parallel:
457. 
458. ### 1. BMO-Style Real-Time Capture (primary)
459. 
460. Inspired by [bmo-agent](https://github.com/joelhans/bmo-agent). A `UserPromptSubmit` hook (`hooks/learning-capture.sh`) fires on every message and detects learning events as they happen:
461. 
462. | Event type | Detection | Example |
463. |------------|-----------|---------|
464. | `correction` | "No,", "Actually…", "That's not what I meant" | "No, use absolute paths here" |
465. | `preference` | "I prefer…", "I always…", "I like…" | "I always want type hints in Python" |
466. | `pattern` | "best practice", "convention", "should always" | "Should always validate before pushing" |
467. 
468. Events are saved to `~/.claude/skills/learned/events/` as JSON. At session end (`hooks/session-reflection.sh`), `scripts/consolidate-learnings.py` clusters similar events and writes SKILL.md files with **short, imperative names** (e.g. `verify-output-completeness`, `cite-published-research`).
469. 
470. ```bash
471. # Manual consolidation (also runs automatically at session end)
472. python3 scripts/consolidate-learnings.py
473. ```
474. 
475. ### 2. Batch Pipeline (secondary — runs on install/update)
476. 
477. For bulk distillation from conversation history. A 4-stage ALMA-inspired pipeline in `extract-conversation-skills.py`:
478. 
479. | Stage | What it does |
480. |-------|-------------|
481. | **1. Ingestion** | Parses conversation histories, classifies user corrections into memory types: `anti_pattern` (failed approaches), `heuristic` (successful strategies), `preference` (style), `domain_knowledge` (project-specific — **rejected**) |
482. | **2. Learning** | Groups similar corrections (Jaccard ≥ 0.5), requires **3+ unique sessions** (configurable via `--min-occurrences`), confidence = success × min(count/20, 1.0) + cross-project bonus, minimum 0.5 |
483. | **3. Consolidation** | Deduplicates (token overlap ≥ 0.85), abstracts raw corrections into **condition + strategy + anti-pattern** via keyword mapping or LLM distillation (`--llm`), quality gates reject empty/generic/single-project patterns |
484. | **4. Output** | Writes concise SKILL.md files with short imperative names (`verify-X`, `avoid-X`), YAML frontmatter (name, description, origin, tags, version, priority) |
485. 
486. **Quality-first design**: Without `--llm`, only skills matching known condition templates are written — producing 0 skills is better than keyword soup. With `--llm`, Ollama distills corrections into proper condition+strategy+anti-pattern form, and generates short semantic names like `cite-published-research-dois` (not sentence fragments).
487. 
488. ### Skill format
489. 
490. Every learned skill follows ECC-compatible structure — frontmatter, `When to Use`, `Operating Principles`, `Anti-patterns`, `Provenance`:
491. 
492. ```markdown
493. ---
494. name: evidence-based-claims
495. description: Require published evidence for scientific claims. Learned from 5 sessions across 3 projects.
496. origin: conversation-pipeline
497. tags: [learned, anti_pattern, universal]
498. version: 1.0.0
499. priority: high
500. ---
501. 
502. # Evidence Based Claims
503. 
504. ## When to Use
505. 
506. Making scientific or factual claims that could be verified against literature.
507. 
508. ## Operating Principles
509. 
510. 1. Cite specific papers (DOI, PMID) or explicitly state "no published evidence found".
511. 2. Distinguish model output from experimental data.
512. 3. Flag claims that lack published support.
513. 
514. ## Anti-patterns
515. 
516. - Fabricating citations or DOIs.
517. - Presenting unverified results as established findings.
518. - Claiming model output as experimental data.
519. 
520. ## Provenance
````

</details>

```
1. #!/bin/bash
2. # setup-copilot.sh — Configure MCP servers for GitHub Copilot CLI
3. # =============================================================================
4. # Writes MCP server config to ~/.copilot/mcp-config.json (read automatically
5. # by copilot on launch; augments built-in github-mcp-server).
6. #
7. # Usage:
8. #   scripts/setup-copilot.sh [--force]
9. #   --force : overwrite existing MCP server entries with template values
10. # =============================================================================
11. set -e
12. 
13. REPO_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")/.." && pwd)"
14. TEMPLATE="$REPO_DIR/configs/copilot-mcp-config.json"
15. TARGET="$HOME/.copilot/mcp-config.json"
16. 
17. log()     { echo -e "\033[1;34m[COPILOT]\033[0m $1"; }
18. success() { echo -e "\033[1;32m[OK]\033[0m $1"; }
19. warn()    { echo -e "\033[1;33m[WARN]\033[0m $1"; }
20. 
21. if [ ! -f "$TEMPLATE" ]; then
22.     warn "Template not found: $TEMPLATE"
23.     exit 1
24. fi
25. 
26. FORCE=false
27. [[ "$*" == *"--force"* ]] && FORCE=true
28. 
29. # Ensure Copilot config directory exists
30. mkdir -p "$(dirname "$TARGET")"
31. 
32. # Determine CA cert path (Zscaler or system default)
33. CA_CERT=""
34. if [ -f "$HOME/.mamba_ca_bundle.pem" ]; then
35.     CA_CERT="$HOME/.mamba_ca_bundle.pem"
36. elif [ -f "/etc/ssl/certs/ca-certificates.crt" ]; then
37.     CA_CERT="/etc/ssl/certs/ca-certificates.crt"
38. fi
39. 
40. log "Installing MCP servers for Copilot CLI..."
41. 
42. python3 << PYEOF
43. import json, os, sys
44. 
45. template_path = "$TEMPLATE"
46. target_path = "$TARGET"
47. ca_cert = "$CA_CERT"
48. home = os.path.expanduser("~")
49. npx_path = "$(which npx 2>/dev/null || echo "npx")"
50. force = $([[ "$FORCE" == "true" ]] && echo "True" || echo "False")
51. 
52. # Load or create target config
53. if os.path.exists(target_path):
54.     with open(target_path) as f:
55.         config = json.load(f)
56. else:
57.     config = {}
58. 
59. if "mcpServers" not in config:
60.     config["mcpServers"] = {}
61. 
62. with open(template_path) as f:
63.     template = json.load(f)
64. 
65. servers = template.get("mcpServers", {})
66. added = []
67. updated = []
68. skipped = []
69. 
70. for name, cfg in servers.items():
71.     # Substitute placeholders
72.     cfg_str = json.dumps(cfg)
73.     cfg_str = cfg_str.replace("{{HOME}}", home)
74.     cfg_str = cfg_str.replace("{{CA_CERT_PATH}}", ca_cert if ca_cert else "")
75.     cfg_str = cfg_str.replace("{{NPX_PATH}}", npx_path)
76.     cfg = json.loads(cfg_str)
77. 
78.     # Remove empty CA cert envs
79.     if "env" in cfg and cfg["env"].get("NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS") == "":
80.         del cfg["env"]["NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS"]
81.         if not cfg["env"]:
82.             del cfg["env"]
83. 
84.     if name in config["mcpServers"] and not force:
85.         skipped.append(name)
86.         continue
87. 
88.     action = "updated" if name in config["mcpServers"] else "added"
89.     config["mcpServers"][name] = cfg
90.     (updated if action == "updated" else added).append(name)
91. 
92. with open(target_path, "w") as f:
93.     json.dump(config, f, indent=2)
94.     f.write("\n")
95. 
96. if added:
97.     print(f"  Added:   {', '.join(added)}")
98. if updated:
99.     print(f"  Updated: {', '.join(updated)}")
100. if skipped:
101.     print(f"  Skipped (exists, use --force): {', '.join(skipped)}")
102. 
103. total = list(config["mcpServers"].keys())
104. print(f"Total MCP servers: {len(total)} — {total}")
105. PYEOF
106. 
107. success "MCP servers applied to Copilot CLI config ($TARGET)"
108. echo ""
109. echo "  Launch Copilot:  ollama launch copilot"
110. echo "  Or directly:     copilot"
111. echo ""
112.
```

</details>

<details>

```
1. #!/bin/bash
2. # =============================================================================
3. # setup-claude-desktop.sh — Add MCP servers + skills to Claude Desktop app
4. # =============================================================================
5. # Standalone setup for the Claude Desktop GUI app (separate from CLI install.sh).
6. # Adds MCP servers to the Desktop config and builds curated skills for a Project.
7. #
8. # Cross-platform:
9. #   macOS:   ~/Library/Application Support/Claude/claude_desktop_config.json
10. #   Linux:   ~/.config/Claude/claude_desktop_config.json
11. #   Windows: %APPDATA%\Claude\claude_desktop_config.json  (run via Git Bash/WSL)
12. #
13. # Skills approach:
14. #   Claude Desktop discovers skills from ~/.claude/skills/ during agent-mode sessions
15. #   (same directory as Claude Code CLI). safe-install.sh already populates this.
16. #   The Customize → Capabilities panel uses IndexedDB (not the filesystem), so
17. #   local skills won't appear there. Use desktop-batch-import.sh to inject skills
18. #   into the Customize panel, or use / slash commands in agent-mode sessions.
19. #
20. # Usage:
21. #   ./scripts/setup-claude-desktop.sh                    # Full setup (MCP + link skills)
22. #   ./scripts/setup-claude-desktop.sh --mcp-only         # MCP servers only
23. #   ./scripts/setup-claude-desktop.sh --skills-only      # Link skills only (no MCP)
24. #   ./scripts/setup-claude-desktop.sh --link-skills       # Symlink from ~/.claude/skills/ (default)
25. #   ./scripts/setup-claude-desktop.sh --package-skills   # Build .skill files for manual upload
26. #   ./scripts/setup-claude-desktop.sh --tier essential    # Minimal skills (~6K tokens)
27. #   ./scripts/setup-claude-desktop.sh --tier standard     # Agents + top commands (~54K tokens)
28. #   ./scripts/setup-claude-desktop.sh --tier full         # All universal skills (~89K tokens, default)
29. #   ./scripts/setup-claude-desktop.sh --force             # Overwrite existing MCP entries
30. # =============================================================================
31. set -e
32. 
33. REPO_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")/.." && pwd)"
34. TEMPLATE="$REPO_DIR/configs/claude-desktop-mcp-servers.json"
35. 
36. BLUE='\033[0;34m'; GREEN='\033[0;32m'; YELLOW='\033[1;33m'; RED='\033[0;31m'; CYAN='\033[0;36m'; NC='\033[0m'
37. log()     { echo -e "${BLUE}[DESKTOP]${NC} $1"; }
38. success() { echo -e "${GREEN}[OK]${NC} $1"; }
39. warn()    { echo -e "${YELLOW}[WARN]${NC} $1"; }
40. error()   { echo -e "${RED}[ERROR]${NC} $1"; exit 1; }
41. 
42. # =============================================================================
43. # Parse arguments
44. # =============================================================================
45. FORCE=false
46. MCP_ONLY=false
47. SKILLS_ONLY=false
48. PACKAGE_SKILLS=false
49. TIER="full"
50. CLEAN=false
51. 
52. while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
53.     case "$1" in
54.         --force)          FORCE=true; shift ;;
55.         --mcp-only)       MCP_ONLY=true; shift ;;
56.         --skills-only)    SKILLS_ONLY=true; shift ;;
57.         --package-skills)  PACKAGE_SKILLS=true; shift ;;
58.         --tier)           TIER="$2"; shift 2 ;;
59.         --clean)          CLEAN=true; shift ;;
60.         --help|-h)
61.             echo "Usage: setup-claude-desktop.sh [--mcp-only] [--skills-only] [--package-skills]"
62.             echo "       [--tier essential|standard|full] [--force] [--clean]"
63.             echo ""
64.             echo "  --package-skills  Build .skill files for manual upload (default: symlink)"
65.             echo "  Without --package-skills, skills are symlinked from ~/.claude/skills/ (recommended)"
66.             exit 0
67.             ;;
68.         *) shift ;;
69.     esac
70. done
71. 
72. echo ""
73. echo "╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗"
74. echo "║   Claude Desktop App — MCP + Skills Setup               ║"
75. echo "╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝"
76. echo ""
77. 
78. # =============================================================================
79. # Detect platform and config path
80. # =============================================================================
81. detect_config_path() {
82.     local os_type
83.     case "$(uname -s)" in
84.         Darwin*)  os_type="macOS" ;;
85.         Linux*)   os_type="Linux" ;;
86.         MINGW*|MSYS*|CYGWIN*)  os_type="Windows" ;;
87.         *)        os_type="Unknown" ;;
88.     esac
89. 
90.     case "$os_type" in
91.         macOS)
92.             DESKTOP_CONFIG="$HOME/Library/Application Support/Claude/claude_desktop_config.json"
93.             ;;
94.         Linux)
95.             DESKTOP_CONFIG="$HOME/.config/Claude/claude_desktop_config.json"
96.             ;;
97.         Windows)
98.             if [ -n "$APPDATA" ]; then
99.                 DESKTOP_CONFIG="$APPDATA/Claude/claude_desktop_config.json"
100.             else
101.                 DESKTOP_CONFIG="$HOME/AppData/Roaming/Claude/claude_desktop_config.json"
102.             fi
103.             ;;
104.         *)
105.             error "Unsupported OS: $(uname -s). Supported: macOS, Linux, Windows (Git Bash/WSL)"
106.             ;;
107.     esac
108. 
109.     log "Platform: $os_type"
110.     log "Config:   $DESKTOP_CONFIG"
111. }
112. 
113. detect_config_path
114. 
115. # =============================================================================
116. # Determine CA cert path (Zscaler or system default)
117. # =============================================================================
118. CA_CERT=""
119. if [ -f "$HOME/.mamba_ca_bundle.pem" ]; then
120.     CA_CERT="$HOME/.mamba_ca_bundle.pem"
121. elif [ -f "/etc/ssl/certs/ca-certificates.crt" ]; then
122.     CA_CERT="/etc/ssl/certs/ca-certificates.crt"
123. fi
124. 
125. # =============================================================================
126. # Part 1: MCP Servers
127. # =============================================================================
128. setup_mcp() {
129.     if [ ! -f "$DESKTOP_CONFIG" ]; then
130.         # Check if Claude Desktop is installed but config doesn't exist yet
131.         local config_dir
132.         config_dir="$(dirname "$DESKTOP_CONFIG")"
133.         if [ -d "$config_dir" ]; then
134.             log "Creating config file (Claude Desktop installed but no config yet)..."
135.             echo '{}' > "$DESKTOP_CONFIG"
136.         else
137.             warn "Claude Desktop not installed (directory not found: $config_dir)"
138.             warn "Install Claude Desktop from https://claude.ai/download then re-run"
139.             return 1
140.         fi
141.     fi
142. 
143.     command -v python3 >/dev/null 2>&1 || error "python3 not found"
144.     [ -f "$TEMPLATE" ] || error "Template not found: $TEMPLATE"
145. 
146.     # Backup
147.     local backup="${DESKTOP_CONFIG}.bak_$(date +%Y%m%d_%H%M%S)"
148.     cp "$DESKTOP_CONFIG" "$backup"
149.     success "Backup: $backup"
150. 
151.     # Apply MCP servers
152.     python3 << PYEOF
153. import json, os, sys
154. 
155. config_path = """$DESKTOP_CONFIG"""
156. template_path = "$TEMPLATE"
157. cli_config_path = os.path.expanduser("~/.claude.json")
158. ca_cert = "$CA_CERT"
159. home = os.path.expanduser("~")
160. force = $([[ "$FORCE" == "true" ]] && echo "True" || echo "False")
161. 
162. try:
163.     with open(config_path) as f:
164.         config = json.load(f)
165. except json.JSONDecodeError:
166.     print(f"WARNING: Existing Desktop config is invalid JSON — resetting to empty config")
167.     config = {}
168. 
169. with open(template_path) as f:
170.     template = json.load(f)
171. 
172. if "mcpServers" not in config:
173.     config["mcpServers"] = {}
174. 
175. # Remove broken HTTP-type entries that Claude Desktop doesn't support
176. # (Desktop only supports stdio-based servers with command/args)
177. removed_http = []
178. for name in list(config["mcpServers"].keys()):
179.     entry = config["mcpServers"][name]
180.     if entry.get("type") == "http" or (not entry.get("command") and not entry.get("args")):
181.         del config["mcpServers"][name]
182.         removed_http.append(name)
183. if removed_http:
184.     print(f"Removed unsupported HTTP-type servers from Desktop config: {removed_http}")
185.     print(f"  (HTTP servers like skillgraph only work in Claude Code CLI, not Desktop)")
186. 
187. # Load CLI config to copy API-key servers (github, exa-web-search)
188. # Only copy stdio-based servers — skip HTTP-type
189. api_key_servers = {}
190. if os.path.exists(cli_config_path):
191.     try:
192.         with open(cli_config_path) as f:
193.             cli_config = json.load(f)
194.         cli_mcp = cli_config.get("mcpServers", {})
195.         for name in ["github", "exa-web-search"]:
196.             if name in cli_mcp and cli_mcp[name].get("type") != "http":
197.                 api_key_servers[name] = cli_mcp[name]
198.     except Exception:
199.         pass
200. 
201. # Merge template servers
202. servers = template.get("mcpServers", {})
203. added, updated, skipped = [], [], []
204. 
205. import shutil, subprocess
206. 
207. # Resolve absolute paths for npx/node — Claude Desktop GUI does NOT
208. # inherit the user's shell PATH (no .zshrc, no nvm, no brew paths).
209. def resolve_bin(name):
210.     """Find absolute path for a binary, checking common locations."""
211.     # Try shell's which first (works if this script runs from a shell)
212.     result = shutil.which(name)
213.     if result:
214.         return result
215.     # Common locations for nvm, homebrew, system
216.     candidates = [
217.         os.path.expanduser(f"~/.nvm/versions/node/*/bin/{name}"),
218.         f"/opt/homebrew/bin/{name}",
219.         f"/usr/local/bin/{name}",
220.         f"/usr/bin/{name}",
221.     ]
222.     import glob
223.     for pattern in candidates:
224.         matches = sorted(glob.glob(pattern), reverse=True)  # newest first
225.         if matches and os.path.isfile(matches[0]):
226.             return matches[0]
227.     return name  # fallback to bare name
228. 
229. npx_abs = resolve_bin("npx")
230. node_abs = resolve_bin("node")
231. resolved = {}
232. 
233. for name, cfg in {**servers, **api_key_servers}.items():
234.     # Substitute placeholders
235.     cfg_str = json.dumps(cfg)
236.     cfg_str = cfg_str.replace("{{HOME}}", home)
237.     cfg_str = cfg_str.replace("{{CA_CERT_PATH}}", ca_cert if ca_cert else "")
238.     cfg = json.loads(cfg_str)
239. 
240.     # Remove empty CA cert envs
241.     if "env" in cfg and cfg["env"].get("NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS") == "":
242.         del cfg["env"]["NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS"]
243.         if not cfg["env"]:
244.             del cfg["env"]
245. 
246.     # Skip HTTP-type servers (no command to resolve)
247.     cmd = cfg.get("command", "")
248.     if cfg.get("type") == "http":
249.         pass
250.     # Fix malformed entries (e.g. github with command="github")
251.     elif cmd not in ("npx", "node") and cmd not in (npx_abs, node_abs):
252.         # Likely a malformed CLI copy — fix to npx
253.         cfg["command"] = npx_abs
254.         cfg["args"] = [a for a in cfg.get("args", []) if a != "npx"]
255.         if not any(a.startswith("@") or a.startswith("-") for a in cfg.get("args", [])):
256.             cfg["args"] = ["-y", f"@modelcontextprotocol/server-{name}"]
257.         cfg.pop("type", None)
258.         resolved[name] = f"fixed malformed command '{cmd}' -> {npx_abs}"
259. 
260.     # Resolve bare npx/node to absolute paths (Desktop app has no shell PATH)
261.     if cfg.get("command") == "npx":
262.         cfg["command"] = npx_abs
263.         resolved[name] = f"npx -> {npx_abs}"
264.     elif cfg.get("command") == "node":
265.         cfg["command"] = node_abs
266.         resolved[name] = f"node -> {node_abs}"
267. 
268.     if name in config["mcpServers"] and not force:
269.         skipped.append(name)
270.     else:
271.         action = "updated" if name in config["mcpServers"] else "added"
272.         config["mcpServers"][name] = cfg
273.         (updated if action == "updated" else added).append(name)
274. 
275. with open(config_path, "w") as f:
276.     json.dump(config, f, indent=2)
277. 
278. print(f"Added:   {added}")
279. print(f"Updated: {updated}")
280. print(f"Skipped: {skipped}")
281. total = list(config["mcpServers"].keys())
282. print(f"Total MCP servers: {len(total)} — {total}")
283. print(f"Binary paths: npx={npx_abs}, node={node_abs}")
284. if resolved:
285.     print(f"Path resolution: {resolved}")
286. if api_key_servers:
287.     print(f"API-key servers copied from CLI config: {list(api_key_servers.keys())}")
288. PYEOF
289. 
290.     success "MCP servers applied to Claude Desktop config"
291.     echo ""
292.     log "Restart Claude Desktop for changes to take effect."
293. }
294. 
295. # =============================================================================
296. # Part 2: Skills (default: symlink, optional: package .skill files)
297. # =============================================================================
298. link_skills() {
299.     # How Claude Desktop discovers skills (reverse-engineered from app.asar):
300.     #   _discoverSkills() reads from this.skillDir, which is join(agentDir, ".claude", "skills")
301.     #   where agentDir = home directory. So it reads ~/.claude/skills/ directories
302.     #   containing SKILL.md files with YAML frontmatter (name + description required).
303.     #   This discovery ONLY runs during agent-mode sessions (agentic coding tool calls).
304.     #   The Customize → Capabilities panel uses IndexedDB, NOT the filesystem —
305.     #   writing to skills-plugin/ doesn't survive app restarts.
306. 
307.     local skills_dir="$HOME/.claude/skills"
308. 
309.     if [ ! -d "$skills_dir" ]; then
310.         warn "~/.claude/skills/ not found — run safe-install.sh first"
311.         return 1
312.     fi
313. 
314.     local count=$(find "$skills_dir" -mindepth 1 -maxdepth 1 -type d ! -name 'learned' 2>/dev/null | wc -l | tr -d ' ')
315. 
316.     if [ "$count" -eq 0 ]; then
317.         warn "No skills found in ~/.claude/skills/ — run safe-install.sh first"
318.         return 1
319.     fi
320. 
321.     success "Skills: $count available in ~/.claude/skills/"
322.     log "Skills work via / slash commands in agent-mode sessions"
323.     log "Customize → Capabilities panel does NOT list local skills (it uses IndexedDB)"
324.     log "To bulk-import into the Customize panel, close Desktop and run:"
325.     log "  $REPO_DIR/scripts/desktop-batch-import.sh"
326. }
327. 
328. # =============================================================================
329. # Part 2b: Package skills as .skill files (for manual upload fallback)
330. # =============================================================================
331. build_skills() {
332.     log "Building curated skills (tier: $TIER)..."
333.     CLEAN_FLAG=""
334.     $CLEAN && CLEAN_FLAG="--clean"
335.     bash "$REPO_DIR/scripts/build-desktop-skills.sh" --tier "$TIER" $CLEAN_FLAG
336. }
337. 
338. # =============================================================================
339. # Execute
340. # =============================================================================
341. if ! $SKILLS_ONLY; then
342.     echo ""
343.     log "Setting up MCP servers..."
344.     echo ""
345.     setup_mcp
346. fi
347. 
348. if ! $MCP_ONLY; then
349.     if $PACKAGE_SKILLS; then
350.         echo ""
351.         log "Packaging .skill files for manual upload..."
352.         echo ""
353.         build_skills
354.     else
355.         echo ""
356.         log "Linking skills from ~/.claude/skills/..."
357.         echo ""
358.         link_skills
359.     fi
360. fi
361. 
362. echo ""
363. echo "╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗"
364. echo "║   Claude Desktop Setup Complete                          ║"
365. echo "╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝"
366. echo ""
367. echo "  Next steps:"
368. echo "    1. Restart Claude Desktop app"
369. echo "    2. MCP servers will appear in Claude Desktop settings"
370. echo "    3. To batch-import skills (bypasses 15-at-a-time UI limit):"
371. echo "       Close Claude Desktop, then run:"
372. echo "       $REPO_DIR/scripts/desktop-batch-import.sh"
373. echo "       (use --force to overwrite, --clean to remove custom skills first)"
374. echo ""
375. if $PACKAGE_SKILLS; then
376. echo "  Manual skill upload (only needed if / commands don't work):"
377. echo "    1. Open Claude Desktop → Customize → Skills"
378. echo "    2. Click '+' → 'Upload a skill'"
379. echo "    3. Select files from: $REPO_DIR/configs/desktop-skills"
380. echo ""
381. echo "  Or select all at once: open \"$REPO_DIR/configs/desktop-skills\""
382. echo ""
383. fi
384. echo "  Token economics:"
385. echo "    MCP servers: zero tokens until invoked"
386. echo "    Skills: discovered from ~/.claude/skills/ during agent-mode sessions"
387. echo "    Customize panel: uses IndexedDB (not filesystem) -- use batch-import.sh"
388. echo ""
389. echo "  Note: skillgraph (78 bioinformatics skills) is an HTTP-type server."
390. echo "  Claude Desktop only supports stdio-based servers in its config."
391. echo "  Use skillgraph in Claude Code CLI (already configured in ~/.claude.json)."
392. echo ""
393.
```

</details>

/Users/chazh/Documents/GitHub/ai-skillweave/README.md:373:| `beads` | Cross-session work item tracking — `bd prime` gives AI-optimised context at session start (injected after `setup-beads.sh` confirms `beads-mcp` is installed) |
/Users/chazh/Documents/GitHub/ai-skillweave/README.md:431:| `claude` / `ollama launch claude` | **~450 native** + lean skills cache | SKILL.md → `~/.claude/skills/` (native `/skills`) + personal learned skills via `lean-skills.txt` (~1-2K tokens, via `--append-system-prompt-file`) + MCP servers |
/Users/chazh/Documents/GitHub/ai-skillweave/README.md:550:./safe-install.sh                                    # ECC only
/Users/chazh/Documents/GitHub/ai-skillweave/README.md:582:### Why Claude was hitting "context limit reached"
/Users/chazh/Documents/GitHub/ai-skillweave/README.md:584:The original installer injected the full combined skills cache (`combined-skills.txt`, ~5.5MB ≈ **1.375M tokens**) into every Claude session via `--append-system-prompt-file`. Claude Sonnet 4.6 and Opus 4.7 have a **200K token** context window — this means the skills injection alone was **6.9× the entire context budget**, triggering an explicit `context_length_exceeded` error from the Anthropic API on every single session.
/Users/chazh/Documents/GitHub/ai-skillweave/README.md:588:### The fix: lean-skills injection
/Users/chazh/Documents/GitHub/ai-skillweave/README.md:590:The installer now injects **only your personal learned skills** (`lean-skills.txt`) at session start — typically **1–2K tokens** (under 1% of the context window). The full combined cache is still built and stored at `~/.claude/skills-cache/combined-skills.txt` for reference and local search, but is never injected automatically.
/Users/chazh/Documents/GitHub/ai-skillweave/README.md:594:| `lean-skills.txt` | ~6-50KB | ~1.5–12K | Injected into every Claude session via `--append-system-prompt-file` |
/Users/chazh/Documents/GitHub/ai-skillweave/README.md:595:| `combined-skills.txt` | ~5.5MB | ~1.375M | Reference only — local search, update-ecc.sh, never auto-injected |
/Users/chazh/Documents/GitHub/ai-skillweave/README.md:607:With `lean-skills.txt` injection, per-session context overhead is minimal. Prompt caching keeps it economical even for heavy users:
/Users/chazh/Documents/GitHub/ai-skillweave/README.md:611:| Session 1 (cache miss) | Full input price for lean-skills tokens (~1-2K tokens) |
/Users/chazh/Documents/GitHub/ai-skillweave/README.md:626:| Lean skills preamble | Short directive injected at top of `lean-skills.txt` | Soft — reinforces CLAUDE.md |
/Users/chazh/Documents/GitHub/ai-skillweave/README.md:828:| MCP servers | 9 auto (incl. beads) + manual API-key servers | 6 auto + API-key servers copied from CLI; skillgraph via Settings → Integrations |
/Users/chazh/Documents/GitHub/ai-skillweave/README.md:837:> **Powered by [beads](https://github.com/gastownhall/beads)** — AI-native cross-session work item tracking. Works with every harness (Claude Code, Codex, Copilot, OpenClaw, Pi) via MCP.
/Users/chazh/Documents/GitHub/ai-skillweave/README.md:839:### What beads does
/Users/chazh/Documents/GitHub/ai-skillweave/README.md:841:Beads lets you create, track, and share work items across AI coding sessions. Unlike session memory (which is per-harness), beads items persist in your project via `AGENTS.md` — a harness-agnostic file every AI tool reads.
/Users/chazh/Documents/GitHub/ai-skillweave/README.md:853:`scripts/setup-beads.sh` is called automatically by `install.sh` (Step 7). It:
/Users/chazh/Documents/GitHub/ai-skillweave/README.md:856:2. Installs `beads` via `brew install beads` (macOS) or the official curl script (Linux)
/Users/chazh/Documents/GitHub/ai-skillweave/README.md:857:3. Installs `beads-mcp` via `uv tool install beads-mcp` (with `pip3 install --user beads-mcp` fallback)
/Users/chazh/Documents/GitHub/ai-skillweave/README.md:858:4. Injects the `beads` MCP entry into `~/.claude.json` and `~/.copilot/mcp-config.json` (only after confirming beads-mcp is installed; skips if already present)
/Users/chazh/Documents/GitHub/ai-skillweave/README.md:859:5. Runs `bd init --quiet --stealth` to initialise beads in the current repo
/Users/chazh/Documents/GitHub/ai-skillweave/README.md:862:# Run just beads setup
/Users/chazh/Documents/GitHub/ai-skillweave/README.md:863:./install.sh --only beads
/Users/chazh/Documents/GitHub/ai-skillweave/README.md:866:scripts/setup-beads.sh
/Users/chazh/Documents/GitHub/ai-skillweave/README.md:867:scripts/setup-beads.sh --force    # overwrite existing beads MCP entry
/Users/chazh/Documents/GitHub/ai-skillweave/README.md:872:`--stealth` means `.beads/` is kept local (in `.gitignore`) — your beads items don't get committed to the project repo. `AGENTS.md` is still updated by `bd init` and is safe to commit.
/Users/chazh/Documents/GitHub/ai-skillweave/README.md:876:The MCP server (`beads-mcp`) makes beads available in any harness that loads `~/.claude.json` (Claude Code, Copilot) or `~/.copilot/mcp-config.json`. For OpenClaw, Pi, and Codex, `bd prime` output can be pasted directly — the `AGENTS.md` file is what matters for cross-session persistence.
```

620.     # Source the current shell's rc file (may fail in non-interactive subshell — that's OK)
621.     # Use subshell to prevent set -e from catching failures inside sourced rc
622.     if [ -n "$ZSH_VERSION" ]; then
623.         (source "$HOME/.zshrc" 2>/dev/null) || true
624.         success "Shell reloaded (.zshrc)"
625.     elif [ -n "$BASH_VERSION" ]; then
626.         (source "$HOME/.bashrc" 2>/dev/null) || true
627.         success "Shell reloaded (.bashrc)"
628.     else
629.         warn "Run 'source $(basename "$primary_rc")' or restart terminal to activate"
630.     fi
631. }
632. 
633. # =============================================================================
634. # Step 4b: Copy learning pipeline scripts to ~/.claude/scripts/
635. # =============================================================================
636. # The generated shell aliases (learn-sync, learn-stats, etc.) reference
637. # ~/.claude/scripts/sync-learned-skills.sh. This step ensures those files exist.
638. 
639. copy_scripts_to_claude_dir() {
640.     local scripts_dest="$HOME/.claude/scripts"
641.     mkdir -p "$scripts_dest"
642. 
643.     local scripts_to_copy=(
644.         "sync-learned-skills.sh"
645.         "extract-conversation-skills.py"
646.         "safe-install.sh"
647.     )
648.     local copied=0
649.     for script in "${scripts_to_copy[@]}"; do
650.         local src="$REPO_DIR/$script"
651.         if [ -f "$src" ]; then
652.             cp "$src" "$scripts_dest/$script"
653.             chmod +x "$scripts_dest/$script" 2>/dev/null || true
654.             copied=$((copied + 1))
655.         fi
656.     done
657.     success "Copied $copied learning pipeline scripts to $scripts_dest/"
658. }
659. 
660. # =============================================================================
661. # Step 4c: Symlink ECC skills into native skills dirs for codex and pi
662. # =============================================================================
663. 
664. _migrate_flat_to_dir_skills() {
665.     # Migrate flat .md files to <name>/SKILL.md directory format.
666.     # Claude Code's /skills command only discovers directory-format skills.
667.     local skills_dir="$HOME/.claude/skills"
668.     [ ! -d "$skills_dir" ] && return 0
669.     local migrated=0
670.     for flat_file in "$skills_dir"/*.md; do
671.         [ ! -f "$flat_file" ] && continue
672.         local skill_name
673.         skill_name=$(basename "$flat_file" .md)
674.         # Skip learned/ subdirectory (uses flat .md by convention)
675.         [ "$skill_name" = "learned" ] && continue
676.         # Skip if directory already exists (already migrated)
677.         if [ -d "$skills_dir/$skill_name" ]; then
678.             rm -f "$flat_file"
679.             continue
680.         fi
681.         mkdir -p "$skills_dir/$skill_name"
682.         mv "$flat_file" "$skills_dir/$skill_name/SKILL.md"
683.         migrated=$((migrated + 1))
684.     done
685.     [ "$migrated" -gt 0 ] && success "Migrated $migrated flat .md skills to directory format"
686.     return 0
687. }
688. 
689. link_native_skills() {
690.     log "Step 4b: Linking ECC skills into native harness skill directories..."
691. 
692.     if [ ! -d "$ECC_DIR/skills" ]; then
693.         warn "ECC skills not installed, skipping native linking"
694.         return 0
695.     fi
696. 
697.     # Claude Code: ~/.claude/skills/<skill-name>/SKILL.md (directory format)
698.     # Claude Code's /skills command ONLY discovers directory-format skills,
699.     # NOT flat .md files. Migrate any stale flat files first.
700.     local claude_skills_dir="$HOME/.claude/skills"
701.     mkdir -p "$claude_skills_dir"
702.     _migrate_flat_to_dir_skills
703.     local claude_count=0
704.     for dir in "$ECC_DIR/skills"/*/; do
705.         skill_name=$(basename "$dir")
706.         # Use SKILL.md if it exists, otherwise find any .md
707.         local src_file=""
708.         if [ -f "$dir/SKILL.md" ]; then
709.             src_file="$dir/SKILL.md"
710.         else
711.             src_file=$(find "$dir" -maxdepth 1 -name '*.md' -type f 2>/dev/null | head -1)
712.         fi
713.         [ -z "$src_file" ] && continue
714.         local dst_dir="$claude_skills_dir/$skill_name"
715.         local dst="$dst_dir/SKILL.md"
716.         if [ ! -f "$dst" ]; then
717.             mkdir -p "$dst_dir"
718.             cp "$src_file" "$dst"
719.             claude_count=$((claude_count + 1))
720.         fi
721.     done
722.     local total_count=$(find "$claude_skills_dir" -mindepth 1 -maxdepth 1 -type d ! -name 'learned' 2>/dev/null | wc -l | tr -d ' ')
723.     if [ "$claude_count" -eq 0 ] && [ "$total_count" -gt 0 ]; then
724.         success "Claude Code: all ECC skills already in ~/.claude/skills/ ($total_count total)"
725.     else
726.         success "Claude Code: $claude_count ECC skills installed to ~/.claude/skills/ ($total_count total)"
727.     fi
728. 
729.     # Codex: handled by update-ecc.sh which properly sanitizes YAML (symlinks
730.     # for clean skills, sanitized copies for skills with block scalars/extra fields).
731.     # safe-install.sh used to skip invalid skills entirely — update-ecc.sh fixes them.
732.     if [ -d "$HOME/.codex/skills" ]; then
733.         log "Codex skills will be synced by update-ecc.sh (proper YAML sanitization)"
734.     else
735.         warn "~/.codex/skills not found — install Codex CLI first"
736.     fi
737. 
738.     # OpenClaw: ~/.openclaw/workspace/skills/<skill-name>/ (real copies, NOT symlinks)
739.     # Must be real files inside workspace root - OpenClaw rejects symlinks escaping the workspace
740.     # Skills appear with source="openclaw-workspace" and status="✓ ready"
741.     if [ -d "$HOME/.openclaw/workspace" ]; then
742.         local openclaw_count=0
743.         local ws_skills="$HOME/.openclaw/workspace/skills"
744.         mkdir -p "$ws_skills"
745.         for dir in "$ECC_DIR/skills"/*/; do
746.             skill_name=$(basename "$dir")
747.             [ ! -f "$dir/SKILL.md" ] && continue
748.             grep -q '^description: *[>|]' "$dir/SKILL.md" 2>/dev/null && continue
749.             if grep -E '^[a-z_]+:' "$dir/SKILL.md" 2>/dev/null | grep -qvE '^(name|description|origin|tools):'; then
750.                 continue
751.             fi
752.             dst="$ws_skills/$skill_name"
753.             if [ ! -d "$dst" ]; then
754.                 mkdir -p "$dst"
755.                 cp "$dir/SKILL.md" "$dst/SKILL.md"
756.                 openclaw_count=$((openclaw_count + 1))
757.             fi
758.         done
759.         success "OpenClaw: $openclaw_count ECC skills copied to workspace ($(ls "$ws_skills" | wc -l | tr -d ' ') total)"
760.     else
```

340.         return 1

343. 
344. # =============================================================================
345. # Step 1: Skills Layer (ECC + shell wrappers)
346. # =============================================================================
347. 
348. should_run() {
349.     [ -z "$ONLY_TARGET" ] || [ "$ONLY_TARGET" = "$1" ]
350. }
351. 
352. if ! $SKIP_SKILLS && should_run "skills"; then
353.     section "Skills Layer"
354.     # safe-install.sh may exit non-zero when 'source <rc>' fails in a non-interactive
355.     # subshell — this is expected and harmless. Capture exit code to report real failures.
356.     safe_exit=0
357.     SAFE_ARGS=""
358.     $WITH_SCIENCE && SAFE_ARGS="$SAFE_ARGS --with-science"
359.     $WITH_CURATED && SAFE_ARGS="$SAFE_ARGS --with-curated"
360.     $WITH_BIO && SAFE_ARGS="$SAFE_ARGS --with-bio"
361.     bash "$REPO_DIR/safe-install.sh" $SAFE_ARGS || safe_exit=$?
362.     if [ "$safe_exit" -eq 0 ]; then
363.         success "ECC skills + shell wrappers installed (run: source $SHELL_RC to activate)"
364.     else
365.         warn "safe-install.sh exited with code $safe_exit — shell integration may need manual source $SHELL_RC"
366.     fi
367.     # Rebuild cache with full skill counts (safe-install.sh caps Codex at 100).
368.     # update-ecc.sh also restores ECC working tree to actual HEAD.
369.     log "Rebuilding skills cache with full counts..."
370.     bash "$REPO_DIR/scripts/update-ecc.sh" --force || true
371.     success "Skills cache rebuilt (all sources, no caps)"
372. else
373.     log "Skipping skills layer"
374. fi
375. 
376. # =============================================================================
377. # Step 1b: Ollama integration→model mapping
378. # =============================================================================
379. if should_run "ollama" || [ -z "$ONLY_TARGET" ]; then
380.     section "Ollama Integrations"
381.     bash "$REPO_DIR/scripts/setup-ollama-config.sh" && success "Ollama integrations configured" || warn "Ollama config skipped (Ollama not started yet)"
382. fi
383. 
384. # =============================================================================
385. # Step 2: Claude Code MCP + Global Instructions
386. # =============================================================================
387. if should_run "claude"; then
388.     section "Claude Code"
389.     install_harness "Claude Code" "claude" "npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code"
390.     if [ -f "$HOME/.claude.json" ]; then
391.         bash "$REPO_DIR/scripts/setup-mcp.sh" && success "Claude Code MCP configured" || warn "MCP setup skipped (run Claude Code once, then re-run)"
392.     else
393.         warn "~/.claude.json not found — run Claude Code once to initialize, then re-run install.sh"
394.     fi
395.     # Global CLAUDE.md: proactive MCP usage + conciseness + token discipline
396.     bash "$REPO_DIR/scripts/setup-claude-md.sh" && success "Global CLAUDE.md installed" || warn "CLAUDE.md setup skipped"
397.     # Pre-tool-use hooks: enforce codesight before broad Glob/Grep searches
398.     bash "$REPO_DIR/scripts/setup-hooks.sh" && success "Claude Code hooks installed" || warn "Hooks setup skipped"
399.     
400.     # BMO-style learning capture hooks (real-time correction detection)
401.     bash "$REPO_DIR/scripts/setup-learning-hook.sh" && success "BMO learning capture hooks configured" || warn "Learning hook setup skipped"
402. fi
403. 
404. # =============================================================================
405. # Step 3: OpenClaw
406. # =============================================================================
407. if should_run "openclaw"; then
408.     section "OpenClaw"
409.     bash "$REPO_DIR/scripts/setup-openclaw.sh" --model "$OLLAMA_MODEL" && success "OpenClaw configured" || warn "OpenClaw setup skipped (not installed yet)"
410. fi
411. 
412. # =============================================================================
413. # Step 4: Codex
414. # =============================================================================
415. if should_run "codex"; then
416.     section "Codex"
417.     install_harness "Codex CLI" "codex" "npm install -g @openai/codex"
418.     bash "$REPO_DIR/scripts/setup-codex.sh" --model "$OLLAMA_MODEL" && success "Codex configured" || warn "Codex setup skipped (not installed yet)"
419. fi
420. 
421. # =============================================================================
422. # Step 5: Pi
423. # =============================================================================
424. if should_run "pi"; then
425.     section "Pi"
426.     install_harness "Pi" "pi" "npm install -g @mariozechner/pi-coding-agent"
427.     bash "$REPO_DIR/scripts/setup-pi.sh" --model "$OLLAMA_MODEL" && success "Pi configured" || warn "Pi setup skipped (not installed yet)"
428. fi
429. 
430. # =============================================================================
431. # Step 6: Copilot CLI
432. # =============================================================================
433. if should_run "copilot"; then
434.     section "Copilot CLI"
435.     install_harness "Copilot CLI" "copilot" "npm install -g @github/copilot"
436.     bash "$REPO_DIR/scripts/setup-copilot.sh" && success "Copilot CLI MCP configured" || warn "Copilot setup skipped (not installed yet)"
437. fi
438. 
439. # =============================================================================
440. # Step 7: Beads integration (bd CLI + beads-mcp + bd init)
441. # =============================================================================
442. if should_run "beads"; then
443.     section "Beads"
444.     bash "$REPO_DIR/scripts/setup-beads.sh" && success "Beads configured" || warn "Beads setup skipped (non-fatal)"
445. fi
446. 
447. # =============================================================================
448. # Step 8: Cleanup stale/invalid skills from previous installs
449. # =============================================================================
450. if [ -f "$REPO_DIR/scripts/cleanup-invalid-skills.sh" ]; then
451.     log "Cleaning up invalid skills from previous installs..."
452.     bash "$REPO_DIR/scripts/cleanup-invalid-skills.sh" 2>/dev/null || warn "Skill cleanup had issues (non-fatal)"
453. fi
454. 
455. # Done
456. # =============================================================================
457. echo ""
458. echo "╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗"
459. echo "║   Setup Complete ($OS_TYPE / $USER_SHELL)"
460. echo "╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝"
461. echo ""
462. echo "  Reload shell:   source $SHELL_RC"
463. echo ""
464. echo "  Launch harnesses:"
465. echo "    ollama launch claude      → Claude Code with MCP + skills"
466. echo "    ollama launch openclaw    → OpenClaw with web tools + subagents"
467. echo "    ollama launch pi          → Pi with subagents"
468. echo "    ollama launch codex       → Codex via ollama-launch provider"
469. echo "    ollama launch copilot     → Copilot CLI with MCP servers"
470. echo ""

930. # Step 5: Uninstall
931. # =============================================================================
932. 
933. uninstall() {
934.     log "Uninstalling skills layer..."
935. 
936.     # Remove shell integration from all possible rc files
937.     for rc_file in "$HOME/.zshrc" "$HOME/.bashrc" "$HOME/.bash_profile" "$HOME/.profile"; do
938.         if [ -f "$rc_file" ]; then
939.             if grep -q "# Skills Layer" "$rc_file" 2>/dev/null; then
940.                 sed -i.bak '/# Skills Layer/,/# End Skills Layer/d' "$rc_file" 2>/dev/null || true
941.                 success "Removed shell integration from $rc_file"
942.             fi
943.         fi
944.     done
945. 
946.     # Remove installed skill library clones
947.     rm -rf "$ECC_DIR"
948.     rm -rf "$CURATED_DIR"
949.     rm -rf "$SCIENCE_DIR"
950.     rm -rf "$CLAWBIO_DIR"
951.     rm -rf "$SKILLS_CACHE_DIR"
952. 
953.     # Remove only ECC-originated skills from Claude Code native skills dir.
954.     # Does NOT rm -rf ~/.claude/skills to protect user-created and learned skills.
955.     if [ -d "$HOME/.claude/skills" ]; then
956.         local removed_count=0
957.         # Remove symlinks (Pi/ECC-style) and dirs we installed
958.         for entry in "$HOME/.claude/skills"/*/; do
959.             [ -L "$entry" ] && rm "$entry" && removed_count=$((removed_count + 1)) && continue
960.             [ -f "${entry}SKILL.md" ] && rm -rf "$entry" && removed_count=$((removed_count + 1))
961.         done
962.         success "Removed $removed_count installed skills from ~/.claude/skills/"
963.         # Remove the learned/ subdir only if user explicitly asks
964.         # (learned skills are personal — never auto-delete)
965.     fi
966. 
967.     # Remove copied learning pipeline scripts
968.     local scripts_dest="$HOME/.claude/scripts"
969.     for script in "sync-learned-skills.sh" "extract-conversation-skills.py" "safe-install.sh"; do
970.         [ -f "$scripts_dest/$script" ] && rm "$scripts_dest/$script" && success "Removed $scripts_dest/$script"
971.     done
972.     # Remove scripts dir if now empty
973.     rmdir "$scripts_dest" 2>/dev/null || true
974. 
975.     success "Uninstall complete"
976.     echo ""
977.     echo "Note: ~/.claude/skills/learned/ (personal learned skills) was preserved."
978.     echo "Run 'source ~/.zshrc' or 'source ~/.bashrc' or restart terminal to fully clean up."
979. }
980. 
981. # =============================================================================
982. # Step 6: Show usage
983. # =============================================================================
984. 
985. show_usage() {
```
