```
1. #!/bin/bash
2. # =============================================================================
3. # Cross-Harness Skill Learner & Sync
4. # =============================================================================
5. # 1. Runs the 4-stage extraction pipeline on all harness histories
6. # 2. Tracks feedback/decay for existing learned skills
7. # 3. Syncs learned skills to all harnesses (codex, pi, openclaw)
8. # 4. Optionally prunes decayed skills
9. #
10. # Usage:
11. #   ./sync-learned-skills.sh              # full extract + sync
12. #   ./sync-learned-skills.sh --dry-run    # preview only
13. #   ./sync-learned-skills.sh --verbose    # detailed output
14. #   ./sync-learned-skills.sh --stats      # show usage/decay stats
15. #   ./sync-learned-skills.sh --prune      # archive decayed skills
16. #   ./sync-learned-skills.sh --sync-only  # skip extraction, just sync
17. # =============================================================================
18. 
19. set -e
20. 
21. DRY_RUN=false
22. VERBOSE=false
23. STATS_ONLY=false
24. PRUNE=false
25. SYNC_ONLY=false
26. USE_LLM=true  # LLM distillation is now default
27. SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
28. EXTRACTOR="$SCRIPT_DIR/extract-conversation-skills.py"
29. LEARNED_DIR="$HOME/.claude/skills/learned"
30. LOG_FILE="$SCRIPT_DIR/shared-learnings/sync.log"
31. LEARNINGS_MD="$SCRIPT_DIR/shared-learnings/learnings.md"
32. 
33. BLUE='\033[0;34m'; GREEN='\033[0;32m'; YELLOW='\033[1;33m'; CYAN='\033[0;36m'; NC='\033[0m'
34. log()     { echo -e "${BLUE}[LEARN]${NC} $1"; }
35. success() { echo -e "${GREEN}[OK]${NC} $1"; }
36. warn()    { echo -e "${YELLOW}[WARN]${NC} $1"; }
37. info()    { echo -e "${CYAN}[INFO]${NC} $1"; }
38. 
39. for arg in "$@"; do
40.     case $arg in
41.         --dry-run)   DRY_RUN=true ;;
42.         --verbose)   VERBOSE=true ;;
43.         --stats)     STATS_ONLY=true ;;
44.         --prune)     PRUNE=true ;;
45.         --sync-only) SYNC_ONLY=true ;;
46.         --no-llm)    USE_LLM=false ;;
47.     esac
48. done
49. 
50. mkdir -p "$SCRIPT_DIR/shared-learnings"
51. mkdir -p "$LEARNED_DIR"
52. 
53. # Stats-only mode
54. if $STATS_ONLY; then
55.     python3 "$EXTRACTOR" --stats ${VERBOSE:+--verbose}
56.     exit 0
57. fi
58. 
59. # Prune mode
60. if $PRUNE; then
61.     log "Pruning decayed skills..."
62.     python3 "$EXTRACTOR" --prune ${DRY_RUN:+--dry-run} ${VERBOSE:+--verbose}
63.     exit 0
64. fi
65. 
66. echo ""
67. echo "╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗"
68. echo "║   Cross-Harness Skill Learner                            ║"
69. echo "╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝"
70. echo ""
71. log "Started: $(date)"
72. 
73. # =============================================================================
74. # Step 1: Extract learned skills from all harness histories
75. # =============================================================================
76. 
77. if ! $SYNC_ONLY; then
78.     log "Step 1: Extracting patterns from conversation histories (4-stage pipeline)..."
79. 
80.     if [ ! -f "$EXTRACTOR" ]; then
81.         warn "Extractor not found: $EXTRACTOR"
82.         warn "Skipping extraction — will still sync existing learned skills"
83.     else
84.         EXTRACT_ARGS="--output $LEARNED_DIR"
85.         $VERBOSE && EXTRACT_ARGS="$EXTRACT_ARGS --verbose"
86.         $DRY_RUN && EXTRACT_ARGS="$EXTRACT_ARGS --dry-run"
87.         # Incremental extraction: only process new conversation files
88.         EXTRACT_ARGS="$EXTRACT_ARGS --incremental"
89. 
90.         # LLM distillation is now default; use --no-llm to disable
91.         if $USE_LLM; then
92.             EXTRACT_ARGS="$EXTRACT_ARGS --llm"
93.             log "LLM distillation enabled (default; use --no-llm to disable)"
94.         else
95.             log "LLM distillation disabled (--no-llm)"
96.         fi
97. 
98.         if python3 "$EXTRACTOR" $EXTRACT_ARGS; then
99.             success "Extraction complete"
100.         else
101.             warn "Extraction failed — see error output above"
102.         fi
103.     fi
104. else
105.     log "Step 1: Skipping extraction (--sync-only)"
106. fi
107. 
108. LEARNED_COUNT=$(ls "$LEARNED_DIR"/*.md 2>/dev/null | grep -v "/.usage" | wc -l | tr -d ' ')
109. log "Current learned skills: $LEARNED_COUNT"
110. 
111. # =============================================================================
112. # Step 2: Sync learned skills to all harnesses
113. # =============================================================================
114. 
115. log "Step 2: Syncing learned skills to all harnesses..."
116. SYNCED=0
117. 
118. sync_skill_to_harness() {
119.     local src_file="$1"
120.     local dst_dir="$2"
121.     local harness="$3"
122.     local skill_name
123.     skill_name=$(basename "$src_file" .md)
124. 
125.     # Skip dotfiles and SKILL.md
126.     [[ "$skill_name" == .* ]] && return 0
127.     [[ "$skill_name" == "SKILL" ]] && return 0
128. 
129.     local dst_skill_dir="$dst_dir/$skill_name"
130. 
131.     if $DRY_RUN; then
132.         info "  [dry-run] Would sync: $skill_name → $harness"
133.         return 0
134.     fi
135. 
136.     if [ -f "$dst_skill_dir/SKILL.md" ] && \
137.        [ "$src_file" -ot "$dst_skill_dir/SKILL.md" ]; then
138.         $VERBOSE && info "  Up-to-date: $skill_name in $harness"
139.         return 0
140.     fi
141. 
142.     mkdir -p "$dst_skill_dir"
143.     cp "$src_file" "$dst_skill_dir/SKILL.md"
144.     info "  Synced: $skill_name → $harness"
145.     SYNCED=$((SYNCED + 1))
146. }
147. 
148. # Sync to codex
149. if [ -d "$HOME/.codex/skills" ]; then
150.     for skill_file in "$LEARNED_DIR"/*.md; do
151.         [ -f "$skill_file" ] || continue
152.         sync_skill_to_harness "$skill_file" "$HOME/.codex/skills" "codex"
153.     done
154.     success "Codex: synced"
155. fi
156. 
157. # Sync to pi
158. PI_SKILLS_DIR="$HOME/.pi/agent/skills"
159. if [ -d "$HOME/.pi/agent" ]; then
160.     mkdir -p "$PI_SKILLS_DIR"
161.     for skill_file in "$LEARNED_DIR"/*.md; do
162.         [ -f "$skill_file" ] || continue
163.         sync_skill_to_harness "$skill_file" "$PI_SKILLS_DIR" "pi"
164.     done
165.     success "Pi: synced"
166. fi
167. 
168. # Sync to openclaw workspace skills
169. OPENCLAW_WS_SKILLS="$HOME/.openclaw/workspace/skills"
170. if [ -d "$HOME/.openclaw/workspace" ]; then
171.     mkdir -p "$OPENCLAW_WS_SKILLS"
172.     for skill_file in "$LEARNED_DIR"/*.md; do
173.         [ -f "$skill_file" ] || continue
174.         skill_name=$(basename "$skill_file" .md)
175.         [[ "$skill_name" == .* ]] && continue
176.         [[ "$skill_name" == "SKILL" ]] && continue
177.         dst_dir="$OPENCLAW_WS_SKILLS/$skill_name"
178.         if $DRY_RUN; then
179.             info "  [dry-run] Would sync: $skill_name → openclaw"
180.         elif [ ! -f "$dst_dir/SKILL.md" ] || [ "$skill_file" -nt "$dst_dir/SKILL.md" ]; then
181.             mkdir -p "$dst_dir"
182.             cp "$skill_file" "$dst_dir/SKILL.md"
183.             info "  Synced: $skill_name → openclaw"
184.             SYNCED=$((SYNCED + 1))
185.         fi
186.     done
187.     success "OpenClaw: synced"
188. fi
189. 
190. # Claude skills directory symlink
191. CLAUDE_SKILLS_LEARNED="$HOME/.claude/skills/learned"
192. if [ -d "$HOME/.claude/skills" ] && [ ! -d "$CLAUDE_SKILLS_LEARNED" ]; then
193.     ln -sfn "$LEARNED_DIR" "$CLAUDE_SKILLS_LEARNED" 2>/dev/null || true
194. fi
195. 
196. # =============================================================================
197. # Step 3: Update learnings log
198. # =============================================================================
199. 
200. log "Step 3: Updating shared learnings log..."
201. {
202.     echo ""
203.     echo "## Sync: $(date)"
204.     echo "- Learned skills total: $LEARNED_COUNT"
205.     echo "- Newly synced: $SYNCED"
206.     echo "- Harnesses updated: codex, pi, openclaw"
207. } >> "$LEARNINGS_MD" 2>/dev/null || true
208. 
209. echo "$(date) | learned=$LEARNED_COUNT synced=$SYNCED dry_run=$DRY_RUN" >> "$LOG_FILE" 2>/dev/null || true
210. 
211. echo ""
212. if $DRY_RUN; then
213.     warn "DRY RUN — no changes written"
214. else
215.     success "Sync complete: $SYNCED skills propagated across harnesses"
216.     success "Learned skills total: $LEARNED_COUNT"
217. fi
218. echo ""
```

</details>


### ✅ `view`

**view the file at /Users/chazh/Documents/GitHub/ai-skillweave/scripts/consolidate-learnings.py.**

/Users/chazh/Documents/GitHub/ai-skillweave/scripts/consolidate-learnings.py

<details>
<summary>276 lines</summary>

```
1. #!/usr/bin/env python3
2. """
3. BMO-Style Learning Consolidation
4. Converts captured learning events into skills at session end.
5. Based on: https://github.com/joelhans/bmo-agent
6. """
7. 
8. import json
9. import os
10. import re
11. from pathlib import Path
12. from collections import defaultdict
13. from datetime import datetime
14. 
15. LEARNINGS_DIR = Path.home() / ".claude" / "skills" / "learned" / "events"
16. SKILLS_DIR = Path.home() / ".claude" / "skills" / "learned"
17. 
18. def load_events():
19.     """Load all pending learning events."""
20.     events = []
21.     if not LEARNINGS_DIR.exists():
22.         return events
23.     
24.     for f in LEARNINGS_DIR.glob("*.json"):
25.         try:
26.             with open(f) as fp:
27.                 data = json.load(fp)
28.                 if data.get("status") == "pending":
29.                     events.append(data)
30.         except:
31.             continue
32.     return sorted(events, key=lambda e: e.get("timestamp", ""))
33. 
34. def cluster_events(events):
35.     """
36.     Cluster similar events together.
37.     Uses sentence-transformer semantic similarity when available,
38.     falling back to Jaccard word overlap.
39.     """
40.     if not events:
41.         return {}
42.     
43.     # Try semantic clustering with sentence-transformers
44.     if len(events) >= 5:
45.         try:
46.             from sentence_transformers import SentenceTransformer
47.             import numpy as np
48.             
49.             texts = [e.get("message", "") for e in events]
50.             encoder = SentenceTransformer('all-MiniLM-L6-v2')
51.             embeddings = encoder.encode(texts, show_progress_bar=False)
52.             
53.             # Normalize
54.             norms = np.linalg.norm(embeddings, axis=1, keepdims=True)
55.             norms[norms == 0] = 1
56.             embeddings = embeddings / norms
57.             
58.             clusters = []
59.             used = set()
60.             for i in range(len(events)):
61.                 if i in used:
62.                     continue
63.                 cluster = [events[i]]
64.                 used.add(i)
65.                 for j in range(i + 1, len(events)):
66.                     if j in used:
67.                         continue
68.                     sim = float(np.dot(embeddings[i], embeddings[j]))
69.                     if sim >= 0.72:
70.                         cluster.append(events[j])
71.                         used.add(j)
72.                 # Cluster key: type + first 3 words of first event
73.                 first_msg = events[i].get("message", "")
74.                 words = re.findall(r"\b\w+\b", first_msg.lower())
75.                 key_words = [w for w in words[:3] if len(w) > 3]
76.                 cluster_key = f"{events[i].get('type', 'unknown')}_{'-'.join(key_words) or 'general'}"
77.                 clusters.append((cluster_key, cluster))
78.             
79.             return dict(clusters)
80.         except ImportError:
81.             pass  # Fall through to keyword-based clustering
82.         except Exception:
83.             pass  # Fall through on any error
84.     
85.     # Fallback: improved keyword-based clustering with Jaccard similarity
86.     stop_words = {
87.         "the", "a", "an", "is", "are", "was", "were", "be", "been", "being",
88.         "have", "has", "had", "do", "does", "did", "will", "would", "could",
89.         "should", "may", "might", "must", "shall", "can", "need", "dare",
90.         "ought", "used", "to", "of", "in", "for", "on", "with", "at", "by",
91.         "from", "as", "into", "through", "during", "before", "after", "above",
92.         "below", "between", "under", "again", "further", "then", "once", "that",
93.         "this", "these", "those", "i", "you", "he", "she", "it", "we", "they",
94.         "what", "which", "who", "whom", "whose", "where", "when", "why", "how",
95.         "all", "each", "every", "both", "few", "more", "most", "other", "some",
96.         "such", "no", "nor", "not", "only", "own", "same", "so", "than", "too",
97.         "very", "just", "also", "now", "here", "there", "if", "but", "or", "and",
98.     }
99.     
100.     def get_keywords(msg):
101.         words = re.findall(r"\b\w+\b", msg.lower())
102.         return set(w for w in words if len(w) > 3 and w not in stop_words)
103.     
104.     clusters = []
105.     used = set()
106.     for i in range(len(events)):
107.         if i in used:
108.             continue
109.         cluster = [events[i]]
110.         used.add(i)
111.         kw_i = get_keywords(events[i].get("message", ""))
112.         
113.         for j in range(i + 1, len(events)):
114.             if j in used:
115.                 continue
116.             kw_j = get_keywords(events[j].get("message", ""))
117.             union = kw_i | kw_j
118.             inter = kw_i & kw_j
119.             sim = len(inter) / len(union) if union else 0
120.             if sim >= 0.5:
121.                 cluster.append(events[j])
122.                 used.add(j)
123.                 kw_i |= kw_j  # Merge keywords for transitive clustering
124.         
125.         first_msg = events[i].get("message", "")
126.         words = re.findall(r"\b\w+\b", first_msg.lower())
127.         key_words = [w for w in words[:3] if len(w) > 3]
128.         cluster_key = f"{events[i].get('type', 'unknown')}_{'-'.join(key_words) or 'general'}"
129.         clusters.append((cluster_key, cluster))
130.     
131.     return dict(clusters)
132. 
133. def create_skill(cluster_name, events):
134.     """Create a skill from a cluster of events."""
135.     if not events:
136.         return None
137.     
138.     messages = [e.get("message", "") for e in events]
139.     event_type = events[0].get("type", "pattern")
140.     projects = set(e.get("project", "unknown") for e in events)
141.     sessions = set(e.get("session", "unknown") for e in events)
142.     
143.     # Generate skill name from cluster (Codex limit: 64 chars)
144.     skill_name = cluster_name.replace("_", "-").replace(" ", "-")[:64]
145.     skill_name = skill_name.rstrip("-")
146.     
147.     # Build skill content based on type
148.     if event_type == "correction":
149.         # Extract the correction pattern
150.         condition = "When the user corrects your approach or output"
151.         strategy = messages[0][:300] if messages else "Apply the correction immediately and verify"
152.         anti_pattern = "Ignoring user corrections or repeating the same mistake"
153.         priority = "critical"
154.     elif event_type == "preference":
155.         condition = "When working on tasks involving user preferences"
156.         strategy = messages[0][:300] if messages else "Apply stated preferences consistently"
157.         anti_pattern = "N/A"
158.         priority = "high"
159.     else:  # pattern
160.         condition = "When encountering recurring patterns or best practices"
161.         strategy = messages[0][:300] if messages else "Follow established patterns"
162.         anti_pattern = "N/A"
163.         priority = "medium"
164.     
165.     # Quote description if it contains YAML-special characters
166.     desc = f"Learned from {len(events)} session event(s)"
167.     if ':' in desc or '"' in desc:
168.         desc = f'"{desc}"'
169.     evidence = "\n".join([f"- {m[:200]}" for m in messages[:5]])
170.     
171.     skill_content = f"""---
172. name: {skill_name}
173. description: {desc}
174. origin: bmo-learning-capture
175. tags: [learned, {event_type}, auto-generated]
176. priority: {priority}
177. ---
178. 
179. # {skill_name.replace("-", " ").title()}
180. 
181. ## When to Use
182. 
183. {condition}.
184. 
185. ## Operating Principles
186. 
187. 1. {strategy}.
188. 
189. """
190.     if anti_pattern != "N/A":
191.         skill_content += f"""## Anti-patterns
192. 
193. - {anti_pattern}.
194. 
195. """
196.     
197.     skill_content += f"""## Evidence
198. 
199. {evidence}
200. 
201. ## Provenance
202. 
203. - **Events:** {len(events)}
204. - **Sessions:** {len(sessions)}
205. - **Projects:** {len(projects)}
206. - **First captured:** {events[0].get('timestamp', 'unknown')}
207. - **Last captured:** {events[-1].get('timestamp', 'unknown')}
208. """
209.     
210.     return skill_name, skill_content
211. 
212. def sanitize_filename(name):
213.     """Create safe filename from skill name."""
214.     # Keep only alphanumeric, hyphens, underscores
215.     safe = re.sub(r'[^a-z0-9\-_]', '', name.lower())
216.     # Remove consecutive hyphens/underscores
217.     safe = re.sub(r'[-_]+', '-', safe)
218.     # Limit length (Codex skill name limit: 64 chars)
219.     return safe[:64] or "learned-skill"
220. 
221. def write_skill(skill_name, content):
222.     """Write skill to file."""
223.     skill_file = SKILLS_DIR / f"{sanitize_filename(skill_name)}.md"
224.     
225.     with open(skill_file, "w") as f:
226.         f.write(content)
227.     
228.     return skill_file
229. 
230. def mark_processed(events):
231.     """Mark events as processed."""
232.     for event in events:
233.         event_ts = event.get("timestamp")
234.         for f in LEARNINGS_DIR.glob("*.json"):
235.             try:
236.                 with open(f) as fp:
237.                     data = json.load(fp)
238.                 if data.get("timestamp") == event_ts:
239.                     data["status"] = "processed"
240.                     data["processed_at"] = datetime.now().isoformat()
241.                     with open(f, "w") as fp:
242.                         json.dump(data, fp, indent=2)
243.                     break
244.             except:
245.                 continue
246. 
247. def main():
248.     print("╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗")
249.     print("║   BMO-Style Learning Consolidation                      ║")
250.     print("╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝\n")
251.     
252.     events = load_events()
253.     print(f"Found {len(events)} pending learning events")
254.     
255.     if not events:
256.         print("No events to consolidate")
257.         return
258.     
259.     clusters = cluster_events(events)
260.     print(f"Clustered into {len(clusters)} groups\n")
261.     
262.     skills_created = 0
263.     for cluster_name, cluster_events in clusters.items():
264.         result = create_skill(cluster_name, cluster_events)
265.         if result:
266.             skill_name, content = result
267.             skill_file = write_skill(skill_name, content)
268.             print(f"✓ Created: {skill_file.name} (from {len(cluster_events)} events)")
269.             mark_processed(cluster_events)
270.             skills_created += 1
271.     
272.     print(f"\n[OK] Created {skills_created} skills from {len(events)} events")
273. 
274. if __name__ == "__main__":
275.     main()
276.
```

</details>


### ✅ `view`

**view the file at /Users/chazh/Documents/GitHub/ai-skillweave/README.md.**

/Users/chazh/Documents/GitHub/ai-skillweave/README.md (lines 540-640)

<details>
<summary>101 lines</summary>

````
540. - **Batch**: On `safe-install.sh` (use `--no-learn` to skip), on `update-ecc.sh` (Step 4: learn-sync), or manually via `learn-sync` alias
541. - Skills propagate to all harnesses via `sync-learned-skills.sh`
542. 
543. Both paths write to `~/.claude/skills/learned/` using the same ECC-compatible SKILL.md format.
544. 
545. Only **2 of 4 memory types** produce generalizable skills: `heuristic` and `anti_pattern`. Preferences are per-user; domain knowledge is project-specific. This ensures learned skills are universally applicable, not project noise.
546. 
547. ### Installing ECC
548. 
549. ```bash
550. ./safe-install.sh                                    # ECC only
551. ./safe-install.sh --with-science                     # ECC + K-Dense scientific skills (134 skills)
552. ./safe-install.sh --with-bio                         # ECC + ClawBio bioinformatics (56 skills)
553. ./safe-install.sh --with-science --with-bio          # Full install: all skill sources
554. ```
555. 
556. ### Keeping ECC Up to Date
557. 
558. When ECC adds new skills upstream, pull and rebuild without a full re-install:
559. 
560. ```bash
561. scripts/update-ecc.sh           # Pull latest ECC + rebuild cache + re-sync all harnesses
562. scripts/update-ecc.sh --check   # Just check if updates are available
563. scripts/update-ecc.sh --force   # Force cache rebuild even if already up to date
564. ```
565. 
566. To update K-Dense scientific skills, re-run with `--with-science`:
567. 
568. ```bash
569. ./safe-install.sh --with-science   # Re-clone K-Dense repo + rebuild cache + re-sync
570. ```
571. 
572. To update ClawBio bioinformatics skills, re-run with `--with-bio`:
573. 
574. ```bash
575. ./safe-install.sh --with-bio   # Re-clone ClawBio repo + rebuild cache + re-sync
576. ```
577. 
578. ---
579. 
580. ## Context Window & Skills Injection
581. 
582. ### Why Claude was hitting "context limit reached"
583. 
584. The original installer injected the full combined skills cache (`combined-skills.txt`, ~5.5MB ≈ **1.375M tokens**) into every Claude session via `--append-system-prompt-file`. Claude Sonnet 4.6 and Opus 4.7 have a **200K token** context window — this means the skills injection alone was **6.9× the entire context budget**, triggering an explicit `context_length_exceeded` error from the Anthropic API on every single session.
585. 
586. Ollama models (Kimi, Qwen, etc.) silently truncate prompts to their `num_ctx` limit instead of erroring — which is why `ollama launch kimi` seemed to "work" while `ollama launch claude` kept failing.
587. 
588. ### The fix: lean-skills injection
589. 
590. The installer now injects **only your personal learned skills** (`lean-skills.txt`) at session start — typically **1–2K tokens** (under 1% of the context window). The full combined cache is still built and stored at `~/.claude/skills-cache/combined-skills.txt` for reference and local search, but is never injected automatically.
591. 
592. | File | Size | Tokens | Used for |
593. |------|------|--------|---------|
594. | `lean-skills.txt` | ~6-50KB | ~1.5–12K | Injected into every Claude session via `--append-system-prompt-file` |
595. | `combined-skills.txt` | ~5.5MB | ~1.375M | Reference only — local search, update-ecc.sh, never auto-injected |
596. 
597. The ~450 ECC/K-Dense/ClawBio SKILL.md files in `~/.claude/skills/` are loaded natively by Claude Code via its built-in `/skills` feature — no injection overhead, loaded on-demand, no context window impact.
598. 
599. ### Prompt caching (still active)
600. 
601. Even with lean injection, Claude Code's prompt caching is still configured via `tengu_system_prompt_global_cache: true` in `~/.claude.json` — the lean block is cached after session 1 and reused across sessions.
602. 
603. ---
604. 
605. ## Token Efficiency — Skills Injection + Prompt Caching
606. 
607. With `lean-skills.txt` injection, per-session context overhead is minimal. Prompt caching keeps it economical even for heavy users:
608. 
609. | Event | Cost |
610. |-------|------|
611. | Session 1 (cache miss) | Full input price for lean-skills tokens (~1-2K tokens) |
612. | Session 2+ same day (cache hit) | ~90% discount on cached tokens |
613. | Each conversation turn | Only new tokens in the exchange |
614. 
615. > **Note:** Exact costs depend on your plan and model. Check [Anthropic's pricing page](https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/about-claude/pricing) for current rates.
616. 
617. ---
618. 
619. ## MCP Tool Enforcement
620. 
621. Three layers ensure Claude actually uses MCP tools instead of raw file scanning:
622. 
623. | Layer | Mechanism | Strength |
624. | ----- | --------- | -------- |
625. | `~/.claude/CLAUDE.md` | Global instructions loaded every session | Soft — can be ignored |
626. | Lean skills preamble | Short directive injected at top of `lean-skills.txt` | Soft — reinforces CLAUDE.md |
627. | `hooks/codesight-redirect.sh` | PreToolUse hook blocks broad Glob/Grep | **Hard — actually stops the call** |
628. 
629. ### How the hook works
630. 
631. When Claude attempts a broad codebase search (any `**` glob pattern or bare Glob in a codesight-enabled project), the hook:
632. 
633. 1. Detects `.codesight/` exists in the project tree
634. 2. Blocks the tool call (exit 2 → message sent back to Claude)
635. 3. Tells Claude to call `codesight_get_summary` first
636. 4. After the first reminder per session, allows all subsequent searches through (no repeated nagging)
637. 
638. ```bash
639. # Hook fires on: Glob("**/*.ts"), Grep(path="/your/project", ...)
640. # Passes through: Read("/path/to/specific/file.ts"), Grep("specific-function-name")
````

</details>


### ✅ `view`

<details>
<summary>46 lines</summary>

````
800. # 2. Open the output folder
801. open configs/desktop-skills/
802. ```
803. 
804. Then in Claude Desktop:
805. 
806. 1. Go to **Customize** → **Skills**
807. 2. Click **+** → **Upload a skill**
808. 3. Select `.skill` files from `configs/desktop-skills/` (you can select multiple)
809. 
810. To update skills later (e.g. after `update-ecc.sh`), re-run `build-desktop-skills.sh` and re-upload.
811. 
812. ### Token economics (Desktop)
813. 
814. | Component | Token cost |
815. |-----------|-----------|
816. | MCP servers | Zero until invoked |
817. | Skills (full tier) | ~90K+ tokens on first message, cached after that |
818. | Skills (standard tier) | ~55K+ tokens on first message, cached after that |
819. 
820. Skills are injected as Project instructions (system prompt) and cached by Claude after the first turn — similar to prompt caching in Claude Code CLI.
821. 
822. ### CLI vs Desktop comparison
823. 
824. | Feature | Claude Code CLI | Claude Desktop GUI |
825. |---------|----------------|-------------------|
826. | Setup script | `install.sh` | `scripts/setup-claude-desktop.sh` |
827. | Config file | `~/.claude.json` | `~/Library/Application Support/Claude/claude_desktop_config.json` |
828. | MCP servers | 9 auto (incl. beads) + manual API-key servers | 6 auto + API-key servers copied from CLI; skillgraph via Settings → Integrations |
829. | Skills injection | ~450 files via native `/skills` + lean cache (~1-2K tokens) | 88 + personal + K-Dense via Project instructions |
830. | Prompt caching | `tengu_system_prompt_global_cache: true` | Built-in Project caching |
831. | Shell wrappers | `_claude_with_skills` in `.bashrc`/`.zshrc` | N/A (GUI app) |
832. 
833. ---
834. 
835. ## Beads Integration
836. 
837. > **Powered by [beads](https://github.com/gastownhall/beads)** — AI-native cross-session work item tracking. Works with every harness (Claude Code, Codex, Copilot, OpenClaw, Pi) via MCP.
838. 
839. ### What beads does
840. 
841. Beads lets you create, track, and share work items across AI coding sessions. Unlike session memory (which is per-harness), beads items persist in your project via `AGENTS.md` — a harness-agnostic file every AI tool reads.
842. 
843. | Command | What it does |
844. |---------|-------------|
845. | `bd prime` | Gives Claude an AI-optimised context dump of all open work items — run at session start |
````

/Users/chazh/Documents/GitHub/ai-skillweave/configs/claude-mcp-servers.json

<details>

```
1. {
2.   "_comment": "MCP servers for Claude Code (~/.claude.json mcpServers key).",

140.     local install_cmd="$3"
141.     if command -v "$cmd" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
142.         success "$name already installed: $(command -v "$cmd")"
143.         return 0
144.     fi
145.     log "Installing $name..."
146.     if eval "$install_cmd"; then
147.         success "$name installed"
148.         return 0
149.     else
150.         warn "$name installation failed — install manually, then re-run"
151.         return 1
152.     fi
153. }
154. 
155. # --- git ---
156. if [ "$OS_TYPE" = "macOS" ]; then
157.     _install_if_missing git "git" "brew install git"
158. else
159.     if command -v apt-get >/dev/null 2>&1; then
160.         _install_if_missing git "git" "sudo apt-get update -qq && sudo apt-get install -y -qq git"
161.     elif command -v dnf >/dev/null 2>&1; then
162.         _install_if_missing git "git" "sudo dnf install -y git"
163.     elif command -v pacman >/dev/null 2>&1; then
164.         _install_if_missing git "git" "sudo pacman -S --noconfirm git"
165.     fi
166. fi
167. 
168. # --- node + npm ---
169. if ! command -v node >/dev/null 2>&1 || ! command -v npm >/dev/null 2>&1; then
170.     log "Node.js/npm not found — installing..."
171.     if [ "$OS_TYPE" = "macOS" ]; then
172.         brew install node 2>/dev/null || warn "brew install node failed"
173.     else
174.         # Use NodeSource setup for LTS Node 22
175.         curl -fsSL https://deb.nodesource.com/setup_22.x | bash - 2>/dev/null && \
176.             apt-get install -y nodejs 2>/dev/null || \
177.             warn "NodeSource install failed — install node manually"
178.     fi
179.     # Verify
180.     if command -v node >/dev/null 2>&1 && command -v npm >/dev/null 2>&1; then
181.         success "Node.js $(node --version) + npm $(npm --version) installed"
182.     else
183.         warn "Node.js/npm still not available — npx-based MCP servers won't work"
184.     fi
185. else
186.     success "Node.js $(node --version) + npm $(npm --version) already installed"
187. fi
188. 
189. # --- ollama ---
190. if ! command -v ollama >/dev/null 2>&1; then
191.     log "Ollama not found — installing..."
192.     curl -fsSL https://ollama.com/install.sh | sh && \
193.         success "Ollama installed" || \
194.         warn "Ollama install failed — install manually from https://ollama.com"
195. else
196.     success "Ollama already installed: $(command -v ollama)"
197. fi
198. 
199. # --- python3 + pip ---
200. if ! command -v python3 >/dev/null 2>&1; then
201.     log "python3 not found — installing..."
202.     if [ "$OS_TYPE" = "macOS" ]; then
203.         brew install python3 2>/dev/null || warn "brew install python3 failed"
204.     else
205.         if command -v apt-get >/dev/null 2>&1; then
206.             sudo apt-get update -qq && sudo apt-get install -y -qq python3 python3-pip python3-venv
207.         elif command -v dnf >/dev/null 2>&1; then
208.             sudo dnf install -y python3 python3-pip
209.         elif command -v pacman >/dev/null 2>&1; then
210.             sudo pacman -S --noconfirm python python-pip
211.         fi
212.     fi
213. fi
214. if command -v python3 >/dev/null 2>&1; then
215.     success "python3: $(command -v python3)"
216. else
217.     error "python3 is required but could not be installed automatically"
218. fi
219. 
220. # =============================================================================
221. # Step 0: Preflight checks
222. # =============================================================================
223. section "Preflight"
224. 
225. command -v python3 >/dev/null 2>&1 || error "python3 required ($PKG_HINT python3)"
226. 
227. # Install Python dependencies for auto-learning pipeline
228. log "Installing Python dependencies for skill extraction (sentence-transformers, scikit-learn)..."
229. _install_python_deps() {
230.     local pkgs="sentence-transformers>=3.0.0 scikit-learn>=1.5.0"
231.     # Detect virtualenv: --user is forbidden inside venv/conda
232.     local in_venv=""
233.     if python3 -c "import sys; sys.exit(0 if (sys.prefix != sys.base_prefix) else 1)" 2>/dev/null; then
234.         in_venv=1
235.     fi
236.     if [ -n "$in_venv" ]; then
237.         log "Detected Python virtualenv — skipping --user flag"
238.         if python3 -m pip install --quiet $pkgs 2>&1; then
239.             success "Python deps installed (venv)"
240.             return 0
241.         fi
242.     else
243.         # Try multiple pip invocation strategies; show errors so user can diagnose
244.         if python3 -m pip install --user --quiet $pkgs 2>&1; then
245.             success "Python deps installed (user site)"
246.             return 0
247.         fi
248.         warn "pip --user failed — trying without --user..."
249.         if python3 -m pip install --quiet $pkgs 2>&1; then
250.             success "Python deps installed (system site)"
251.             return 0
252.         fi
253.     fi
254.     if command -v pip3 >/dev/null 2>&1 && pip3 install --quiet $pkgs 2>&1; then
255.         success "Python deps installed via pip3"
256.         return 0
257.     fi
258.     warn "All pip install attempts failed (see errors above)"
259.     warn "Try manually: python3 -m pip install sentence-transformers scikit-learn"
260.     return 1
261. }
262. _install_python_deps || warn "Python deps missing — the learning pipeline will fail until fixed"
263. 
264. command -v node >/dev/null 2>&1 || warn "node not found — npx-based MCP servers won't work ($PKG_HINT node)"
265. command -v git >/dev/null 2>&1 || warn "git not found — ECC skills install will be skipped"
266. 
267. if command -v ollama >/dev/null 2>&1; then
268.     success "ollama: $(command -v ollama)"
269.     if curl -s --max-time 3 http://localhost:11434/api/status >/dev/null 2>&1; then
270.         success "Ollama server: running"
271. 
272.         # Check if requested model is available
273.         if ollama list 2>/dev/null | grep -q "^${OLLAMA_MODEL}"; then
274.             success "Model available: $OLLAMA_MODEL"
275.         else
276.             warn "Model '$OLLAMA_MODEL' not in local list (may be cloud model — OK)"
277.             warn "If local model needed: ollama pull $OLLAMA_MODEL"
278.         fi
279.     else
280.         if [ "$OS_TYPE" = "macOS" ]; then
281.             warn "Ollama server not running — start it: open /Applications/Ollama.app"
282.         else
283.             warn "Ollama server not running — start it: ollama serve"
284.         fi
285.         warn "Continuing setup; start Ollama before using harnesses"
286.     fi
287. else
288.     warn "ollama not installed — install from https://ollama.com"
289.     warn "Continuing setup without Ollama; harnesses that need it will fail at runtime"
290. fi
291. 
292. # Disable Zscaler tray if running (macOS only, no sudo needed)
293. if [ "$OS_TYPE" = "macOS" ] && launchctl list 2>/dev/null | grep -q "zscaler"; then
294.     log "Detected Zscaler — disabling tray (no sudo needed)..."
295.     bash "$REPO_DIR/scripts/disable-zscaler.sh" --tray 2>/dev/null || true
296. fi
297. 
298. # =============================================================================
299. # Step 0b: Pre-install npx-based MCP servers globally (prevents timeout on first launch)
300. # =============================================================================
301. if command -v npm >/dev/null 2>&1; then
302.     log "Pre-installing MCP server packages globally (prevents first-launch timeouts)..."
303.     # Install silently in background; failures are non-fatal (npx will fallback)
304.     npm install -g @modelcontextprotocol/server-memory \
305.         @modelcontextprotocol/server-sequential-thinking \
306.         @upstash/context7-mcp@latest \
307.         @playwright/mcp \
308.         codesight \
309.         token-optimizer-mcp 2>/dev/null || warn "Some MCP packages failed to pre-install (npx fallback will work but may be slower on first launch)"
310.     success "MCP server packages pre-installed"
311. else
312.     warn "npm not found — MCP servers will be installed on first launch (may timeout)"
313. fi
314. 
315. # =============================================================================
316. # Harness installer helper
317. # =============================================================================
318. install_harness() {
319.     local name="$1"
320.     local cmd="$2"
321.     local install_cmd="$3"
322. 
323.     if command -v "$cmd" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
324.         success "$name already installed: $(command -v "$cmd")"
325.         return 0
326.     fi
327. 
328.     if ! command -v npm >/dev/null 2>&1; then
329.         warn "npm not found — cannot auto-install $name"
330.         warn "Install manually, then re-run install.sh"
331.         return 1
332.     fi
333. 
334.     log "Installing $name..."
335.     if eval "$install_cmd"; then
336.         success "$name installed"
337.         return 0
338.     else
339.         warn "$name installation failed — install manually, then re-run"
340.         return 1
```

**view the file at /Users/chazh/Documents/GitHub/ai-skillweave/scripts/setup-ollama-config.sh.**

/Users/chazh/Documents/GitHub/ai-skillweave/scripts/setup-ollama-config.sh

<details>
<summary>84 lines</summary>

```
1. #!/bin/bash
2. # =============================================================================
3. # setup-ollama-config.sh — Apply Ollama integration→model mapping
4. # =============================================================================
5. # Applies configs/ollama-integrations.json to ~/.ollama/config.json.
6. # Preserves last_selection and any other non-integrations keys.
7. # Safe to re-run (idempotent).
8. #
9. # Usage:
10. #   scripts/setup-ollama-config.sh
11. # =============================================================================
12. set -e
13. 
14. REPO_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")/.." && pwd)"
15. TEMPLATE="$REPO_DIR/configs/ollama-integrations.json"
16. TARGET="$HOME/.ollama/config.json"
17. 
18. BLUE='\033[0;34m'; GREEN='\033[0;32m'; YELLOW='\033[1;33m'; RED='\033[0;31m'; NC='\033[0m'
19. log()     { echo -e "${BLUE}[OLLAMA]${NC} $1"; }
20. success() { echo -e "${GREEN}[OK]${NC} $1"; }
21. warn()    { echo -e "${YELLOW}[WARN]${NC} $1"; }
22. error()   { echo -e "${RED}[ERROR]${NC} $1"; exit 1; }
23. 
24. echo ""
25. echo "╔══════════════════════════════════════╗"
26. echo "║   Ollama Integrations Setup          ║"
27. echo "╚══════════════════════════════════════╝"
28. echo ""
29. 
30. command -v python3 >/dev/null 2>&1 || error "python3 not found"
31. [ -f "$TEMPLATE" ] || error "Template not found: $TEMPLATE"
32. 
33. if [ ! -f "$TARGET" ]; then
34.     warn "~/.ollama/config.json not found — Ollama not configured yet"
35.     warn "Start Ollama first (open /Applications/Ollama.app), then re-run"
36.     exit 0
37. fi
38. 
39. # Backup
40. BACKUP="$TARGET.bak_setup_$(date +%Y%m%d_%H%M%S)"
41. cp "$TARGET" "$BACKUP"
42. success "Backup: $BACKUP"
43. 
44. python3 << PYEOF
45. import json, os
46. 
47. target_path = "$TARGET"
48. template_path = "$TEMPLATE"
49. 
50. with open(target_path) as f:
51.     config = json.load(f)
52. 
53. with open(template_path) as f:
54.     template = json.load(f)
55. 
56. # Only update the integrations key — preserve last_selection and other keys
57. new_integrations = template.get("integrations", {})
58. 
59. # Remove comment/metadata keys (start with _)
60. new_integrations = {k: v for k, v in new_integrations.items() if not k.startswith("_")}
61. 
62. old_integrations = config.get("integrations", {})
63. config["integrations"] = new_integrations
64. 
65. with open(target_path, "w") as f:
66.     json.dump(config, f, indent=2)
67. 
68. # Show what changed
69. for name, cfg in new_integrations.items():
70.     old_models = old_integrations.get(name, {}).get("models", [])
71.     new_models = cfg.get("models", [])
72.     if old_models != new_models:
73.         print(f"  Updated {name}: {old_models} → {new_models}")
74.     else:
75.         print(f"  Unchanged {name}: {new_models}")
76. 
77. print(f"\nlast_selection preserved: {config.get('last_selection','?')}")
78. PYEOF
79. 
80. success "Ollama integrations applied: $TARGET"
81. echo ""
82. log "Changes take effect on next Ollama launch."
83. echo ""
84.
```

</details>


### ✅ `view`

**view the file at /Users/chazh/Documents/GitHub/ai-skillweave/scripts/setup-mcp.sh.**

/Users/chazh/Documents/GitHub/ai-skillweave/scripts/setup-mcp.sh

<details>
<summary>112 lines</summary>

```
1. #!/bin/bash
2. # =============================================================================
3. # setup-mcp.sh — Inject MCP servers into Claude Code (~/.claude.json)
4. # =============================================================================
5. # Merges configs/claude-mcp-servers.json into the mcpServers key of
6. # ~/.claude.json. Safe to re-run (idempotent — won't overwrite existing entries).
7. #
8. # Usage:
9. #   scripts/setup-mcp.sh [--force]
10. #   --force : overwrite existing MCP server entries with template values
11. # =============================================================================
12. set -e
13. 
14. REPO_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")/.." && pwd)"
15. TEMPLATE="$REPO_DIR/configs/claude-mcp-servers.json"
16. TARGET="$HOME/.claude.json"
17. 
18. BLUE='\033[0;34m'; GREEN='\033[0;32m'; YELLOW='\033[1;33m'; RED='\033[0;31m'; NC='\033[0m'
19. log()     { echo -e "${BLUE}[MCP]${NC} $1"; }
20. success() { echo -e "${GREEN}[OK]${NC} $1"; }
21. warn()    { echo -e "${YELLOW}[WARN]${NC} $1"; }
22. error()   { echo -e "${RED}[ERROR]${NC} $1"; exit 1; }
23. 
24. FORCE=false
25. [[ "$*" == *"--force"* ]] && FORCE=true
26. 
27. # Determine CA cert path (Zscaler or system default)
28. CA_CERT=""
29. if [ -f "$HOME/.mamba_ca_bundle.pem" ]; then
30.     CA_CERT="$HOME/.mamba_ca_bundle.pem"
31. elif [ -f "/etc/ssl/certs/ca-certificates.crt" ]; then
32.     CA_CERT="/etc/ssl/certs/ca-certificates.crt"
33. fi
34. 
35. echo ""
36. echo "╔══════════════════════════════════════╗"
37. echo "║   MCP Setup for Claude Code          ║"
38. echo "╚══════════════════════════════════════╝"
39. echo ""
40. 
41. # Prereqs
42. command -v python3 >/dev/null 2>&1 || error "python3 not found"
43. [ -f "$TARGET" ] || error "~/.claude.json not found. Install Claude Code first."
44. [ -f "$TEMPLATE" ] || error "Template not found: $TEMPLATE"
45. 
46. # Backup
47. BACKUP="$TARGET.bak_mcp_$(date +%Y%m%d_%H%M%S)"
48. cp "$TARGET" "$BACKUP"
49. success "Backup: $BACKUP"
50. 
51. # Apply
52. python3 << PYEOF
53. import json, os, sys
54. 
55. target_path = os.path.expanduser("~/.claude.json")
56. template_path = "$TEMPLATE"
57. ca_cert = "$CA_CERT"
58. home = os.path.expanduser("~")
59. npx_path = "$(which npx 2>/dev/null || echo "npx")"
60. force = $([[ "$FORCE" == "true" ]] && echo "True" || echo "False")
61. 
62. with open(target_path) as f:
63.     config = json.load(f)
64. 
65. with open(template_path) as f:
66.     template = json.load(f)
67. 
68. if "mcpServers" not in config:
69.     config["mcpServers"] = {}
70. 
71. servers = template.get("mcpServers", {})
72. added, updated, skipped = [], [], []
73. 
74. for name, cfg in servers.items():
75.     # Substitute placeholders
76.     cfg_str = json.dumps(cfg)
77.     cfg_str = cfg_str.replace("{{HOME}}", home)
78.     cfg_str = cfg_str.replace("{{CA_CERT_PATH}}", ca_cert if ca_cert else "")
79.     cfg_str = cfg_str.replace("{{NPX_PATH}}", npx_path)
80.     cfg = json.loads(cfg_str)
81. 
82.     # Remove empty CA cert envs
83.     if "env" in cfg and cfg["env"].get("NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS") == "":
84.         del cfg["env"]["NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS"]
85.         if not cfg["env"]:
86.             del cfg["env"]
87. 
88.     if name in config["mcpServers"] and not force:
89.         skipped.append(name)
90.     else:
91.         action = "updated" if name in config["mcpServers"] else "added"
92.         config["mcpServers"][name] = cfg
93.         (updated if action == "updated" else added).append(name)
94. 
95. # Enable global prompt cache for system prompt (skills injection)
96. config.setdefault("cachedGrowthBookFeatures", {})["tengu_system_prompt_global_cache"] = True
97. 
98. with open(target_path, "w") as f:
99.     json.dump(config, f, indent=2)
100. 
101. print(f"Added:   {added}")
102. print(f"Updated: {updated}")
103. print(f"Skipped: {skipped}")
104. print(f"Total active MCP servers: {list(config['mcpServers'].keys())}")
105. print(f"tengu_system_prompt_global_cache: True (skills injection cached across sessions)")
106. PYEOF
107. 
108. success "MCP servers applied to $TARGET"
109. echo ""
110. log "Restart Claude Code (or run: claude) for changes to take effect."
111. echo ""
112.
```

</details>

766. ### What gets configured
767. 
768. **MCP servers** (6 from template + any API-key servers found in your CLI config — zero token cost until invoked):
769. 
770. | Server | Purpose |
771. |--------|---------|
772. | `codesight` | Codebase summaries, routes, schema, hot files |
773. | `context7` | Live library/framework docs |
774. | `memory` | Persistent memory across sessions |
775. | `sequential-thinking` | Chain-of-thought reasoning |
776. | `token-optimizer` | 95%+ context reduction via deduplication |
777. | `playwright` | Browser automation |
778. | `github` | GitHub API (copied from CLI config if configured) |
779. | `exa-web-search` | Neural web search (copied from CLI config if configured) |
780. 
781. > **Note:** `skillgraph` (bioinformatics skills via MCP) is an HTTP-type server. Claude Desktop's config file does not support remote/HTTP servers — add it via **Settings → Integrations** in the Desktop UI instead. It works natively in Claude Code CLI (`~/.claude.json`).
782. 
783. **Curated skills** — built into `configs/claude-desktop-project-instructions.md`:
784. 
785. | Tier | Skills | Size | Tokens | What's included |
786. |------|--------|------|--------|-----------------|
787. | `essential` | varies | varies | varies | Personal learned skills only (from `~/.claude/skills/learned/`) |
788. | `standard` | 50 + personal | ~220KB+ | ~55K+ | + 27 universal agents + 23 top commands |
789. | `full` | 88 + personal | ~360KB+ | ~90K+ | + 27 universal agents + ALL ~61 universal commands |
790.

760.     else
761.         warn "~/.openclaw/workspace not found — install openclaw first"
762.     fi
763. 
764.     # Pi: ~/.pi/agent/skills/<skill-name>/ (each MUST have SKILL.md)
765.     if [ -d "$HOME/.pi/agent" ]; then
766.         mkdir -p "$HOME/.pi/agent/skills"
767.         local pi_count=0
768.         for dir in "$ECC_DIR/skills"/*/; do
769.             skill_name=$(basename "$dir")
770.             # Skip dirs without SKILL.md - they break pi's skill scanner
771.             [ ! -f "$dir/SKILL.md" ] && continue
772.             if [ ! -e "$HOME/.pi/agent/skills/$skill_name" ]; then
773.                 ln -s "$dir" "$HOME/.pi/agent/skills/$skill_name"
774.                 pi_count=$((pi_count + 1))
775.             fi
776.         done
777.         success "Pi: $pi_count ECC skills linked ($(ls "$HOME/.pi/agent/skills/" | wc -l | tr -d ' ') total)"
778.     else
779.         warn "~/.pi/agent not found — install pi first"
780.     fi
781. 
782.     # K-Dense Scientific Skills: install into Claude Code native skills dir
783.     # These are directory-based skills (SKILL.md + references/ + scripts/ + assets/)
784.     # Claude Code ONLY discovers <name>/SKILL.md directory format
785.     if [ -d "$SCIENCE_DIR/scientific-skills" ]; then
786.         local science_count=0
787.         for dir in "$SCIENCE_DIR/scientific-skills"/*/; do
788.             skill_name=$(basename "$dir")
789.             [ ! -f "$dir/SKILL.md" ] && continue
790.             # Claude Code: directory format (<name>/SKILL.md)
791.             local dst_dir="$claude_skills_dir/$skill_name"
792.             local dst="$dst_dir/SKILL.md"
793.             if [ ! -f "$dst" ]; then
794.                 mkdir -p "$dst_dir"
795.                 cp "$dir/SKILL.md" "$dst"
796.                 science_count=$((science_count + 1))
797.             fi
798.         done
799.         success "Claude Code: $science_count K-Dense scientific skills installed to ~/.claude/skills/"
800. 
801.         # OpenClaw: real copies for K-Dense skills (sanitize YAML)
802.         if [ -d "$HOME/.openclaw/workspace" ]; then
803.             local oc_science_count=0
804.             for dir in "$SCIENCE_DIR/scientific-skills"/*/; do
805.                 skill_name=$(basename "$dir")
806.                 [ ! -f "$dir/SKILL.md" ] && continue
807.                 dst="$ws_skills/$skill_name"
808.                 if [ ! -d "$dst" ]; then
809.                     mkdir -p "$dst"
810.                     # Copy SKILL.md (sanitize if needed)
811.                     if grep -q '^description: *[>|]' "$dir/SKILL.md" 2>/dev/null || \
812.                        grep -E '^[a-z_]+:' "$dir/SKILL.md" 2>/dev/null | grep -qvE '^(name|description|origin|tools|license|allowed-tools|metadata|compatibility):'; then
813.                         # Needs sanitization — copy only SKILL.md with sanitized content
814.                         python3 -c "
815. import re, sys
816. with open('$dir/SKILL.md') as f:
817.     content = f.read()
818. parts = content.split('---', 2)
819. if len(parts) >= 3:
820.     print('---' + parts[1] + '---' + parts[2])
821. else:
822.     print(content)
823. " > "$dst/SKILL.md" 2>/dev/null || cp "$dir/SKILL.md" "$dst/SKILL.md"
824.                     else
825.                         cp "$dir/SKILL.md" "$dst/SKILL.md"
826.                     fi
827.                     # Copy references/ and scripts/ and assets/ if they exist
828.                     cp -r "$dir/references" "$dst/" 2>/dev/null || true
829.                     cp -r "$dir/scripts" "$dst/" 2>/dev/null || true
830.                     cp -r "$dir/assets" "$dst/" 2>/dev/null || true
831.                     oc_science_count=$((oc_science_count + 1))
832.                 fi
833.             done
834.             success "OpenClaw: $oc_science_count K-Dense scientific skills copied to workspace"
835.         fi
836. 
837.         # Pi: symlink K-Dense skills
838.         if [ -d "$HOME/.pi/agent" ]; then
839.             local pi_science_count=0
840.             for dir in "$SCIENCE_DIR/scientific-skills"/*/; do
841.                 skill_name=$(basename "$dir")
842.                 [ ! -f "$dir/SKILL.md" ] && continue
843.                 if [ ! -e "$HOME/.pi/agent/skills/$skill_name" ]; then
844.                     ln -s "$dir" "$HOME/.pi/agent/skills/$skill_name"
845.                     pi_science_count=$((pi_science_count + 1))
846.                 fi
847.             done
848.             success "Pi: $pi_science_count K-Dense scientific skills linked"
849.         fi
850. 
851.         # Codex: handled by update-ecc.sh which properly sanitizes YAML
852.         if [ -d "$HOME/.codex/skills" ]; then
853.             log "Codex K-Dense skills will be synced by update-ecc.sh (proper YAML sanitization)"
854.         fi
855.     fi
856. 
857.     # ClawBio Bioinformatics Skills: install into all harness native skill dirs
858.     if [ -d "$CLAWBIO_DIR/skills" ]; then
859.         local bio_count=0
860.         for dir in "$CLAWBIO_DIR/skills"/*/; do
861.             skill_name=$(basename "$dir")
862.             [ ! -f "$dir/SKILL.md" ] && continue
863.             # Claude Code: directory format (<name>/SKILL.md)
864.             local dst_dir="$claude_skills_dir/$skill_name"
865.             if [ ! -f "$dst_dir/SKILL.md" ]; then
866.                 mkdir -p "$dst_dir"
867.                 cp "$dir/SKILL.md" "$dst_dir/SKILL.md"
868.                 # Copy Python scripts and examples if present (ClawBio includes executable scripts)
869.                 cp -r "$dir/examples" "$dst_dir/" 2>/dev/null || true
870.                 cp "$dir"/*.py "$dst_dir/" 2>/dev/null || true
871.                 bio_count=$((bio_count + 1))
872.             fi
873.         done
874.         [ $bio_count -gt 0 ] && success "Claude Code: $bio_count ClawBio skills installed to ~/.claude/skills/"
875. 
876.         # OpenClaw: real copies (sanitize YAML like K-Dense skills)
877.         if [ -d "$HOME/.openclaw/workspace" ]; then
878.             local oc_bio_count=0
879.             for dir in "$CLAWBIO_DIR/skills"/*/; do
880.                 skill_name=$(basename "$dir")
881.                 [ ! -f "$dir/SKILL.md" ] && continue
882.                 dst="$ws_skills/$skill_name"
883.                 if [ ! -d "$dst" ]; then
884.                     mkdir -p "$dst"
885.                     if grep -q '^description: *[>|]' "$dir/SKILL.md" 2>/dev/null || \
886.                        grep -E '^[a-z_]+:' "$dir/SKILL.md" 2>/dev/null | grep -qvE '^(name|description|origin|tools|version|tags|trigger_keywords|metadata|compatibility|license|allowed-tools):'; then
887.                         python3 -c "
888. import re, sys
889. with open('$dir/SKILL.md') as f:
890.     content = f.read()
891. parts = content.split('---', 2)
892. if len(parts) >= 3:
893.     print('---' + parts[1] + '---' + parts[2])
894. else:
895.     print(content)
896. " > "$dst/SKILL.md" 2>/dev/null || cp "$dir/SKILL.md" "$dst/SKILL.md"
897.                     else
898.                         cp "$dir/SKILL.md" "$dst/SKILL.md"
899.                     fi
900.                     cp "$dir"/*.py "$dst/" 2>/dev/null || true
901.                     cp -r "$dir/examples" "$dst/" 2>/dev/null || true
902.                     oc_bio_count=$((oc_bio_count + 1))
903.                 fi
904.             done
905.             [ $oc_bio_count -gt 0 ] && success "OpenClaw: $oc_bio_count ClawBio skills copied to workspace"
906.         fi
907. 
908.         # Pi: symlink ClawBio skills
909.         if [ -d "$HOME/.pi/agent" ]; then
910.             local pi_bio_count=0
911.             for dir in "$CLAWBIO_DIR/skills"/*/; do
912.                 skill_name=$(basename "$dir")
913.                 [ ! -f "$dir/SKILL.md" ] && continue
914.                 if [ ! -e "$HOME/.pi/agent/skills/$skill_name" ]; then
915.                     ln -s "$dir" "$HOME/.pi/agent/skills/$skill_name"
916.                     pi_bio_count=$((pi_bio_count + 1))
917.                 fi
918.             done
919.             [ $pi_bio_count -gt 0 ] && success "Pi: $pi_bio_count ClawBio skills linked"
920.         fi
921. 
922.         # Codex: handled by update-ecc.sh
923.         if [ -d "$HOME/.codex/skills" ]; then
924.             log "Codex ClawBio skills will be synced by update-ecc.sh (proper YAML sanitization)"
925.         fi
926.     fi
927. }
928. 
929. # =============================================================================
930. # Step 5: Uninstall
```

</details>

985. show_usage() {
986.     # Count skills
987.     ECC_COUNT=0
988.     ANTHROPIC_COUNT=0
989.     CODEX_COUNT=0
990.     SCIENCE_COUNT=0
991.     BIO_COUNT=0
992.     if [ -d "$ECC_DIR/skills" ]; then
993.         ECC_COUNT=$(find "$ECC_DIR/skills" -maxdepth 2 -name 'SKILL.md' 2>/dev/null | wc -l | tr -d ' ')
994.     fi
995.     if [ -d "$CURATED_DIR/anthropic-official" ]; then
996.         ANTHROPIC_COUNT=$(find "$CURATED_DIR/anthropic-official" -name 'SKILL.md' 2>/dev/null | wc -l | tr -d ' ')
997.     fi
998.     if [ -d "$CURATED_DIR/openai-codex" ]; then
999.         CODEX_COUNT=$(find "$CURATED_DIR/openai-codex" -name 'SKILL.md' 2>/dev/null | wc -l | tr -d ' ')
1000.     fi
1001.     if [ -d "$SCIENCE_DIR/scientific-skills" ]; then
1002.         SCIENCE_COUNT=$(find "$SCIENCE_DIR/scientific-skills" -name 'SKILL.md' 2>/dev/null | wc -l | tr -d ' ')
1003.     fi
1004.     if [ -d "$CLAWBIO_DIR/skills" ]; then
1005.         BIO_COUNT=$(find "$CLAWBIO_DIR/skills" -name 'SKILL.md' 2>/dev/null | wc -l | tr -d ' ')
1006.     fi
1007.     TOTAL_COUNT=$((ECC_COUNT + ANTHROPIC_COUNT + CODEX_COUNT + SCIENCE_COUNT + BIO_COUNT))
1008. 
1009.     # Determine which rc file to tell the user to source
1010.     local reload_rc
1011.     case "$USER_SHELL" in
1012.         zsh)  reload_rc="~/.zshrc" ;;
1013.         bash) reload_rc="~/.bashrc" ;;
1014.         *)    reload_rc="~/.bashrc" ;;
1015.     esac
1016. 
1017.     cat << USAGE
```

391.             # Update name field to match truncated directory name
392.             lines = content.splitlines()
393.             for i, line in enumerate(lines):
394.                 if line.startswith("name: "):
395.                     lines[i] = f"name: {codex_name}"
396.                     break
397.             with open(os.path.join(dst_path, "SKILL.md"), "w") as f:
398.                 f.write("\n".join(lines) + "\n")
399.         else:
400.             os.symlink(skill_dir, dst_path)
401.         return True
402.     return False
403. 
404. # --- OpenClaw: real file copies (sanitized) for all skill sources ---
405. if os.path.isdir(os.path.join(home, ".openclaw", "workspace")):
406.     os.makedirs(openclaw_ws, exist_ok=True)
407.     for skill_name, skill_dir in all_skills.items():
408.         if sync_to_harness_real(skill_name, skill_dir, openclaw_ws):
409.             stats["openclaw"]["updated"] += 1
410.     stats["openclaw"]["total"] = len([d for d in os.listdir(openclaw_ws) if os.path.isdir(os.path.join(openclaw_ws, d))])
411. 
412. # --- Pi: symlinks for all skill sources ---
413. if os.path.isdir(os.path.join(home, ".pi", "agent")):
414.     os.makedirs(pi_skills, exist_ok=True)
415.     for skill_name, skill_dir in all_skills.items():
416.         target = os.path.join(pi_skills, skill_name)
417.         if not os.path.exists(target):
418.             try:
419.                 os.symlink(skill_dir, target)
420.                 stats["pi"]["added"] += 1
421.             except (OSError, FileExistsError):
422.                 pass  # Skip if symlink fails (e.g., target already exists)
423.     stats["pi"]["total"] = len(os.listdir(pi_skills))
424. 
425. # --- Codex: symlinks where clean, sanitized copies where needed ---
426. if os.path.isdir(os.path.join(home, ".codex")):
427.     os.makedirs(codex_skills, exist_ok=True)
428.     for skill_name, skill_dir in all_skills.items():
429.         if sync_to_harness_symlink_or_sanitize(skill_name, skill_dir, codex_skills):
430.             stats["codex"]["added"] += 1
431.     stats["codex"]["total"] = len(os.listdir(codex_skills))
432. 
433. # --- Claude Code: directory-based skills (<name>/SKILL.md) ---
434. # Claude Code's /skills command ONLY discovers <name>/SKILL.md directory format,
435. # NOT flat .md files. Migrate stale flat files and sync all skills.
436. claude_skills = os.path.join(home, ".claude", "skills")
437. if os.path.isdir(os.path.join(home, ".claude")):
438.     os.makedirs(claude_skills, exist_ok=True)
439.     # Migrate stale flat .md files to directory format
440.     migrated = 0
441.     for flat_file in glob.glob(os.path.join(claude_skills, "*.md")):
442.         skill_name = os.path.splitext(os.path.basename(flat_file))[0]
443.         dir_path = os.path.join(claude_skills, skill_name)
444.         if os.path.isdir(dir_path):
445.             # Directory already exists; remove stale flat file
446.             os.remove(flat_file)
447.             migrated += 1
448.             continue
449.         # Move flat file into directory format
450.         os.makedirs(dir_path, exist_ok=True)
451.         shutil.move(flat_file, os.path.join(dir_path, "SKILL.md"))
452.         migrated += 1
453.     # Sync all skill sources
454.     claude_updated = 0
455.     for skill_name, skill_dir in all_skills.items():
456.         src = os.path.join(skill_dir, "SKILL.md")
457.         if not os.path.exists(src):
458.             continue
459.         dst_dir = os.path.join(claude_skills, skill_name)
460.         dst = os.path.join(dst_dir, "SKILL.md")
461.         src_mtime = os.path.getmtime(src)
462.         dst_mtime = os.path.getmtime(dst) if os.path.exists(dst) else 0
463.         if src_mtime > dst_mtime:
464.             os.makedirs(dst_dir, exist_ok=True)
465.             shutil.copy2(src, dst)
466.             claude_updated += 1
467.     # Also sync learned skills (flat .md in learned/ subdir — leave as-is)
468.     claude_total = len([d for d in os.listdir(claude_skills)
469.                         if os.path.isdir(os.path.join(claude_skills, d)) and d != "learned"])
470.     if migrated > 0:
```

</details>


### 💬 Copilot

## Blocking Issues

1. **Unsafe uninstall deletes user-owned Claude skills**
   - **Impact:** `safe-install.sh --uninstall` removes **any** `~/.claude/skills/*/SKILL.md` directory, not just repo-installed skills. That can delete user-created/manual skills.
   - **Severity:** Blocking
   - **Refs:** `safe-install.sh:953-961`, `README.md:351-352`, `install.sh:20,93-95`
   - **Fix:** Track installed skills in a manifest during install/update, and uninstall **only** manifest-listed entries. Do not infer ownership from `SKILL.md` existing.

2. **Uninstall leaves stale/broken harness skill links behind**
   - **Impact:** Uninstall removes source repos (`$ECC_DIR`, `$SCIENCE_DIR`, `$CLAWBIO_DIR`) but does **not** clean Pi/Codex/OpenClaw skill targets. Pi/Codex can be left with dangling symlinks; OpenClaw can keep stale copied skills.
   - **Severity:** Blocking
   - **Refs:** `safe-install.sh:947-951`, `safe-install.sh:772-775,843-845,914-915`, `scripts/update-ecc.sh:400,419,425-431`
   - **Fix:** Use the same manifest to remove installed entries from `~/.pi/agent/skills`, `~/.codex/skills`, `~/.openclaw/workspace/skills`, and `~/.claude/skills` on uninstall.

---

## Non-Blocking Issues

1. **`configs/zshrc-skills-block.sh` is out of sync with the actual installed shell block**
   - **Impact:** Manual/reference use misses Copilot wrapper support; `learn-sync-dry` behavior also differs.
   - **Severity:** Non-Blocking
   - **Refs:** `configs/zshrc-skills-block.sh:46-58`, `safe-install.sh:530-547`
   - **Fix:** Make `generate_skills_block()` render from the template, or make the template the single source of truth. Add `_copilot_with_skills` + `alias copilot=`, and reconcile `learn-sync-dry`.

2. **`verify.sh` does not verify Copilot at all**
   - **Impact:** Repo supports Copilot, but health checks can pass while Copilot is broken/misconfigured.
   - **Severity:** Non-Blocking
   - **Refs:** `install.sh:433-436`, `scripts/verify.sh:240-249,251-278`, `README.md:188`
   - **Fix:** Add a Copilot section checking:
     - `copilot` binary
     - `~/.copilot/mcp-config.json`
     - expected MCP servers
     - `beads` entry in Copilot config
     - optional shell alias `copilot=`

3. **README / comments are stale about `safe-install.sh` defaults**
   - **Impact:** Commands documented as “ECC only” / “ECC + science” do not match actual defaults (`WITH_SCIENCE=true`, `WITH_BIO=true`, `WITH_CURATED=false`).
   - **Severity:** Non-Blocking
   - **Refs:** `safe-install.sh:53-57`, `README.md:40,94-97,326,550-553`, `safe-install.sh:5,489`
   - **Fix:** Either change defaults or rewrite docs/examples. As written:
     - bare `./safe-install.sh` is **not** ECC-only
     - `--with-science` does **not** mean science-only
     - “all skill sources” also needs curated skills called out correctly

4. **`docs/TROUBLESHOOTING.md` is referenced but missing**
   - **Impact:** Broken doc links / stale repo structure.
   - **Severity:** Non-Blocking
   - **Refs:** `README.md:192,882`; actual `docs/` listing has no such file
   - **Fix:** Add the file or remove the references.

5. **Beads README text contradicts itself**
   - **Impact:** One section says beads works with every harness “via MCP”; later text says only Claude Code/Copilot get `beads-mcp`, while OpenClaw/Pi/Codex use `bd prime`/`AGENTS.md`.
   - **Severity:** Non-Blocking
   - **Refs:** `README.md:837`, `README.md:876`
   - **Fix:** Reword to: “works with every harness; via MCP in Claude Code/Copilot, via `bd prime` + `AGENTS.md` elsewhere.”

6. **`install.sh --help` is missing supported flags/targets**
   - **Impact:** CLI help is incomplete vs parser behavior.
   - **Severity:** Non-Blocking
   - **Refs:** `install.sh:82-91`, `install.sh:102-109`
   - **Fix:** Add `--with-curated`, `--without-curated`, `--learn`, `--no-learn`, and missing `--only` targets like `skills`/`ollama` if they are intended public options.

---

## Suggestions

1. **`setup-beads.sh` always prints “Updated”**
   - **Impact:** Misleading output only.
   - **Severity:** Suggestion
   - **Refs:** `scripts/setup-beads.sh:166-179`
   - **Fix:** Capture `existed = "beads" in config[key]` before assignment.

2. **Context-window wording is inconsistent, but not wrong**
   - **Impact:** Mild doc confusion.
   - **Severity:** Suggestion
   - **Refs:** `README.md:582-585`
   - **Fix:** Say Claude Code surfaces **“context limit reached”**, wrapping Anthropic’s `context_length_exceeded`.

3. **Lean-skills size/token claims are internally inconsistent**
   - **Impact:** Mildly misleading sizing guidance.
   - **Severity:** Suggestion
   - **Refs:** `README.md:590-595`, `README.md:611`
   - **Fix:** Pick one framing: either “typically 1–2K tokens” or present a clearly labeled worst-case range.

---

## Confirmed Non-Issues from Your List

1. **`"${entry}SKILL.md"` double slash**
   - Not a real bug on supported systems. The real uninstall problem is ownership detection, not the `//`.

2. **`install.sh` python3 check after bootstrap**
   - Fine. It gives a clearer final error if bootstrap failed.

3. **`local src="$REPO_DIR/$script"` in `copy_scripts_to_claude_dir()`**
   - Fine. The subsequent `[ -f "$src" ]` check is what matters.

4. **`lean-skills.txt` using `~/.claude/skills/learned/*.md`**
   - Current behavior is correct.
   - **Why:** learned skills are written as **flat `.md` files** in `~/.claude/skills/learned/`, while subdirs like `events/` are not skills and should be excluded.
   - **Refs:** `scripts/consolidate-learnings.py:221-228`, `extract-conversation-skills.py:1173`, `sync-learned-skills.sh:108,150-174`

5. **Ollama listed as “Optional (warn)”**
   - Basically accurate for the installer: `install.sh` warns and continues without Ollama.
   - Worth clarifying that Ollama-backed harnesses still need it at runtime.

6. **Claude Desktop beads omission**
   - **Not a confirmed bug in current repo behavior.**
   - Desktop setup/docs explicitly describe a 6-server Desktop template and do not promise beads there.
   - If desired, this is a separate feature gap, not current drift.

If you want, I can turn this into a patch-ready checklist by file.

/Users/chazh/Documents/GitHub/ai-skillweave/scripts/setup-copilot.sh

<details>
<summary>112 lines</summary>

```
1. #!/bin/bash
2. # setup-copilot.sh — Configure MCP servers for GitHub Copilot CLI
3. # =============================================================================
4. # Writes MCP server config to ~/.copilot/mcp-config.json (read automatically
5. # by copilot on launch; augments built-in github-mcp-server).
6. #
7. # Usage:
8. #   scripts/setup-copilot.sh [--force]
9. #   --force : overwrite existing MCP server entries with template values
10. # =============================================================================
11. set -e
12. 
13. REPO_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")/.." && pwd)"
14. TEMPLATE="$REPO_DIR/configs/copilot-mcp-config.json"
15. TARGET="$HOME/.copilot/mcp-config.json"
16. 
17. log()     { echo -e "\033[1;34m[COPILOT]\033[0m $1"; }
18. success() { echo -e "\033[1;32m[OK]\033[0m $1"; }
19. warn()    { echo -e "\033[1;33m[WARN]\033[0m $1"; }
20. 
21. if [ ! -f "$TEMPLATE" ]; then
22.     warn "Template not found: $TEMPLATE"
23.     exit 1
24. fi
25. 
26. FORCE=false
27. [[ "$*" == *"--force"* ]] && FORCE=true
28. 
29. # Ensure Copilot config directory exists
30. mkdir -p "$(dirname "$TARGET")"
31. 
32. # Determine CA cert path (Zscaler or system default)
33. CA_CERT=""
34. if [ -f "$HOME/.mamba_ca_bundle.pem" ]; then
35.     CA_CERT="$HOME/.mamba_ca_bundle.pem"
36. elif [ -f "/etc/ssl/certs/ca-certificates.crt" ]; then
37.     CA_CERT="/etc/ssl/certs/ca-certificates.crt"
38. fi
39. 
40. log "Installing MCP servers for Copilot CLI..."
41. 
42. python3 << PYEOF
43. import json, os, sys
44. 
45. template_path = "$TEMPLATE"
46. target_path = "$TARGET"
47. ca_cert = "$CA_CERT"
48. home = os.path.expanduser("~")
49. npx_path = "$(which npx 2>/dev/null || echo "npx")"
50. force = $([[ "$FORCE" == "true" ]] && echo "True" || echo "False")
51. 
52. # Load or create target config
53. if os.path.exists(target_path):
54.     with open(target_path) as f:
55.         config = json.load(f)
56. else:
57.     config = {}
58. 
59. if "mcpServers" not in config:
60.     config["mcpServers"] = {}
61. 
62. with open(template_path) as f:
63.     template = json.load(f)
64. 
65. servers = template.get("mcpServers", {})
66. added = []
67. updated = []
68. skipped = []
69. 
70. for name, cfg in servers.items():
71.     # Substitute placeholders
72.     cfg_str = json.dumps(cfg)
73.     cfg_str = cfg_str.replace("{{HOME}}", home)
74.     cfg_str = cfg_str.replace("{{CA_CERT_PATH}}", ca_cert if ca_cert else "")
75.     cfg_str = cfg_str.replace("{{NPX_PATH}}", npx_path)
76.     cfg = json.loads(cfg_str)
77. 
78.     # Remove empty CA cert envs
79.     if "env" in cfg and cfg["env"].get("NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS") == "":
80.         del cfg["env"]["NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS"]
81.         if not cfg["env"]:
82.             del cfg["env"]
83. 
84.     if name in config["mcpServers"] and not force:
85.         skipped.append(name)
86.         continue
87. 
88.     action = "updated" if name in config["mcpServers"] else "added"
89.     config["mcpServers"][name] = cfg
90.     (updated if action == "updated" else added).append(name)
91. 
92. with open(target_path, "w") as f:
93.     json.dump(config, f, indent=2)
94.     f.write("\n")
95. 
96. if added:
97.     print(f"  Added:   {', '.join(added)}")
98. if updated:
99.     print(f"  Updated: {', '.join(updated)}")
100. if skipped:
101.     print(f"  Skipped (exists, use --force): {', '.join(skipped)}")
102. 
103. total = list(config["mcpServers"].keys())
104. print(f"Total MCP servers: {len(total)} — {total}")
105. PYEOF
106. 
107. success "MCP servers applied to Copilot CLI config ($TARGET)"
108. echo ""
109. echo "  Launch Copilot:  ollama launch copilot"
110. echo "  Or directly:     copilot"
111. echo ""
112.
```

</details>

<sub>⏱️ 37m 51s</sub>

### ✅ `bash`

**Parse Copilot binary for skill loading paths**

$ # Search for how Copilot loads its skills - it's a bundled node script
# Look for "skills" directory references or skill-related paths
python3 -c "
import re
with open('/opt/homebrew/bin/copilot') as f:
    src = f.read()

# Find skill-related patterns
patterns = [
    r'skill[s]?[^\w]',
    r'SKILL\.md',
    r'disabledSkills',
    r'availableSkills',
    r'skillsDir',
    r'skills_dir',
    r'\.claude',
]
for pat in patterns:
    matches = re.findall(r'.{0,60}' + pat + r'.{0,60}', src, re.IGNORECASE)[:3]
    if matches:
        print(f'=== {pat} ===')
        for m in matches:
            print(' ', m.strip())
" 2>/dev/null | head -80

<sub>⏱️ 39m 56s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

This is a major finding. Copilot CLI natively reads `~/.claude/skills/` — that's why you see ~400 skills. Let me verify the scope and then fix all remaining documentation + code issues.

<sub>⏱️ 39m 57s</sub>

### ✅ `bash`

**Count skills and find env var for extra dirs**

$ COPILOT_DIR="/opt/homebrew/Cellar/node/25.9.0_2/lib/node_modules/@github/copilot"
# Count total skill-related files  
ls ~/.claude/skills/ 2>/dev/null | wc -l
echo "dirs in skills:"
ls ~/.claude/skills/ 2>/dev/null
echo "---"
# Count SKILL.md files (what copilot actually reads)
find ~/.claude/skills -name "SKILL.md" 2>/dev/null | wc -l
echo "---"
# Also check what env var skillDirectories uses
python3 -c "
import re
with open('$COPILOT_DIR/app.js') as f:
    src = f.read()
# find CPr definition 
m = re.search(r'CPr\s*=\s*[\"\'](.*?)[\"\']\s*[,;]', src)
if m: print('CPr env var name:', m.group(1))
# search for COPILOT_SKILL_DIRS or similar
for pat in ['COPILOT_SKILL', 'SKILL_DIR', 'skillDir']:
    hits = [src[max(0,m.start()-40):m.start()+80] for m in re.finditer(pat, src, re.I)][:2]
    if hits:
        print(pat, ':', hits[0][:100])
" 2>/dev/null

49:> **Bioinformatics pipeline skills served via MCP from [variomeanalytics/bioinformatics-agent-skills](https://github.com/variomeanalytics/bioinformatics-agent-skills)** — covering variant analysis, drug discovery, single-cell RNA-seq, genome-wide association studies, and 15+ database query skills (ClinVar, gnomAD, COSMIC, Ensembl, UniProt, and more).
60:This knowledge graph approach means you can ask "what pipeline takes me from FASTQ to DEGs?" and get a real answer with evidence — something on-disk skill files alone can't provide.
124:ollama launch copilot     # Copilot CLI + MCP servers
137:| **Copilot CLI** | Configures MCP servers (including beads) in `~/.copilot/mcp-config.json` |
164:│   ├── copilot-mcp-config.json   ← MCP servers for Copilot CLI
184:│   ├── setup-copilot.sh          ← Apply Copilot CLI MCP config
287:> **Note:** OpenClaw, Pi, Codex, and Copilot are optional. `install.sh` will skip harnesses that aren't installed and show a warning. Ollama is also optional — the installer warns but continues without it.
289:> **Copilot CLI caveat:** Copilot loads MCP servers from `~/.copilot/mcp-config.json` (run `scripts/setup-copilot.sh` to configure), but it does **not** load the `~/.claude/skills/` directory natively — SKILL.md skills are a Claude Code/OpenClaw/Pi/Codex format. Copilot uses `.github/copilot-instructions.md` for per-repo guidance instead.
341:./install.sh --only copilot
486:**Quality-first design**: Without `--llm`, only skills matching known condition templates are written — producing 0 skills is better than keyword soup. With `--llm`, Ollama distills corrections into proper condition+strategy+anti-pattern form, and generates short semantic names like `cite-published-research-dois` (not sentence fragments).
545:Only **2 of 4 memory types** produce generalizable skills: `heuristic` and `anti_pattern`. Preferences are per-user; domain knowledge is project-specific. This ensures learned skills are universally applicable, not project noise.
584:The original installer injected the full combined skills cache (`combined-skills.txt`, ~5.5MB ≈ **1.375M tokens**) into every Claude session via `--append-system-prompt-file`. Claude Sonnet 4.6 and Opus 4.7 have a **200K token** context window — this means the skills injection alone was **6.9× the entire context budget**, triggering an explicit `context_length_exceeded` error from the Anthropic API on every single session.
588:### The fix: lean-skills injection
590:The installer now injects **only your personal learned skills** (`lean-skills.txt`) at session start — typically **1–2K tokens** (under 1% of the context window). The full combined cache is still built and stored at `~/.claude/skills-cache/combined-skills.txt` for reference and local search, but is never injected automatically.
697:> **Powered by [codesight](https://github.com/Houseofmvps/codesight)** — *See your codebase clearly.* Universal AI context generator that maps routes, schema, components, dependencies, and more for Claude Code, Cursor, Copilot, Codex, and any AI coding tool.
837:> **Powered by [beads](https://github.com/gastownhall/beads)** — AI-native cross-session work item tracking. Works with every harness (Claude Code, Codex, Copilot, OpenClaw, Pi) via MCP.
858:4. Injects the `beads` MCP entry into `~/.claude.json` and `~/.copilot/mcp-config.json` (only after confirming beads-mcp is installed; skips if already present)
876:The MCP server (`beads-mcp`) makes beads available in any harness that loads `~/.claude.json` (Claude Code, Copilot) or `~/.copilot/mcp-config.json`. For OpenClaw, Pi, and Codex, `bd prime` output can be pasted directly — the `AGENTS.md` file is what matters for cross-session persistence.
