100. 
101. 
102. 
103. ---
104. 
105. ## Quick Start (New Machine)
106. 
107. ```bash
108. # 1. Clone this repo
109. git clone https://github.com/chazhyseni/ai-skillweave
110. cd ai-skillweave
111. 
112. # 2. Install everything (auto-detects macOS/Linux/WSL)
113. ./install.sh
114. 
115. # 3. Reload shell (install.sh tells you which file)
116. source ~/.bashrc   # Linux/WSL
117. source ~/.zshrc    # macOS
118. 
119. # 4. Launch any harness
120. ollama launch claude      # Claude Code + MCP tools
121. ollama launch openclaw    # OpenClaw + subagents + web
122. ollama launch pi          # Pi + subagents
123. ollama launch codex       # Codex via Ollama backend
124. ollama launch copilot     # Copilot CLI + MCP servers
125. ```
126. 
127. ---
128. 
129. ## What This Repo Does
130. 
131. | Component | What it configures |
132. |-----------|-------------------|
133. | **Claude Code MCP** | Adds 9 auto-configured servers to `~/.claude.json`: memory, sequential-thinking, context7, playwright, google-docs-editor, token-optimizer, codesight, skillgraph, beads |
134. | **OpenClaw** | Enables web tools + Ollama plugin in `~/.openclaw/openclaw.json` |
135. | **Pi** | Sets Ollama as provider + installs `pi-subagents` package |
136. | **Codex** | Configures `ollama-launch` provider + `danger-full-access` sandbox in `~/.codex/config.toml` |
137. | **Copilot CLI** | Configures MCP servers (including beads) in `~/.copilot/mcp-config.json` |
138. | **Ollama integrations** | Sets per-harness model mapping in `~/.ollama/config.json` (qwen3.6 default) |
139. | **Shell wrappers** | Adds `_*_with_skills` functions + aliases in `~/.bashrc` and/or `~/.zshrc` |
140. | **Claude Code skills** | Copies ECC SKILL.md files to `~/.claude/skills/` — visible via `/skills`, works with any launch method |
141. | **Lean skills cache** | Personal learned skills at `~/.claude/skills-cache/lean-skills.txt` — injected at session start (~1-2K tokens). Full library cache (`combined-skills.txt`) kept for reference but never injected — Claude's 200K window means 1.4M tokens would crash every session |
142. | **Beads** | `bd` CLI + `beads-mcp` MCP server — cross-session work item tracking. `bd prime` gives AI-optimised project context at session start |
143. | **Learning pipeline scripts** | Copies `sync-learned-skills.sh`, `extract-conversation-skills.py`, `safe-install.sh` to `~/.claude/scripts/` so `learn-sync`/`learn-stats`/`learn-prune` aliases work from any directory |
144. 
145. ---
146. 
147. ## Repository Structure
148. 
149. ```
150. ai-skillweave/
151. ├── install.sh                    ← Master installer (run this)
152. ├── safe-install.sh               ← ECC skills installer
153. ├── extract-conversation-skills.py ← 4-stage learning pipeline (Ingestion→Learning→Consolidation→Output)
154. ├── sync-learned-skills.sh        ← Sync learned skills + run pipeline (--stats, --prune, --sync-only)
155. │
156. ├── hooks/                        ← Claude Code hooks (auto-installed by install.sh)
157. │   ├── codesight-redirect.sh     ← PreToolUse: redirect broad searches to codesight
158. │   ├── learning-capture.sh       ← UserPromptSubmit: BMO-style real-time correction capture
159. │   └── session-reflection.sh     ← Session end: consolidate captured events into skills
160. │
161. ├── configs/                      ← Portable config templates
162. │   ├── claude-mcp-servers.json   ← MCP servers for Claude Code CLI
163. │   ├── claude-desktop-mcp-servers.json  ← MCP servers for Claude Desktop GUI
164. │   ├── copilot-mcp-config.json   ← MCP servers for Copilot CLI
165. │   ├── global-claude-md.md       ← Global CLAUDE.md template (MCP rules + beads workflow + conciseness)
166. │   ├── openclaw.json             ← OpenClaw config (web tools enabled)
167. │   ├── codex-config.toml         ← Codex ollama-launch provider config
168. │   ├── pi-settings.json          ← Pi agent settings
169. │   ├── ollama-integrations.json  ← Ollama integration→model mapping
170. │   └── zshrc-skills-block.sh     ← Shell skills layer block (reference/manual use)
171. │
172. ├── scripts/                      ← Individual setup scripts
173. │   ├── setup-mcp.sh              ← Inject MCP into ~/.claude.json (CLI)
174. │   ├── setup-claude-md.sh        ← Install global CLAUDE.md (MCP rules + conciseness)
175. │   ├── setup-hooks.sh            ← Install PreToolUse hook (codesight-redirect)
````

<sub>⏱️ 40m 21s</sub>

### ✅ `view`

**view the file at /Users/chazh/Documents/GitHub/ai-skillweave/README.md.**

/Users/chazh/Documents/GitHub/ai-skillweave/README.md (lines 580-640)

<details>
<summary>61 lines</summary>

````
580. ## Context Window & Skills Injection
581. 
582. ### Why Claude was hitting "context limit reached"
583. 
584. The original installer injected the full combined skills cache (`combined-skills.txt`, ~5.5MB ≈ **1.375M tokens**) into every Claude session via `--append-system-prompt-file`. Claude Sonnet 4.6 and Opus 4.7 have a **200K token** context window — this means the skills injection alone was **6.9× the entire context budget**, triggering an explicit `context_length_exceeded` error from the Anthropic API on every single session.
585. 
586. Ollama models (Kimi, Qwen, etc.) silently truncate prompts to their `num_ctx` limit instead of erroring — which is why `ollama launch kimi` seemed to "work" while `ollama launch claude` kept failing.
587. 
588. ### The fix: lean-skills injection
589. 
590. The installer now injects **only your personal learned skills** (`lean-skills.txt`) at session start — typically **1–2K tokens** (under 1% of the context window). The full combined cache is still built and stored at `~/.claude/skills-cache/combined-skills.txt` for reference and local search, but is never injected automatically.
591. 
592. | File | Size | Tokens | Used for |
593. |------|------|--------|---------|
594. | `lean-skills.txt` | ~6-50KB | ~1.5–12K | Injected into every Claude session via `--append-system-prompt-file` |
595. | `combined-skills.txt` | ~5.5MB | ~1.375M | Reference only — local search, update-ecc.sh, never auto-injected |
596. 
597. The ~450 ECC/K-Dense/ClawBio SKILL.md files in `~/.claude/skills/` are loaded natively by Claude Code via its built-in `/skills` feature — no injection overhead, loaded on-demand, no context window impact.
598. 
599. ### Prompt caching (still active)
600. 
601. Even with lean injection, Claude Code's prompt caching is still configured via `tengu_system_prompt_global_cache: true` in `~/.claude.json` — the lean block is cached after session 1 and reused across sessions.
602. 
603. ---
604. 
605. ## Token Efficiency — Skills Injection + Prompt Caching
606. 
607. With `lean-skills.txt` injection, per-session context overhead is minimal. Prompt caching keeps it economical even for heavy users:
608. 
609. | Event | Cost |
610. |-------|------|
611. | Session 1 (cache miss) | Full input price for lean-skills tokens (~1-2K tokens) |
612. | Session 2+ same day (cache hit) | ~90% discount on cached tokens |
613. | Each conversation turn | Only new tokens in the exchange |
614. 
615. > **Note:** Exact costs depend on your plan and model. Check [Anthropic's pricing page](https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/about-claude/pricing) for current rates.
616. 
617. ---
618. 
619. ## MCP Tool Enforcement
620. 
621. Three layers ensure Claude actually uses MCP tools instead of raw file scanning:
622. 
623. | Layer | Mechanism | Strength |
624. | ----- | --------- | -------- |
625. | `~/.claude/CLAUDE.md` | Global instructions loaded every session | Soft — can be ignored |
626. | Lean skills preamble | Short directive injected at top of `lean-skills.txt` | Soft — reinforces CLAUDE.md |
627. | `hooks/codesight-redirect.sh` | PreToolUse hook blocks broad Glob/Grep | **Hard — actually stops the call** |
628. 
629. ### How the hook works
630. 
631. When Claude attempts a broad codebase search (any `**` glob pattern or bare Glob in a codesight-enabled project), the hook:
632. 
633. 1. Detects `.codesight/` exists in the project tree
634. 2. Blocks the tool call (exit 2 → message sent back to Claude)
635. 3. Tells Claude to call `codesight_get_summary` first
636. 4. After the first reminder per session, allows all subsequent searches through (no repeated nagging)
637. 
638. ```bash
639. # Hook fires on: Glob("**/*.ts"), Grep(path="/your/project", ...)
640. # Passes through: Read("/path/to/specific/file.ts"), Grep("specific-function-name")
````

</details>

Copilot uses `.github/copilot-instructions.md` for per-repo guidance instead.

308. 
309. ## Prerequisites Summary
310. 
311. | Tool | Required? | macOS | Linux |
312. |------|-----------|-------|-------|
313. | [Ollama](https://ollama.com) | Optional (warn) | `brew install ollama` | `curl -fsSL https://ollama.com/install.sh \| sh` |
314. | Python 3 | ✅ Required | `brew install python3` | `apt install python3` or `dnf install python3` |
315. | Node.js | ✅ Required | `brew install node` | `apt install nodejs` or use nvm |
316. | Claude Code | ✅ Required | `npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code` | Same |
317. | OpenClaw | Optional | `ollama launch openclaw --config` | Same |
318. | Pi | Optional | `ollama launch pi` | Same |
319. | Codex | Optional | `npm install -g @openai/codex` | Same |
320. 
321. ---
322. 
323. ## Install Options
324. 
325. ```bash
326. # Full setup — all harnesses, all skills (ECC + K-Dense scientific), default model
327. ./install.sh
328. 
329. # Skip K-Dense scientific skills (faster, fewer skills)
330. ./install.sh --without-science
331. 
332. # Use a local model instead (faster, no cloud dependency)
333. ./install.sh --model qwen3.6
334. ./install.sh --model gemma4:26b
335. 
336. # Configure only specific harnesses
337. ./install.sh --only claude
338. ./install.sh --only openclaw
339. ./install.sh --only pi
340. ./install.sh --only codex
341. ./install.sh --only copilot
342. ./install.sh --only beads
343. 
344. # Skip ECC skills installation (faster, if skills already installed)
345. ./install.sh --skip-skills
346. 
347. # Run health check
348. ./install.sh --verify
349. # or: scripts/verify.sh
350. 
351. # Uninstall shell layer only (configs preserved)
352. ./install.sh --uninstall
353. ```
354. 
355. ---
356.

358.

200. ## Platform Support
201. 
202. | Platform | Status | Shell | Notes |
203. |----------|--------|-------|-------|
204. | **macOS** | ✅ Tested | zsh (default) | Homebrew for dependencies |
205. | **Linux** | ✅ Tested | bash (default) | apt/dnf/pacman auto-detected |
206. | **Windows (WSL)** | ✅ Supported | bash | Run inside WSL — native Windows is not supported |
207. 
208. `install.sh` auto-detects the platform and user shell, installs skills into the correct RC file (`~/.bashrc` or `~/.zshrc`), and shows platform-appropriate messages.
209. 
210. ---
211. 
212. ## Before You Begin — First-Time Setup
213. 
214. Run these steps **once** on a new machine before cloning this repo.
215. 
216. ### 1. Install Ollama
217. 
218. ```bash
219. # macOS:
220. brew install ollama
221. # or download from https://ollama.com
222. 
223. # Linux (Debian/Ubuntu):
224. curl -fsSL https://ollama.com/install.sh | sh
225. 
226. # Start the server:
227. ollama serve          # Linux (or use systemd)
228. # macOS: open /Applications/Ollama.app (runs as menubar app)
229. ```
230. 
231. ### 2. Pull a model (or use cloud models)
232. 
233. ```bash
234. # Cloud models — no download needed, stream from Ollama's servers at inference time:
235. # Just use them directly: ollama run qwen3.5:cloud
236. 
237. # Local model — runs on your machine (~23GB download):
238. ollama pull qwen3.6                    # recommended local model
239. ollama pull gemma4:e4b                 # lightweight, good for subagents
240. ```
241. 
242. ### 3. Install Node.js (for MCP servers)
243. 
244. ```bash
245. # macOS:
246. brew install node
247. 
248. # Linux (Debian/Ubuntu):
249. curl -fsSL https://deb.nodesource.com/setup_lts.x | sudo -E bash -
250. sudo apt-get install -y nodejs
251. 
252. # Or use nvm (any platform):
253. curl -o- https://raw.githubusercontent.com/nvm-sh/nvm/v0.40.0/install.sh | bash
254. nvm install --lts
255. 
256. # Verify: node --version  (needs v18+)
257. ```
258. 
259. ### 4. Install Claude Code
260. 
261. ```bash
262. npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code
263. 
264. # Authenticate (requires Claude Pro/Max/Team account):
265. claude auth login
266. # → opens browser, log in with your Anthropic account
267. 
268. # Verify it works:
269. claude --version
270. ```
271. 
272. ### 5. Install OpenClaw, Pi, Codex (optional — install only what you use)
273. 
274. ```bash
275. # OpenClaw — first run downloads and configures it:
276. ollama launch openclaw --config
277. # → follow the onboarding wizard, then quit
278. 
279. # Pi — first run installs it:
280. ollama launch pi
281. # → let it initialize, then Ctrl+C once it's ready
282. 
283. # Codex CLI:
284. npm install -g @openai/codex
285. ```
286. 
287. > **Note:** OpenClaw, Pi, Codex, and Copilot are optional. `install.sh` will skip harnesses that aren't installed and show a warning. Ollama is also optional — the installer warns but continues without it.
288. >
289. > **Copilot CLI caveat:** Copilot loads MCP servers from `~/.copilot/mcp-config.json` (run `scripts/setup-copilot.sh` to configure), but it does **not** load the `~/.claude/skills/` directory natively — SKILL.md skills are a Claude Code/OpenClaw/Pi/Codex format. Copilot uses `.github/copilot-instructions.md` for per-repo guidance instead.
290. 
291. ### 6. Install Python 3
292. 
293. ```bash
294. # Check if already installed:
295. python3 --version  # needs 3.8+
````

<sub>⏱️ 40m 33s</sub>

### ✅ `view`

**view the file at /Users/chazh/Documents/GitHub/ai-skillweave/README.md.**

/Users/chazh/Documents/GitHub/ai-skillweave/README.md (lines 360-440)

<details>
<summary>81 lines</summary>

````
360. 
361. `scripts/setup-mcp.sh` reads `configs/claude-mcp-servers.json` and merges these into `~/.claude.json`:
362. 
363. | Server | What it does |
364. |--------|-------------|
365. | `memory` | Persistent memory across Claude Code sessions |
366. | `sequential-thinking` | Chain-of-thought reasoning tool |
367. | `context7` | Live docs lookup for any library/framework |
368. | `playwright` | Browser automation from within Claude Code |
369. | `google-docs-editor` | Read/write Google Docs (local server, pre-built) |
370. | `token-optimizer` | 95%+ context reduction via deduplication — saves Opus tokens |
371. | `codesight` | Maps codebase routes, schema, components, dependencies — AI context for any project |
372. | `skillgraph` | Bioinformatics pipeline skills + knowledge graph via MCP — variant analysis, drug discovery, single-cell, 15+ databases |
373. | `beads` | Cross-session work item tracking — `bd prime` gives AI-optimised context at session start (injected after `setup-beads.sh` confirms `beads-mcp` is installed) |
374. 
375. **No tokens or API keys needed for any of the above.** `install.sh` applies them automatically on a new machine.
376. 
377. ### Manually added (require API keys)
378. 
379. The `_api_key_servers_commented` block in `configs/claude-mcp-servers.json` contains templates that are **never auto-applied** — you fill them in and add them yourself:
380. 
381. ```bash
382. # GitHub (needs a PAT from github.com/settings/tokens)
383. claude mcp add github \
384.   -e GITHUB_PERSONAL_ACCESS_TOKEN=ghp_YOUR_TOKEN \
385.   --scope user \
386.   -- npx -y @modelcontextprotocol/server-github
387. 
388. # Exa web search (needs key from exa.ai)
389. claude mcp add exa \
390.   -e EXA_API_KEY=exa_xxx \
391.   --scope user \
392.   -- npx -y exa-mcp-server
393. ```
394. 
395. To add a server from the template: copy it from `_api_key_servers_commented` → move it to `mcpServers` → fill in the real key → run `scripts/setup-mcp.sh --force`.
396. 
397. ---
398. 
399. ## OpenClaw Subagents
400. 
401. OpenClaw's native subagent system works via `~/.openclaw/subagents/`. After setup:
402. 
403. - Web tools (`ollama_web_search`, `ollama_web_fetch`) are enabled via the Ollama plugin
404. - Gateway runs on `localhost:18789`
405. - Skills are loaded from `~/.openclaw/workspace/skills/` (ECC skills copied there)
406. 
407. ---
408. 
409. ## ECC Skills — Everything Claude Code
410. 
411. Skills from **[Everything Claude Code (ECC)](https://github.com/affaan-m/everything-claude-code)** are the largest single source (184 SKILL.md dirs). ECC is a community-maintained library of production-ready AI agent skills covering testing, architecture, security, cloud deployment, language-specific patterns, and more.
412. 
413. ECC skills are structured Markdown prompts (`.md` files) that tell AI agents *how to think* about specific tasks — when to activate, how to approach the problem, examples, and pitfalls.
414. 
415. ### Skill Sources
416. 
417. | Source | Skills | Notes |
418. |--------|--------|-------|
419. | ECC core | 184 SKILL.md dirs | From `affaan-m/everything-claude-code` |
420. | K-Dense scientific | 134 SKILL.md dirs | From `K-Dense-AI/scientific-agent-skills` |
421. | ClawBio bioinformatics | 56 SKILL.md dirs | From `ClawBio/ClawBio` — pipeline skills with Python scripts |
422. | SkillGraph bioinformatics | Bioinformatics pipeline skills | From `variomeanalytics/bioinformatics-agent-skills` via MCP server |
423. | Anthropic official | 17 SKILL.md dirs (85 total .md) | From Anthropic's official skills library |
424. | OpenAI Codex curated | 44 SKILL.md dirs (534 total .md) | From OpenAI's Codex skills collection |
425. | Personal learned | varies | BMO-style real-time capture (corrections detected live via hooks) + batch 4-stage pipeline |
426. 
427. ### What Each Harness Gets
428. 
429. | Harness | Skills | How they load |
430. |---------|--------|--------------|
431. | `claude` / `ollama launch claude` | **~450 native** + lean skills cache | SKILL.md → `~/.claude/skills/` (native `/skills`) + personal learned skills via `lean-skills.txt` (~1-2K tokens, via `--append-system-prompt-file`) + MCP servers |
432. | `ollama launch openclaw` | **~450 skill dirs** | Real SKILL.md copies in `~/.openclaw/workspace/skills/`, YAML-sanitized |
433. | `ollama launch pi` | **~450 skill dirs** | Symlinks in `~/.pi/agent/skills/` |
434. | `ollama launch codex` | **~450 + 5 built-in** | YAML-sanitized copies in `~/.codex/skills/` + Codex system skills |
435. 
436. Native `~/.claude/skills/` installation means skills are visible via Claude Code's `/skills` command and load **regardless of launch method** (direct CLI, `ollama launch`, VSCode extension).
437. 
438. > **YAML sanitization:** Skills with block-scalar descriptions, extra metadata fields (author, version, tags), or nested YAML mappings are automatically sanitized without modifying source files.
439. 
440. ### Cross-Harness Skill Sync
````

</details>

````
440. ### Cross-Harness Skill Sync
441. 
442. When you learn something useful in one session, sync it everywhere:
443. ```bash
444. learn-sync          # Extract patterns + sync to all harnesses
445. learn-sync-dry      # Preview what would sync
446. learn-stats         # Show skill counts, feedback scores, decay status
447. learn-prune         # Archive low-signal skills (feedback × decay < 0.2)
448. ```
449. 
450. ---
451. 
452. ## Harness Evolution — Self-Improving Skills
453. 
454. > Your harnesses get better over time. Corrections and preferences you state during sessions are captured in real-time and automatically distilled into concise, generalizable skills that load in every future session across every harness.
455. 
456. Two complementary learning approaches run in parallel:
457. 
458. ### 1. BMO-Style Real-Time Capture (primary)
459. 
460. Inspired by [bmo-agent](https://github.com/joelhans/bmo-agent). A `UserPromptSubmit` hook (`hooks/learning-capture.sh`) fires on every message and detects learning events as they happen:
461. 
462. | Event type | Detection | Example |
463. |------------|-----------|---------|
464. | `correction` | "No,", "Actually…", "That's not what I meant" | "No, use absolute paths here" |
465. | `preference` | "I prefer…", "I always…", "I like…" | "I always want type hints in Python" |
466. | `pattern` | "best practice", "convention", "should always" | "Should always validate before pushing" |
467. 
468. Events are saved to `~/.claude/skills/learned/events/` as JSON. At session end (`hooks/session-reflection.sh`), `scripts/consolidate-learnings.py` clusters similar events and writes SKILL.md files with **short, imperative names** (e.g. `verify-output-completeness`, `cite-published-research`).
469. 
470. ```bash
471. # Manual consolidation (also runs automatically at session end)
472. python3 scripts/consolidate-learnings.py
473. ```
474. 
475. ### 2. Batch Pipeline (secondary — runs on install/update)
476. 
477. For bulk distillation from conversation history. A 4-stage ALMA-inspired pipeline in `extract-conversation-skills.py`:
478. 
479. | Stage | What it does |
480. |-------|-------------|
481. | **1. Ingestion** | Parses conversation histories, classifies user corrections into memory types: `anti_pattern` (failed approaches), `heuristic` (successful strategies), `preference` (style), `domain_knowledge` (project-specific — **rejected**) |
482. | **2. Learning** | Groups similar corrections (Jaccard ≥ 0.5), requires **3+ unique sessions** (configurable via `--min-occurrences`), confidence = success × min(count/20, 1.0) + cross-project bonus, minimum 0.5 |
483. | **3. Consolidation** | Deduplicates (token overlap ≥ 0.85), abstracts raw corrections into **condition + strategy + anti-pattern** via keyword mapping or LLM distillation (`--llm`), quality gates reject empty/generic/single-project patterns |
484. | **4. Output** | Writes concise SKILL.md files with short imperative names (`verify-X`, `avoid-X`), YAML frontmatter (name, description, origin, tags, version, priority) |
485. 
486. **Quality-first design**: Without `--llm`, only skills matching known condition templates are written — producing 0 skills is better than keyword soup. With `--llm`, Ollama distills corrections into proper condition+strategy+anti-pattern form, and generates short semantic names like `cite-published-research-dois` (not sentence fragments).
487. 
488. ### Skill format
489. 
490. Every learned skill follows ECC-compatible structure — frontmatter, `When to Use`, `Operating Principles`, `Anti-patterns`, `Provenance`:
491. 
492. ```markdown
493. ---
494. name: evidence-based-claims
495. description: Require published evidence for scientific claims. Learned from 5 sessions across 3 projects.
496. origin: conversation-pipeline
497. tags: [learned, anti_pattern, universal]
498. version: 1.0.0
499. priority: high
500. ---
501. 
502. # Evidence Based Claims
503. 
504. ## When to Use
505. 
506. Making scientific or factual claims that could be verified against literature.
507. 
508. ## Operating Principles
509. 
510. 1. Cite specific papers (DOI, PMID) or explicitly state "no published evidence found".
511. 2. Distinguish model output from experimental data.
512. 3. Flag claims that lack published support.
513. 
514. ## Anti-patterns
515. 
516. - Fabricating citations or DOIs.
517. - Presenting unverified results as established findings.
518. - Claiming model output as experimental data.
519. 
520. ## Provenance
521. 
522. - **Confidence:** 0.72
523. - **Unique sessions:** 5
524. - **Projects:** 3
525. - **Harnesses:** 2
526. - **First observed:** 2025-04-10
527. ```
528. 
529. ### Feedback & decay
530.
````

</details>

645. ---
646. 
647. ## Cloud vs Local Models
648. 
649. Ollama offers two model types: **cloud models** (hosted on Ollama's servers, no local GPU needed, require internet) and **local models** (downloaded and run on your machine).
650. 
651. ### Cloud Models
652. 
653. | Model | Context | Best for |
654. |-------|---------|---------|
655. | `qwen3.5:cloud` | 256K | Most capable cloud model — 397B MoE, vision + tools + thinking |
656. | `gemma4:31b-cloud` | 256K | Google's frontier model — strong reasoning, coding, vision |
657. | `glm-5.1:cloud` | — | Zhipu's flagship — top SWE-Bench Pro, agentic coding |
658. | `minimax-m2.7:cloud` | — | MiniMax M2 — coding, agentic workflows, professional tasks |
659. | `nemotron-3-super:cloud` | — | NVIDIA 120B MoE (12B active) — multi-agent applications |
660. 
661. ### Local Models
662. 
663. | Model | Context | Best for |
664. |-------|---------|---------|
665. | `qwen3.6` | 256K | MoE 36B — agentic coding, general-purpose (**Recommended**) |
666. | `gemma4:26b` | 128K | MoE 26B (4B active) — reasoning + vision, efficient |
667. | `devstral-small-2` | 128K | Mistral 24B — software engineering, codebase exploration |
668. | `qwen3:30b` | 256K | MoE 30B (3B active) — fast reasoning |
669. | `gemma4:e4b` | 128K | MoE 4B — edge/on-device, lightweight agent tasks |
670. 
671. > Context windows and model details from [ollama.com/library](https://ollama.com/library). Run `ollama show <model>` locally to verify.
672. 
673. ```bash
674. # Cloud models — no download needed, stream at inference time:
675. # Use directly: ollama run qwen3.5:cloud
676. 
677. # Local models — download to run on your machine:
678. ollama pull qwen3.6
679. ollama pull gemma4:e4b           # lightweight, good for subagents
680. ```
681. 
682. ```bash
683. # Configure for Claude Code (large context tasks):
684. ./install.sh --model qwen3.6
685. 
686. # Configure for OpenClaw / Pi / Codex (cloud reasoning):
687. ./install.sh --model qwen3.5:cloud
688. 
689. # Add a lightweight local fallback (no internet needed):
690. ollama pull gemma4:e4b
691. ```
692. 
693. ---
694. 
695. ## Codebase Context — Codesight Integration
696. 
697. > **Powered by [codesight](https://github.com/Houseofmvps/codesight)** — *See your codebase clearly.* Universal AI context generator that maps routes, schema, components, dependencies, and more for Claude Code, Cursor, Copilot, Codex, and any AI coding tool.
698. 
699. ### What codesight does
700. 
701. When Claude Code is working inside any repo, `codesight --mcp` provides a real-time context map so Claude understands *where things live* without manually exploring files. It generates:
702. 
703. - Route maps, schema, components, library dependencies
704. - `CLAUDE.md` — auto-generated project context for Claude Code
705. - `.cursorrules` — Cursor IDE rules
706. - `codex.md` / `AGENTS.md` — config for Codex and agentic tools
707. - `.codesight/CODESIGHT.md` — full AI context map (~200 tokens vs ~1,100 tokens of manual exploration)
708. 
709. ### How it's integrated
710. 
711. `codesight` runs as an MCP server — one of the 8 servers applied automatically by `./install.sh`:
712. 
713. ```bash
714. # Claude Code queries this server for codebase context automatically
715. npx -y codesight --mcp
716. ```
717. 
718. ### Using codesight in your own repos
719. 
720. ```bash
````

</details>

```
1. #!/bin/bash
2. # =============================================================================
3. # verify.sh — Verify the full agent harness setup is working
4. # =============================================================================
5. # Checks all components: Ollama, Claude Code MCP, OpenClaw, Pi, Codex,
6. # skills layer, and network/proxy status.
7. #
8. # Usage:
9. #   scripts/verify.sh [--fix]
10. #   --fix : attempt to auto-fix issues found
11. # =============================================================================
12. 
13. REPO_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")/.." && pwd)"
14. 
15. # Platform detection
16. case "$(uname -s)" in
17.     Darwin*)                 OS_TYPE="macOS" ;;
18.     Linux*)                  OS_TYPE="Linux" ;;
19.     MINGW*|MSYS*|CYGWIN*)   OS_TYPE="Windows" ;;
20.     *)                       OS_TYPE="Unknown" ;;
21. esac
22. 
23. # Detect user's default shell
24. case "${SHELL##*/}" in
25.     zsh)  USER_SHELL="zsh";  PRIMARY_RC="$HOME/.zshrc" ;;
26.     bash) USER_SHELL="bash"; PRIMARY_RC="$HOME/.bashrc" ;;
27.     *)    USER_SHELL="bash"; PRIMARY_RC="$HOME/.bashrc" ;;
28. esac
29. 
30. BLUE='\033[0;34m'; GREEN='\033[0;32m'; YELLOW='\033[1;33m'; RED='\033[0;31m'; CYAN='\033[0;36m'; NC='\033[0m'
31. ok()   { echo -e "  ${GREEN}✓${NC} $1"; }
32. fail() { echo -e "  ${RED}✗${NC} $1"; FAIL_COUNT=$((FAIL_COUNT+1)); }
33. warn() { echo -e "  ${YELLOW}⚠${NC} $1"; WARN_COUNT=$((WARN_COUNT+1)); }
34. section() { echo ""; echo -e "${CYAN}── $1 ──${NC}"; }
35. 
36. FAIL_COUNT=0; WARN_COUNT=0
37. FIX=false
38. [[ "$*" == *"--fix"* ]] && FIX=true
39. 
40. echo ""
41. echo "╔══════════════════════════════════════╗"
42. echo "║   Agent Harness Verification         ║"
43. echo "╚══════════════════════════════════════╝"
44. 
45. # ── Ollama ──
46. section "Ollama"
47. if command -v ollama >/dev/null 2>&1; then
48.     ok "ollama binary found: $(command -v ollama)"
49. else
50.     fail "ollama not found — install from https://ollama.com"
51. fi
52. 
53. if curl -s --max-time 3 http://localhost:11434/api/status >/dev/null 2>&1; then
54.     ok "Ollama server running (localhost:11434)"
55.     MODELS=$(ollama list 2>/dev/null | tail -n +2 | awk '{print $1}' | tr '\n' ' ')
56.     ok "Models: $MODELS"
57. else
58.     fail "Ollama server not running — start it: ollama serve"
59. fi
60. 
61. # Check config
62. if [ -f "$HOME/.ollama/config.json" ]; then
63.     ok "~/.ollama/config.json present"
64.     LAST_SEL=$(python3 -c "import json; d=json.load(open('$HOME/.ollama/config.json')); print(d.get('last_selection','?'))" 2>/dev/null)
65.     ok "  Last integration: $LAST_SEL"
66. else
67.     warn "~/.ollama/config.json not found"
68. fi
69. 
70. # ── Claude Code ──
71. section "Claude Code (ollama launch claude)"
72. if command -v claude >/dev/null 2>&1; then
73.     ok "claude binary found"
74. else
75.     fail "claude not found — install Claude Code"
76. fi
77. 
78. if [ -f "$HOME/.claude.json" ]; then
79.     ok "~/.claude.json present"
80.     MCP_COUNT=$(python3 -c "import json; d=json.load(open('$HOME/.claude.json')); print(len(d.get('mcpServers',{})))" 2>/dev/null)
```

149. import json, os

151. web_fetch = cfg.get("tools",{}).get("web",{}).get("fetch",{}).get("enabled", False)

155. gw_port = cfg.get("gateway",{}).get("port", "?")

158. print(f"  Web search:    {'✓ enabled' if web_search else '✗ disabled'}")

160. print(f"  Gateway port:  {gw_port}")
161. PYEOF
162. else

169.     fi
170. fi

172. # Check gateway running

174.     ok "OpenClaw gateway: running (port 18789)"
175. else

183. else
184.     warn "pi not found — install Pi agent"
185. fi
186. 
187. if [ -f "$HOME/.pi/agent/settings.json" ]; then

192. print(f"  Model:    {s.get('defaultModel','?')}")
193. print(f"  Provider: {s.get('defaultProvider','?')}")
194. print(f"  Packages: {s.get('packages',[])}")
195. PYEOF

197.     ok "Pi skills directory: $SKILL_COUNT skills linked"
198. else
199.     warn "~/.pi/agent/settings.json not found"
200. fi
201. 
202. # ── Codex ──
203. section "Codex (ollama launch codex)"

205.     ok "codex binary found"
206. else

208. fi
209.

211.     ok "~/.codex/config.toml present"

213.     MODEL=$(grep "^model = " "$HOME/.codex/config.toml" | cut -d'"' -f2)
214.     ok "  Provider: $PROVIDER | Model: $MODEL"
215. else

217. fi
218. 
219. # ── Skills Layer ──
220. section "Shell Skills Layer"
221. 
222. # Check all possible RC files, but primary is based on user's shell
223. skills_found=false
224. for rc_file in "$PRIMARY_RC" "$HOME/.zshrc" "$HOME/.bashrc" "$HOME/.bash_profile" "$HOME/.profile"; do
225.     if [ -f "$rc_file" ] && grep -q "_claude_with_skills\|# Skills Layer" "$rc_file" 2>/dev/null; then
226.         ok "Skills layer in $rc_file"
227.         skills_found=true
228.     fi
229. done
230. if ! $skills_found; then
231.     fail "Skills layer not installed in any shell rc file"
232.     if $FIX; then
233.         echo "    → Running safe-install.sh..."
234.         "$REPO_DIR/safe-install.sh" && ok "Fixed: Skills layer installed"
235.     else
236.         echo "    → Fix: ./safe-install.sh"
237.     fi
238. fi
239. 
240. # Check aliases in the primary rc file
241. for alias_name in claude openclaw codex ollama pi; do
242.     if grep -q "alias $alias_name=" "$PRIMARY_RC" 2>/dev/null; then
243.         ok "alias '$alias_name' configured in $(basename "$PRIMARY_RC")"
244.     elif grep -q "alias $alias_name=" "$HOME/.zshrc" "$HOME/.bashrc" 2>/dev/null; then
245.         ok "alias '$alias_name' configured (in alternate rc)"
246.     else
247.         warn "alias '$alias_name' not found in shell rc files"
248.     fi
249. done

279. 
280. # ── Network / Proxy ──
281. section "Network & Proxy"
282. 
283. if [ "$OS_TYPE" = "macOS" ]; then
284.     PROXY_HTTP=$(scutil --proxy 2>/dev/null | grep "HTTPEnable" | awk '{print $3}')
285.     PROXY_HTTPS=$(scutil --proxy 2>/dev/null | grep "HTTPSEnable" | awk '{print $3}')
286. 
287.     if [ "$PROXY_HTTP" = "1" ] || [ "$PROXY_HTTPS" = "1" ]; then
288.         warn "System HTTP/HTTPS proxy is ENABLED — may intercept Ollama streams"
289.         echo "    → Fix: scripts/disable-zscaler.sh"
290.     else
291.         ok "No system HTTP/HTTPS proxy (Zscaler off)"
292.     fi
293. 
294.     if launchctl list 2>/dev/null | grep -q "zscaler"; then
295.         warn "Zscaler tray still running"
296.         echo "    → Fix: scripts/disable-zscaler.sh --tray"
297.     else
298.         ok "Zscaler tray: not running"
299.     fi
300. else
301.     # Linux/Windows: check environment proxy variables
302.     if [ -n "$http_proxy" ] || [ -n "$https_proxy" ] || [ -n "$HTTP_PROXY" ] || [ -n "$HTTPS_PROXY" ]; then
303.         warn "HTTP proxy env vars set — may intercept Ollama streams"
304.         [ -n "$http_proxy" ] && echo "    http_proxy=$http_proxy"
305.         [ -n "$https_proxy" ] && echo "    https_proxy=$https_proxy"
306.     else
307.         ok "No proxy env vars set"
308.     fi
309. fi
310. 
311. if curl -s --max-time 5 -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" https://ollama.com 2>/dev/null | grep -q "200\|301\|302"; then
312.     ok "ollama.com reachable"
313. else
314.     warn "ollama.com not reachable — cloud models may fail"
315. fi
316. 
317. # ── Summary ──
318. echo ""
319. echo "════════════════════════════════════════"
320. if [ $FAIL_COUNT -eq 0 ] && [ $WARN_COUNT -eq 0 ]; then
321.     echo -e "${GREEN}✓ All checks passed!${NC}"
322. elif [ $FAIL_COUNT -eq 0 ]; then
323.     echo -e "${YELLOW}✓ Passed with $WARN_COUNT warning(s) — review above${NC}"
324. else
325.     echo -e "${RED}✗ $FAIL_COUNT failure(s), $WARN_COUNT warning(s) — run with --fix or check above${NC}"
326. fi
327. echo ""
328.
```

</details>

<sub>⏱️ 40m 48s</sub>

### ✅ `view`

**view the file at /Users/chazh/Documents/GitHub/ai-skillweave/configs/zshrc-skills-block.sh.**

/Users/chazh/Documents/GitHub/ai-skillweave/configs/zshrc-skills-block.sh

<details>
<summary>59 lines</summary>

```
1. 
2. # =============================================================================
3. # Skills Layer — Everything Claude Code
4. # =============================================================================
5. # All harness commands get the ECC skills injected automatically.
6. # Injected by: ai-skillweave/install.sh
7. # To remove:   ai-skillweave/safe-install.sh --uninstall
8. # =============================================================================
9. 
10. # Claude Code: inject personal learned skills as system prompt supplement.
11. # Uses lean-skills.txt (~personal skills only, ~1-2K tokens) NOT combined-skills.txt
12. # (~1.4M tokens which would exceed Claude's 200K context window and crash the session).
13. # The full 450+ skill library is already natively available via Claude Code's /skills
14. # command from ~/.claude/skills/ — no injection needed for those.
15. _claude_with_skills() {
16.     local _skills_file="/tmp/claude-skills-$$.txt"
17.     cat ~/.claude/skills-cache/lean-skills.txt > "$_skills_file" 2>/dev/null
18.     if [ -s "$_skills_file" ]; then
19.         (unset SKILLS_CONTENT CODEX_SYSTEM_PROMPT OPENCLAW_SYSTEM_PROMPT; command claude --append-system-prompt-file "$_skills_file" "$@")
20.     else
21.         (unset SKILLS_CONTENT CODEX_SYSTEM_PROMPT OPENCLAW_SYSTEM_PROMPT; command claude "$@")
22.     fi
23.     rm -f "$_skills_file"
24. }
25. 
26. # OpenClaw: loads SKILL.md files natively from ~/.openclaw/workspace/skills/
27. _openclaw_with_skills() {
28.     (unset SKILLS_CONTENT CODEX_SYSTEM_PROMPT OPENCLAW_SYSTEM_PROMPT; command openclaw "$@")
29. }
30. 
31. # Codex: loads skills natively from ~/.codex/skills/ (ECC skills symlinked there)
32. _codex_with_skills() {
33.     (unset SKILLS_CONTENT OPENCLAW_SYSTEM_PROMPT; command codex "$@")
34. }
35. 
36. # Ollama: pass-through with env cleanup (openclaw/codex/pi load skills natively)
37. _ollama_with_skills() {
38.     (unset SKILLS_CONTENT CODEX_SYSTEM_PROMPT OPENCLAW_SYSTEM_PROMPT OLLAMA_SYSTEM_FILE; command ollama "$@")
39. }
40. 
41. # Pi: loads skills natively from ~/.pi/agent/skills/
42. _pi_with_skills() {
43.     (unset SKILLS_CONTENT CODEX_SYSTEM_PROMPT OPENCLAW_SYSTEM_PROMPT; command pi "$@")
44. }
45. 
46. # Cross-harness skill learner
47. alias learn-sync='bash ~/.claude/scripts/sync-learned-skills.sh'
48. alias learn-sync-dry='bash ~/.claude/scripts/sync-learned-skills.sh --dry-run --verbose'
49. alias learn-stats='bash ~/.claude/scripts/sync-learned-skills.sh --stats'
50. alias learn-prune='bash ~/.claude/scripts/sync-learned-skills.sh --prune'
51. 
52. # Wrapper aliases
53. alias claude='_claude_with_skills'
54. alias openclaw='_openclaw_with_skills'
55. alias codex='_codex_with_skills'
56. alias ollama='_ollama_with_skills'
57. alias pi='_pi_with_skills'
58. # End Skills Layer
59.
```

</details>

25:`ai-skillweave`'s core contribution is **cross-harness delivery**: each skill library was originally designed for a single harness. 545. Only **2 of 4 memory types** produce generalizable skills: `heuristic` and `anti_pattern`. Preferences are per-user; domain knowledge is project-specific. This ensures learned skills are universally applicable, not project noise.
546. 
547. ### Installing ECC
548. 
549. ```bash
550. ./safe-install.sh                                    # ECC only
551. ./safe-install.sh --with-science                     # ECC + K-Dense scientific skills (134 skills)
552. ./safe-install.sh --with-bio                         # ECC + ClawBio bioinformatics (56 skills)
553. ./safe-install.sh --with-science --with-bio          # Full install: all skill sources
554. ```
555. 
556. ### Keeping ECC Up to Date
557. 
558. When ECC adds new skills upstream, pull and rebuild without a full re-install:
559. 
560. ```bash
561. scripts/update-ecc.sh           # Pull latest ECC + rebuild cache + re-sync all harnesses
562. scripts/update-ecc.sh --check   # Just check if updates are available
563. scripts/update-ecc.sh --force   # Force cache rebuild even if already up to date
564. ```
565. 
566. To update K-Dense scientific skills, re-run with `--with-science`:
567. 
568. ```bash
569. ./safe-install.sh --with-science   # Re-clone K-Dense repo + rebuild cache + re-sync
570. ```
571. 
572. To update ClawBio bioinformatics skills, re-run with `--with-bio`:
573. 
574. ```bash
575. ./safe-install.sh --with-bio   # Re-clone ClawBio repo + rebuild cache + re-sync
576. ```
577. 
578. ---
579. 
580. ## Context Window & Skills Injection
````

<sub>⏱️ 41m 19s</sub>

### ✅ `view`

**view the file at /Users/chazh/Documents/GitHub/ai-skillweave/README.md.**

/Users/chazh/Documents/GitHub/ai-skillweave/README.md (lines 860-895)

<details>
<summary>30 lines</summary>

````
860. 
861. ```bash
862. # Run just beads setup
863. ./install.sh --only beads
864. 
865. # Or standalone
866. scripts/setup-beads.sh
867. scripts/setup-beads.sh --force    # overwrite existing beads MCP entry
868. ```
869. 
870. ### Stealth mode
871. 
872. `--stealth` means `.beads/` is kept local (in `.gitignore`) — your beads items don't get committed to the project repo. `AGENTS.md` is still updated by `bd init` and is safe to commit.
873. 
874. ### Harness-agnostic design
875. 
876. The MCP server (`beads-mcp`) makes beads available in any harness that loads `~/.claude.json` (Claude Code, Copilot) or `~/.copilot/mcp-config.json`. For OpenClaw, Pi, and Codex, `bd prime` output can be pasted directly — the `AGENTS.md` file is what matters for cross-session persistence.
877. 
878. ---
879. 
880. ## See Also
881. 
882. - `docs/TROUBLESHOOTING.md` — Common problems and solutions
883. - `~/.claude-everything-claude-code/` — Full ECC skills repository
884. - `~/.claude-scientific-skills/` — K-Dense scientific agent skills repository
885. - `~/.claude-clawbio-skills/` — ClawBio bioinformatics pipeline skills repository
886. - [ClawBio/ClawBio](https://github.com/ClawBio/ClawBio) — Bioinformatics-native pipeline skills with executable Python scripts
887. - [variomeanalytics/bioinformatics-agent-skills](https://github.com/variomeanalytics/bioinformatics-agent-skills) — Bioinformatics pipeline skills + knowledge graph (MCP)
888. - `~/.claude-everything-claude-code/mcp-configs/mcp-servers.json` — Complete MCP server reference
889.
````

156. config_path = "$config_path"

161.

163. if key not in config:

165.

179. print(f"{action} beads MCP entry in {config_path}")
180. PYEOF
181. }

193.     fi

<sub>⏱️ 41m 51s</sub>

### ✅ `view`

**view the file at /Users/chazh/Documents/GitHub/ai-skillweave/safe-install.sh.**

/Users/chazh/Documents/GitHub/ai-skillweave/safe-install.sh (lines 490-555)

<details>
<summary>66 lines</summary>

```
490. # To disable: run ~/.claude/scripts/safe-install.sh --uninstall
491. 
492. # Helper function to inject skills as system prompt (uses file to avoid arg length limits)
493. # Uses lean-skills.txt (personal learned skills only, ~1-2K tokens) instead of
494. # combined-skills.txt (~1.4M tokens — exceeds Claude's 200K context window).
495. # The full 450+ skill library loads natively via ~/.claude/skills/ (/skills command).
496. _claude_with_skills() {
497.     local _skills_file="/tmp/claude-skills-$$.txt"
498.     cat ~/.claude/skills-cache/lean-skills.txt > "$_skills_file" 2>/dev/null
499.     if [ -s "$_skills_file" ]; then
500.         (unset SKILLS_CONTENT CODEX_SYSTEM_PROMPT OPENCLAW_SYSTEM_PROMPT; command claude --append-system-prompt-file "$_skills_file" "$@")
501.     else
502.         (unset SKILLS_CONTENT CODEX_SYSTEM_PROMPT OPENCLAW_SYSTEM_PROMPT; command claude "$@")
503.     fi
504.     rm -f "$_skills_file"
505. }
506. 
507. _openclaw_with_skills() {
508.     # OpenClaw natively loads workspace/skills/ SKILL.md files — no extra env var needed.
509.     # OPENCLAW_SYSTEM_PROMPT_FILE is NOT a real openclaw env var (not in source).
510.     (unset SKILLS_CONTENT CODEX_SYSTEM_PROMPT OPENCLAW_SYSTEM_PROMPT; command openclaw "$@")
511. }
512. 
513. _codex_with_skills() {
514.     # Codex loads skills natively from ~/.codex/skills/ (ECC skills symlinked there)
515.     (unset SKILLS_CONTENT OPENCLAW_SYSTEM_PROMPT; command codex "$@")
516. }
517. 
518. _ollama_with_skills() {
519.     # OLLAMA_SYSTEM_FILE is NOT set (breaks ollama launch codex/pi).
520.     # OPENCLAW_SYSTEM_PROMPT_FILE is not a real openclaw env var — removed.
521.     # OpenClaw loads ECC skills natively from ~/.openclaw/workspace/skills/ SKILL.md files.
522.     (unset SKILLS_CONTENT CODEX_SYSTEM_PROMPT OPENCLAW_SYSTEM_PROMPT OLLAMA_SYSTEM_FILE; command ollama "$@")
523. }
524. 
525. _pi_with_skills() {
526.     # Pi loads skills natively from ~/.pi/agent/skills/ (ECC skills symlinked there)
527.     (unset SKILLS_CONTENT CODEX_SYSTEM_PROMPT OPENCLAW_SYSTEM_PROMPT; command pi "$@")
528. }
529. 
530. _copilot_with_skills() {
531.     # Copilot CLI does NOT load ~/.claude/skills/ natively (different format).
532.     # It reads ~/.copilot/mcp-config.json for MCP servers and .github/copilot-instructions.md
533.     # for per-repo guidance. This wrapper ensures a clean environment.
534.     (unset SKILLS_CONTENT CODEX_SYSTEM_PROMPT OPENCLAW_SYSTEM_PROMPT; command copilot "$@")
535. }
536. 
537. # Wrapper aliases
538. alias claude='_claude_with_skills'
539. alias openclaw='_openclaw_with_skills'
540. alias codex='_codex_with_skills'
541. alias ollama='_ollama_with_skills'
542. alias pi='_pi_with_skills'
543. alias copilot='_copilot_with_skills'
544. 
545. # Learning pipeline commands
546. alias learn-sync='bash ~/.claude/scripts/sync-learned-skills.sh'
547. alias learn-sync-dry='bash ~/.claude/scripts/sync-learned-skills.sh --dry-run'
548. alias learn-stats='bash ~/.claude/scripts/sync-learned-skills.sh --stats'
549. alias learn-prune='bash ~/.claude/scripts/sync-learned-skills.sh --prune'
550. # End Skills Layer
551. SKILLS_BLOCK
552. }
553. 
554. setup_shell_integration() {
555.     log "Step 4: Setting up shell integration..."
```

</details>

<sub>⏱️ 45m 13s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

Pushed. Here's what I found and fixed:

**The key discovery:** Copilot CLI natively reads `~/.claude/skills/` — it's hardcoded in the Copilot binary's skill discovery function as `personal-claude` source. That's exactly why you see ~400 skills (246 SKILL.md files from ECC/K-Dense/ClawBio). The previous documentation was completely wrong about this.

**What was fixed in commit `00283f1`:**

1. **README Copilot caveat** — replaced false "does NOT load ~/.claude/skills/" with accurate description of native skill discovery
2. **README "What Each Harness Gets"** — added Copilot row with how discovery works (`disabledSkills`, `COPILOT_SKILLS_DIRS` env var)
3. **README "Installing ECC"** — fixed: bare `./safe-install.sh` defaults to ECC+science+bio (not ECC-only)
4. **`configs/zshrc-skills-block.sh`** — added missing `_copilot_with_skills()` + `alias copilot=`; fixed comment to reflect native loading; synced `learn-sync-dry` flags
5. **`safe-install.sh`** — fixed `_copilot_with_skills()` comment (now accurate)
6. **`scripts/verify.sh`** — added full Copilot CLI section (binary check, mcp-config, server count, beads entry, SKILL.md count)
7. **`install.sh --help`** — added `--with-curated`, `--without-curated`, `--learn`, `--no-learn`
8. **`scripts/setup-beads.sh`** — fixed "Updated" vs "Added" logic
9. **`docs/TROUBLESHOOTING.md`** — created (was referenced in README but didn't exist)

---

For EACH script, identify:
1. **Bugs** — logic errors, incorrect paths, wrong variable names, broken conditionals, wrong exit codes
2. **Security issues** — unquoted variables, arbitrary code execution, path traversal risks
3. **Portability issues** — macOS-only commands that break on Linux, or vice versa (especially md5, sed -i, grep -P, etc.)
4. **Stale/outdated references** — references to combined-skills.txt instead of lean-skills.txt, wrong install paths, deprecated flags
5. **Missing functionality** — things the script claims to do but doesn't actually do, missing --force flags, missing idempotency
6. **Inconsistencies** — differences between this script's behavior and what safe-install.sh or install.sh expects

Report findings as a structured list: SCRIPT → ISSUE TYPE → LINE NUMBER → DESCRIPTION → SEVERITY (blocking/high/medium/low).

Be thorough and specific. Show exact line numbers and code snippets where possible. Focus on real bugs, not style preferences.

|-----------|------|-------------|----------|
| **Security** | 72 | `SEEN_FILE=$(mktemp)` unquoted in trap cleanup (line 72). If filename contains spaces, causes issues. Fix: `trap "rm -f \"$SEEN_FILE\"" EXIT` | high |
| **Portability** | 53, 306 | `ls | wc -l | tr -d ' '` — non-portable across BSD/GNU. Fails on macOS with BSD wc when trailing spaces vary. Better: `ls | wc -l` with `$((...))` arithmetic or `find ... -maxdepth 1 -type f \| wc -l` | medium |
| **Bug** | 158 | `skill_entries = """$(IFS=$'\n'; echo "${SKILL_LIST[*]}")"""` — relies on Python 3 INSIDE bash with `<<PYEOF` heredoc. IFS modification only works in parent bash shell. Array is passed correctly, but this pattern is fragile. Potential issue if SKILL_LIST contains special characters. | medium |
| **Security** | 157-158 | Unquoted variable expansion in Python heredoc: `output_dir = """$OUTPUT_DIR"""` is safe (triple-quoted), but `skill_entries = """$(...)"""` could break if entries contain triple quotes or newlines. | medium |
| **Stale Reference** | 288 | PREAMBLE injected is hardcoded and references "lean-skills.txt" but build-desktop-skills.sh doesn't build that — it's built by update-ecc.sh and safe-install.sh. PREAMBLE should match actual workflow. | low |

---

### **cleanup-invalid-skills.sh**

| Issue Type | Line | Description | Severity |
|-----------|------|-------------|----------|
| **Portability** | 34 | `[ ${#skill_name} -gt 64 ]` — bash-specific syntax (string length). POSIX-compatible: use `expr length "$skill_name"` or similar. Works on most systems but not strict POSIX shells. | low |
| **Logic Bug** | 71-72 | Lines 71-72: Loop over skill directories, but doesn't check if `$skill_dir` is actually a directory before calling `basename` and passing to `check_skill_dir()`. The outer `[ -d "$skill_dir" ] || continue` guards this, but the guard is on line 72, after function call on line 75. Order is correct, but comment clarifies it. | low |
| **Missing Check** | 87-115 | Script checks learned skills in Codex (line 88) but the "learned" directory structure in Codex is unknown. Script assumes `$HOME/.codex/skills/` contains individual learned skill directories, but Codex's actual skill storage format is never validated. Risk: deletes valid skills. | medium |

---

### **consolidate-learnings.py**

| Issue Type | Line | Description | Severity |
|-----------|------|-------------|----------|
| **Import Error** | 30 | Bare `except:` on line 30 silently ignores all errors, including JSON parsing failures. If a learning event JSON is corrupted, it's silently skipped with no warning. Better: `except (json.JSONDecodeError, IOError) as e:` with logging. | medium |
| **Security** | 218 | `event_str = cluster_name.replace("_", "-").replace(" ", "-")[:64]` — cluster_name derived from user event messages (line 76). Not directly exploitable, but if event message contains path traversal or special chars, could cause issues. Truncation at 64 helps but no validation. | low |
| **Missing Feature** | 32 | Events sorted by timestamp (line 32), but `events[0].get("timestamp", "")` comparison is string-based, not datetime. If timestamps are ISO 8601 strings, lexicographic sort happens to work, but this is fragile. Better: parse to datetime. | medium |
| **Logic Bug** | 159 | `elif event_type == "preference":` — creates identical skill content as "pattern" type (lines 160-162), differing only in condition/strategy strings. Two event types producing nearly identical skills suggests incomplete design. | low |

---

### **desktop-batch-import.sh**

| Issue Type | Line | Description | Severity |
|-----------|------|-------------|----------|
| **Portability** | 67-70 | Python inline invocation to find manifest.json: `find ... -exec sh -c '...' _ {} \;` with Python. Unquoted `$1` in the inline shell script (line 68) can break if manifest path contains spaces. Better: use while loop with process substitution or xargs with proper escaping. | medium |
| **Security** | 68 | `json.load(open("$1"))` — `$1` is unquoted in Python. If path contains double quotes or special chars, JSON parsing fails without clear error. Use `open('$1', 'r')` with proper quoting. | medium |
| **Bug** | 91 | `python3 -c "... print(len(d.get('skills',[])))` — runs before manifest is fully populated. If manifest is malformed or empty, `len([])` returns 0, not an error. No validation that manifest structure is correct. | low |
| **Inconsistency** | 79 | Line 79: `SESSION_DIR=$(dirname "$SESSION_DIR")` — converts manifest.json path to session directory. But SESSION_DIR is found via `find`, which returns the manifest.json path, then dirname is applied. This assumes manifest is always at `<session-dir>/manifest.json`, which is correct but fragile. No validation. | low |
| **Missing Feature** | 100 | `--clean` flag removes all non-anthropic skills (line 109), but doesn't validate that remaining anthropic skills are actually valid. If an anthropic skill entry is corrupted in manifest, it's preserved. | low |

---

### **disable-zscaler.sh**

| Issue Type | Line | Description | Severity |
|-----------|------|-------------|----------|
| **Portability** | 40 | `scutil --proxy` — macOS-only command. Script is explicitly macOS-only (header says "macOS"), so this is acceptable. However, script doesn't check platform before using this command. If run on Linux, will fail with "scutil: command not found" without clear error message. | low |
| **Portability** | 40, 62 | `awk '{print $3}'` — assumes fixed column positions. If output format changes, parsing fails silently. Better: use grep + cut with explicit field delimiters. | low |
| **Logic Bug** | 87 | `if [[ "$*" == *"--tray"* ]]; then` after already processing tray unload (line 80-85). If `--tray` flag is set, the script unloads tray twice (once on line 82, then calls check_status which calls launchctl list again). Not a bug, but redundant. | low |
| **Security** | 106 | `if sudo -n true 2>/dev/null; then` — checks if sudo works without password. If sudo fails silently, script assumes it's not available. Better: capture exit code explicitly and warn if sudo is required but not available. | low |

---

### **setup-claude-desktop.sh**

| Issue Type | Line | Description | Severity |
|-----------|------|-------------|----------|
| **Bug** | 160 | `force = $([[ "$FORCE" == "true" ]] && echo "True" || echo "False")` — bash double-bracket syntax inside single-quoted heredoc is NOT evaluated. This creates Python variable `force` as a literal string (e.g., `"False"`), not a Python boolean. Line 268 then uses `if name in config["mcpServers"] and not force:` where `force` is a string. In Python, `not "False"` is falsy (empty string is falsy), so this works by accident, but `not "True"` is also falsy! **BUG: --force flag doesn't work correctly.** | blocking |
| **Security** | 237 | `cfg_str.replace("{{HOME}}", home)` — unquoted home path. If home contains regex special chars or backslashes, JSON parsing could fail. Better: use json.loads() with proper escaping. | medium |
| **Missing Validation** | 212-227 | `resolve_bin()` function searches for npx/node but doesn't validate the found binary is executable or in PATH. If a non-executable file exists at the path, shutil.which may return it. Better: verify `os.access(path, os.X_OK)`. | low |
| **Bug** | 314 | `link_skills()` function counts available skills with `find ... -type d ! -name 'learned'`, but doesn't verify these are actual skill directories (containing SKILL.md). Could count empty directories. | low |
| **Inconsistency** | 309-318 | `link_skills()` says "Skills work via / slash commands" but doesn't actually DO anything (it's a reporting function). Function name suggests it symlinks, but it doesn't. Name is misleading. | low |

---

### **setup-claude-md.sh**

| Issue Type | Line | Description | Severity |
|-----------|------|-------------|----------|
| **Portability** | 54 | `sed` with `re.sub()` in Python regex (`re.escape()` + `re.DOTALL`). This is Python code, so portable. However, if this script calls sed anywhere (it doesn't), it would be non-portable. Current script is OK. | low |
| **Bug** | 56 | `re.escape(marker) + r'.*?' + re.escape(marker_end)` — regex pattern uses ungreedy `.*?`, but DOTALL is set (line 56). This correctly handles multi-line markers. No issue here. | low |

---

### **setup-codex.sh**

| Issue Type | Line | Description | Severity |
|-----------|------|-------------|----------|
| **Portability** | 54 | `sed -e "s|...|...|g"` — uses `|` as delimiter (good for paths), but sed syntax is POSIX. However, `sed -e` with multiple `-e` options on same command line is GNU sed specific. BSD sed requires separate command invocations or multiline format. **This will fail on macOS with BSD sed.** | blocking |
| **Logic Bug** | 43 | `exit 0` when `~/.codex` doesn't exist. This silently skips setup instead of failing. If user forgets to install Codex, they won't know setup was skipped. Should be `exit 1` or at least a clearer warning. | medium |
| **Bug** | 40-44 | Lines 40-44: Check if `~/.codex` exists, but then apply sed to template which references `~/.codex/config.toml` (line 16, TARGET). If target directory doesn't exist, sed creates TARGET file at `~/.codex/config.toml`, but the directory `.codex` doesn't exist. `dirname "$TARGET"` is `~/.codex`, which doesn't exist. **Directory creation is missing.** | blocking |

---

### **setup-learning-hook.sh**

| Issue Type | Line | Description | Severity |
|-----------|------|-------------|----------|
| **Bug** | 26 | `hook_path = "$HOOK_PATH"` — single quotes around variable name in Python string. Bash variable `$HOOK_PATH` is correctly interpolated in heredoc (not single-quoted), but this is fragile. If HOOK_PATH contains quotes or special chars, Python string breaks. Better: use proper Python string escaping. | medium |
| **Logic Bug** | 36-40 | Loop transforms hook structure (lines 36-48), but if "hooks" key doesn't exist initially, it's created empty (line 34). However, the loop iterates over existing hook_types, so if no hook types exist yet, loop does nothing, then "UserPromptSubmit" is ensured to exist (line 50). This is correct but convoluted. | low |

---

### **setup-ollama-config.sh**

| Issue Type | Line | Description | Severity |
|-----------|------|-------------|----------|
| **Logic Bug** | 33-36 | `if [ ! -f "$TARGET" ]` — exits early if ~/.ollama/config.json doesn't exist. But script claims to be "idempotent" (line 7). If Ollama hasn't run yet, should create default config or at least create the directory. Current behavior silently skips. | medium |

---

### **setup-openclaw.sh**

| Issue Type | Line | Description | Severity |
|-----------|------|-------------|----------|
| **Unquoted Variable** | 57 | `target_path = os.path.expanduser("~/.openclaw/openclaw.json")` — hardcoded path is OK, but line 58 has `template_path = "$TEMPLATE"` (unquoted in Python). If TEMPLATE contains quotes or special chars, path breaks. Better: `template_path = """$TEMPLATE"""` for safety. | low |
| **Missing Directory Check** | 43-46 | Lines 43-46: Check if `~/.openclaw/openclaw.json` exists, but don't check if `~/.openclaw/` directory exists. If it doesn't, the backup copy operation (line 51: `cp "$TARGET" "$BACKUP"`) will fail. | high |

---

### **setup-pi.sh**

| Issue Type | Line | Description | Severity |
|-----------|------|-------------|----------|
| **Bug** | 94-95 | Lines 94-95: Create PI_SKILLS_DIR from json.load() output, but variable is used OUTSIDE the Python block (line 96). `PI_SKILLS_DIR` is set inside a nested Python -c invocation (line 94), so it's not available in bash on line 96. **This will cause mkdir to fail with unset variable.** | blocking |
| **Portability** | 94 | Python invocation: `python3 -c "import json; print(..."` — if json.load fails (invalid JSON), print() returns empty string, then `[ -n "" ]` is false, so mkdir is skipped. Should validate json_output before using. | medium |

---

### **update-ecc.sh**

| Issue Type | Line | Description | Severity |
|-----------|------|-------------|----------|
| **Portability** | 90 | `git log HEAD..origin/main --oneline 2>/dev/null \| wc -l` — assumes main branch. GitHub default is main, but older repos use master. Script handles this on line 92 with fallback, but line 90 only checks main. `git log HEAD..origin/master` on main-branch repos returns 0 silently. **Logic error: --behind count is wrong on master-branch repos.** | medium |
| **Logic Bug** | 73 | `DIRTY=$(git status --short skills/ 2>/dev/null \| grep -c '^.[^?]' || true)` — counts modified files in skills/. But git status output is always stdout (not stderr), so 2>/dev/null is unnecessary. grep count is correct (matches "^.[^?]" = modified lines that aren't untracked). This works but is confusing. | low |
| **Portability** | 495 | `stat -c %Y "$LOCK_FILE" 2>/dev/null \|\| stat -f %m "$LOCK_FILE"` — mixes GNU stat (`-c`) with BSD stat (`-f`). Correct (fallback works), but fragile. Both commands will run if first fails; second should use `&&`. Better: `stat -c %Y ... 2>/dev/null \|\| stat -f %m ... 2>/dev/null` (catch both errors). Current code runs both and gets exit code of second if first fails, which is correct but unclear. | low |
| **Security** | 504 | `SYNC_SCRIPT="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")/.." && pwd)/sync-learned-skills.sh"` — path constructed from BASH_SOURCE. If script is symlinked or sourced, BASH_SOURCE may not reflect actual location. Script is executable (#!/bin/bash), so this is OK, but symlink handling should be tested. | low |
| **Missing Idempotency** | 502-510 | Learning pipeline lock file (line 490) uses `/tmp/ai-skillweave-learn.lock` which is world-writable. Different user running this script could interfere with lock. Should use user-specific directory or check owner. | medium |

---

### **safe-install.sh** (lines 1-100, 400-600 cross-reference)

| Issue Type | Line | Description | Severity |
|-----------|------|-------------|----------|
| **Missing Feature** | 471-476 | Lines 471-476: lean-skills.txt created only if learned skills exist. But if no learned skills exist, file is created empty (line 475: `> "$SKILLS_CACHE_DIR/lean-skills.txt"`). However, wrapper function on line 498 (in later lines) uses `cat ~/.claude/skills-cache/lean-skills.txt > "$_skills_file"`. If lean cache is empty, empty file is created, but `[ -s "$_skills_file" ]` checks if file has content (line 499). This is correct (empty cache is handled). | low |
| **Inconsistency** | 495 | Comment says "combined-skills.txt (~1.4M tokens)" but script never loads/uses this variable for injection (see wrapper on line 500, which uses lean-skills.txt). Misleading comment. | low |

---

### **install.sh** (lines 1-50, 300-500 cross-reference)

| Issue Type | Line | Description | Severity |
|-----------|------|-------------|----------|
| **Logic Bug** | 374 | `bash "$REPO_DIR/scripts/update-ecc.sh" --force \|\| true` — always succeeds (pipe to true). If update-ecc.sh has real errors, they're swallowed. Better: `\|\| warn "update-ecc.sh had issues"` to at least report. | low |
| **Missing Feature** | 369 | Comment says "shell integration may need manual source", but doesn't tell user HOW to manually source. Should print actual command: `echo "Run: source $SHELL_RC"` | low |

---

## **CROSS-CUTTING ISSUES**

### **Stale/Outdated References**
- **build-desktop-skills.sh** line 145: References `./safe-install.sh --with-science` but this flag doesn't exist in safe-install.sh usage (lines 1-100 show `--with-science` as default, no --without-science flag visible... actually line 72 shows `--without-science` exists). Command is correct, but documentation could be clearer.
  
- **Multiple scripts**: References to "combined-skills.txt" vs "lean-skills.txt" inconsistent. Some scripts mention "combined-skills.txt" but it's deprecated in favor of "lean-skills.txt" (per safe-install.sh lines 472-473).

### **Missing Error Handling**
- **consolidate-learnings.py**: No validation that `LEARNINGS_DIR` and `SKILLS_DIR` are writable. If permissions are wrong, script fails silently.
- **update-ecc.sh**: git operations assume origin is correct remote. If user has multiple remotes, git fetch could pull from wrong one.

### **Security Issues (Command Injection)**
- **setup-claude-desktop.sh** line 157: `template_path = "$TEMPLATE"` — unquoted in Python heredoc. If TEMPLATE contains special chars, Python JSON parser breaks without clear error.
- **desktop-batch-import.sh** line 68: Unquoted `$1` in embedded Python/sh code.

---

## **SUMMARY BY SEVERITY**

### **BLOCKING (Must Fix)**
1. **setup-codex.sh:54** — `sed -e` syntax is GNU-specific, fails on BSD macOS
2. **setup-codex.sh:40-44** — Missing directory creation before sed output
3. **setup-pi.sh:94-95** — Python variable not exported to bash, mkdir fails
4. **setup-claude-desktop.sh:160** — `--force` flag doesn't work (Python boolean parsing bug)

### **HIGH (Likely Production Impact)**
1. **build-desktop-skills.sh:72** — unquoted mktemp in trap
2. **cleanup-invalid-skills.sh:87-115** — assumes Codex skill structure without validation
3. **setup-openclaw.sh:43-46** — missing directory existence check before file operations

### **MEDIUM (Should Fix)**
1. **desktop-batch-import.sh:67-70, 68** — unquoted paths in find/python invocation
2. **consolidate-learnings.py:30** — bare except silences errors
3. **setup-learning-hook.sh:26** — unquoted variable in Python string
4. **setup-ollama-config.sh:33-36** — exits silently instead of failing/creating config
5. **setup-pi.sh:94** — missing error handling for JSON parsing
6. **update-ecc.sh:90** — git branch logic broken for master-branch repos
7. **update-ecc.sh:502-510** — world-writable lock file security issue

---

**Most Critical Findings:**
- **setup-codex.sh** is completely broken on macOS (sed -i)
- **setup-pi.sh** has logic error that prevents skill directory creation
- **setup-claude-desktop.sh --force** flag is non-functional
- Multiple scripts have unquoted variable injection risks in heredocs and Python code
```

</details>

<sub>⏱️ 48m 13s</sub>

### ✅ `bash`

**Verify update-ecc.sh branch logic**

$ sed -n '60,105p' /Users/chazh/Documents/GitHub/ai-skillweave/scripts/update-ecc.sh

<details>
<summary>39 lines</summary>

```
git checkout -- skills/ 2>/dev/null || true
    success "Git tracking initialized + skills restored to current HEAD"
fi

log "Checking for ECC updates..."
cd "$ECC_DIR"

CURRENT=$(git rev-parse HEAD)
git fetch origin --quiet

REMOTE=$(git rev-parse origin/main 2>/dev/null || git rev-parse origin/master 2>/dev/null)

# Always ensure working tree skills match HEAD (guards against stale installs)
DIRTY=$(git status --short skills/ 2>/dev/null | grep -c '^.[^?]' || true)
if [ "$DIRTY" -gt 0 ] 2>/dev/null; then
    log "Working tree has $DIRTY stale skill files — restoring from HEAD..."
    git checkout -- skills/ 2>/dev/null || true
    success "Skills working tree restored to HEAD"
fi

if [ "$CURRENT" = "$REMOTE" ]; then
    success "ECC already up to date ($(git log -1 --format='%h %s' HEAD))"
    if [[ "$*" != *"--check"* ]]; then
        echo ""
        log "Run with --force to rebuild cache anyway:"
        echo "  scripts/update-ecc.sh --force"
        echo ""
    fi
    [[ "$*" == *"--force"* ]] || exit 0
else
    BEHIND=$(git log HEAD..origin/main --oneline 2>/dev/null | wc -l | tr -d ' ')
    log "ECC has $BEHIND new commit(s). Pulling..."
    git pull origin main --quiet 2>/dev/null || git pull origin master --quiet
    NEW=$(git rev-parse HEAD)
    success "Updated: $(git log -1 --format='%h %s' HEAD)"
    echo ""
    log "New skills:"
    git diff "$CURRENT" "$NEW" --name-only -- skills/ | head -20
fi

[[ "$*" == *"--check"* ]] && exit 0

CURATED_DIR="$HOME/.claude-curated-skills"

# =============================================================================
# Step 2: Rebuild combined skills cache (matches safe-install.sh priority order)
<exited with exit code 0>
```

events = []
    if not LEARNINGS_DIR.exists():
        return events
    
    for f in LEARNINGS_DIR.glob("*.json"):
        try:
            with open(f) as fp:
                data = json.load(fp)
                if data.get("status") == "pending":
                    events.append(data)
        except:
            continue
    return sorted(events, key=lambda e: e.get("timestamp", ""))

def cluster_events(events):
    """
    Cluster similar events together.
    Uses sentence-transformer semantic similarity when available,
    falling back to Jaccard word overlap.
    """
    if not events:
        return {}
    
    # Try semantic clustering with sentence-transformers
    if len(events) >= 5:
        try:
            from sentence_transformers import SentenceTransformer
            import numpy as np
            
            texts = [e.get("message", "") for e in events]
            encoder = SentenceTransformer('all-MiniLM-L6-v2')
---
def mark_processed(events):
    """Mark events as processed."""
    for event in events:
        event_ts = event.get("timestamp")
        for f in LEARNINGS_DIR.glob("*.json"):
            try:
                with open(f) as fp:
                    data = json.load(fp)
                if data.get("timestamp") == event_ts:
                    data["status"] = "processed"
                    data["processed_at"] = datetime.now().isoformat()
                    with open(f, "w") as fp:
                        json.dump(data, fp, indent=2)
                    break
            except:
                continue

def main():
    print("╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗")

python3 << PYEOF
import json
from pathlib import Path

settings_file = Path.home() / ".claude" / "settings.json"
hook_path = "$HOOK_PATH"

try:
    with open(settings_file) as f:
        settings = json.load(f)
    
    # Validate and fix malformed hooks structure
    if "hooks" not in settings:
        settings["hooks"] = {}
    
    for hook_type in list(settings["hooks"].keys()):
        entries = settings["hooks"][hook_type]
        if not isinstance(entries, list):
            settings["hooks"][hook_type] = []
            continue
        cleaned = []
        for entry in entries:
            if not isinstance(entry, dict):
                continue
            if "hooks" not in entry or not isinstance(entry["hooks"], list):
                entry["hooks"] = []
            cleaned.append(entry)
        settings["hooks"][hook_type] = cleaned
    
    if "UserPromptSubmit" not in settings["hooks"]:
        settings["hooks"]["UserPromptSubmit"] = []
    
    # Add learning capture hook if not already present
    hook_exists = any(
        any(hook.get("command") == hook_path for hook in entry.get("hooks", []))
---
echo "╔══════════════════════════════════════╗"
echo "║   Ollama Integrations Setup          ║"
echo "╚══════════════════════════════════════╝"
echo ""

command -v python3 >/dev/null 2>&1 || error "python3 not found"
[ -f "$TEMPLATE" ] || error "Template not found: $TEMPLATE"

if [ ! -f "$TARGET" ]; then
    warn "~/.ollama/config.json not found — Ollama not configured yet"
    warn "Start Ollama first (open /Applications/Ollama.app), then re-run"
    exit 0
fi

# Backup
BACKUP="$TARGET.bak_setup_$(date +%Y%m%d_%H%M%S)"
cp "$TARGET" "$BACKUP"
success "Backup: $BACKUP"

python3 << PYEOF
import json, os

<details>
<summary>60 lines</summary>

```
#!/bin/bash
# =============================================================================
# cleanup-invalid-skills.sh — Remove stale/invalid learned skills
# =============================================================================
# Deletes skills with:
#   - Names > 64 characters (truncated, invalid)
#   - Invalid YAML (mapping values not allowed, etc.)
#   - Empty/missing condition or strategy
#
# Safe to run (only touches learned skills, not curated/official).
# Moves deleted skills to ~/.claude/skills/learned/.archive/ instead of rm.
# =============================================================================
set -e

BLUE='\033[0;34m'; GREEN='\033[0;32m'; YELLOW='\033[1;33m'; RED='\033[0;31m'; NC='\033[0m'
log()     { echo -e "${BLUE}[CLEANUP]${NC} $1"; }
success() { echo -e "${GREEN}[OK]${NC} $1"; }
warn()    { echo -e "${YELLOW}[WARN]${NC} $1"; }
error()   { echo -e "${RED}[ERROR]${NC} $1"; }

ARCHIVE_DIR="$HOME/.claude/skills/learned/.archive"
mkdir -p "$ARCHIVE_DIR"

REMOVED=0
SKIPPED=0

# Function to check if a skill directory is invalid
check_skill_dir() {
    local dir="$1"
    local skill_name
---
}

# Clean learned skills in Claude Code
CLAUDE_LEARNED="$HOME/.claude/skills/learned"
if [ -d "$CLAUDE_LEARNED" ]; then
    log "Checking Claude Code learned skills: $CLAUDE_LEARNED"
    for skill_dir in "$CLAUDE_LEARNED"/*; do
        [ -d "$skill_dir" ] || continue
        [ "$(basename "$skill_dir")" = ".archive" ] && continue

        status=$(check_skill_dir "$skill_dir")
        if [ "$status" != "OK" ]; then
            skill_name=$(basename "$skill_dir")
            warn "Removing $skill_name — $status"
            mv "$skill_dir" "$ARCHIVE_DIR/$skill_name-$(date +%s)"
            REMOVED=$((REMOVED + 1))
        else
            SKIPPED=$((SKIPPED + 1))
        fi
    done
fi

# Clean learned skills in Codex
CODEX_LEARNED="$HOME/.codex/skills"
if [ -d "$CODEX_LEARNED" ]; then
    log "Checking Codex learned skills: $CODEX_LEARNED"
    for skill_dir in "$CODEX_LEARNED"/*; do
        [ -d "$skill_dir" ] || continue
        skill_name=$(basename "$skill_dir")

        # Only check skills that look like learned skills (long names or flat .md)
        if [ ${#skill_name} -gt 64 ]; then
            warn "Removing Codex skill $skill_name — name too long (${#skill_name} chars)"
            mv "$skill_dir" "$ARCHIVE_DIR/codex-$skill_name-$(date +%s)"
            REMOVED=$((REMOVED + 1))
            continue
<exited with exit code 0>
```

</details>

<sub>⏱️ 49m 10s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

The agent's blocking findings were mostly false positives after verifying. The real bugs are:

1. **`update-ecc.sh` line 90** — hardcodes `origin/main` for BEHIND count; repos using `master` show "0 new commits" even when updates exist
2. **`consolidate-learnings.py`** — bare `except:` catches `SystemExit`/`KeyboardInterrupt` too

Everything else was either intentional (exit 0 when prereq not installed), working correctly (`--force` in setup-claude-desktop.sh evaluates to Python `True`/`False` literals), or very low risk. Let me fix the real ones:

<sub>⏱️ 50m 16s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

All done. Here's a summary of what was just fixed and what the full audit resolution looks like:

**Pushed `4ecec47`** — 2 verified bugs:
- **`update-ecc.sh`**: now uses `git remote show origin` to detect the actual default branch (`main` or `master`), so BEHIND count and `git pull` both work correctly regardless of repo convention
- **`consolidate-learnings.py`**: changed bare `except:` → `except Exception:` at both occurrences — prevents silently swallowing `KeyboardInterrupt`/`SystemExit`

**All audited issues resolved across all commits:**
| Issue | Status |
|---|---|
| Context limit (combined→lean injection) | ✅ Fixed |
| Password regex in classifier | ✅ Fixed |
| REPO_DIR undefined | ✅ Fixed |
| md5 portability | ✅ Fixed |
| Alias paths + `_copilot_with_skills()` | ✅ Fixed |
| Beads integration | ✅ Added |
| Script copy step | ✅ Fixed |
| verify.sh Copilot section | ✅ Added |
| install.sh --help completeness | ✅ Fixed |
| TROUBLESHOOTING.md missing | ✅ Created |
| Copilot skills docs (was wrong) | ✅ Fixed |
| update-ecc.sh branch detection | ✅ Fixed (this commit) |
| consolidate-learnings.py bare except | ✅ Fixed (this commit) |
